view.mc_trades.h1.title: // MC TRADES (BOOTSTRAP)
view.mc_trades.hint.cdf: Cumulative share of curves with ending equity ≤ x. Cyan dashed = start equity (read off "% of paths losing money"). Easier than the histogram for tail-probability questions.
view.mc_trades.hint.about: Draws N independent synthetic equity curves of length L by sampling R-multiples with replacement. Surfaces percentile + ruin-probability distribution so you can see realistic worst cases, not just expectancy. Deterministic given seed.
view.mc_trades.hint.histogram: Histogram of ending equity across all curves. Cyan dot = start equity. Red = 5th percentile (downside). Yellow = median. Green = 95th percentile.
view.mc_trades.err.invalid: Inputs invalid — non-empty R, n_curves > 0, trades_per_curve > 0

view.launcher.title: // HOME
view.live_scanner.title: // LIVE SCANNER · DayTradeDash replacement
view.tutorial.title: // TUTORIAL
view.tutorial.hint.density_chart: Visible (HTML-stripped) characters per section. Orthogonal to discrete item count: reveals dense-prose sections (long expository paragraphs) vs sparse sections (mostly bullet/link lists).
view.tutorial.empty_density_chart: No tutorial sections to chart density.
view.catalysts.title: // CATALYST RADAR · LIVE
view.catalyst_correlations.title: // CATALYST → PRICE-ACTION CORRELATIONS · LIVE
view.catalyst_correlations.intro: Every fresh SEC filing or PR-wire headline opens a 60-second watch window on its extracted tickers. The first trade that moves ≥2% from the catalyst-time baseline locks in a correlation row. Sentiment is scored by a conservative lexicon (no LLM). Latency column is the time from catalyst publish to the threshold-crossing trade.
view.catalyst_correlations.th.title: Headline
view.catalyst_correlations.empty.no_rows: No catalyst correlations yet. Waiting for SEC / PR-wire catalyst + ≥2% tape reaction within 60s.
view.uoa_stream.title: // UOA STREAM · LIVE
view.uoa_stream.intro: Rotating poller scans the top-20 most-active live-tick symbols every 60s, fetches each option chain off Yahoo, and runs the UOA scanner with default thresholds (vol/OI ≥ 2, volume ≥ 500, premium ≥ $100K). Above-ask / below-bid fills are flagged as aggressor side. TTS fires on hits above $500K.
view.uoa_stream.empty.no_rows: No UOA hits yet. The rotation poller starts 15s after server boot and scans on a 60s cycle.
view.gamma_squeeze.title: // GAMMA SQUEEZE CANDIDATES · LIVE
view.gamma_squeeze.intro: Polls the top-10 most-active symbols' Yahoo option chains every 90s, computes per-strike dealer-net gamma exposure with inline Black-Scholes, and flags any chain whose total GEX is negative ≥ $250M AND spot is within 2% of the largest-negative-strike pin. Negative GEX = dealers short gamma = momentum-amplifying re-hedging. TTS on every fresh hit.
view.gamma_squeeze.empty.no_rows: No gamma squeeze candidates yet. Poller scans every 90s — waiting for a chain with negative total GEX ≥ $250M AND spot within 2% of pin strike.
view.htb_ranker.title: // HARD-TO-BORROW SQUEEZE RANKER
view.htb_ranker.intro: Real borrow-fee data requires IB / Markit. This is the standard free-data proxy: composite of short_pct_float (30%) + days_to_cover (20%) + month-over-month change in shares short (30%) + inverse-float (20%), scored 0-100. Top-30 most-active symbols refresh server-side every 30 min from Finnhub free tier.
view.htb_ranker.empty.no_rows: No ranked HTB rows yet. First refresh runs 30s after server boot then every 30 min.
view.breadth_divergence.title: // BREADTH DIVERGENCE · LIVE
view.breadth_divergence.intro: Samples (SPY price, breadth composite score) every 5 min into a 60-sample rolling window (~5 hrs of intraday). Flags BEARISH when price drifts up ≥1% while breadth averages ≤ -15 (rally on weak internals), or BULLISH when price drifts down ≥1% while breadth averages ≥ +15 (selling on strong internals). Regime changes only — no spam on persistent state.
view.breadth_divergence.empty.no_rows: No divergence events yet. Window fills over the first ~30 min of trading.
view.rvol_accel.title: // REL-VOL ACCELERATION · LIVE
view.rvol_accel.intro: Every tape print folds into a per-symbol 1-minute bucket. When the last 3 consecutive bars strictly increase in volume AND the latest bar is ≥ 5× the prior baseline (mean of older bars), a fresh acceleration event fires. Same-run duplicates are suppressed until the sequence breaks and re-forms.
view.rvol_accel.empty.no_rows: No acceleration events yet. Need ≥5 min of trades before the detector arms.
view.insider_stream.title: // INSIDER FORM 4 STREAM · LIVE
view.insider_stream.intro: Subscribes to the SEC EDGAR catalyst stream, filters to Form 4 filings, fetches each one's XML, and extracts insider name, title (officer/director/10%-owner), transaction code, shares, and price-per-share. Cluster insider buys are the academically-best free signal — Lakonishok & Lee 2001 found ~5-10% annual outperformance over the next 6-12 months. TTS fires on every officer Buy ≥ $1M.
view.insider_stream.empty.no_rows: No insider Form 4 events yet. Waiting for EDGAR to push fresh Form 4 filings — typically dozens per market hour.
view.insider_stream.empty.no_top_buys: No insider buys in the last 30 days above the filter threshold.
view.insider_clusters.title: // INSIDER CLUSTER SCORING (30-DAY)
view.insider_clusters.intro: Riding on the live insider_stream Form-4 firehose, this view groups buys per symbol over the trailing 30 days and ranks by a composite of distinct-insider-count + role-mix (officers weighted 3×, directors 1.5×, 10%-owners 1×) + recency-weighted $ value (7-day half-life). Cohen, Malloy & Pomorski 2012 documented that opportunistic clusters of ≥3 insiders predict ~12% alpha over the next 12 months — one of the largest single-signal edges available from free data. Sells are excluded from this ranking; the existing Insider Form 4 view shows them separately.
view.insider_clusters.empty.no_rows: No insider clusters in the trailing 30 days. Detector needs ≥2 distinct insiders buying open-market on the same symbol — sells excluded.
view.earnings_revisions.title: // EARNINGS REVISION TRACKER
view.earnings_revisions.intro: For each symbol, pulls the Yahoo earningsTrend.epsTrend period (preferring +1q if available, else 0q) and computes revision velocity: rev_pct_30d, rev_pct_90d, and an accelerating flag when the 30-day move dominates the 90-day move (≥40% of magnitude with the same sign). Composite score blends absolute magnitudes with the acceleration bonus. Sources are Womack 1996 (recommendation upgrades → ~3% abnormal returns over 30 days) and follow-ups documenting analyst-forecast-revision momentum as a PEAD-cousin signal.
view.earnings_revisions.empty.hint: Enter symbols and click Scan — fetches Yahoo quoteSummary per name.
view.earnings_revisions.empty.no_rows: No revision rows — Yahoo earningsTrend may be empty or fetch failed.
view.earnings_revisions.status.scanning: scanning… one Yahoo quoteSummary call per symbol, ~120ms each
view.sector_timing.title: // SECTOR RS ROTATION TIMING
view.sector_timing.intro: Sits on top of the existing sector_rotation report (11 SPDR sectors vs SPY, 60-day daily-RS sparkline per sector) and folds three timing signals into a per-sector composite score: (1) fresh 20d-MA-above-60d-MA crossover today, (2) 5-day OLS slope of cumulative RS exceeding the 20-day slope (acceleration), (3) current cumulative RS within 5% of — or above — its 60-day high (breakout proximity). Ranks sectors entering leadership above sectors currently in steady leadership — that's where the rotation-trade alpha lives.
view.sector_timing.empty.no_rows: No sector timing rows — sector_rotation report empty or daily bars not yet populated.
view.pead.title: // POST-EARNINGS DRIFT (PEAD)
view.pead.intro: Ball & Brown 1968 → Chordia/Shivakumar 2006: stocks beating consensus EPS drift positively for ~60 trading days; misses drift negatively. Score = sign(surprise) × return_20d. Positive score means the drift confirmed the surprise direction — a tradeable PEAD setup. Filter is min |surprise| ≥ 2%.
view.pead.empty.no_rows: No PEAD rows. Earnings calendar may be empty or all surprises were below the 2% noise floor.
view.sentiment_velocity.title: // SENTIMENT VELOCITY · LIVE
view.sentiment_velocity.intro: Wraps the existing WSB + Stocktwits mentions firehose. Every 15 min the detector queries hour-over-hour ranked counts. A symbol fires when its ratio is ≥3× and current count ≥20 for 2 consecutive checks. 12-hour cooldown per symbol blocks spam. Single hour of quiet resets the consecutive counter — sustained acceleration is the signal, one-off spikes aren't. TTS on every fresh velocity event.
view.sentiment_velocity.empty.no_rows: No velocity events yet. Detector arms after 30s and re-checks every 15 min.
view.confluence.title: // CONFLUENCE · MULTI-SCANNER INTERSECTION · LIVE
view.confluence.intro: Subscribes to every scanner broadcast (after-hours, catalysts, catalyst correlations, gamma squeeze, halts, insider buys/sells, RVOL accel, sentiment velocity, squeeze detector, UOA) and ranks symbols by a confluence score: Σ source_weight × recency_weight(half-life 4h) + diversity_bonus. Diversity bonus kicks in from the 3rd distinct source. The top of this list is where the most edges agree right now — highest-conviction long candidates. Insider sells subtract from score. TTS fires when a row breaks score 10.
view.confluence.empty.no_rows: No confluence rows yet. Detector needs trailing-window events from ≥ min-sources distinct scanners — give the scanners a few minutes to populate.
view.vrp.title: // VOL RISK PREMIUM (IV ÷ RV)
view.vrp.intro: For each of the top-30 most-active symbols, computes annualised 20-day realized vol from daily closes (Yahoo cache) and ATM call implied vol from the option expiration nearest 30 DTE. SELL direction sorts by descending IV/RV — overpriced premium, candidates for short straddles / iron condors / credit spreads. BUY direction ranks ascending — underpriced premium, contrarian long-vol setups. Historical SPX mean IV/RV ≈ 1.2-1.3; anything above ~1.6 for a single name is meaningfully rich. Refreshes every 60 min in the background.
view.vrp.empty.no_rows: No VRP rows yet. Refresher starts ~60s after boot and updates hourly.
view.pairs_coint.title: // PAIRS COINTEGRATION SCANNER
view.pairs_coint.intro: For every symbol-pair in the input list: OLS regression to find hedge ratio β, spread = y - β·x, AR(1) coefficient ρ on the spread, half-life of mean reversion = -ln(2) / ln(ρ), and current z-score. Surfaces pairs with |z| ≥ 2 AND half-life ≤ 30 days — the classic stat-arb entry signal. Default universe is liquid mega-caps + ETFs. Pure compute over daily bars (180-day lookback).
view.pairs_coint.empty.hint: Enter symbols and click Scan — first scan can take ~10-30s depending on bar-cache state.
view.pairs_coint.empty.no_symbols: Enter at least two comma-separated symbols.
view.pairs_coint.empty.no_rows: No cointegrated pairs with |z|≥2 and half-life≤30d in this universe. Try a longer lookback or a different symbol set.
view.pairs_coint.status.scanning: scanning… OLS + AR(1) on every pair, may take a moment for the first run
view.ipo_lockups.title: // IPO LOCKUP EXPIRATIONS · 60-DAY HORIZON
view.ipo_lockups.intro: For every IPO in the trailing 200 days that's priced + tradeable, projects the standard 180-day lockup expiration. Surfaces ones expiring within the next 60 days. Field & Hanka 2001 and follow-ups document a measurable price drop in the days around lockup expiry from insider supply hitting the tape — a mechanical edge that persists because the constraint is structural. Unlocked-share estimate is conservative (3× IPO float; actual S-1 tranches typically 5-8×).
view.ipo_lockups.th.name: Name
view.ipo_lockups.empty.no_rows: No lockup expirations in the next 60 days, or Finnhub returned no priced IPOs in the trailing 200d.
view.iv_term.title: // IV TERM STRUCTURE · CALENDAR-SPREAD CANDIDATES
view.iv_term.intro: For each top-30 most-active symbol, pulls ATM call IV from every available expiration (up to 10) and computes the term-structure shape. Inversion score = front-2-expiry avg IV − back-2-expiry avg IV. Above ~5pp (≥0.05) signals an event-priced front-month → classic calendar spread entry: sell the rich front-month, buy the relatively-underpriced back-month. P&L crystallises when the front-month IV decays to the long-term level. Refreshes every 4 hours.
view.iv_term.empty.no_rows: No IV term-structure rows yet. Background refresher samples every 4 hours; first scan runs ~90s after boot.
view.sp500_predict.title: // S&P 500 INCLUSION PREDICTOR
view.sp500_predict.intro: Scores a user-supplied list of symbols against the official S&P DJI methodology criteria — market cap ≥ $22.7B, public float ≥ 50%, annualised $-volume / FFMC ≥ 1.0, US-domiciled, trailing-4Q aggregate AND most-recent-quarter GAAP net income > 0. Composite 0-100 + per-criterion pass/fail. Names scoring 100 with passes_all are mechanical-inclusion candidates for the next quarterly rebalance — historically a ~6% announcement-day pop + ~3% effective-date pop from passive-fund forced buying (Lynch & Mendenhall 1997, Chen Noronha Singal 2004, follow-ups since). First scan can take 30-90s while Yahoo quoteSummary fetches each name.
view.sp500_predict.empty.hint: Enter symbols and click Scan — fetches Yahoo quoteSummary per name (paced).
view.sp500_predict.empty.no_rows: No symbols scored — check input or backend connectivity.
view.sp500_predict.status.scanning: scanning… one Yahoo quoteSummary per symbol, ~120ms each
view.dividend_capture.title: // DIVIDEND CAPTURE / ARB SCANNER
view.dividend_capture.intro: Two strategies in one ranking. Long capture buys before ex-div, sells after — empirically the price drop captures only ~60-80% of the dividend, leaving a ~30% retention edge per cycle. Short arb shorts the price-drop portion, pays borrow + tx friction. Borrow cost is proxied from short_pct_float (no paid SLB feed): <5%→0.25%, 5-15%→2%, 15-30%→8%, >30%→25% annualised. Ranking by best variant — high-yield easy-to-borrow names favour short arb; high-yield HTB names favour long. Default universe is a hand-picked liquid-dividend-payer list.
view.dividend_capture.empty.hint: Enter symbols and click Scan — fetches Yahoo dividends + Finnhub short stats per name.
view.dividend_capture.empty.no_rows: No positive-edge dividend capture candidates in this universe. Try a longer-yield list or check that ex-dates are within 45 days.
view.dividend_capture.status.scanning: scanning… one Yahoo dividends + one Finnhub short-stats call per symbol
view.multi_broker.title: // MULTI-BROKER POSITIONS
view.multi_broker.intro: Fans out positions calls across every linked broker, normalises into one row per (symbol, broker, account), and aggregates totals by symbol. Brokers without stored credentials are skipped silently; brokers whose call fails contribute zero positions and surface an error row. Currently wired: alpaca + tradier; ibkr / schwab / tastytrade add in follow-up commits. Below the totals table is a KILL-SWITCH that cancels every open order + closes every position across all linked brokers — destructive, gated by a typed confirmation token.
view.multi_broker.empty.no_positions: No positions found. Configure broker credentials in Settings, or all positions are flat.
view.multi_broker.kill.intro: Cancels every open order and closes every position across all linked brokers. Destructive — no undo. Requires typing KILL-ALL-NOW in the confirmation dialog. Use only when you genuinely want everything flat right now (model malfunction, risk-gate trip, emergency exit).
view.multi_broker.kill.dialog.title: 🚨 KILL-SWITCH CONFIRMATION
view.multi_broker.kill.dialog.message: This will cancel every open order and close every position across every linked broker. There is no undo. Type KILL-ALL-NOW to confirm.
view.halts.title: // HALT SCANNER · LIVE
view.after_hours.title: // AFTER-HOURS MOVERS · LIVE
view.after_hours.intro: Classifies every Finnhub / Polygon / Alpaca trade by US-Eastern wall clock (PRE 04:00–09:30, POST 16:00–20:00) and ranks the largest signed moves vs the prior RTH close. Voice alert fires once per symbol when it crosses ±5%.
view.after_hours.empty.no_movers: No after-hours movers above the current filter. Threshold lower or wait for the tape to populate.

view.margin_runway.h1.title: // MARGIN RUNWAY
view.margin_runway.h2.projection: Equity vs maintenance under hypothetical price decline
view.margin_runway.hint.buffer_chart: Direct gap (equity − maintenance requirement) at each drop %. Crosses zero exactly at the call point. Easier to read than the level chart for "how many dollars before the call?"
view.margin_runway.empty_buffer_chart: Provide valid inputs to chart the equity buffer.
view.margin_runway.hint.about: Runway % = how far the position can fall before broker issues a margin call. Reg-T retail accounts default to 25% maintenance; brokers often raise it on concentrated / volatile names.
view.margin_runway.hint.chart: Cyan = equity after price drop. Yellow = maintenance requirement after drop. Margin call hits at the crossover (red dot). X axis: % price decline from current.

view.risk_parity.h1.title: // RISK PARITY
view.risk_parity.h2.weights_chart: RP vs EW weight per asset
view.risk_parity.tip.blob: One asset per line: SYMBOL vol (vol may be decimal 0.15 or 15%)
view.risk_parity.tip.allocate: Run the risk-parity allocator on the assets above
view.risk_parity.hint.about: Naive risk-parity: weight ∝ 1/σ, normalized to 1. Each asset contributes the same dollar variance — low-vol assets get a larger weight. Assumes uncorrelated returns; for real correlation matrices use HRP (coming soon).
view.risk_parity.hint.weights: Solid bars = risk-parity weight. Hollow = naive 1/N equal weight. The wider the gap, the more vol dispersion in the basket.
view.risk_on_off.h1.title: // RISK-ON / RISK-OFF
view.risk_on_off.hint.noise_multiple: Each signal's raw value divided by its noise floor (0.1% for SPY/Gold/DXY, 1bp for yields), keeping the sign. ±1 = at the floor; ±10 = ten×the floor. Reveals whether the regime is screaming or barely past the noise threshold.
view.risk_on_off.empty_noise_chart: No signals to chart noise multiples.
view.risk_on_off.tip.evaluate: Score the cross-asset snapshot and classify the regime
view.risk_on_off.hint.about: Heuristic regime: SPY direction +1, Gold inverse +1, DXY inverse +1, Yields +1 (positive correlation). Score ≥ +2 = ON; ≤ -2 = OFF; else neutral. Noise floor 0.1% / ±1bp.
view.risk_reward.h1.title: // RISK / REWARD
view.risk_reward.h2.levels_chart: Price levels: stop / entry / R-multiples / target
view.risk_reward.h2.dollars_chart: Dollar risk vs reward vs net
view.risk_reward.tip.compute: Size the position to your risk budget and report R:R + scale-outs
view.risk_reward.hint.about: qty = risk_budget / (stop_distance × multiplier). Breakeven win-rate = 1 / (1 + R). Scale-out plan: 1/3 at 1R, 1/3 at 2R, 1/3 at target.

view.wash_sale.tip.closings: All closed trades (winners ignored, losers checked against opens)
view.wash_sale.tip.openings: Recent buys — used as replacement-purchase candidates
view.wash_sale.tip.detect: Detect ±30-day replacement buys and estimate disallowed loss
view.wash_sale.hint.about: ±30-day bidirectional window. Boundary 30 days exactly = INSIDE. Disallowed loss = loss × min(replacement_qty, close_qty) / close_qty. Symbol match only — substantially-identical (options / ETFs / share classes) is out of scope.

view.buying_power.empty_chart: Need positive equity to chart sensitivity.
view.buying_power.empty_share_chart: Need positive equity + leverage to chart shares.
view.buying_power.tip.compute: Compute max notional + max shares for the account / leverage rules
view.buying_power.hint.about: FINRA Rule 4210 + Reg-T. Sub-$5 stocks require 100% initial in standard Reg-T (no leverage on penny stocks) — but PDT day-trades override even that. Portfolio margin treated as ~3× overnight, 6× PDT-day.
view.buying_power.note.sub5: sub-$5 stock — Reg-T requires 100% initial margin
view.buying_power.pdt.overnight: PDT flagged, overnight hold — no 4× boost
view.margin_call.h1.title: // MARGIN CALL DISTANCE
view.margin_call.h2.dropdown_chart: Equity %-of-LMV across a market drop sweep
view.margin_call.empty_chart: Provide a positive LMV to chart the drop sweep.
view.margin_call.h2.cushion_chart: Dollar cushion across market drop sweep
view.margin_call.hint.cushion_chart: Dollar cushion (equity − trigger LMV) at each market drop %. Reveals at exactly what drop the cushion crosses zero in dollar terms — useful because traders think in dollars at risk, not in equity ratios.
view.margin_call.empty_cushion_chart: Provide a positive LMV and valid maintenance% to chart cushion.
view.margin_call.tip.evaluate: Compute the dollar cushion before the broker issues a margin call
view.margin_call.hint.about: Trigger LMV = debt / (1 − maintenance%). Cushion = LMV − trigger. Cushion = 0 is NOT yet in call (Rust uses strict <). Reg-T retail standard is 25% maintenance; brokers raise for small-cap / volatile names.
view.margin_call.hint.bar: Cyan = current equity (LMV − debt). Yellow = trigger LMV. Bar shows current LMV; mark is where the call hits.
view.margin_call.empty: No positions — no margin call risk.
view.vix_term_structure.h1.title: // VIX TERM STRUCTURE
view.vix_term_structure.hint.premium_cha: Each tenor's IV as a percentage premium or discount to the VIX front month. Reveals curve shape independent of absolute level — useful when comparing low-vol vs high-vol regimes. Yellow dashed = VIX front (zero premium).
view.vix_term_structure.empty_premium_ch: Need a positive VIX front-month to chart premium.
view.vix_term_structure.tip.analyze: Classify VIX/VIX3M ratio + slope into contango / backwardation regime
view.vix_term_structure.hint.about: VIX/VIX3M ratio < 0.80 steep contango · < 1.00 contango · < 1.05 flat · < 1.20 backwardation · ≥ 1.20 severe. Slope = sum of consecutive-tenor diffs. Boundaries are strict <; e.g. ratio = 1.05 exactly → backwardation.
view.vix_term_structure.hint.chart: X axis: tenor in days. Y axis: implied vol. Upward slope = contango (normal). Downward = backwardation (fear).
view.vix_term_structure.note.steep_conta: very calm market — short vol favored
view.vix_term_structure.note.flat: neutral — front end aligning with mid term
view.vix_term_structure.note.backwardati: near-term fear — caution on long exposure
view.vix_term_structure.note.severe_back: extreme fear — historically a contrarian buy signal but in the moment indicates active stress
view.currency_exposure.h1.title: // CURRENCY EXPOSURE
view.currency_exposure.h2.positions_hint: (per line: SYMBOL CCY notional_in_native; negative = short)
view.currency_exposure.h2.fx_hint: (per line: CCY rate; rate = how many home-units = 1 foreign-unit)
view.currency_exposure.h2.net_chart: Net exposure per currency — directional FX bet (long vs short)
view.currency_exposure.empty_net_chart: No currency buckets to chart net exposure.
view.currency_exposure.tip.positions: Open positions in native currency. Group by CCY at analyze time.
view.currency_exposure.tip.fx: Spot FX rates to your home currency. Missing rate → 0 exposure.
view.currency_exposure.tip.analyze: Aggregate exposure by currency and flag overweight (>25%) buckets
view.currency_exposure.hint.about: Home currency gets rate 1.0. Missing FX → 0 exposure (defensive; warn in chart). Overweight = currency > 25% of total home-gross AND not the home currency. Buckets sorted by gross_home DESC.
view.bond_duration.h1.title: // BOND DURATION
view.bond_duration.h2.pv_chart: Discounted PV of each cash flow
view.bond_duration.empty_pv: No cash flows to chart PV.
view.bond_duration.tip.build_bond: Generate a standard coupon-bond CF schedule and load it into the textarea
view.bond_duration.tip.cash_flows: Each line: time-from-now in years + cash amount (par + final coupon on the last line)
view.bond_duration.tip.compute: Compute Macaulay + Modified duration and the price sensitivity grid
view.bond_duration.hint.builder: Generates a standard coupon-bond cash-flow schedule and replaces the textarea below.
view.bond_duration.hint.about: Macaulay = weighted-avg time-to-CF. Modified = Macaulay / (1 + ytm/m). Rule of thumb: ΔP/P ≈ -ModDur × Δy. Compounding 2 = semi-annual (US Treasury convention); 1 = annual.
view.bond_duration.hint.sensitivity: Linear approximation only — for large yield moves convexity dominates. Good enough for ±100 bps shocks on intermediate paper.
view.carry_score.h1.title: // CARRY SCORE
view.carry_score.h2.diff_chart: Score sensitivity to rate differential (fixed vol)
view.carry_score.empty_chart: Provide rates to chart score sensitivity.
view.carry_score.empty_diff_chart: Provide positive vol to chart differential sensitivity.
view.carry_score.tip.score: Compute the Sharpe-style FX-carry attractiveness score
view.carry_score.hint.about: Score = (long − funding) / vol. ≥ 1.0 strong · ≥ 0.5 okay · < 0.5 poor · negative differential overrides. Sharpe-like FX-carry attractiveness; ignores skew + jump risk.
view.carry_score.note.strong: Strong risk-adjusted carry — vol is comfortably below the differential.
view.carry_score.note.okay: Acceptable carry — sizing should respect the half-vol cushion.
view.carry_score.note.poor: Differential too thin for the vol; one realized move erases the yield.
view.carry_score.note.negative: Funding costs MORE than the long pays — anti-carry; consider reversing the pair.
view.yield_curve.h1.title: // YIELD CURVE
view.yield_curve.hint.short_spread_chart: Each tenor's yield minus the 3M short-end yield, in basis points. Positive = term-premium normal; negative across the belly = inversion. Yellow dashed = the 3M anchor (zero). Orthogonal to the absolute-yield curve above.
view.yield_curve.empty_short_spread_char: Need a valid 3M front yield to chart spreads.
view.yield_curve.tip.classify: Classify curve shape (normal / flat / inverted / humped) + spreads
view.yield_curve.hint.about: Priority: 2Y/10Y inversion → INVERTED (recession). Else humped (5Y peak) → HUMPED. Else all-spreads < 25 bps → FLAT. Else mostly non-decreasing → NORMAL. Otherwise → FLAT (mixed).
view.yield_curve.hint.chart: X axis: tenor in years. Y axis: yield %. Upward slope = normal expansion. Downward = inversion = recession signal.
view.yield_curve.note.humped: 5Y peak with both ends lower — rare transitional shape
view.cost_basis.h1.title: // COST BASIS
view.cost_basis.h2.method_compare_chart: Realized total across all 4 methods — tax-optimization landscape
view.cost_basis.empty_method_chart: Provide lots, qty, and price to compare methods.
view.cost_basis.tip.lots: Each open cost lot in this position — id, acquired date, qty, cost basis per share
view.cost_basis.tip.compute: Apply selected method + sell qty; emit per-lot closing schedule
view.cost_basis.tip.optimize: Switch to whichever method minimizes the tax bill for these lots + price
view.cost_basis.hint.about: FIFO = oldest first (IRS default). LIFO = newest. HIFO = highest cost (minimize gain). LOFO = lowest cost (maximize gain — useful for tax-loss carryforward). Realized = (price − cost) × qty per lot.
view.stop_loss_backtest.h1.title: // STOP-LOSS BACKTEST
view.stop_loss_backtest.h2.realized_char: Realized P&L per trade (stop-hits in red)
view.stop_loss_backtest.h2.trades_hint: (per line: entry MAE MFE actual_exit — MAE/MFE are POSITIVE magnitudes)
view.stop_loss_backtest.tip.trades: Each row: trade entry price + worst adverse excursion (MAE) + best favorable (MFE) + actual exit
view.stop_loss_backtest.tip.simulate: Replay all trades through the stop method and compute realized P&L
view.stop_loss_backtest.hint.about: Replays each trade through the chosen stop rule. If MAE breaches the stop, exit at stop (realized = stop − entry). Otherwise exit at actual_exit. Same engine as the best-of view but compares one method instead of N.
view.stop_loss_backtest.method.atr_multi: ATR × N — exit at entry ∓ N×ATR
view.futures_roll.h1.title: // FUTURES ROLL SCHEDULE
view.futures_roll.tip.positions: Each open futures contract — symbol, signed contracts, expiry date
view.futures_roll.tip.schedule: Classify each position into now / soon / comfortable / expired buckets
view.futures_roll.hint.about: Urgency: < 0d EXPIRED · ≤ window NOW · ≤ 2× window SOON · else COMFORTABLE. Most futures traders roll 5-10 days before expiry to avoid physical delivery.
view.heatmap_dow_hour.h1.title: // DOW × HOUR HEATMAP
view.heatmap_dow_hour.h2.trades_hint: (per line: YYYY-MM-DD hour net_pnl  OR  YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm net_pnl)
view.heatmap_dow_hour.h2.hour_chart: Aggregate P&L per hour (across all days)
view.heatmap_dow_hour.empty_chart: No trades to chart per hour.
view.heatmap_dow_hour.h2.dow_chart: Aggregate P&L per day of week (across all hours)
view.heatmap_dow_hour.hint.dow_chart: Sum of net P&L per weekday. Reveals which calendar days are systematically profitable vs lossy independent of intraday timing.
view.heatmap_dow_hour.empty_dow_chart: No trades to chart per day-of-week.
view.heatmap_dow_hour.tip.trades: Each closed trade: date + entry hour (UTC) + net P&L. Open trades skipped.
view.heatmap_dow_hour.tip.build: Bucket trades into 7×24 day-of-week × hour-of-day grid
view.heatmap_dow_hour.hint.about: 7×24 grid. Rows = day of week (Sun → Sat). Cols = hour of day (0 → 23 UTC). Cell color = net P&L (green = winning, red = losing, blank = no trades).
view.atr_cone.h1.title: // ATR CONE
view.atr_cone.tip.project: Project ±1σ / ±2σ bands forward using σ_N = ATR × √N
view.atr_cone.hint.about: σ_N = ATR × √N (Brownian scaling). Bands at ±1σ and ±2σ. Stops inside ±1σ get knocked out by noise; targets outside ±2σ need an edge to hit. Horizon capped at 1000 days server-side.
view.atr_cone.hint.chart: Center cyan = entry. Yellow = ±1σ band (≈68% range). Red = ±2σ band (≈95% range).
view.atr_cone.hint.width_chart: |upper_2σ − lower_2σ| vs days forward. Confirms the √N scaling: doubling horizon ≈ ×√2 width.
view.round_levels.h1.title: // ROUND-NUMBER LEVELS
view.round_levels.h2.dist_chart: Distance to current price ($) per level
view.round_levels.h2.levels: Detected levels (sorted by distance from current price)
view.round_levels.tip.detect: Walks integer prices in [price-window, price+window], classifies by largest divisor, returns nearest above/below
view.round_levels.hint.about: Major = ÷1000, ÷500, ÷100. Medium = ÷50, ÷25. Minor = any other integer. Windows over the integer cap short-circuit to empty (memory guard).
view.round_levels.empty: No round-number levels in the current window.
view.kyles_lambda.h1.title: // KYLE'S LAMBDA
view.kyles_lambda.h2.flow_hint: (per line: price_change signed_volume; + = net buy, − = net sell)
view.kyles_lambda.hint.scatter: The raw data Kyle's OLS is fit on. Tight line ≈ high R² = trustworthy λ. Cloud = low R² = noisy estimate. Orange dashed = overall-mean λ fit line through origin.
view.kyles_lambda.empty_scatter_chart: Need matched Δp / signed_volume pairs to chart.
view.kyles_lambda.tip.flow: Per-bar paired (Δp, signed_volume). Signed = Lee-Ready classified or buy-vs-sell net.
view.kyles_lambda.tip.compute: Rolling closed-form OLS slope (no intercept). λ = Σxy / Σx².
view.kyles_lambda.hint.about: Kyle (1985): Δp = λ · signed_volume + ε. Rolling closed-form OLS, no intercept. LOW |λ| = deep liquid book. HIGH |λ| = thin book. Sign flip → flow-vs-price regime change.
view.kyles_lambda.empty: No bars yet — paste flow above.
view.hawkes.h1.title: // HAWKES INTENSITY
view.hawkes.h2.chart: λ(t) over query grid (• = event timestamps)
view.hawkes.hint.inter_arrival: Sorted gaps between consecutive events. Pure Poisson with rate μ ≈ exponential distribution; Hawkes clustering bunches small gaps on the left. Yellow dashed line = expected mean gap 1/μ.
view.hawkes.empty_inter: Need at least 2 events for inter-arrival gaps.
view.hawkes.tip.events: Sorted ascending. Strictly before query t contributes excitation.
view.hawkes.tip.compute: λ(t) = μ + Σ α·exp(−β(t − t_i)) over events strictly before t.
view.hawkes.hint.about: λ(t) = μ + Σ α·exp(−β(t − t_i)). Branching ratio α/β: stable < 1, critical near 1, explosive ≥ 1. Used in HFT trade-clustering, news-cascade modeling.
view.hawkes.empty: No intensities yet — paste events above.
view.hawkes.err.server_rejected: Server rejected inputs (params or sort failed validation)
view.kagi.h1.title: // KAGI CHART
view.kagi.h2.moves_chart: Per-line signed move (up positive, down negative)
view.kagi.hint.moves: Bar magnitude per kagi line, signed by direction. Reveals the line-level strength distribution that the cumulative polyline above obscures by stacking. Yellow dashed = zero reference.
view.kagi.empty_moves_chart: No kagi lines to chart per-line moves.
view.kagi.tip.closes: Comma/whitespace separated close prices; lines are drawn until price reverses by ≥ reversal.
view.kagi.tip.compute: Direction holds while extending; flips when reverse-distance ≥ reversal threshold.
view.kagi.hint.about: Direction holds until price reverses by ≥ reversal against the running extreme. Yang (thick) = up line crossing prior peak. Yin (thin) = down line crossing prior trough. Time axis is line-count, not bars — characteristic of Kagi.
view.kagi.empty: No Kagi lines yet — paste closes above.
view.rp_solver.h1.title: // RISK-PARITY SOLVER (ERC)
view.rp_solver.h2.cov_hint: (one row per line; comma/whitespace cells; must be square + symmetric)
view.rp_solver.h2.rc_chart: Risk contribution share per asset (% of σ)
view.rp_solver.hint.rc_chart: Each asset's risk contribution as a fraction of total portfolio volatility. At ERC convergence every bar sits on the yellow 1/N reference. Bars above the line = risk-overweight; below = risk-underweight.
view.rp_solver.tip.cov: Paste a square symmetric covariance matrix; each row/column is one asset.
view.rp_solver.tip.solve: Spinu (2013) fixed-point: w_i ← (1/n)·σ_p/(Σw)_i, normalize, repeat until max |RC − target| < tolerance.
view.rp_solver.hint.about: Spinu (2013) fixed-point ERC. Each asset's contribution to portfolio variance is equalized — concentration is risk-weighted, not dollar-weighted. Used by Bridgewater All Weather, AQR, others. Converges in O(n²) per iteration.
view.rp_solver.empty: No solution yet — paste a covariance matrix above.
view.rp_solver.err.degenerate: Numerical degeneracy (singular covariance or zero variance)
view.rp_solver.err.server_rejected: Server rejected inputs (singular covariance or bad params)
view.vap.h1.title: // VOLUME AT PRICE
view.vap.h2.cum_chart: Cumulative volume share by price bin (low → high)
view.vap.hint.cum_chart: Running cumulative volume fraction as price rises through the bins. Reveals concentration: a steep early rise = volume sits at the lows; a steep late rise = at the highs; a near-diagonal = uniform. Orthogonal to the per-bin histogram above. Yellow dashed = 50 % accumulation reference.
view.vap.empty_cum_chart: No bins to chart cumulative share.
view.vap.tip.bars: One bar per line: high low volume. Each bar's volume is split across price buckets it overlaps.
view.vap.tip.compute: Bucket [min low, max high] into N bins; each bar contributes proportional to its overlap with each bin.
view.vap.hint.about: Each bar's volume is split across price buckets it overlaps, proportional to overlap length. POC = bin with the most volume (price the market accepted most). Value Area = bracket around POC covering N% of total volume.
view.vap.empty: No volume profile yet — paste bars above.
view.hhi.h1.title: // HERFINDAHL (HHI)
view.hhi.h2.positions_hint: (per line: "weight" or "label weight"; absolute or normalized weights)
view.hhi.h2.lorenz_chart: Lorenz curve — cumulative share of weight by rank
view.hhi.hint.lorenz: Cumulative normalized weight from largest to smallest position. Yellow dashed line = perfect equal-weight line. The further the curve sits below the diagonal, the more concentrated the portfolio.
view.hhi.empty_lorenz: No positions to chart cumulative concentration.
view.hhi.tip.positions: One position per line. Single token = weight only (auto-labeled). Two tokens = label + weight.
view.hhi.tip.compute: HHI = Σwᵢ² over positive weights. Auto-normalizes if weights don't sum to 1.
view.hhi.hint.about: HHI = Σwᵢ². Scaled 0–10,000 (DOJ scale). Effective N = 1/HHI = "equivalent equal-weight positions." DOJ antitrust thresholds: ≥ 1500 moderate · ≥ 2500 highly concentrated.
view.hhi.col.label: Position
view.hhi.empty: No positions yet — paste weights above.
view.hhi.err.degenerate: Numerical degeneracy (all-zero weights or sum non-positive)
view.roll.h1.title: // ROLL SPREAD (1984)
view.roll.h2.prices_hint: (one price per token; comma/whitespace separated; # comments ignored)
view.roll.hint.hist: 20-bucket histogram of populated per-bar spread values in $. Reveals whether liquidity stays tight (unimodal narrow), regime-switches (bimodal), or has fat tails (occasional liquidity holes) — orthogonal to the rolling time series above.
view.roll.empty_hist: Not enough spread points to histogram.
view.roll.tip.prices: Trade prints; one price per token. The Roll estimator infers spread from negative serial covariance of consecutive Δp.
view.roll.tip.compute: spread = 2·√(−cov(Δp_t, Δp_{t−1})) when cov < 0; otherwise 0 (positive cov = informed trading).
view.roll.hint.about: spread = 2·√(−cov(Δp_t, Δp_{t−1})). Negative serial covariance from bid/ask bouncing reveals the implicit spread; positive cov (trending) collapses estimate to 0. No quotes needed — works from trade prints alone.
view.roll.empty: No spread series yet — paste prices above.
view.tlb.h1.title: // THREE-LINE BREAK
view.tlb.hint.move_chart: Signed per-line magnitude. Reveals which lines represented strong trend pushes vs small qualifying-but-weak moves. Orthogonal to the absolute polyline above. Yellow dashed = zero.
view.tlb.tip.closes: Comma/whitespace separated close prices; lines continue on every new close, flip only on N-line break.
view.tlb.tip.compute: Continuation: close beyond prior line's close. Reversal: close past the open of the last N same-direction lines.
view.tlb.hint.about: Continuation: close beyond prior line's close. Reversal requires breaking the OPEN of the last N lines (default 3). N=2 is sensitive, N=5 is slow.
view.tlb.empty: No TLB lines yet — paste closes above.
view.mcp.h1.title: // MOMENTUM CRASH PROTECTION
view.mcp.h2.returns_hint: (one per token; raw decimal 0.012 or pct "1.2%"; # comments ignored)
view.mcp.hint.drawdown: Per-bar drawdown from peak for each equity curve. Cyan = managed, red dashed = raw. The gap shows where the crash filter saved drawdown vs holding the unscaled strategy.
view.mcp.empty_dd_chart: No managed series to chart drawdown.
view.mcp.tip.returns: One momentum-strategy return per token. Raw decimal (0.012) or percent-suffix (1.2%).
view.mcp.tip.compute: w_t = min(target_vol / forecast_vol_t, max_leverage). Crash filter zeros leverage when trailing cumret < threshold.
view.mcp.hint.about: w_t = min(target_vol / forecast_vol_t, max_leverage). Crash filter zeros leverage when the trailing crash-lookback cumret < threshold. Defaults: 60-day vol, 22-day crash filter, 15% target ann. vol, −20% crash threshold.
view.mcp.empty: No managed series yet — paste returns above.
view.mcp.err.degenerate: Numerical degeneracy (series too short or bad params)
view.eff_spread.h1.title: // EFFECTIVE SPREAD
view.eff_spread.h2.obs_hint: (per line: trade_price current_mid delayed_mid quoted_spread direction)
view.eff_spread.h2.spread_chart: Effective vs realized spread per observation
view.eff_spread.h2.ratio_chart: Effective/quoted ratio per observation — <1 = price improvement, >1 = trade-through
view.eff_spread.empty_ratio_chart: No observations with quoted spread to chart ratio.
view.eff_spread.tip.obs: Per line: trade_price current_mid delayed_mid quoted_spread direction. Delayed mid is the mid 5min post-trade.
view.eff_spread.tip.compute: effective = 2·D·(trade − mid). realized = 2·D·(trade − delayed_mid). impact = effective − realized.
view.eff_spread.hint.about: effective = 2·D·(trade − mid). realized = 2·D·(trade − mid_delayed). impact = effective − realized. eff/quoted ratio: <1 = price improvement, >1 = trade-through. D=+1 buy, −1 sell.
view.eff_spread.err.degenerate: Numerical degeneracy (no valid observations after filtering)
view.wmp.h1.title: // WEIGHTED MIDPRICE
view.wmp.hint.spread_chart: Bid-ask spread divided by midpoint at each snapshot. Reveals liquidity quality independent of imbalance direction. Wide spreads dilute the microprice signal; tight spreads make microprice-vs-midpoint deviation meaningful. Orthogonal to the deviation/imbalance chart above.
view.wmp.empty_spread_chart: No snapshots to chart relative spread.
view.wmp.tip.quotes: Per line: bid_price bid_size ask_price ask_size. Top-of-book quote snapshots.
view.wmp.tip.compute: microprice = (bid·ask_sz + ask·bid_sz) / (bid_sz + ask_sz). Biases toward the lighter side.
view.wmp.hint.about: Stoikov (2017) microprice = (bid·ask_sz + ask·bid_sz) / (bid_sz + ask_sz). Biases toward the side with LESS size — the side likely to trade through soonest. Quote imbalance ∈ [−1, +1].
view.mvar.h1.title: // MARGINAL / COMPONENT VaR
view.mvar.h2.portfolio_hint: (weights section, blank line, then k×k covariance matrix)
view.mvar.h2.contrib: Contribution to portfolio VaR (% of total)
view.mvar.hint.weight_vs_contrib: Position weight on x, % contribution to portfolio VaR on y. Above the yellow y=x line = the position's risk share exceeds its weight (concentration). Below the line = diversifier.
view.mvar.empty_wvc_chart: No positions to chart weight vs VaR contribution.
view.mvar.tip.portfolio: Two sections: weights (one per line, optionally label-prefixed), blank line, then a square symmetric covariance matrix.
view.mvar.tip.compute: marginal_i = z·(Σw)_i / vol. component_i = w_i · marginal_i. Σ components = portfolio VaR.
view.mvar.hint.about: marginal_i = z·(Σw)_i / vol. component_i = w_i · marginal_i. Σ components = portfolio VaR. Σ pct = 100%. Marginal answers "how does VaR change if I add one unit of position i?" Component splits total VaR by position.
view.mvar.empty: No analysis yet — paste a portfolio above.
view.mvar.err.degenerate: Numerical degeneracy (dim mismatch / NaN / non-positive variance)
view.range_bar.h1.title: // RANGE BAR CHART
view.range_bar.h2.ticks_chart: Tick count per bar (effort to traverse the range)
view.range_bar.hint.ticks: How many prints it took to traverse target_range per bar. Many ticks = absorption / chop. Few ticks = momentum impulse blasting through. Independent of OHLC level.
view.range_bar.empty_ticks_chart: No bars to chart tick counts.
view.range_bar.tip.prints: Per line: price size. Each print contributes to the in-progress bar; bar closes when high − low ≥ target_range.
view.range_bar.tip.compute: high-low spread ≥ target_range → bar closes at the trigger print, new bar opens at same price.
view.range_bar.hint.about: Each bar accumulates prints until high − low ≥ target_range, then closes and starts a new bar. Time is ignored. Flat markets emit nothing; volatile markets emit many bars. Trailing partial bars are not emitted.
view.range_bar.empty: No bars yet — paste prints above.
view.tick_bar.h1.title: // TICK BAR CHART
view.tick_bar.h2.vol_chart: Per-bar volume (size aggregated within each tick window)
view.tick_bar.hint.vol_chart: Each bar's total trade size. Since each bar spans the same number of ticks (not the same wall-clock time), high-volume bars are heavy-print-size moments. Orthogonal to the OHLC chart above.
view.tick_bar.tip.prints: Per line: price size. Each print contributes to the in-progress bar; bar closes once tick_count reaches N.
view.tick_bar.tip.compute: Emit a new OHLC bar every N prints. Trailing partial bars are dropped.
view.tick_bar.hint.about: One OHLC bar per N prints. Time is ignored — useful in liquid markets where 1-minute bars contain wildly different trade counts. Trailing partial bars are not emitted.
view.tick_bar.empty: No bars yet — paste prints above.
view.vol_bar.h1.title: // VOLUME BAR CHART
view.vol_bar.h2.ticks_chart: Per-bar tick count (prints aggregated within each fixed-volume window)
view.vol_bar.hint.ticks_chart: Number of trade prints each bar consumed before crossing the volume threshold. Mirror of the OHLC view: since every bar carries the same volume, low-tick bars are heavy-print-size moments (few big trades) and high-tick bars are light-print-size moments (lots of small trades). Orthogonal to the price chart above.
view.vol_bar.empty_ticks_chart: No bars to chart tick counts.
view.vol_bar.tip.prints: Per line: price size. Each print contributes to the in-progress bar; bar closes when Σ volume ≥ volume_per_bar.
view.vol_bar.tip.compute: Emit a new OHLC bar each time accumulated volume reaches volume_per_bar. Trailing partial bars dropped.
view.vol_bar.hint.about: Each bar accumulates prints until cumulative volume ≥ volume_per_bar, then closes. Time is ignored. Useful in futures where calendar bars contain wildly different volumes (open vs lunch lull). Trailing partial bars are not emitted.
view.vol_bar.empty: No bars yet — paste prints above.
view.dollar_bar.h1.title: // DOLLAR BAR CHART
view.dollar_bar.h2.density_chart: Tick density per bar — how many prints aggregated into each bar
view.dollar_bar.empty_density_chart: Build bars to chart tick density.
view.dollar_bar.tip.prints: Per line: price size. Each print contributes price × size to the in-progress bar; bar closes when Σ ≥ dollars_per_bar.
view.dollar_bar.tip.compute: Emit a new OHLC bar each time accumulated notional reaches dollars_per_bar. Trailing partial bars dropped.
view.dollar_bar.hint.about: Each bar accumulates prints until Σ(price × size) ≥ dollars_per_bar. López de Prado (AFML) showed dollar bars best approximate i.i.d. returns for ML — they normalize for both activity AND price-level shifts. Trailing partial bars dropped.
view.dollar_bar.empty: No bars yet — paste prints above.
view.act_share.h1.title: // ACTIVE SHARE
view.act_share.h2.weights_hint: (per line: symbol portfolio_w benchmark_w — decimals or "40%" form)
view.act_share.h2.weights_chart: Portfolio vs benchmark weights per name
view.act_share.tip.weights: One row per name: symbol portfolio_weight benchmark_weight. Use decimals (0.30) or percent ('30%'). Names absent from one side should appear with weight 0.
view.act_share.hint.about: AS = ½·Σ|w_port − w_bench|. Range [0, 1]: 0 = closet indexer, 1 = disjoint from benchmark. Cremers & Petajisto found AS ≥ 0.60 correlates with managers who actually outperform their benchmark net of fees.
view.brinson.h1.title: // BRINSON ATTRIBUTION
view.brinson.h2.inputs_hint: (per line: sector port_w bench_w port_r bench_r — decimals or "5%")
view.brinson.h2.cum_chart: Cumulative total effect (A + S + I) per sector
view.brinson.tip.inputs: One row per sector: name port_weight bench_weight port_return bench_return. Weights/returns accept decimal or percent.
view.brinson.hint.about: A_i = (w_p − w_b)·(r_b,i − r_b,total). S_i = w_b·(r_p − r_b). I_i = (w_p − w_b)·(r_p − r_b). Σ(A+S+I) = portfolio total − benchmark total. Allocation answers "did we overweight winning sectors?" Selection answers "did we pick better stocks within each sector?"
view.brinson.empty: No sectors yet — paste inputs above.
view.brinson.empty_cum: No sectors yet — paste inputs above.
view.equivol.h1.title: // EQUIVOLUME BARS
view.equivol.h2.chart: Cumulative bar widths (each bar plotted at its center vs price midpoint)
view.equivol.h2.kind_chart: Bar count by Arms kind (Narrow / Normal / Wide / Power)
view.equivol.empty_kind_chart: Compute widths to chart kind counts.
view.equivol.tip.bars: One bar per line: high low volume. Width is allocated proportional to volume.
view.equivol.tip.compute: width_i = volume_i / Σvol × total_width. Tagged Narrow/Normal/Wide/Power per Arms convention.
view.equivol.hint.about: width_i = volume_i / Σvol × total_width. Tags: Narrow (≤0.5× avg vol) · Wide (>1.5× avg vol) · Power (wide AND range >1.5× avg range = strong conviction). Σ widths = total_width.
view.imb_bar.h1.title: // TICK IMBALANCE BAR
view.imb_bar.h2.chart: Signed imbalance + cumulative close per bar
view.imb_bar.h2.cum_imb_chart: Cumulative signed imbalance per bar (bar-grain OBV)
view.imb_bar.hint.cum_imb: Running Σ of signed imbalance bar-by-bar. Persistent buying drives a monotonic up-curve; persistent selling drives down; alternating flow wiggles around zero. Bar-grain analogue of OBV / cumulative delta.
view.imb_bar.empty_cum: No bars to chart cumulative imbalance.
view.imb_bar.tip.prints: Per line: price size. Sign rule: +1 uptick, −1 downtick, prior sign on tie.
view.imb_bar.tip.compute: Σ sign × size accumulates; bar closes when |Σ| ≥ imbalance_threshold. Trailing partial bars dropped.
view.imb_bar.hint.about: sign = +1 (uptick) / −1 (downtick) / prior_sign (tie). Bar closes when |Σ sign × size| ≥ imbalance_threshold. Trailing partial bars dropped. López de Prado AFML: better i.i.d. approximation than time bars.
view.blit.h1.title: // BLACK-LITTERMAN
view.blit.h2.blob_hint: (4 sections separated by blank lines: labels, equilibrium returns, n×n covariance, "tau X". Optional 5th: views as "name p_1 .. p_n q ω")
view.blit.h2.vol_chart: Prior σ vs posterior σ per asset
view.blit.tip.blob: Paste model inputs as 4 (or 5 with views) blank-line-separated sections.
view.blit.tip.compute: μ_bl = [(τΣ)⁻¹ + PᵀΩ⁻¹P]⁻¹·[(τΣ)⁻¹π + PᵀΩ⁻¹Q]. Σ_bl = Σ + A⁻¹.
view.blit.hint.about: μ_bl = [(τΣ)⁻¹ + PᵀΩ⁻¹P]⁻¹ · [(τΣ)⁻¹π + PᵀΩ⁻¹Q]. Σ_bl = Σ + A⁻¹. Low ω = high confidence (pulls posterior to view). High τ = weaker prior (more weight on views).

view.adf.h1.title: // ADF STATIONARITY TEST
view.adf.h2.series_hint: (one value per token; whitespace + commas separated; # comments)
view.adf.tip.series: Paste series; one value per token. Long series (≥200 points) recommended for reliable inference.
view.adf.tip.compute: Δy_t = α + γ·y_{t-1} + Σφ·Δy_{t-i} + ε. t-stat = γ / SE(γ).
view.adf.hint.about: Δy_t = α + γ·y_{t-1} + Σφ·Δy_{t-i} + ε. H₀: unit root (non-stationary). Reject when t-stat < critical (1% = −3.43, 5% = −2.86, 10% = −2.57). More-negative = more stationary.
view.adf.err.degenerate: Numerical degeneracy (series too short / NaN / singular regression)
view.aroon.h1.title: // AROON INDICATOR
view.aroon.tip.bars: Per line: high low. Aroon needs ≥ period+1 bars to emit.
view.aroon.hint.about: AroonUp = 100·(period − bars_since_high)/period. AroonDown = 100·(period − bars_since_low)/period. Oscillator = Up − Down ∈ [−100, +100]. ≥ 80 = strong trend. Crossovers signal reversals.

view.amihud.h1.title: // AMIHUD ILLIQUIDITY
view.amihud.h2.pairs_hint: (per line: return dollar_volume — return as 0.012 or "1.2%")
view.amihud.tip.pairs: One pair per line: return dollar_volume. Returns as decimal or pct-suffix; dollar volume in same currency.
view.amihud.tip.compute: illiq_t = |r_t|/dv_t · 10^6; rolling mean over period bars (zero/NaN bars skipped).
view.amihud.hint.about: illiq_t = |r_t|/dollar_vol_t · 10⁶. Higher = less liquid. Mean over rolling period bars. S&P 500 large-caps ≈ 0.0001; penny stocks ≈ 1.0+.

view.amihud.empty_dvol: No dollar-volume series yet — paste pairs above.
view.breadth.h1.title: // ZWEIG BREADTH THRUST
view.breadth.tip.days: Per line: advancing-issues declining-issues. NYSE breadth or any other index.
view.breadth.tip.compute: EMA of advancing/(adv+dec). Thrust triggers when EMA went from <low to >high within window.
view.breadth.hint.about: ratio_t = adv/(adv+dec). EMA over ema_period bars. Thrust triggers when EMA went from below low_threshold to above high_threshold within max_window_bars. Zweig defaults: 10 / 10 / 0.40 / 0.615.
view.breadth.empty: No breadth thrust series yet — paste days above.
view.breadth.empty_cum: Need ≥ 2 days for cum A-D chart.
view.bbsq.h1.title: // BOLLINGER SQUEEZE
view.bbsq.h2.closes_hint: (one value per token; whitespace + commas separated)
view.bbsq.tip.closes: Close prices, one per token. Needs ≥ lookback bars to emit squeeze states.
view.bbsq.hint.about: width = 2·n_stdev·σ/mean·100. squeeze_on if width ≤ min(width over lookback)·(1+slack). Defaults: 20 / 2.0 / 125 / 0.05. Squeezes precede volatility expansion.
view.bbsq.empty: No squeeze series yet — paste closes above.
view.bbsq.empty_close: No squeeze series yet — paste closes above.
view.bop.h1.title: // BALANCE OF POWER
view.bop.tip.bars: One OHLC bar per line. BOP measures who controlled each bar — buyers (+) or sellers (−).
view.bop.tip.compute: BOP = (close − open)/(high − low), clamped to [−1, +1]. Smoothed by SMA over smoothing_period.
view.bop.hint.about: BOP = (close − open) / (high − low) per bar. Clamped to [−1, +1]. Smoothed by SMA over smoothing_period bars. ≥ +0.5 = strong bull · ≤ −0.5 = strong bear · ±0.1 = balanced. Crossovers between raw and smoothed signal momentum shifts.

view.bop.empty_cum: No BOP series yet — paste bars above.
view.anch_mom.h1.title: // ANCHORED MOMENTUM
view.anch_mom.h2.closes_hint: (one per token; "NaN" tolerated; comments + blanks ignored)
view.anch_mom.tip.closes: Close prices — one per token. Anchor index is 0-based.
view.anch_mom.tip.compute: raw = (close − anchor_close) / anchor_close. Smoothed via linear-weight WMA over smooth_period bars.
view.anch_mom.hint.about: raw_i = (close_i − close_anchor) / close_anchor for i ≥ anchor. Smoothed = WMA(raw, smooth_period) with linear weights 1..N. smooth_period=1 → raw series. NaN bars block any window they fall in.
view.anch_mom.empty: No momentum series yet — paste closes above.
view.acf.h1.title: // AUTOCORRELATION (ACF)
view.acf.h2.series_hint: (one value per token; comments + blanks ignored)
view.acf.tip.series: Time series — one value per token. Bartlett bands shrink as √n grows.
view.acf.hint.about: ρ̂(k) = Σ(x_t−x̄)(x_{t−k}−x̄) / Σ(x_t−x̄)². ρ̂(0) = 1 by definition. Bartlett 95% bands = ±1.96/√n. Lags outside bands are statistically significant — evidence against white noise.

view.acf.empty_sig_chart: No ACF yet — paste series above.
view.acf.err.degenerate: Numerical degeneracy (series too short / NaN / zero variance)
view.beta.h1.title: // BETA REGRESSION
view.beta.h2.chart: Asset vs benchmark scatter + regression line
view.beta.tip.pairs: Per-period paired returns. Caller pre-aligns the series; same length on both sides.
view.beta.tip.compute: β = cov(asset, bench) / var(bench). α = mean(asset) − β·mean(bench).
view.beta.hint.about: β = cov(asset, bench) / var(bench). α = mean(asset) − β·mean(bench). R² = corr². Beta-neutral hedge: short $X·β of benchmark per $X long. α is per-period return after removing market exposure.
view.beta.empty: No beta estimate yet — paste paired returns above.
view.beta.empty_resid: No residuals — paste paired returns above.
view.beta.err.degenerate: Numerical degeneracy (zero-variance benchmark / NaN / short series)
view.brier.h1.title: // BRIER SCORE
view.brier.tip.pairs: Per line: probability + actual outcome (0 or 1). Caller pre-pairs each forecast with its eventual realization.
view.brier.tip.compute: BS = (1/N)·Σ(p−y)². Decomposed via Murphy into Reliability − Resolution + Uncertainty.
view.brier.hint.about: BS = (1/N)·Σ(p−y)². Range [0, 1]: 0 = perfect, 0.25 = balanced coin-flip. Murphy: BS = Reliability − Resolution + Uncertainty. Skill = 1 − BS/Uncertainty (> 0 beats climatology).
view.brier.empty: No forecasts yet — paste pairs above.
view.brier.empty_hist: No forecasts yet — paste pairs above.
view.bpv.h1.title: // BIPOWER VARIATION
view.bpv.tip.returns: High-frequency returns (intraday or daily). BPV needs ≥ 4 bars; longer = tighter test.
view.bpv.tip.compute: BPV = (π/2)·Σ|r_t|·|r_{t-1}|. Jump variation = max(0, RV−BPV). z-stat ~ N(0,1) under H₀ (no jumps).
view.bpv.hint.about: BPV = (π/2)·Σ|r_i|·|r_{i−1}|. Jump-robust IV estimator. Jump variation = max(0, RV − BPV). Huang-Tauchen z = √n·(RV−BPV)/BPV/√(θ·max(1, TQ/BPV²)) ~ N(0, 1) under H₀ (no jumps).
view.bpv.row.rv_note: Σ r² — total variance incl. jumps
view.bpv.row.bpv_note: Jump-robust IV estimate; consistent for continuous component
view.bpv.row.jump_note: max(0, RV − BPV); estimate of jump variation contribution
view.bpv.row.tq_note: Robust IQ estimator; scales the z-stat under heteroskedasticity
view.bpv.row.z_note: Standard-normal under H₀ (no jumps); large positive = jump
view.bpv.row.p_note: P(Z > z); reject H₀ when p < α (typ. 0.05)
view.bpv.empty: No BPV report yet — paste returns above.
view.chx.h1.title: // CHANDELIER EXIT
view.chx.h2.bars_hint: (3 tokens per line: high low close; ≥ period + 1 bars)
view.chx.h2.cushion_chart: Close-to-stop cushion (% of close) — negative = flip imminent
view.chx.empty_cushion_chart: Provide bars to chart close-to-stop cushion.
view.chx.tip.compute: Compute Chandelier Exit (HH/LL ± multiplier·ATR ratchet + flip).
view.chx.hint.about: ATR trailing stop hanging from HH (longs) or LL (shorts) over lookback. Direction flips when close crosses opposite stop; ratchets in favorable direction only. Defaults: period=22, mult=3.0.
view.chol.h1.title: // CHOLESKY DECOMPOSITION
view.chol.h2.matrix_hint: (one row per line; n×n with n ≤ 50)
view.chol.h2.coupling_chart: Per-row max |L[i][j]| for j<i (coupling strength to prior variables)
view.chol.empty_coupling_chart: Cholesky factor not available for coupling chart.
view.chol.tip.matrix: Whitespace or comma-separated rows; A must be symmetric and positive-definite.
view.chol.tip.compute: Compute Cholesky factor A = L · Lᵀ.
view.chol.hint.about: Lower-triangular factorization A = L · Lᵀ for symmetric positive-definite matrices. Used in correlated Monte Carlo (L·z transforms uncorrelated draws to a target covariance) and stable matrix inversion. Returns no factor if A is not symmetric or not positive-definite.
view.chol.no_factor: No factor — A is not positive-definite or not symmetric.
view.chol.empty: Paste a matrix to populate summary.
view.abc.h1.title: // ABC PATTERN
view.abc.h2.swings_hint: (one swing per line: index price kind; kind = high|low)
view.abc.h2.bcret_chart: B retrace vs C extension (per event)
view.abc.tip.swings: Whitespace or comma-separated swing pivots: index price kind (kind = high or low).
view.abc.tip.compute: Detect ABC correction events across the swing sequence.
view.abc.hint.about: Detects 3-pivot ABC corrections (A high → B low → C high, or mirror). B's location must satisfy ab/(ab+bc) ∈ [min_b_retrace, max_b_retrace]; C must extend bc ≥ ab · min_c_extension.
view.abs.h1.title: // ABSORPTION DETECTOR
view.abs.h2.bars_hint: (4 tokens per line: high low close volume)
view.abs.tip.bars: One bar per line: high low close volume (whitespace/comma).
view.abs.tip.period: Lookback window for range-per-volume and volume baselines.
view.abs.tip.threshold: Bar qualifies if range/vol < baseline · threshold (smaller = tighter ratio).
view.abs.tip.vol_mul: Bar qualifies if volume > baseline · multiplier.
view.abs.tip.compute: Detect absorption bars (heavy volume + minimal range progress).
view.abs.hint.about: Flags bars where range-per-volume is much smaller than recent baseline AND volume is much larger — one side absorbed the other's flow. Direction inferred from close vs midpoint and prior close.
view.cvi.h1.title: // CHANDE VOLATILITY INDEX
view.cvi.h2.bars_hint: (2 tokens per line: high low; ≥ ema_period + roc_period bars)
view.cvi.h2.range_chart: Per-bar range (high − low) — the underlying signal CVI smooths and differentiates
view.cvi.empty_range_chart: Provide bars to chart per-bar range.
view.cvi.tip.bars: Whitespace/comma-separated high low pairs per line.
view.cvi.tip.compute: Compute Chande Volatility Index (% change in EMA of range).
view.cvi.hint.about: Chande CVI = (EMA(range, ema_period) − EMA(range, ema_period) [roc_period bars ago]) / prev × 100. > 0 = expanding range; < 0 = contracting; |CVI| > 30 = notable shift. Distinct from Chaikin Volatility (different smoothing nuances). Defaults: 10 / 10.
view.cti.h1.title: // CHANDE TREND INDEX
view.cti.empty_dist_chart: Compute CTI to chart its value distribution.
view.cti.tip.compute: Compute Pearson correlation between closes and linear ramp.
view.cti.hint.about: CTI = Pearson correlation of closes vs perfect linear ramp 1..N. Range [−1, +1]: +1 = perfect uptrend, −1 = perfect downtrend, 0 = no trend / random walk. A TREND-STRENGTH metric, distinct from CMO (momentum). Default period=14.
view.cmo.h1.title: // CHANDE MOMENTUM OSCILLATOR
view.cmo.empty_dist_chart: Compute CMO to chart its value distribution.
view.cmo.hint.about: Unsmoothed RSI variant: CMO = 100 × (Σ ups − Σ downs) / (Σ ups + Σ downs). Range [−100, +100]. > +50 = strong upside (overbought); < −50 = strong downside (oversold). Responds faster than RSI to single large bars. Default period=14.
view.cks.h1.title: // CHANDE-KROLL STOP
view.cks.h2.bars_hint: (3 tokens per line: high low close; ≥ p + q bars)
view.cks.h2.spread_chart: Stop spread (short_stop − long_stop) — positive = long bias active
view.cks.empty_spread_chart: Provide bars to chart stop spread.
view.cks.hint.about: Two-pass volatility trailing stop: long_stop = highest(HH(p) − x·ATR(p), q); short_stop = lowest(LL(p) + x·ATR(p), q). When long_stop crosses BELOW short_stop, long bias is active. Defaults: p=10, x=1.0, q=9. Companion to Parabolic SAR, Volatility Stop.
view.cdmi.h1.title: // CHANDE DYNAMIC MOMENTUM INDEX
view.cdmi.h2.price_chart: Closes (underlying price) — for visual correlation with DMI signals
view.cdmi.empty_price_chart: Provide closes to chart price with DMI zone overlay.
view.cdmi.hint.about: Volatility-adapted RSI: vi = stdev / avg_stdev; td = round(td_const / vi) clamped to [td_min, td_max]. Quiet markets stretch the RSI period (more smoothing); volatile markets shrink it (more responsiveness). 0..100 range like classic RSI. Defaults: td_const=14, std_period=5, td_min=5, td_max=30.
view.chosc.h1.title: // CHAIKIN OSCILLATOR
view.chosc.h2.bars_hint: (4 tokens per line: high low close volume; ≥ slow bars)
view.chosc.empty_adl_chart: Provide bars to chart cumulative ADL.
view.chosc.tip.compute: Compute Chaikin MACD on cumulative ADL.
view.chosc.hint.about: MACD-style: CO = EMA(ADL, fast) − EMA(ADL, slow). Positive = short-term ADL momentum above long-term (buying pressure). Zero-line crossovers + price/CO divergences are the primary signals. Distinct from CMF (bounded rolling MFV). Defaults: 3/10.
view.csm.h1.title: // CENTERED SMOOTHED MOMENTUM
view.csm.h2.raw_chart: Raw momentum (unsmoothed; close[t] − close[t−n]) — what SuperSmoother filters
view.csm.empty_raw_chart: Provide closes to chart raw momentum.
view.csm.hint.about: Ehlers' SuperSmoother-filtered momentum: mom_t = close_t − close_{t−momentum_period}; csm_t = SuperSmoother(mom). Centered at zero. Positive = up-momentum, zero-cross signals trend turn. Defaults: momentum=10, smooth=8.
view.car4.h1.title: // CARHART 4-FACTOR MODEL
view.car4.h2.series_hint: (6 tokens per line: PORT MKT SMB HML WML RF — ≥ 10 valid rows)
view.car4.tip.series: Whitespace/comma-separated PORT MKT SMB HML WML RF per line.
view.car4.tip.compute: Compute Carhart 4-factor regression with standard errors.
view.car4.hint.about: Carhart (1997) extension of Fama-French 3-factor: Mkt (market excess), SMB (size), HML (value), WML (momentum) regress portfolio excess returns. β coefficients = factor loadings; α (intercept) = unexplained excess return. |t-stat| > 1.96 ≈ 5% significant. Non-finite rows skipped silently.
view.car4.err.degenerate: Regression degenerate — check sample size, finite values, and factor variation.
view.csi.h1.title: // CANDLE STRENGTH INDEX
view.csi.h2.bars_hint: (4 tokens per line: open high low close)
view.csi.h2.raw_chart: Raw per-bar body/range ratio (unsmoothed — what EMA is averaging)
view.csi.empty_raw_chart: Provide bars to chart raw body/range ratio.
view.csi.tip.compute: Compute EMA-smoothed (close − open) / (high − low) body-to-range ratio.
view.csi.hint.about: Per-bar signed (close − open) / (high − low) ratio, smoothed by an EMA. Range [−1, +1]: +1 = green marubozu, −1 = red marubozu, 0 = doji or balanced. Persistent positive readings = sustained buying commitment. Default period=14.
view.bg.h1.title: // BREUSCH-GODFREY SERIAL CORRELATION
view.bg.tip.pairs: Whitespace/comma-separated x and y values per line.
view.bg.tip.compute: Run Breusch-Godfrey LM test for serial correlation up to order p.
view.bg.hint.about: Tests H₀: OLS residuals have no serial correlation up to lag p. Fits y = α + β·x, regresses ε̂ on (1, x, ε̂_{t-1}…ε̂_{t-p}); LM = n_aux · R² ~ χ²(p). Allows lagged regressors (unlike Durbin-Watson). Reject → use HAC / Newey-West SEs.
view.bg.err.degenerate: Test degenerate — check sample size, lag, and x variance.
view.cam.h1.title: // CAMARILLA PIVOTS
view.cam.h2.session_hint: (HIGH LOW CLOSE — optional CURRENT_PRICE for zone verdict)
view.cam.h2.dist_chart: Signed distance to each level (current − level)
view.cam.empty_dist_chart: No current price to chart distance.
view.cam.tip.session: 3 or 4 numeric tokens: HIGH LOW CLOSE [CURRENT_PRICE].
view.cam.hint.about: 8 intraday S/R levels derived from prior session H/L/C × 1.1 constant. Trade rules: L3/H3 = reversal zones (long L3, short H3); H4/L4 = breakout / breakdown triggers. Symmetric around close. Pivot = (H + L + C) / 3.
view.burke.h1.title: // BURKE RATIO
view.burke.hint.about: Burke = (R − Rf) / √(Σ DD²). Penalizes only drawdown variance (vs Sharpe's full vol). Modified Burke annualizes by × √periods_per_year. Per-trough drawdowns measured between each pair of consecutive new highs.
view.burke.no_dd: No drawdown episodes in this series.
view.burke.err.degenerate: Compute degenerate — check series length and values.
view.bp.h1.title: // BREUSCH-PAGAN HETEROSKEDASTICITY
view.bp.h2.aux_chart: Squared residuals (ê²) vs x — BP auxiliary regression
view.bp.tip.pairs: Whitespace/comma-separated x and y values per line.
view.bp.tip.compute: Run Breusch-Pagan (1979) LM test for heteroskedasticity.
view.bp.hint.about: Tests H₀: OLS residual variance is independent of x. Fits y = α + β·x, then regresses ê² on x. LM = n · R²_aux ~ χ²(1) under H₀. p < 0.05 → reject homoskedasticity → use White / HC standard errors. Univariate predictor only.
view.bp.err.degenerate: Test degenerate — need ≥ 10 pairs with x variance.
view.borrow.h1.title: // BORROW RATE INDICATOR
view.borrow.tip.compute: Compute borrow rate change + stress classification.
view.borrow.hint.about: Tracks per-bar annualized securities-lending fee + N-bar rate of change. Classification: < 1% Low-Available · 1–10% Normal · 10–50% Tight · 50–200% Hard-to-Borrow · ≥ 200% OR change ≥ 100% Extreme Squeeze. Rising borrow cost flags increasing short demand → squeeze risk.
view.bbosc.h1.title: // BOLLINGER OSCILLATORS
view.bbosc.hint.about: Combined %B + Bandwidth oscillators. %B = (close − lower) / (upper − lower) ∈ [0, 1] at bands; > 1 breakout / < 0 breakdown. Bandwidth = (upper − lower) / middle squeezes absolute width into a unitless vol regime signal — low values flag the TTM-squeeze setup.
view.bbd.h1.title: // BOLLINGER BAND DISTANCE
view.bbd.tip.compute: Compute distance to nearest Bollinger Band, normalized by band width.
view.bbd.hint.about: BBD = min(|close − upper|, |close − lower|) / band_width. 0 = close exactly at one of the bands; 0.5 = midline; > 0.5 = outside the band (breakout). Distinct from %B which is signed position. Defaults: period=20, n_stdev=2.0.
view.bbpb.h1.title: // BOLLINGER %B
view.bbpb.hint.about: %B = (close − lower) / (upper − lower). Reads 0 at the lower band, 0.5 at midline, 1 at upper band. > 1 = breakout above upper; < 0 = breakdown below lower. Defaults: period=20, n_stdev=2.0.
view.bbwp.h1.title: // BOLLINGER BANDWIDTH PERCENTILE
view.bbwp.hint.about: BBWP = percent rank of BBW within the rolling lookback window. Range [0, 100]. < 10 = compression/squeeze (energy building); > 90 = expansion (vol top). Defaults: BB period=20, n_stdev=2.0, lookback=252.
view.bbw.h1.title: // BOLLINGER BAND WIDTH + %B
view.bbw.hint.about: Bollinger Bands: middle = SMA(close, period); upper = middle + k·σ; lower = middle − k·σ. BBW = (upper−lower)/middle measures volatility regime. %B = (close−lower)/(upper−lower) positions price within the bands (>1 above upper, <0 below lower). Defaults: 20 / 2.0.
view.bavr.h1.title: // BID/ASK VOLUME RATIO
view.bavr.validate.bar_vol_num: bars[{i}] bid_volume / ask_volume must be numbers
view.bavr.h2.bars_hint: (2 tokens per line: bid_volume ask_volume; ≥ period bars)
view.bavr.tip.compute: Compute rolling Σ bid / Σ ask over `period` bars.
view.bavr.hint.about: Rolling Σ bid / Σ ask over `period` bars (Lee-Ready classified trades). > 1.5 → sell pressure (sellers hitting bids). < 0.67 → buy pressure (buyers lifting offers). ≈ 1.0 balanced. Default period=60.
view.bartlett.h1.title: // BARTLETT VARIANCE TEST
view.bartlett.validate.total_obs_min: total observations must exceed group count
view.bartlett.h2.groups_hint: (one group per line: LABEL v1 v2 v3 ...)
view.bartlett.tip.groups: Each line: LABEL then ≥ 2 numeric observations.
view.bartlett.tip.compute: Test H₀: all groups share the same variance.
view.bartlett.hint.about: Tests H₀: all k groups share the same variance. χ² = ((N−k)·ln(σ²_pooled) − Σ(n_i−1)·ln(σ²_i)) / correction. Under H₀, χ² ~ χ²(k−1). More powerful than Levene if data is normal — sensitive to non-normality.
view.bartlett.col.label: Group
view.bartlett.err.degenerate: Test degenerate — need ≥ 2 groups with ≥ 2 obs and positive variance.
view.beta_shrink.h1.title: // BETA SHRINKAGE (Vasicek)
view.beta_shrink.h2.assets_hint: (one asset per line: SYMBOL r1 r2 r3 ...)
view.beta_shrink.empty_weight_chart: No assets to chart by weight.
view.beta_shrink.tip.assets: One SYMBOL per line followed by its return series.
view.beta_shrink.tip.compute: Vasicek (1973) Bayesian beta shrinkage toward cross-sectional mean.
view.beta_shrink.hint.about: Vasicek (1973) Bayes-optimal shrinkage: β̂ = w·β_OLS + (1−w)·β̄, w = σ²_cs/(σ²_cs + se²_OLS). Pulls noisy estimates toward cross-sectional mean β̄. High-se assets get strong shrinkage; tight-fit assets stay near OLS. Reduces estimation error vs raw OLS beta.
view.beta_shrink.err.degenerate: Shrinkage degenerate — check market variance and series lengths.
view.ado.h1.title: // A/D OSCILLATOR
view.ado.h2.bars_hint: (4 tokens per line: high low close volume; ≥ period bars)
view.ado.hint.about: Per-bar value = CLV × Volume, where CLV = ((C−L)−(H−C))/(H−L) ∈ [−1, +1]. EMA(period) smooths the per-bar series. Reads as current buying pressure — distinct from cumulative ADL. Default period=14.
view.asi.h1.title: // ACCUMULATION SWING INDEX
view.asi.h2.bars_hint: (4 tokens per line: open high low close)
view.asi.hint.about: Wilder's cumulative Swing Index: quantifies real price moves with OHLC + prior bar reference. ASI breakouts of prior extremes confirm genuine trend changes. limit_move is the market's max allowed per-bar move (Wilder used futures limits; ~10% of prior close for equities).
view.adl.h1.title: // ACCUMULATION / DISTRIBUTION LINE
view.adl.h2.bars_hint: (4 tokens per line: high low close volume)
view.adl.tip.compute: Compute Chaikin ADL (cumulative Money Flow Volume).
view.adl.hint.about: Cumulative running sum of Money Flow Volume. MFM = ((C−L)−(H−C))/(H−L) ∈ [−1, +1]; MFV = MFM × Volume. Rising ADL = accumulation (closes near highs); falling ADL = distribution (closes near lows). Watch for price/ADL divergence.
view.atrts.h1.title: // ATR TRAILING STOP
view.atrts.h2.bars_hint: (3 tokens per line: high low close; ≥ period+1 bars)
view.atrts.h2.gap_chart: Stop-gap % per bar (long & short breathing room)
view.atrts.tip.compute: Compute long & short trailing stops with N×ATR + ratchet.
view.atrts.hint.about: Long stop = close − N×ATR, ratcheted up. Short stop = close + N×ATR, ratcheted down. Triggered when close crosses the stop. Defaults: period=14, mult=3.0.
view.atrc.h1.title: // ATR CHANNEL
view.atrc.h2.bars_hint: (3 tokens per line: high low close; ≥ period+1 bars)
view.atrc.tip.compute: Compute ATR Channel (EMA/SMA midline + Wilder-ATR bands).
view.atrc.hint.about: Volatility envelope: middle = EMA/SMA(close, period); upper/lower = middle ± multiplier × Wilder-ATR(period). Width tracks volatility; close above upper signals breakout, below lower signals breakdown. Defaults: 20 / 2.0× / EMA.
view.atrend.h1.title: // ALPHATREND
view.atrend.h2.bars_hint: (3 tokens per line: high low close; ≥ period+1 bars)
view.atrend.tip.compute: Compute AlphaTrend with ATR trail and RSI gate.
view.atrend.hint.about: Trailing trend line built from ATR (SMA-of-TR) ratcheted up while RSI ≥ 50 and ratcheted down when RSI < 50. Multiplier widens/tightens the trail. Defaults: period=14, mult=1.0.
view.alma.h1.title: // ALMA (Arnaud Legoux MA)
view.alma.hint.about: FIR filter with Gaussian-shaped weights. Offset=1.0 puts peak at the most recent bar (lowest lag, near-EMA limit); 0.0 puts it at the oldest (smoothing only); 0.5 is centered. Larger sigma sharpens the kernel — less noise rejection, faster response. Defaults: 9/0.85/6.
view.arch_lm.h1.title: // ARCH-LM TEST
view.arch_lm.tip.compute: Run Engle (1982) ARCH-LM test for conditional heteroscedasticity.
view.arch_lm.hint.about: Regresses ê²ₜ on q lagged squared residuals. LM = (n−q)·R² ~ χ²(q) under H₀ (homoscedasticity). Reject when LM exceeds tabulated χ²(q) critical value — implies GARCH-style modeling is warranted.
view.arch_lm.err.degenerate: Test degenerate — check series length, lags, and variance.
view.ad_norm.h1.title: // ANDERSON-DARLING NORMALITY
view.ad_norm.empty_ecdf: Need ≥ 2 values with non-zero variance for ECDF.
view.ad_norm.tip.compute: Run AD test (Stephens 1986) — full empirical CDF, more powerful than JB in tails.
view.ad_norm.hint.about: A² uses the full empirical CDF — more powerful in the tails than Jarque-Bera. Stephens (1986) small-sample correction is applied. Critical values: α=0.10 → 0.631; α=0.05 → 0.752; α=0.025 → 0.873; α=0.01 → 1.035.
view.ad_norm.empty: Paste a sample to populate summary.
view.ad_norm.err.degenerate: Test degenerate — need ≥ 8 obs and non-zero variance.
view.block_boot.h1.title: // BLOCK BOOTSTRAP
view.block_boot.h2.data_hint: (returns or P&L increments — one per token)
view.block_boot.tip.data: Whitespace/comma-separated numbers. $/% prefixes stripped; (n) is negative.
view.block_boot.tip.compute: Resample blocks of consecutive obs to preserve serial dependence.
view.block_boot.hint.about: Draws blocks of consecutive observations with replacement to preserve serial dependence. Block size ≈ ⌊n^(1/3)⌋ for daily returns; larger blocks for stronger autocorrelation. Reports original statistic, bootstrap mean/stdev, and 95% percentile CI.
view.block_boot.err.degenerate: Bootstrap degenerate — check data length vs block size and resample count.
view.boot_pnl.h1.title: // BOOTSTRAP P&L CI
view.boot_pnl.h2.trades_hint: (one per token; "$50" or "(50)" for losses accepted)
view.boot_pnl.tip.trades: Per-trade realized P&L. iid bootstrap requires trades be independent — use block bootstrap for time-series.
view.boot_pnl.tip.compute: Resample trades with replacement, sum to total per resample, report quantiles + P(total > 0).
view.boot_pnl.hint.about: Resamples per-trade P&L with replacement. Reports mean / median / 90% CI / 95% CI / Pr(total > 0). iid bootstrap is appropriate for trade-level data where trades are independent. For serially-dependent returns use block bootstrap.
view.boot_pnl.empty: No bootstrap yet — paste trade P&Ls above.
