view.monte_carlo.fbm.title: fBm path
view.monte_carlo.fbm.caption: Single fractional Brownian motion realization. Higher Hurst H → smoother / more persistent.
view.monte_carlo.terminal.title: Terminal-price distribution (normal approximation)
view.monte_carlo.terminal.caption: Density curve is a normal approximation around (mean, stdev). For jump models the real distribution is skewed and fat-tailed — use the reported skew to gauge how misleading the normal is.
view.risk_gate.error.no_account: No account selected. Pick one in the topbar account strip.
view.dashboards.prompt.paste_import: Paste a previously-exported dashboards JSON blob:
view.risk_gate.h3.block_ratio_chart: Block ratio per rule (% of fires that are blocks)
view.risk_gate.hint.block_ratio: Per-rule share of fires that are blocks. Yellow dashed = 50%. High = harsh rule (most fires kill the trade). Low = soft rule (mostly warnings).
view.risk_gate.empty_ratio_chart: No fire data to chart block ratio.
view.kelly.note.no_edge: No edge: p × b = {pb} < q = {q}. Don't trade.
view.kelly.note.tiny_edge: Edge is tiny (< 1% Kelly). Position sizes will be tiny too.
view.kelly.note.large_edge: Edge is very large (full-Kelly > 50%). Half-Kelly recommended due to extreme drawdown risk.
view.time_in_force.label.placed_at_hint: (your local time → UTC at the wire)
view.tutorial.data_sources.paths: All data lands in embedded Postgres at ~/Library/Application Support/com.menketechnologies.traderview/traderview/pg-data. Logs at ~/Library/Application Support/traderview/traderview.log.
view.tutorial.risk_gate.engine: Engine lives in traderview_core::risk_gate — pure compute, 21 unit tests pin the rule semantics + serde compat + preset rule sets.
view.vpin.tick_stream.help: Paste price volume per line (whitespace- or comma-separated). Lines starting with # are ignored. Demo data injects a benign random-walk regime followed by a toxic burst so the VPIN line clearly crosses the 0.5 threshold.
view.trade_reviews.intro: Every closed trade with |R| ≥ 2 auto-queues here for forced reflection. Big wins teach as much as big losses — review them both. Five fixed questions per review keep the dataset comparable across hundreds of trades.
view.trade_reviews.hint.mood_chart: Count of reviews per mood value (awful → great). Orthogonal to setup-tag distribution: reveals the emotional pattern of recent reviewed trades, independent of which setups they were.
view.three_bar_reversal.help: Paste open high low close per line. Detection rule (fixed): bar1 trend bar, small middle (body ≤ 50% of bar1's body), bar3 closes past bar1's opposite extreme. Demo includes one classic bullish pattern at bars 2-3-4 and one classic bearish pattern at bars 11-12-13.
view.spread_tracker.help: Paste bid ask per line. Demo loads 300 samples that include a 20-sample pathological burst near the end (a feed glitch / circuit breaker analog).
view.range_expansion.help: Paste high low close per line. ATR is computed locally (Wilder smoothing). Demo loads 30 bars with engineered compression resolving UP and a second compression resolving DOWN.
view.risk_gate.dry_run.hint: Same call POST /api/risk-gate/evaluate the New Trade form will make before submitting. Use this to verify your rules.
view.order_flow.help: Paste price volume bid ask per line. Trades at ask = BUY, at bid = SELL, mid-spread falls back to tick rule (vs prior price). Demo loads 400 ticks with engineered net-buy pressure.
view.dashboards.storage_hint: Dashboards live in browser localStorage and mirror to your account when signed in. Drag tiles in EDIT mode to reorder.
view.ha_reversal.help: Paste open high low close per line. Bars are converted to Heikin-Ashi candles client-side; the backend detects color-flip reversal events on the HA series. Demo loads 30 bars with bull-bear-bull regime structure.
view.alligator.help: Paste high low per line. Median price ((H+L)/2) drives the three SMMAs. Demo loads 50 bars cycling through sleep → uptrend → sleep → downtrend so all three biases are visible.
view.murrey_math.help: Paste high low close per line. Murrey auto-detects the octave by rounding the lookback range to a power-of-2 base. Demo loads 80 bars in a ~10-point oscillating range.
view.demarker.help: Paste high low per line. Demo loads 60 bars cycling through uptrend → chop → downtrend so OB and OS readings both fire.
view.market_impact.help: Paste qty adv slippage_bps per line. Negative slippage is a favorable fill. Demo loads 400 trades that visibly cliff past 1% ADV.
view.cypher_pattern.help: Paste index price H|L per line. Pivots must alternate high/low. Demo loads a 5-pivot bullish Cypher with tolerance 0.10 (the more permissive setting most retail screeners use).
view.chandelier_stop.help: Paste high low close per line. ATR computed locally (Wilder smoothing). Demo loads 60 bars: 40-bar uptrend → 20-bar reversal — long stop should trigger on the way down.
view.per_symbol_slippage.help: Paste symbol slippage_bps per line. Signed bps — positive = trader-favorable (beat benchmark). Demo loads 108 fills across 6 symbols spanning the full execution-quality spectrum from ETF to micro-cap.
view.cup_and_handle.help: Paste high low close per line. Demo loads a synthetic 122-bar cup with an 8% handle that triggers the canonical IBD pivot buy-point.
view.drawdown_throttle.tier_hint: Tiers must be ascending by min_dd. The active tier is the LARGEST min_dd that current DD ≥ it.
view.exports.row.executions_desc: Every raw fill: timestamp, side, qty, price, fee, broker order id.
view.exports.row.trades_desc: Aggregated open→close positions with entry/exit avg, P/L, MFE/MAE, risk.
view.tutorial.quickstart.step1: Open Accounts (or tile 🏦 Accounts) and add at least one broker — the dashboard P/L stays empty until something is bound.
view.tutorial.quickstart.step2: Open Import (or tile ⤴ Import) and upload a broker CSV (12 importers supported). Trades roll up automatically.
view.tutorial.quickstart.step3: Hit <kbd>Cmd</kbd>+<kbd>K</kbd> to open the launcher. Type to filter all 74 tiles. Press Enter on the first match to navigate.
view.tutorial.quickstart.step4: Hit <kbd>?</kbd> anywhere outside a text field to re-open this tutorial.
view.accounts_overview.tip.accounts: Count of accounts contributing to this aggregation
view.accounts_overview.tip.closed_trades: Total closed trades across all accounts — W/L count below
view.accounts_overview.tip.today_pnl: Realized P/L for trades closed today (UTC date)
view.accounts_overview.tip.mtd_pnl: Realized P/L month-to-date — resets on the 1st UTC
view.accounts_overview.tip.ytd_pnl: Realized P/L year-to-date — resets on Jan 1 UTC
view.accounts_overview.tip.alltime_pnl: Realized P/L across every closed trade, all accounts
view.accounts_overview.tip.open_position: Number of open positions; notional dollar value beneath
view.accounts_overview.tip.unrealized: Mark-to-market unrealized P/L on open positions; day Δ beneath
view.live_positions.hint.notional: Per-symbol notional size. Reveals capital concentration independent of P/L performance — a 60% notional in one name is concentration risk even if it's green today.
view.live_positions.empty_notional_chart: No open positions to chart notional.
view.live_positions.tip.open_positions: Count of trades whose status is open in this account
view.live_positions.tip.total_notional: Sum of |qty × last_price × multiplier| across every open position
view.live_positions.tip.unrealized_pnl: (last − entry_avg) × qty × multiplier, signed by long/short
view.live_positions.tip.day_pnl: (last − quote.prev_close) × qty × multiplier — today's session move
view.live_positions.tip.biggest_winner: Open position with the largest positive unrealized P/L right now
view.live_positions.tip.biggest_loser: Open position with the largest negative unrealized P/L right now
view.risk.h2.daily_pnl_chart: Recent daily P&L vs goal / max-loss
view.risk.hint.cum_pnl: Running sum of daily net P&L. Reveals trajectory (uptrend, drawdown, treading water) independent of any single day's result.
view.risk.empty_cum_chart: No recent daily P&L to chart cumulative.
view.risk.empty_chart: No recent daily P&L to chart.
view.mood_analytics.tip.samples: Total (mood, P/L) pairs in the union of per-trade + per-day sources
view.mood_analytics.tip.pearson: Pearson ρ across all pairs; >0 happy=good, <0 happy=overconfident loss
view.mood_analytics.tip.buckets: Number of distinct mood buckets with at least one observation
view.mood_analytics.tip.sources: How the sample splits: per-trade (cleanest) / per-day (broader signal)
view.portfolio_allocator.hint.click_allo: Click Allocate to run all three allocators.
view.walk_forward.intro: Rolling in-sample / out-of-sample sweep. For each window we (1) sweep the full preset grid on the IS slice, (2) pick the best params, (3) run those params on the OOS slice with the running equity. OOS slices stitch into one continuous equity curve. Walk-forward efficiency = avg_oos / avg_is. Ratios < 0.5 indicate the strategy is curve-fit (looks great in-sample, dies forward). Compare against the baseline — a single best fit on the entire series — to see how much of the headline backtest return is hindsight bias.
view.american_option.chart_caption: Click Price first. The chart plots the European Black-Scholes price across a spot range (instant, no MC) and overlays a single MC point at your input spot to show where the LSMC estimate sits relative to the parametric European curve.

view.tutorial.data_sources.quotes: Quotes / fundamentals / charts: Yahoo Finance v8/v10. Cached 60s per symbol in Postgres.
view.tutorial.data_sources.live_ticks: Live ticks: Finnhub WebSocket. Free tier = 25 syms / connection; chunked automatically.
view.tutorial.data_sources.sentiment: Sentiment: Reddit r/wallstreetbets JSON, StockTwits public stream.
view.tutorial.data_sources.webull: Webull: session tokens you paste from your own browser. Read-only.
view.tutorial.risk_gate.preset_beginner: Beginner — 7 rules. 1% max per trade, 3% per day, 3 consec losses stop you, 15-min cool-down, 25% max position, plan + stop required. Strictest.
view.tutorial.risk_gate.preset_intermedi: Intermediate — 5 rules. Same per-trade cap, 5% daily, 4 streak, 5-min cool-down, stop required (plan optional).
view.tutorial.risk_gate.preset_aggressiv: Aggressive — 2 rules. Daily-loss cap + cool-down only. Assumes you manage per-trade risk yourself.
view.tutorial.risk_gate.step_tune: Tune — toggle individual rules off via the checkbox column. Delete with the link. Add custom rules with the form below the list.
view.tutorial.risk_gate.step_dryrun: Dry-run a hypothetical trade in the bottom panel to see exactly what the gate would say. Same call the new-trade form makes on submit.
view.tutorial.risk_gate.step_snapshot: Compliance snapshot at top — synthetic probe that surfaces which rules would fire on ANY entry right now (max daily loss already hit, in cool-down after the last loss, etc).
view.tutorial.risk_gate.step_paper: Paper trades also gate. Paper trading is where you build the habit; if rules only fired live, you'd practice rule-breaking.
view.tutorial.risk_gate.step_webhook: Webhook on Block. Every Block-severity veto fires to every enabled webhook (Discord / Slack / generic) so you get a public record of every rule save.
view.expenses.receipt.dropzone: Drop receipt — JPG, PNG, WebP, or PDF. OCR runs in the background; results auto-match candidate transactions.
view.webull.summary.connect: Connect — paste tokens from your browser session
view.webull.summary.instructions:
1. Open webull.com in another tab and log in (complete MFA / trade pin).
2. Open DevTools → Network → click any tradeapi.webullbroker.com request.
3. Copy the request headers did, access_token, and t_token.
4. Paste below. Tokens are held in process memory only — never written to disk.
view.tutorial.live.live_scanner: 6-panel real-time scanner (Gappers, Gainers, Losers, HOD, Volume, Ross 5-Pillar). Needs a free Finnhub API key + a comma-sep symbol universe (25 syms / WS connection, chunks automatically).
view.tutorial.live.halts: Polls Nasdaq trade-halt RSS every 3s. Voice alerts spell tickers letter-by-letter so TTS pronounces SPCE, not "space".
view.tutorial.live.catalysts: SEC EDGAR (6s) + Business Wire / PR Newswire / GlobeNewswire / AccessWire (30s). Ticker NER extracts $SYM, (SYM), and NYSE:/NASDAQ: prefixes. Voice fires when a watchlist symbol appears.
view.tutorial.live.premarket: Cross-asset opening drive — index futures, commodities, crypto, FX, plus today's high-importance economic events. ATR-multiple shows how outsized the overnight move is.
view.tutorial.trading.live_positions: Open positions from imported trades, re-priced with fresh quotes. Shows unrealized P/L, day P/L, and total cost basis.
view.tutorial.trading.paper_trade: Simulated execution against the same quote pipeline as live. Order types: market, limit, stop, stop-limit. Reset starting cash anytime.
view.tutorial.trading.plans: Write the setup before you take the trade. Link a plan to an execution post-fill — the plan vs. outcome lands in your discipline scorecard.
view.tutorial.trading.risk_gate: Pre-trade rules that BLOCK bad trades before they reach the broker. Define max loss per trade / per day, max consecutive losses, cool-down after a loss, max open positions, blocked symbols, require-plan, require-stop. Every new trade form runs these rules first and refuses to submit if any Block-severity rule fires (warnings get a confirm-then-proceed prompt).
view.tutorial.trading.position_size: Three modes: Kelly fraction (from your historical win-rate), fixed-fractional (% of equity per trade), R-based (max loss in dollars / stop distance). Correlation-aware: caps concentration when symbols co-move.
view.tutorial.journal.trade_reviews: Forced reflection on |R| ≥ 2 trades. Tracks plan-quality, execution-quality, emotion. Surfaces "reviews needed" count on the dashboard.
view.tutorial.journal.discipline: Rule-violation tracker. Defines hard limits (max R per trade, max losses per day, no FOMO entries) and counts breaches.
view.tutorial.journal.mood: Self-rated mood entered with each daily journal correlates against same-day P/L. Surfaces "you trade worse when…" patterns.
view.tutorial.research.charts: OHLC + indicator overlays. Draw lines/arrows/rectangles; drawings persist per symbol per user.
view.tutorial.research.research: Per-symbol dossier: quote, fundamentals, news, earnings history, recommendations, insider trades, dividends, holders.
view.tutorial.research.discovery: Three layers of discovery. Screener = manual criteria. Scanners = 24 Warrior/Zendoo presets (gap-and-go, opening drive, low-float surge…). Top Signals = ranked live signal leaderboard.
view.tutorial.research.darkpool_si: Off-exchange % print rankings and FINRA Reg-SHO daily short volume. Use to flag squeezable setups.
view.tutorial.research.options: Full options chain with Greeks. Vol surface plots the IV grid across strikes × expiries. Earnings IV scans pre-event IV crush and computes straddle EV.
view.tutorial.strategy.walk_forward: Rolling in-sample / out-of-sample sweep. Reveals overfit parameters that look great in-sample but fall apart OOS.

view.alligator.hint.mouth_width: |lips − jaw| over time: wide = trending (alligator eating); near zero = sleeping. Use as a regime-strength signal independent of direction.
view.alligator.hint.cyan_median_price_bl:
Cyan = median price. Blue = jaw (13-SMMA, +8 shift).
                Red = teeth (8-SMMA, +5 shift). Green = lips (5-SMMA, +3 shift).
                Lines fan out (green above red above blue) = hunting up. Reverse stack =
                hunting down. Intertwined = sleeping.
view.alligator.hint.one_cell_per_bar_gre:
One cell per bar — green up, red down, grey sleeping.
                Visualizes regime transitions across the full series at a glance.
view.almgren_chriss.hint.controls_how_fr:
κ = √(λσ²/η) controls how front-loaded the trajectory is. λ→0 collapses to TWAP
                (uniform liquidation, minimum impact, maximum timing risk). λ→∞ front-loads
                aggressively, eating impact cost to dump timing risk. The frontier shows the
                cost/variance tradeoff over a geometric λ ladder centred on your chosen λ.
view.almgren_chriss.hint.each_dot_is_a_s:
Each dot is a solve at one λ. The dashed white marker is
                your currently selected λ. Move down-right = more patient. Up-left =
                more urgent.
view.american_option.vol_chart_caption: European BS price across σ ∈ [0.05, 1.0] at fixed input spot, plus a single LSMC point at the input σ.
view.american_option.tip.kind: Call or Put; early-exercise premium typically shows up on puts (no-div spot)
view.american_option.tip.sigma: Volatility (decimal); enters the GBM process for path generation
view.american_option.tip.steps: Time steps per path; more = finer early-exercise grid
view.american_option.tip.paths: Monte Carlo paths; standard error shrinks as 1/√N
view.american_option.tip.price: Run LSMC; compare against Black-Scholes European reference
view.american_option.hint.longstaff_schw:
Longstaff-Schwartz 2001 regression-Monte-Carlo. Larger paths shrink the standard
                error as 1/√N. Default 5,000 paths gives ~1% SE for at-the-money options.

view.bocpd.h2.expected_run_length_bars_s: Expected run length (bars since last change)
view.bocpd.tip.hazard: Per-bar prior on regime change (0.0001–0.5); lower = sharper detections
view.bocpd.tip.threshold: Min posterior P to flag as a change point (0–1); 0 shows every bar
view.bocpd.tip.top_k: Max change points to list (1–20), sorted by probability desc
view.bocpd.tip.detect: Run Adams-MacKay BOCPD; render returns + CP overlay + run-length chart
view.bocpd.tip.returns: Return series (decimal); one per token; # comments OK
view.bocpd.hint.hazard_the_per_bar_prior:
Hazard = the per-bar prior probability that the underlying distribution
                changes. Lower hazard → fewer, sharper detections. The threshold filters
                the displayed change points by posterior probability — change to 0 to see
                every bar's score.
view.bocpd.hint.returns_on_left_axis_gre:
Returns on left axis (grey). Change-point probability on right axis (red);
                values near 1 mark bars where BOCPD is confident the regime shifted.
view.bocpd.hint.sawtooth_pattern_climbs_:
Sawtooth pattern: climbs by 1 each calm bar, drops to ~0 at every detected
                change point. Long flat-rising stretches = stable regime; frequent resets =
                noisy regime with many detected breaks.
view.breadth.empty_value_chart: No breadth indicators to chart values.
view.breadth.hint.intraday_tape_regime_n:
Intraday tape regime: NYSE TICK (instantaneous up-tick count),
            TRIN (Arms Index — volume bias), Advance-Decline issues, Up-Down volume,
            CBOE Put-Call ratio. Composite score combines all five into a -100..+100
            regime gauge. Polls every 60s.
view.breadth.hint.put_call_is_a_contrari: * Put-Call is a contrarian indicator at extremes — very low PCR = complacency (often near tops), very high PCR = fear (often near bottoms).
view.breadth.hint.no_breadth_tickers_ret: No breadth tickers returned data — try in market hours.

SEC EDGAR (every 6s) + Business Wire / PR Newswire / GlobeNewswire /
            AccessWire (every 30s). Tickers auto-extracted via NER.
            Filter for your watchlist on the left; voice alerts fire when
            any of those symbols appear in a fresh filing or press release.

view.choppiness.hint.formula_ci_100_log1: Formula: CI = 100 × log10(ΣTR / (max H − min L)) / log10(period). Default period 14. Reference bands: 61.8 (choppy line), 38.2 (trending line).

view.community.tip.title: Thread title — shown in the category list and forms the slug

view.community.th.title: Title
view.compare.hint.valuation_chart: Trailing-twelve-month P/E ratio per symbol. Reveals valuation premium/discount across the comparison set — orthogonal to returns. Lower P/E doesn't always mean cheaper (sector/growth differences), but the spread shows relative valuation discipline.
view.compare.empty_pe_chart: No trailing P/E data to chart.
view.compare.hint.side_by_side_fundament:
Side-by-side fundamentals + multi-horizon returns + 252-bar relative-strength
            overlay (each line rebased to 100 at the window start). Up to 4 symbols, comma-separated.
            Best metric in each row is highlighted in green; worst in red.
view.compare.hint.no_cached_bars_for_any: no cached bars for any symbol — populate the prices cache first.
view.corr_matrix.h2.most_correlated_pair: Most correlated pairs (overlap risk if both long)
view.corr_matrix.h2.avg_chart: Average ρ to every other symbol (per-symbol crowdedness)
view.corr_matrix.hint.avg_chart: Each symbol's mean correlation to every other symbol in the matrix. High = "in the crowd" (whole basket moves together — concentration risk); low = "outlier / diversifier". Orthogonal to per-pair distribution above.
view.corr_matrix.empty_avg_chart: Need at least 2 symbols to chart average ρ.
view.corr_matrix.hint.pairwise_pearson_c:
Pairwise Pearson correlation on log-returns of cached daily bars
            with intersected dates (pairs need ≥30 common observations to score). Red = strongly
            positive (moves together — overlap risk if both long), green = strongly negative
            (true diversifier), grey = uncorrelated. The diagonal is fixed at 1.
view.corr_matrix.hint.pick_a_watchlist_o: Pick a watchlist or paste symbols and compute.
view.cov_denoiser.empty_cum_chart: Cleaned spectrum has zero total variance.
view.cov_denoiser.hint.marchenko_pastur_:
Marchenko-Pastur clipping: eigenvalues inside the noise bulk are flattened
                to their average; only signal eigenvalues survive. Trace is preserved.
                Pipe the cleaned cov into the Portfolio Allocator for more robust weights
                than raw-sample-cov input.
view.cov_denoiser.hint.cyan_markers_orig:
Cyan markers = original sample eigenvalues (sorted descending).
                Red dashed line = λ_max bulk threshold. Anything above the line is
                "signal" (preserved); anything at or below is "noise" (replaced by the
                bulk average — visible as the orange flat segment in the cleaned series).
view.crypto.h2.from_ath_chart: Top-10 % from ATH — drawdown from cycle peak
view.crypto.hint.coingecko_rate_limit_hi: CoinGecko rate limit hit — retry in a minute.
view.csv_wizard.hint.failure_reason_char: Counts of distinct failure reasons across rejected rows (truncated to first 40 chars). Reveals systemic causes — a single dominant bar usually means one bad column mapping; many small bars mean inconsistent per-row data. Orthogonal to the overall outcome breakdown above.
view.csv_wizard.tip.file: Select a .csv file; the wizard parses headers + first rows before mapping
view.csv_wizard.tip.commit: Run dedupe-aware insert into the active account; same file twice is blocked
view.csv_wizard.tip.inserted: Rows that became new executions in the account
view.csv_wizard.tip.skipped: Rows matching an existing execution on (broker_order_id, executed_at, symbol, side, qty, price)
view.csv_wizard.tip.failures: Rows the parser rejected; reasons listed beneath the cards
view.csv_wizard.tip.import_id: Server import-batch id; quote it when reporting issues with this import
view.csv_wizard.hint.auto_guesses_based_: Auto-guesses based on header names. Adjust as needed.
view.csv_wizard.hint.map_at_least_the_re: Map at least the required fields to preview.
view.cup_and_handle.h2.cup_profile_chart: Cup profile — closes normalized within the cup span (curvature symmetry)
view.cup_and_handle.empty_profile_chart: Detect a pattern with a meaningful cup span to chart its profile.
view.cup_and_handle.tip.bars: OHLC bars: 'high low close' per line; whitespace or comma OK; # comments skipped
view.cup_and_handle.tip.demo: Load a synthetic 122-bar cup with an 8% handle that hits the IBD pivot
view.cup_and_handle.tip.detect: Scan candidate cups + handles; return the most-recent qualifying pattern
view.cup_and_handle.hint.canonical_ibd_d:
Canonical IBD defaults: 30-250 cup bars, 10-33% depth, ≤5% rim asymmetry,
                5-25 handle bars, ≤15% handle dip. Loosen depth/handle to surface near-misses
                during base-building.
view.cup_and_handle.hint.yellow_left_rim:
Yellow = left rim. Magenta = trough. Cyan = right rim. Orange =
                handle low. Green dashed = pivot (IBD buy-point = right-rim high).
view.custom_indicators.hint.save_named_i: Save named indicator + parameter combos (SMA, EMA, RSI, ATR, ADX/DI, Stochastic, Bollinger, MACD). The Charts tab gets a multi-select to overlay any of them on the SVG cursor. Backend evaluates the chosen presets against cached bars and returns one series per output line (Bollinger / MACD / ADX emit 3, Stochastic emits 2, scalars emit 1).
view.custom_indicators.th.name: Name
view.cusum.h2.detector_state_chart: CUSUM detector state — G+ and G− vs threshold band
view.cusum.empty_state_chart: Provide a series to chart the detector state.
view.cusum.tip.series: One signed value per line (returns/vol-normalized series); # comments OK
view.cusum.tip.demo: Load 200 synthetic bars with a clean regime flip at index 100
view.cusum.tip.autofit: Compute mean + stdev from the series and fill the reference inputs
view.cusum.tip.mean: Baseline mean the CUSUM tracks deviations against
view.cusum.tip.sd: Reference stdev; threshold + slack are expressed as multiples of this
view.cusum.tip.thr: Trigger level in stdevs; lower = chatty detector, higher = laggy
view.cusum.tip.slk: Per-bar deduction to suppress noise; 0.5 × stdev is conventional
view.cusum.tip.detect: Run Page-Hinkley CUSUM and mark up/down regime-shift events
shortcut.cusum_autofit: Auto-fit reference mean + stdev from the series
view.cusum.hint.paste_one_signed_value_p:
Paste one signed value per line — typically log-returns or
                vol-normalized returns. Demo loads 200 bars where mean flips at bar 100.
view.cusum.hint.threshold_stdevs_referen:
threshold_stdevs × reference_stdev = trigger level. Slack subtracts a
                small amount each bar to suppress noise (typically 0.5 × stdev).
                A high threshold + zero slack = chatty detector. Low threshold + large
                slack = laggy but stable.
view.cusum.hint.cyan_the_series_itself_g:
Cyan = the series itself. Green dots = UP change-points
                (regime mean shifted higher). Red dots = DOWN change-points (mean shifted lower).
                Marker height = CUSUM value at the firing bar (how hard the threshold was busted).
view.cypher_pattern.h2.quality_chart: Per-match geometric quality (lower = closer to ideal Cypher)
view.cypher_pattern.empty_quality_chart: No Cypher matches to chart quality.
view.cypher_pattern.hint.cyan_pivot_pric:
Cyan = pivot price connecting line. Yellow dots = pivots. When
                a Cypher matches, X/A/B/C/D markers are highlighted on the first match's pivots.
view.daily_loss_limit.h2.equity_sweep_ch: Binding limit vs account equity — where $ cap takes over from % cap
view.daily_loss_limit.empty_chart: Provide a positive binding limit to chart the sweep.
view.daily_loss_limit.empty_eqsweep_char: Provide positive equity, pct, and non-negative $ cap to chart the equity sweep.
view.daily_loss_limit.hint.binding_limit: Binding limit = the SMALLER of (account_equity × max_pct) and (max_$ cap when set). Pct-only mode: set max_$ to 0.
view.daily_loss_limit.hint.track_marks_a:
Track marks at warning (yellow) / cut-size (orange) / kill (red).
                Fill color reflects current state. 0% = no loss; 100% = at the binding cap.
view.darkpool.h2.latest_vs_avg_chart: Latest % vs avg % per symbol — recent divergence from baseline

view.taxwiz.title: File Taxes
view.taxwiz.subtitle: Guided interview — auto-populates from your receipts, OCR's W-2 / 1099, computes your federal return, exports a PDF for paper filing or transcription to IRS Free File Fillable Forms.
view.taxwiz.personal.subtitle: Tell us your filing status — this drives bracket thresholds, the standard deduction, and CTC phase-out limits.
view.taxwiz.income.subtitle: Upload your W-2 or 1099s for OCR, or enter manually. Schedule C and Schedule E auto-fill from your receipts after you click Auto-populate.
view.taxwiz.income.w2.empty: No W-2s yet. Upload an image or add one manually.
view.taxwiz.income.sched_c.hint: Auto-populates from receipts tagged 'business' — manual override available.
view.taxwiz.adjustments.subtitle: Reduces AGI directly. Half of SE tax is added automatically when you have self-employment income.
view.taxwiz.deductions.subtitle: We pick the larger of standard and itemized automatically. Override below if you want to force standard.
view.taxwiz.deductions.force_std: Force standard deduction even if itemized is larger
view.taxwiz.credits.subtitle: Child Tax Credit + Other Dependents. EITC is not computed — see IRS Publication 596.
view.taxwiz.credits.eitc_claim: EITC claim (compute via IRS Pub 596, enter here)
view.taxwiz.credits.estimated_paid: Estimated tax payments made this year
view.taxwiz.other.subtitle: Self-employment tax — computed automatically from Schedule C net profit. Half of this is deductible (Schedule 1 line 15).
view.taxwiz.review.subtitle: All numbers verified by the engine. Bottom line:
view.taxwiz.late_penalty.hint: Estimate FTF + FTP penalty + § 6601 interest for a delinquent return. Compute is local — nothing is filed.
view.taxwiz.edu.hint: AOTC: max $2,500/student, 40% refundable. LLC: max $2,000/return, non-refundable. Both phase out $80k-$90k Single / $160k-$180k MFJ; MFS ineligible.
view.taxwiz.edu.mfs_ineligible: MFS filers are not eligible for education credits.
view.taxwiz.ira.hint: Traditional IRA deductibility depends on workplace-plan coverage. Roth limit is the contribution cap itself. Base $7,000 + $1,000 catch-up at 50+.
view.taxwiz.ira.no_phaseout: Neither spouse covered by workplace plan — fully deductible at any MAGI.
view.taxwiz.hsa.hint: Requires HDHP enrollment. Self-only $4,300, family $8,550, +$1,000 at 55+. Pro-rated by months of HDHP coverage.
view.taxwiz.review.qbi_warn: ⚠ QBI: you're above the SSTB threshold. The simple 20% calc may overstate — verify the W-2 wage / UBIA test manually.
view.taxwiz.download.subtitle: A multi-page PDF: cover summary, Form 1040 detail, Schedule C, Schedule SE, Schedule E.
view.taxwiz.download.disclaimer: We do NOT e-file — that requires IRS Authorized e-File Provider status. Use the PDF to paper-file, or transcribe to IRS Free File Fillable Forms.
view.taxwiz.download.next_step: After downloading, transcribe to IRS Free File Fillable Forms:
view.taxwiz.download.irs_link: Open IRS Free File Fillable Forms
view.taxwiz.safe_harbor.hint: Are you covered against the underpayment penalty? Compares your YTD payments + withholding against the lower of 100%/110% prior-year tax or 90% current-year tax.
view.taxwiz.what_if.hint: Change one input, see refund/owed delta against your saved draft. Nothing is persisted.
view.taxwiz.toast.autopopulated: Auto-populated Schedule C + E + estimated payments
view.taxwiz.toast.ocr_running: Running OCR on the uploaded form…
view.taxwiz.toast.nec_imported: 1099-NEC nonemployee compensation imported to Schedule C
view.taxwiz.toast.form_unknown: Detected {kind} — applied to general income field
view.budget.title: Budget
view.budget.empty: No category budgets yet. Add one below to start tracking.
view.categorize.subtitle: Group every line item by canonical merchant and apply one category to all of them. Picks roll forward — the next OCR of the same merchant uses your choice as the default.
view.categorize.toggle.default_only: Only items still on a parser default
view.categorize.empty: Nothing to triage — every item already has a confirmed category.
view.expenses.tax.merchants.empty: No merchants in this period — upload receipts to see the rollup.
