view.sec179.note: Section 179 capped at net business income; excess carries forward. Bonus depreciation has NO income cap and can create a NOL.
view.retire.h1.title: // RETIREMENT MAXIMIZER
view.retire.hint.intro: Self-employed traders can shelter 20-25% of net business income tax-deferred via SEP IRA or Solo 401k. Compare side-by-side and pick the structure that shelters more given your numbers.
view.retire.solo401k.note: Best for: high-income SE traders. Allows Roth + after-tax (mega-backdoor) contributions. Requires solo 401k plan setup ($0-300 typical).
view.retire.sep.note: Best for: simple setup, no plan administration. Lower shelter than Solo 401k once income exceeds ~$200k. SIMPLE to open at most brokers (Fidelity / Schwab).
view.retire.ira.note: Deduction phased out by AGI if covered by employer plan (incl. SEP/Solo 401k). Roth IRA phases out at high income too — backdoor Roth required.
view.splits.h1.title: // STOCK SPLITS HISTORY
view.splits.hint.intro: Per-symbol historical splits + reverse splits. Reverse splits often signal distressed companies trying to maintain listing — typically bearish setup for short-term traders. Forward splits historically bullish (NVDA, TSLA).
view.mf.h1.title: // MUTUAL FUND PROFILE
view.mf.hint.intro: Finnhub mutual fund endpoints — profile, top holdings, sector mix, country mix. Compare expense ratios + tilt vs your own holdings.
view.mf.profile.name: Name
view.mf.th.name: Name
view.mf.th.label: Label
view.uspto.h1.title: // USPTO PATENTS
view.uspto.hint.intro: US Patent & Trademark Office filings by symbol. R&D-intensive companies' patent velocity correlates with tech momentum (semis, biotech, EVs). Surge in patents = pre-launch R&D signal.
view.uspto.th.title: Title
view.home_office.h1.title: // HOME OFFICE DEDUCTION
view.home_office.hint.intro: IRC § 280A — deduct a portion of home expenses for the area regularly + exclusively used for business. Method compare side-by-side; pick whichever yields a larger deduction (you can change methods year-to-year).
view.home_office.simplified.note: No depreciation recapture. Easiest method — no records of utilities etc. needed.
view.home_office.actual.note: Higher upside but depreciation reduces home cost basis (gain recaptured on sale). Requires receipts + utility records.
view.home_office.recommend.savings: Saves ${diff} vs. the other method ({better} wins)
view.home_office.disclaimer: Office must be regularly + exclusively used for business. Daycare + storage exceptions exist. Excess deduction (above biz income) carries over only under actual method.
view.t1099.h1.title: // 1099 INCOME TRACKER
view.t1099.hint.intro: Log every 1099 you receive (or expect to receive) by payer + form type. Match against your books in February to spot mismatches BEFORE filing — the IRS sees both copies, so unreported 1099 income triggers a CP2000.
view.t1099.empty: No 1099 records for this year yet.
view.meals.h1.title: // MEAL DEDUCTION TRACKER
view.meals.hint.intro: IRC § 274(n) — generally 50% deductible. Track per-meal so audit-ready. Required substantiation: date, amount, place, business purpose, names of attendees + business relationship.
view.meals.empty: No meals logged for this year.
view.bizcat.h1.title: // BUSINESS / PERSONAL CATEGORIZER
view.bizcat.hint.intro: Regex-based auto-tagging of expense transactions. Edit the rules below to match your stack. 'Ambiguous' tag flags transactions you need to split manually (fuel %, software dual-use, etc.).
view.depr.h1.title: // EQUIPMENT DEPRECIATION
view.depr.hint.intro: MACRS GDS half-year convention schedule. Each asset gets a per-year depreciation expense over its class life. Pair with Section 179 for year-1 immediate expensing; whatever doesn't fit Sec 179 depreciates here.
view.travel.h1.title: // TRAVEL PER-DIEM TRACKER
view.travel.hint.intro: Log business trips with destination + nights + lodging actual + per-diem M&IE. Per-diem M&IE is 75% on travel days. Pick the higher of actual receipts or per-diem on Schedule C line 24a (travel).
view.qbi.h1.title: // QBI § 199A CALCULATOR
view.qbi.hint.intro: IRC § 199A — 20% pass-through deduction. Day-traders with TTS election qualify. Trading is a 'specified service trade or business' (SSTB) — deduction phases out + disappears at high income (single $291k / MFJ $584k, 2024).
view.qbi.regime.below: Below threshold — full deduction allowed (subject to taxable income cap).
view.qbi.regime.above_wage_limited: Above phase-out (non-SSTB) — W-2 wage limit applies.
view.qbi.regime.phasing_sstb: In phase-out range (SSTB) — partial deduction reduced.
view.qbi.regime.phasing_non_sstb: In phase-out range (non-SSTB) — partial wage limitation.
view.qbi.notes.body: Traders without TTS election: NO QBI. Investment income (capital gains, dividends, interest) is explicitly excluded from QBI by statute. TTS election + § 475(f) mark-to-market converts trading P&L to ordinary income, which CAN be QBI but is SSTB-limited at high income. § 199A SUNSETS after 2025 unless extended.
view.statetax.h1.title: // STATE TAX ESTIMATOR
view.statetax.hint.intro: Compare effective state tax burden across states at your income level. Trader-relocation hot list (FL, TX, WA, NV, TN) at top. NY / CA / NJ at the bottom — relocating could save 5-13% of your gross.
view.statetax.disclaimer: Single-filer ordinary income only. Excludes local (NYC, San Francisco, Denver), property, sales tax. Federal still applies. Capital gains taxed differently in some states (WA 7% > $250k). Confirm domicile rules before relocating (NY claws back former residents aggressively).
tile.depreciation.label: Depreciation
tile.depreciation.desc: MACRS GDS 3/5/7/10/15-year schedules with year-1 + accumulated totals
view.scorp.h1.title: // S-CORP ELECTION CALCULATOR
view.scorp.hint.intro: S-corp election saves SE tax on the portion NOT paid as W-2 wages. Reasonable-comp rule: IRS expects ~40-60% of profit as W-2 for service businesses. Below ~$80k net income, S-corp overhead usually exceeds savings.
view.scorp.recommend.marginal: Marginal — consider but track admin burden
view.scorp.caveats.body: Reasonable comp is enforced — IRS reclassifies low-balled wages. S-corp adds Form 1120-S, K-1 issuance, quarterly payroll filings, state franchise taxes (CA $800/yr, NY/IL fees). Below ~$80k net income, overhead usually exceeds savings. Above $400k+ income, FICA cap reduces marginal benefit. Many traders use a SINGLE-OWNER S-corp specifically for QBI + SE savings.
view.nol.h1.title: // NOL TRACKER
view.nol.hint.intro: IRC § 172 post-TCJA: NOLs carry forward indefinitely, no carryback. Each year you can use NOLs to offset up to 80% of taxable income. Track yearly generation + utilization to see your remaining carryforward.
view.nol.note: Note: TCJA 80% taxable-income cap means even a large NOL can't fully offset a profitable year. Plan trade timing accordingly — a Section 475(f) mark-to-market election would convert this trading loss to ordinary, which generates QBI-eligible NOLs.
view.augusta.h1.title: // AUGUSTA RULE § 280A(g)
view.augusta.hint.intro: Rent your home to your business up to 14 days/year tax-free to you personally, deductible to the business. Named after Augusta GA homeowners who rent during The Masters. Requires comparable Airbnb/VRBO comps + documented business purpose.
view.augusta.rate.note: Comp must be reasonable — pull comps from Airbnb / VRBO / Peerspace for the same space + zip code. Keep screenshots dated as evidence.
view.augusta.empty: No rental days logged this year.
view.augusta.disclaimer: Requires C-corp or S-corp business structure (sole-prop won't work — you'd be paying yourself). Rental income to YOU is tax-free under § 280A(g) when ≤ 14 days. Business deducts the rent (Schedule C / Form 1120-S). Keep written corporate minutes documenting business need + comparable-rate research.
view.charity.h1.title: // CHARITABLE PLANNER
view.charity.hint.intro: Donating appreciated stock instead of cash = avoid cap-gains tax AND get full FMV deduction. Compare side-by-side. Bunching multiple years of giving into one tax year + using a Donor Advised Fund (DAF) maximizes itemized deductions in alternating years.
view.charity.label.cost_basis: Your cost basis on the stock
view.charity.bunch.body: Bunching {n} years of $X gift = ${gift} in one year. Total tax savings: ${savings}. Off-years take standard deduction.
view.charity.bunch.note: Fidelity / Schwab / Vanguard offer free DAFs. Front-load multiple years of giving into one year, get the itemized deduction, then take the standard deduction in the off-years. Distribute from the DAF to charities at your pace.
view.fbar.h1.title: // FBAR + FORM 8938 TRACKER
view.fbar.hint.intro: US persons with foreign financial accounts must report them. FBAR triggers at $10k aggregate any-point. Form 8938 has higher thresholds. Many traders trip FBAR by holding IBKR-UK, Saxo, IG Markets — IBKR US-only doesn't count. FBAR penalty: $10k non-willful, $100k+ willful per year.
view.fbar.deadlines: FBAR (FinCEN 114): April 15 with automatic Oct 15 extension. Form 8938: attaches to your 1040.
view.fbar.empty: No foreign accounts for this year.
view.s1256.h1.title: // SECTION 1256 60/40
view.s1256.hint.intro: Futures + SPX/NDX/VIX options + forwards: 60% long-term / 40% short-term regardless of holding period. Mark-to-market at year-end. Net § 1256 loss can carry BACK 3 years (unique benefit vs. regular capital loss).
view.s1256.loss_carryback: Net § 1256 LOSS — can carry BACK up to 3 years to offset prior § 1256 gains. File Form 6781 + amend prior returns (Form 1040-X). Carryforward is also OK.
view.wash.h1.title: // WASH SALE TRACKER
view.wash.hint.intro: IRC § 1091 — loss disallowed if you buy the same (or substantially identical) security within 30 days before or after the loss sale. Disallowed loss adds to basis of the replacement shares. TTS + § 475(f) election EXEMPTS you from wash sales entirely — major reason traders elect MTM.
view.hsa.h1.title: // HSA MAXIMIZER
view.hsa.hint.intro: Triple tax advantage: deductible going in, tax-free growth, tax-free withdrawal for qualified medical. Stealth retirement account: pay medical out-of-pocket now, save receipts, reimburse yourself decades later. Requires HDHP (high-deductible health plan) coverage.
view.hsa.strategy.body: Max HSA + invest in low-fee index funds. Pay current medical out-of-pocket from taxable account. Save every medical receipt. At age 65+, reimburse yourself for decades of receipts tax-free — or use after 65 for any purpose (taxed as ordinary, no penalty, like a Traditional IRA).
view.hsa.warning.ca_nj: California + New Jersey + Alabama do NOT recognize HSAs — contributions are subject to state income tax. NH + TN don't tax interest/dividends so HSA growth still escapes state tax there.
view.f988.h1.title: // § 988 FOREX TRACKER
view.f988.hint.intro: Spot forex defaults to ordinary income (§ 988). Loss-side advantage: 100% deductible vs only $3k/yr capital-loss limit. Gain-side disadvantage: taxed at marginal rate vs. potential LT cap-gain. Annual election out to § 1256 available for major-pair forwards.
view.f988.loss_note: Net forex LOSS — § 988 ordinary treatment fully deductible against ordinary income (no $3k cap). Election out to § 1256 would have made it a capital loss capped at $3k/yr against ordinary income.
view.f988.empty: No forex positions for this year.
view.rd.h1.title: // R&D CREDIT § 41
view.rd.hint.intro: Alternative Simplified Credit (ASC): 14% of QREs above 50% of 3-year average. Trader algos, backtesting frameworks, ML models qualify if they pass the 4-part test. Startups (< 5 yrs revenue) can offset up to $500k/yr against payroll tax instead of income tax.
view.rd.test.purpose: Permitted purpose: develop new or improved product, process, or software
view.rd.test.uncertainty: Elimination of uncertainty: tech feasibility / methodology unknown at start
view.rd.test.experimentation: Process of experimentation: systematic trial-and-error / modeling
view.rd.test.technological: Technological in nature: CS / engineering / math / physical sciences
view.rd.cap_174.body: R&D expenses are no longer deductible in the year incurred — must be amortized over 5 years (15 for foreign). Year-1 deduction = 10% of QREs (mid-year convention). Big cash-flow impact on R&D-heavy businesses. Credit (Form 6765) is unaffected.
view.rd.qre.algo: Algo trading model development + backtesting time
view.rd.qre.ml: ML model training (signals, NLP on news, sentiment)
view.rd.qre.contractor: 65% of qualified contractor wages (vs 100% of W-2)
view.rd.qre.cloud: Cloud compute used for R&D (AWS / GCP / Lambda)
view.rd.qre.supplies: Tangible supplies consumed in development (sensors, FPGAs)
view.mtm.h1.title: // § 475(f) MTM ELECTION
view.mtm.hint.intro: THE election for active traders. Converts trading P&L from capital to ordinary (no $3k loss cap, no wash sales), enables QBI § 199A. Year-end positions are marked-to-market for tax purposes. Hard deadline: April 15 of the year you want it to first apply, attached to your prior year return.
view.mtm.deadline.note: Filed BY April 15 of current year: § 475(f) MTM applies for current year forward. Missed it? Next opportunity: next April 15 for next year forward. Can request relief via Rev. Proc. 2015-13 for late election but rarely granted.
view.mtm.benefit.ordinary: Trading P&L is ORDINARY income/loss — no $3k/yr capital-loss cap
view.mtm.benefit.qbi: Trading P&L is QBI-eligible for § 199A 20% deduction
view.mtm.benefit.nol: Losses generate NOLs — carry forward indefinitely (vs. capital-loss carryover)
view.mtm.benefit.full_deduct: Trading expenses fully deductible (Schedule C, no 2% AGI floor)
view.mtm.drawback.mtm_realization: Forced year-end mark-to-market — phantom gains on open positions
view.mtm.drawback.ordinary_rates: Gains taxed at ordinary rates (no LT 15-20% cap-gains advantage)
view.mtm.drawback.investment_segregation: Must SEGREGATE investments from trading account — invested holdings keep cap-gains treatment
view.mtm.drawback.no_revoke: Hard to revoke — Form 3115 + IRS consent + 5-year wait if voluntary
view.mtm.drawback.se_tax: If TTS+MTM via S-corp or sole-prop: trading income may be SE-taxable
view.mtm.checklist.tts: Confirm TTS qualified (trades 720+ per year, regular daily activity)
view.mtm.checklist.attach: Attach signed statement to PRIOR year 1040 by April 15
view.mtm.checklist.text: Statement language: 'Pursuant to IRC § 475(f)(1), the taxpayer hereby elects to use the mark-to-market method of accounting for securities and traders in securities, effective for the taxable year beginning [date]...'
view.mtm.checklist.form_3115: File Form 3115 with the FIRST election-year return (accounting method change)
view.mtm.checklist.form_4797: Report MTM gains/losses on Form 4797 Part II (ordinary)
view.mtm.checklist.schedule_c: Trading expenses on Schedule C, trading income on Form 4797
view.mtm.rec.tts_first_body: Without TTS qualification, you cannot make a § 475(f) election. Build trading volume + frequency + business-like operation first.
view.mtm.rec.consider_body: If you have TTS + net trading LOSSES, MTM is almost always a win. If you have consistent LONG-TERM gains, the loss of LT cap-gains rates may outweigh the benefits.
view.mtm.rec.filed_continue_body: File Form 4797 for MTM gains/losses each year. Schedule C for trading expenses. Form 3115 only once (first year).
view.tts.verdict.fail: DOES NOT QUALIFY — too few critical factors
view.qsbs.h1.title: // § 1202 QSBS TRACKER
view.qsbs.hint.intro: 100% federal exclusion of gain on Qualified Small Business Stock held > 5 years (acquired 2010-09-28+). Per-issuer cap: greater of $10M or 10× cost basis. Excluded gain also escapes NIIT 3.8% and (for some) state tax.
view.qsbs.req.c_corp: Issued by US C-corp (or eligible entity)
view.qsbs.req.assets: Gross assets ≤ $50M at issue and immediately after
view.qsbs.req.original: Acquired at ORIGINAL ISSUE (not secondary market)
view.qsbs.req.active: 80%+ of assets used in active business throughout holding period
view.qsbs.req.qualified: Qualified trade or business (NOT services, finance, farming, extraction, hospitality)
view.qsbs.req.cash_payment: Paid cash, property, or service (not stock-for-stock)
view.edu.h1.title: // EDUCATION CREDITS
view.edu.hint.intro: AOC: up to $2,500/student/yr (first 4 years undergrad, 40% REFUNDABLE). LLC: up to $2,000/return (any post-secondary, non-refundable). Can't claim both for same student same year. AGI phase-outs apply.
view.edu.label.name: Student name
view.edu.th.name: Name
view.edu.rec.aoc_when: First 4 years undergrad, full-time, eligible degree program. Refundable portion = real cash even if no tax owed.
view.edu.rec.llc_when: Grad school, part-time, single class, or year 5+. Non-refundable but applies broadly to any post-secondary.
view.edu.rec.note: Can mix per student per year — claim AOC for the qualifying undergrad, LLC for the grad-school spouse. Same dollar can't double-count.
view.acct.h1.title: // ACCOUNTABLE PLAN REIMBURSEMENTS
view.acct.hint.intro: S-corp owner's secret weapon — § 62(c) + Treas. Reg. 1.62-2. Business reimburses owner-employee for business expenses. Deductible to corp, NON-taxable wage exclusion to owner. Replaces the dead 2106 employee deduction (killed by TCJA 2018-2025).
view.acct.req.business_connection: Business connection — expense incurred while performing services for employer
view.acct.req.substantiation: Substantiation — receipts + business purpose within reasonable time (~60 days)
view.acct.req.return_excess: Return excess advances — within 120 days of expense
view.acct.req.written_plan: WRITTEN plan adopted by corporate resolution before first reimbursement
view.acct.req.warning: Without ALL 4 elements: reimbursements become taxable W-2 wages. Worst of both worlds — owner pays SE/FICA on the reimbursement.
view.dcfsa.h1.title: // DEPENDENT CARE FSA
view.dcfsa.hint.intro: $5,000/yr pre-tax for child care (under 13) or disabled spouse/dependent care. INTERACTS with the Child & Dependent Care Credit (§ 21) — can't use the same dollars for both. DCFSA usually wins above ~$43k AGI.
view.dcfsa.rec.note: DCFSA contributions reduce W-2 taxable wages AND escape FICA — that's the 7.65% FICA savings advantage. Above $43k AGI the CDCC drops to 20% which makes DCFSA almost always the winner at any meaningful income.
view.ev.h1.title: // EV TAX CREDIT § 30D
view.ev.hint.intro: New EV: $7,500 ($3,750 critical minerals + $3,750 battery components). Used EV: lesser of $4,000 or 30% of sale price. Both have MAGI caps. New: MSRP cap $55k cars / $80k trucks/SUVs/vans. Used: $25k price cap, 2+ yr old. Transferable to dealer at point of sale (immediate $7,500 off vs. tax-time credit).
view.ev.fail.used_price: Sale price exceeds used-EV cap of ${cap}
view.ev.fail.age: Used EV must be 2+ years old
view.ev.qualified: All caps met. Transfer credit to dealer at point of sale to capture immediately (vs. waiting until you file).
view.ev.note.transfer: Point-of-sale transfer (2024+): credit applied as down payment at dealer; no wait until filing
view.ev.note.recapture: If MAGI later exceeds cap, credit must be repaid as tax
view.ev.note.final_assembly: Final assembly in N. America required (check fueleconomy.gov VIN lookup)
view.ev.note.lookback: MAGI test = current year OR prior year, whichever lower
view.ev.note.dealer_only: Used EV credit ONLY available at registered dealer (no private sale)
view.ftc.h1.title: // FOREIGN TAX CREDIT § 901
view.ftc.hint.intro: Reduces US tax dollar-for-dollar by foreign income taxes paid. Common triggers: dividend WHT on foreign stocks (15% per US treaties), capital gains tax on IBKR-UK / Saxo holdings. Under $300 single / $600 MFJ passive: no Form 1116 needed — claim directly on Schedule 3.
view.ftc.note: FTC limit = US tax × (foreign income / total income). Excess carries back 1 year + forward 10. For passive-cat income only with foreign tax ≤ threshold, skip Form 1116 — direct claim on Schedule 3.
view.ftc.empty: No foreign tax entries for this year.
view.roth.h1.title: // ROTH CONVERSION LADDER
view.roth.hint.intro: Convert Traditional IRA / 401(k) → Roth in low-income years to fill the lower brackets. Each year's conversion has a 5-year seasoning period before principal can be withdrawn penalty-free (under 59½). Strategic for FIRE.
view.roth.note.5yr: 5-year seasoning per conversion: $50k converted in 2024 not withdrawable as principal until 2029
view.roth.note.early_retirement: FIRE strategy: convert during 50-65 gap years before SS / RMDs kick in
view.roth.note.tax_brackets: Best opportunities: $0-23k MFJ (10%), $23-94k MFJ (12%) — fill these annually
view.roth.note.irmaa: Watch IRMAA cliff at 65 — Medicare Part B/D surcharges trigger at MAGI tiers
view.roth.note.pay_tax_outside: Pay conversion tax from TAXABLE account, not the conversion itself, for max benefit
view.roth.note.tcja_sunset: 2025 brackets reset higher post-TCJA — front-load conversions before then
view.gift.h1.title: // GIFT TAX EXCLUSION TRACKER
view.gift.hint.intro: 2024: $18,000/recipient/year exclusion ($36k MFJ split-gift). Lifetime $13.61M unified with estate tax. Gifts > annual exclusion trigger Form 709 (even when no tax due). 529 superfund: $90k single / $180k MFJ in one year, 5-year spread. 2026 cliff: lifetime drops to ~$7M.
view.gift.warning.sunset: 2026 SUNSET: lifetime exemption drops from ~$13.6M to ~$7M unless Congress acts. Consider using up exemption pre-2026 if you're estate-tax-exposed.
view.gift.empty: No gifts recorded for this year.
tile.dcfsa.label: DCFSA
tile.dcfsa.desc: Dependent Care FSA + CDCC optimizer — combined shelter for child-care costs
view.ce25d.h1.title: // § 25D CLEAN ENERGY CREDIT
view.ce25d.hint.intro: 30% federal credit on solar PV, solar water heaters, geothermal, residential wind, fuel cells, battery storage (3+ kWh). Uncapped except fuel cells ($500/kW). Rate stays 30% through 2032, then 26% (2033), 22% (2034), 0% (2035+). Excess credit carries forward indefinitely against future tax.
view.savers.h1.title: // SAVER'S CREDIT § 25B
view.savers.hint.intro: 10/20/50% credit on first $2,000 contributed to retirement accounts ($4,000 MFJ). Up to $1,000 single / $2,000 MFJ credit. Disqualifiers: full-time student, dependent claimed on someone else's return, under 18. Generous below $46k MFJ — overlooked because many qualifying people don't file. Non-refundable.
view.savers.dis.dependent: Claimed as dependent on someone else's return
view.iir.h1.title: // INHERITED IRA RMD
view.iir.hint.intro: Post-SECURE Act rules: non-spouse non-EDB beneficiaries must empty inherited IRA within 10 years. SECURE 2.0 clarified: if decedent was already taking RMDs at death, beneficiary must also take annual RMDs in years 1-9 AND empty by year 10. Spouses + EDBs (disabled, chronically ill, minor children, siblings < 10 yrs younger) can use life-expectancy stretch.
view.iir.desc.10_year: Must fully distribute by Dec 31 of 10th year after death. If decedent was taking RMDs, also annual RMDs in years 1-9.
view.iir.desc.spouse_own: Treat the inherited IRA as your own. Apply your own RMD age (73 / 75). Most flexible option.
view.iir.desc.spouse_inherited: Keep as inherited IRA. Use Single Life Table. Can withdraw at any age penalty-free.
view.iir.desc.edb_life: Eligible Designated Beneficiary: disabled, chronically ill, minor child of decedent (until age 21), sibling < 10 years younger. Uses Single Life Table.
view.qcd.h1.title: // QCD TRACKER
view.qcd.hint.intro: Qualified Charitable Distribution: direct IRA → charity transfer. NOT taxable income, COUNTS toward RMD. 2024 cap: $105k/yr per taxpayer. Age 70½+ required (even though RMD age is 73+). Great for retirees who don't itemize — gives them charitable benefit without itemizing.
view.qcd.note: QCD MUST go directly from IRA to charity (custodian-to-custodian). Taking the cash first and donating doesn't qualify — that's regular RMD + itemized deduction.
view.qcd.empty: No QCDs recorded for this year.
view.nua.h1.title: // NUA STRATEGY
view.nua.hint.intro: NUA (Net Unrealized Appreciation): employer stock in 401(k) distributed in-kind. Cost basis taxed ORDINARY at distribution. Appreciation taxed LT cap gains when sold (even sold next day). Massive win if your employer stock is low-basis + highly appreciated. Compare to rollover.
view.nua.h2.headline: Bottom line
view.nua.rec.use_nua: Use NUA — strong advantage over rollover
view.nua.rec.roll_over: Roll to IRA — NUA disadvantageous here
view.nua.req.lump_sum: Lump-sum distribution: entire 401(k) balance distributed in ONE tax year
view.nua.req.triggering_event: Triggering event: separation, death, disability, age 59½
view.nua.req.in_kind: Employer stock distributed IN-KIND (don't sell inside the plan first)
view.nua.req.no_rollover: Do NOT roll employer stock to IRA — that destroys NUA treatment
view.nua.req.other_assets: Non-employer-stock assets CAN be rolled to IRA in same lump-sum
view.nua.note: Rule of thumb: NUA wins when basis ratio < ~40% AND you'll hold stock 5+ years after distribution. Below 25% basis ratio, NUA almost always wins. SECURE 2.0 considered restricting NUA but the rule survived intact.
view.kiddie.h1.title: // KIDDIE TAX § 1(g)
view.kiddie.hint.intro: Unearned income > $2,600 (2024) taxed at parent's marginal rate. Applies to: under-18, OR full-time student 19-23 whose earned income < 50% of support. Defeats the classic 'gift appreciated stock to kid' strategy unless kid is past age threshold or has substantial earned income.
view.kiddie.status.applies_reason: Under 18, OR full-time student 19-23 with earned income < 50% of support.
view.kiddie.status.exempt_reason: Age threshold passed or earned income sufficient — child taxed at own rate only.
view.kiddie.strategy: Strategy: gift basis-stepped stock to kids ONLY if they're 18+ AND have low income (use 0% LT cap-gains bracket up to $47,025 in 2024). Under 18: limit gifts to $2,600/yr of unearned income to stay within kid-rate tier. 529 plans bypass kiddie tax entirely (growth is tax-free).
view.qoz.h1.title: // OPPORTUNITY ZONE TRACKER
view.qoz.hint.intro: § 1400Z-2: defer cap gains by reinvesting in a QOF within 180 days. Hold 5y: 10% step-up. Hold 7y: 15% (BY 2026). Hold 10+y: 100% step-up on the QOF gain (deferred gain still taxed Dec 31, 2026). After Dec 31, 2026, the deferred gain is recognized regardless of whether you've sold.
view.qoz.cliff_note: CLIFF: original deferred gain recognized Dec 31, 2026 regardless of hold. New QOF investments after 2021 can no longer earn the 7-year 15% step-up. 10-year 100% exclusion still available for new investments through 2026.
view.529roth.h1.title: // 529 → ROTH ROLLOVER
view.529roth.hint.intro: SECURE 2.0 (2024+): roll up to $35,000 lifetime from a 529 plan to a Roth IRA in the BENEFICIARY's name. 529 must be 15+ years old. Annual cap = Roth limit ($7,000 in 2024). Beneficiary must have earned income equal to or exceeding the rollover that year. Solves the 'what if my kid doesn't need 529' problem.
view.529roth.req.15_year: 529 plan must be 15+ years old (oldest version of the plan, NOT the beneficiary)
view.529roth.req.beneficiary: Beneficiary of 529 = owner of receiving Roth IRA (same person)
view.529roth.req.earned_income: Beneficiary must have earned income ≥ rollover amount that year
view.529roth.req.5_year_seasoning: Contributions in last 5 years CANNOT be rolled (5-year seasoning per contribution)
view.529roth.req.no_income_cap: Roth income phase-out does NOT apply to this rollover (huge for high earners!)
view.529roth.req.no_change_beneficiary: Changing beneficiary may reset the 15-year clock (IRS guidance pending)
view.sehealth.h1.title: // SE HEALTH INSURANCE DEDUCTION
view.sehealth.hint.intro: § 162(l): SE taxpayers deduct health / dental / vision / LTC premiums ABOVE-the-line (Schedule 1 line 17). LTC capped by age. Limited to SE net income after ½ SE deduction. Does NOT reduce SE tax base. Disqualified if you (or spouse) had subsidized employer coverage that month.
view.sehealth.note.above_line: Above-the-line deduction: reduces AGI (helps unlock other phase-outs)
view.sehealth.note.no_se: Does NOT reduce SE tax base — only income tax
view.sehealth.note.month_by_month: Disqualification is month-by-month (employer subsidy any month = no deduction THAT month)
view.sehealth.note.medicare: Medicare premiums (Parts B, D, Medigap) qualify if you're on Medicare + still SE
view.sehealth.note.ltc_cap_age: LTC age-cap brackets adjust annually for inflation
view.sehealth.note.s_corp: S-corp owners: company pays premiums, reports as W-2 wages, owner deducts via § 162(l) on personal return
view.mbr.h1.title: // MEGA BACKDOOR ROTH
view.mbr.hint.intro: After-tax 401(k) contributions converted to Roth via in-service rollover OR in-plan Roth conversion. 2024 total 415(c) cap: $69,000. Subtract employee deferral + employer match = available after-tax space. Up to $46,000/yr extra Roth space on top of the regular $7k IRA limits. Requires your 401(k) plan document to allow after-tax + conversions.
view.mbr.not_eligible: Your plan must allow BOTH after-tax contributions AND in-service Roth conversions for the mega backdoor to work. Check your Summary Plan Description (SPD) or ask HR. About 50% of large-company plans now permit this since 2018-ish.
view.mbr.step.contribute: Contribute up to the after-tax limit (not Roth) to 401(k)
view.mbr.step.convert: Within days/weeks: convert to Roth 401(k) or rollover to Roth IRA
view.mbr.step.minimize_growth: Convert ASAP to minimize taxable growth between contribution and conversion
view.mbr.step.no_tax: After-tax dollars: no tax owed on conversion (basis = contribution)
view.mbr.step.growth_taxed: Any growth between contribution & conversion = taxable ordinary on conversion
view.mbr.step.no_income_cap: NO income phase-out (unlike regular Roth IRA contribution)
view.mbr.target_plans_body: Microsoft, Google, Meta, Amazon, NVIDIA, Apple, Tesla, Salesforce — most tech FAANG+ allow this. Federal employees: TSP does NOT allow it. Most small employer 401(k)s don't allow it. Check your SPD or call provider.
view.costseg.h1.title: // COST SEGREGATION
view.costseg.hint.intro: Engineering study reclassifies building components from 27.5/39 yr to 5/7/15 yr classes — typically 20-40% of basis. Combined with bonus depreciation (60% in 2024), front-loads decades of depreciation into year-1. Study cost: $3-15k. Catch-up via Form 3115 (no amended returns needed).
view.costseg.note: Caveat: accelerated depreciation is RECAPTURE'd as ordinary income (25%) at sale. Best for: real-estate pros, STR with material participation, holders 10+ years, or those planning § 1031 exchange (recapture continues to defer).
view.pal.h1.title: // PASSIVE LOSS § 469
view.pal.hint.intro: Passive losses can only offset passive INCOME unless: (1) $25k special allowance for active-participation rental real estate (phased to 0 at $150k MAGI), (2) Real Estate Professional Status (750 hrs + 50%), or (3) material participation (7-test). Unused PALs SUSPEND, released on full disposition of activity.
view.pal.empty: No passive activities for this year.
view.s1031.h1.title: // § 1031 LIKE-KIND EXCHANGE
view.s1031.hint.intro: Real estate only (post-TCJA 2018). Strict deadlines: 45 days to identify replacement property in writing, 180 days to close. Boot (cash, debt relief) = taxable. Basis carries over. Death = step-up basis wipes out deferred gain. QI required — can't touch proceeds.
view.s453.h1.title: // § 453 INSTALLMENT SALE
view.s453.hint.intro: § 453: recognize gain pro-rata as payments come in. Gross profit % = (sale price − basis − selling expenses) / contract price. Each payment's gain = payment × gross profit %. Receivables > $150k: interest-charge on deferred tax. NOT available for inventory or marketable securities. Useful for real estate + business sales.
view.s453.caution.depreciation_recap: Depreciation recapture (§ 1245/1250) recognized FULLY in year of sale, NOT installment
view.s453.caution.related_party: Related-party rule: buyer reselling within 2 years triggers full recognition
view.s453.caution.no_inventory: No installment treatment for inventory or marketable securities
view.s453.caution.election_out: Can elect OUT of installment (report all up front) — helpful in loss years
view.s453.caution.interest_charge: Receivables > $150k: § 453A interest charge on deferred tax at AFR-ish rate
view.str.h1.title: // SHORT-TERM RENTAL LOOPHOLE
view.str.hint.intro: Reg. § 1.469-1T(e)(3)(ii)(A): if average guest stay ≤ 7 days, the property is NOT a 'rental activity' under § 469 — it's a trade/business. Material participation makes losses fully deductible against ORDINARY income (vs PAL-suspended). Combined with cost-seg + bonus dep = massive year-1 paper loss that shelters W-2 / trading income. Heavily audited.
view.str.test.2: Test 2: Your hours > all others combined
view.str.test.3: Test 3: 100+ hours AND more than anyone else
view.str.test.4: Test 4: 100+ hrs + total all activities > 500 hrs
view.str.audit_note: AUDIT RISK: this is one of the most heavily audited tax positions. Keep CONTEMPORANEOUS time logs (date, hours, task description). IRS has won multiple recent cases (Coleman, Sezonov) on insufficient logs. Use Stessa / REI Hub / time-tracking app. Cost-seg + STR loophole + W-2 income = the 'lazy 1040' strategy that shelters six-figure W-2 income.
view.amt.h1.title: // AMT § 55 CALCULATOR
view.amt.hint.intro: Parallel tax system: regular taxable income + preferences/adjustments = AMTI. (AMTI − AMT exemption) × 26% (28% above $232,600) = tentative AMT. Pay greater of regular or AMT. ISO exercise = #1 AMT trigger. AMT paid generates MTC (Minimum Tax Credit) — carries forward against future regular tax owed.
view.amt.note.mtc: AMT paid generates a Minimum Tax Credit — recoverable against future regular tax (Form 8801)
view.amt.note.iso_basis: ISO AMT basis ≠ regular basis after exercise. Sale calc differs by FMV at exercise.
view.amt.note.salt_capped: SALT addback only matters if itemizing > standard deduction (post-TCJA most don't)
view.amt.note.amt_repeal: TCJA raised exemption — most middle-class taxpayers no longer hit AMT. ISO exercises + private-activity bonds still trigger it.
view.amt.note.same_day_sale: Same-day ISO exercise & sell = NSO treatment, no AMT preference
view.iso.h1.title: // ISO EXERCISE CALCULATOR
view.iso.hint.intro: Incentive Stock Options § 422. Bargain element (FMV − strike) is AMT preference at exercise (not regular income). Hold 2 yrs from grant + 1 yr from exercise = QUALIFYING disposition: all gain as LT cap-gains. Disqualifying = some ordinary (W-2 reported). $100k ISO grant cap per year (excess treated as NSO).
view.iso.no_sale_note: Add sale date + price above to see disposition treatment.
view.iso.strat.early_exercise: Early exercise (just-vested with low FMV) minimizes bargain element → less AMT
view.iso.strat.83b: Early exercise + 83(b) election starts holding-period clock immediately
view.iso.strat.disqualifying_same_year: Disqualifying disposition same calendar year as exercise = no AMT preference (regular income only)
view.iso.strat.mtc: MTC recovers AMT paid against FUTURE regular tax. Plan multi-year recovery.
view.iso.strat.cash_for_amt: Save cash for AMT bill: 28% × bargain element. Don't get blindsided in April.
view.nso.h1.title: // NSO EXERCISE CALCULATOR
view.nso.hint.intro: Non-Qualified Stock Options. Bargain element (FMV − strike) = ordinary income at exercise, reported on W-2 box 1, plus FICA + Medicare + state. Federal supplemental W/H: 22% (37% over $1M). At sale: gain = sale price − FMV at exercise, ST or LT depending on hold-after-exercise.
view.nso.label.ytd_w2_wages: YTD W-2 wages BEFORE this exercise ($)
view.nso.tip.w_h_short: 22% supplemental W/H is OFTEN insufficient if you're in 32%+ bracket — save cash for April
view.nso.tip.year_end_timing: Exercise late December to defer FICA cost into next year's SS base (if not already capped)
view.nso.tip.same_day: Same-day exercise & sell = no cap-gains exposure (sale price ≈ FMV)
view.nso.tip.cashless: Cashless exercise: company sells enough shares to cover exercise cost + taxes — no out-of-pocket
view.nso.tip.spread_out: Spread exercise across years to avoid bracket-jump in high-income year
view.rsu.h1.title: // RSU VEST TRACKER
view.rsu.hint.intro: Each vest: FMV at vest = ordinary income (W-2 box 1), supplemental W/H 22% (37% over $1M YTD). Default: sell-to-cover at vest, you keep the rest. Basis = FMV at vest. Subsequent sale: gain or loss from vest FMV. The 22% supplemental W/H is often insufficient at high incomes — make estimated payments to avoid April surprise.
view.rsu.empty: No vests recorded for this year.
view.rsu.note: If federal shortfall > $1,000, you may owe Q4 estimated tax or underpayment penalty. Adjust W-4 ('additional withholding') or pay 1040-ES Q4.
view.espp.h1.title: // ESPP § 423 CALCULATOR
view.espp.hint.intro: Qualifying disposition: 2 yrs from offering date + 1 yr from purchase date. Discount × FMV-at-offering = ordinary income (capped at gain), rest = LT cap-gain. Disqualifying: discount × FMV-at-purchase = ordinary, rest = ST/LT by hold. $25k/yr employee cap (offering price). 15% discount + lookback = guaranteed ~17.6%+ return at purchase.
view.espp.strat.always_enroll: If you can afford it, ALWAYS enroll at max % — 15% discount + lookback = ~17.6% guaranteed minimum return at purchase
view.espp.strat.sell_immediately: Sell-immediately strategy: locks in ~15% discount as ordinary income, no holding risk
view.espp.strat.qualifying_wait: Qualifying-wait: requires concentrated bet on employer stock (correlated with W-2 income)
view.espp.strat.25k_cap: $25k/yr ESPP cap is based on stock at OFFERING price, not purchase
view.espp.strat.broker_basis: CRITICAL: broker basis is often WRONG (basis = purchase price, but ordinary income is reported on W-2). Adjust basis on Form 8949 to avoid double-tax!
view.bdr.h1.title: // BACKDOOR ROTH
view.bdr.hint.intro: High earners above MAGI phase-out can't contribute to Roth directly. Workaround: contribute non-deductible to Traditional IRA, then convert to Roth. Pro-rata trap: if you have ANY pre-tax IRA balance (Traditional / Rollover / SEP / SIMPLE — but NOT 401(k)), conversion is partly taxable. Form 8606 tracks basis annually.
view.bdr.cleanup.solo_401k: Roll pre-tax IRA balances INTO your Solo 401(k) or employer 401(k) — 401(k) NOT included in pro-rata calc
view.bdr.cleanup.conversion_full: Or fully convert pre-tax IRAs to Roth (taxable, but cleans the slate permanently)
view.bdr.cleanup.spousal: Spousal IRAs are SEPARATE — your spouse's pre-tax balance doesn't affect yours
view.bdr.cleanup.year_end: Year-end balance is what matters for pro-rata — not contribution date
view.bdr.step.contribute: Contribute up to limit to Traditional IRA (mark non-deductible)
view.bdr.step.convert: Within days: convert to Roth IRA
view.bdr.step.form_8606: File Form 8606 to track basis (must file EVERY year you have basis, lifetime!)
view.bdr.step.step_transaction: No 'step-transaction doctrine' enforcement to date — IRS has acquiesced
view.bdr.step.repeat_annually: Repeat annually — total tax-free Roth space accumulated over decades
view.cbwash.h1.title: // CROSS-BROKER WASH SALES
view.cbwash.hint.intro: Wash sale rules apply at the TAXPAYER level, not account level. Sell at IBKR + repurchase at Webull within ±30 days = wash sale. Neither broker's 1099-B catches this — you must self-report. Same applies across spousal accounts and IRAs. Log all sells + buys here, auto-detect. TTS + § 475(f) MTM EXEMPTS you from wash-sale rules entirely.
view.able.h1.title: // ABLE ACCOUNT § 529A
view.able.hint.intro: Tax-advantaged savings for individuals with disabilities. Disability onset before age 26 (raised to 46 starting 2026 per SECURE 2.0). $18k/yr cap (2024). ABLE-to-Work adds up to $14,580 extra from employed beneficiary's own earnings. $100k balance EXCLUDED from SSI asset test.
view.able.benefit.tax_free: Tax-free growth + tax-free withdrawals for qualified disability expenses (housing, transportation, education, employment, health, financial management)
view.able.benefit.ssi: First $100k excluded from SSI asset test ($2k normally cuts off SSI)
view.able.benefit.medicaid: Full balance excluded from Medicaid asset test (in most states)
view.able.benefit.saver_credit: Contributions qualify for Saver's Credit if income low enough
view.able.benefit.529_rollover: SECURE 2.0: 529 plans can be rolled over to ABLE accounts (family member)
view.able.benefit.atw_2026: ABLE Age Adjustment Act: onset-age threshold raises 26 → 46 starting 2026
view.able.providers: Providers vary by state. Top: Ohio STABLE, Tennessee ABLE TN, Virginia ABLEnow. Most allow out-of-state residents. Compare fees + investment options.
view.ce.h1.title: // CONSERVATION EASEMENT § 170(h)
view.ce.hint.intro: 50% AGI charitable deduction (100% for qualified farmers). 15-year carryforward. Donate a permanent restriction on real property to qualified org (land trust). Syndicated deals with 2.5×+ deduction ratio = Listed Transaction (Notice 2017-10) — Form 8886 required + high audit risk + 40% accuracy-related penalty if disallowed.
view.ce.listed_warning_body: Notice 2017-10 and Notice 2023-30 listed syndicated conservation easements as 'Listed Transactions.' IRS won 90%+ of these cases at Tax Court (Bosque Canyon, Mill Road, Plateau Holdings, etc.). Form 8886 required for every investor + every promoter. 40% accuracy-related penalty if disallowed. Inflation Reduction Act 2022 capped deduction at 2.5× basis for pass-through syndications. Most CPAs will refuse to sign these.
view.ce.req.qualified_org: Donee must be qualified organization (governmental unit OR 501(c)(3) land trust)
view.ce.req.qualified_purpose: Conservation purpose: outdoor recreation, natural habitat, open space, historic preservation
view.ce.req.perpetuity: Restriction must be PERPETUAL (binds future owners forever)
view.ce.req.appraisal: Qualified appraisal required (deductions > $500,000 attach to return)
view.ce.req.baseline: Baseline documentation report at time of donation
view.ce.req.real_property: Real property interest — easements, remainder interests, restrictions
view.ce.req.surface_water: Mineral interests retained must not allow surface mining
view.lihtc.h1.title: // LIHTC § 42 TRACKER
view.lihtc.hint.intro: Federal credit for affordable housing investors. 10-year stream of credits. 9% credit: new construction / substantial rehab (competitive state allocation). 4% credit: acquisition / bond-financed (non-competitive). 15-year compliance + 15-year extended-use. Subject to passive activity rules unless REP. Sold by syndicators to corporate / passive investors at 80-95¢ on the dollar.
view.lihtc.note: Recapture risk: if compliance fails in years 1-15, credits CLAW BACK pro-rata. Most syndicators absorb this risk via 'yield guarantee'. Investors typically buy at 80-95¢ per credit dollar, earning 4-7% after-tax IRR + losses to shelter against passive income.
tile.lihtc.label: LIHTC § 42
tile.lihtc.desc: Low Income Housing Credit 10-yr stream + 9% / 4% allocation + compliance period
view.mlp.h1.title: // MLP / K-1 TRACKER
view.mlp.hint.intro: MLPs (EPD, MPLX, ET, MMP) issue K-1s, NOT 1099-DIVs. K-1s ship March–April, delaying tax returns. In an IRA: $1,000 aggregate UBTI triggers Form 990-T and tax PAID FROM THE IRA. Outside IRA: § 469 passive losses suspend until disposition.
view.mlp.warning.ubti: UBTI ≥ $1,000 in your IRA. Custodian files Form 990-T on behalf of the IRA. Tax is paid FROM the IRA. Many investors avoid MLPs in IRAs; switch to MLP ETFs (AMLP, MLPX) which pay corporate tax internally.
view.mlp.empty: No K-1 holdings recorded for this year.
view.hrc.h1.title: // HISTORIC REHAB CREDIT § 47
view.hrc.hint.intro: 20% credit on qualified rehab expenditures for certified historic structures. Building must be NPS-listed or contributing to a Registered Historic District. Post-TCJA: credit spread over 5 years (20%/yr). Passive activity rules apply unless REP.
view.hrc.qual.certified: Building must be a Certified Historic Structure (per NPS)
view.hrc.qual.substantial: 'Substantial rehabilitation': QRE > basis OR > $5,000, whichever is higher, in any 24-month period
view.hrc.qual.qre: QREs: structural rehab, NOT acquisition cost, NOT enlargement, NOT site work
view.hrc.qual.approval: 3-part NPS approval required: Part 1 (eligibility), Part 2 (proposed work), Part 3 (completed work)
view.hrc.qual.recapture: Recapture if disposed within 5 years: pro-rata clawback
view.hrc.qual.depreciation: Building basis reduced by 100% of credit taken (since 2018)
view.hrc.combined.body: Historic rehab credits frequently combine with LIHTC § 42 (low-income housing), opportunity zone § 1400Z deferral, and state historic credits (NY, MA, GA, RI, MO, VA — most have 20-25% state add-on). A typical certified historic + LIHTC + state credit + OZ stack can recover 40-55% of project cost via tax incentives.
view.da.h1.title: // DISABLED ACCESS § 44
view.da.hint.intro: Small business only. Up to $5,000 credit/year for ADA-compliance expenditures. Eligible: gross receipts ≤ $1M prior year OR ≤ 30 FTE employees. Credit = 50% of expenditures between $250 and $10,250. Combine with § 190 Barrier Removal deduction ($15,000/yr).
view.da.elig.receipts: Prior year gross receipts ≤ $1 million, OR
view.da.elig.either: Either test passes = eligible for the credit
view.da.qual.removal: Removal of architectural / communication barriers (ramps, restrooms, doors)
view.da.qual.printed: Print materials in alternate formats (Braille, audio, large print)
view.da.qual.equipment: Adaptive equipment / modifications (computer access, hearing devices)
view.da.qual.acquisition: Acquisition or modification of equipment / devices for disabled use
view.da.warning.cap: Eligible expenditure exceeds $10,250 cap — excess flows to § 190 deduction or capitalized.
view.da.stacking.body: You CANNOT double-dip — every dollar counts for ONE provision. Best practice: claim § 44 credit first (50% pass-through), then apply § 190 to the next $15,000 of expenditures. Above that, normal § 263A capitalization.
view.f181.h1.title: // § 181 FILM / TV / THEATER EXPENSING
view.f181.hint.intro: 100% year-1 deduction for qualified film, TV, and live theatrical productions — up to $15M per production ($20M low-income area). Must be a qualified production: 75% of compensation paid in the US. Restored by TCJA, extended indefinitely by CAA-21. Owners deduct in year placed in service.
view.f181.qual.production_type: Qualifying types: motion picture, TV series, live theatrical
view.f181.qual.us_compensation: ≥ 75% of total compensation paid for US-located services
view.f181.qual.first_44: For TV: only first 44 episodes eligible
view.f181.qual.cap_15m: $15M cap per production ($20M in low-income census tract)
view.f181.qual.election: Make election on year-1 return; passive activity rules apply
view.f181.label.title: Title
view.f181.th.title: Title
view.pd.h1.title: // PARTIAL DISPOSITION § 1.168(i)-8
view.pd.hint.intro: When replacing a building component (roof, HVAC, windows, plumbing) on commercial / rental property, ELECT to dispose of the OLD component on Form 4797. Take loss = remaining undepreciated basis. Stop future depreciation on the disposed component. Election made on return for year of disposition. Missed? File Form 3115 (Rev. Proc. 2015-13 auto change #205) — no IRS consent needed.
view.pd.why.loss: Recognize loss = unrecovered basis on Form 4797
view.pd.why.stop_dep: Stop depreciating phantom basis on a component no longer there
view.pd.why.tpr: Required by Tangible Property Regs to avoid double-depreciation
view.pd.why.no_recapture: No § 1250 recapture (component was fully residential / commercial real)
view.pd.why.3115_missed: Form 3115 for missed years: § 481(a) catch-up, negative income adj.
view.tts.h1.title: // TRADER TAX STATUS SCORER
view.tts.hint.intro: TTS isn't a checkbox — it's a facts-and-circumstances test from case law (Holsinger, Endicott, Vines, Mayer). Without TTS, you can't make a § 475(f) MTM election. Score yourself against documented benchmarks: ≥720 total trades/yr, daily activity (≥4 hrs/day), short avg holds (under 31 days), business-like setup, profit-from-short-term-moves intent.
view.tts.label.only_trader: Trading is your sole / primary occupation?
view.tts.case.holsinger: Holsinger v. Comm'r (2008) — DENIED TTS: 372 trades, $3M volume insufficient
view.tts.case.endicott: Endicott v. Comm'r (2013) — DENIED: only 1.7-yr avg hold, options writer not trader
view.tts.case.vines: Vines v. Comm'r (2006) — GRANTED relief for late MTM § 9100 election
view.tts.case.mayer: Mayer v. Comm'r (1994) — DENIED: investments, not trading; 600 trades / 5.3-yr hold
view.tts.case.chen: Chen v. Comm'r (2004) — DENIED: 323 trades, 94 trading days, lacks frequency
view.tts.benefit.expenses: Schedule C business expense deduction (no 2% floor, no AMT)
view.tts.benefit.retirement: Solo 401(k), SEP IRA contributions on trading income
view.tts.benefit.475f: Eligible to make § 475(f) MTM election (no wash sales, ordinary treatment)
view.475f.h1.title: // § 475(f) MTM ELECTION
view.475f.hint.intro: Trader Tax Status holders elect ordinary treatment: all positions marked to market Dec 31, gains/losses ordinary, NO wash-sale rules, NO $3,000 annual capital loss limit. Election deadline: April 15 of the year you want it effective (for new entities: 75 days from formation). Revocation requires Form 3115.
view.475f.timing.individual: Individual: by April 15 of election year (e.g., for 2026 effective, file by April 15, 2026)
view.475f.timing.entity: New entity (LLC / S-corp): within 75 days of formation
view.475f.timing.statement: Attach election statement to extension OR original return
view.475f.timing.form_3115_in: Form 3115 (Method Change) due with first MTM return
view.475f.timing.late: Late? Vines § 9100 relief (good cause) OR Rev. Proc. 99-17
view.s195.h1.title: // § 195 START-UP COST AMORTIZATION
view.s195.hint.intro: $5,000 immediate deduction in year of business opening + remainder amortized over 180 months (15 years). Phase-out: $5k cap reduces $1-for-$1 starting at $50k total expenses; gone at $55k. § 248 follows same rule for organizational costs (legal fees to form C-corp / partnership).
view.s195.qual.travel: Travel + consulting fees to secure suppliers, customers
view.s195.qual.legal: Legal / accounting fees BEFORE opening (not § 248 org)
view.s195.nonqual.investigating: Investigating but NOT starting (only if business not pursued)
view.s195.warning.phaseout: Total start-up costs exceed $50,000 phase-out threshold. Immediate deduction reduced $1-for-$1. Above $55,000 the entire $5k cap is gone — all costs go to 180-month amortization.
view.s1244.h1.title: // § 1244 ORDINARY LOSS ON SBS
view.s1244.hint.intro: Convert capital loss to ORDINARY loss up to $50k single / $100k MFJ when small business stock goes south. Stock must be in a domestic corp with $1M aggregate capital raised (cumulative). You must be the ORIGINAL ISSUEE, not a transferee. Major rescue for failed startup investors.
view.s1244.qual.domestic: Domestic C-corp or S-corp (not LLC, not foreign)
view.s1244.qual.aggregate: Corp aggregate capital raised at issuance ≤ $1,000,000
view.s1244.qual.passive: < 50% of gross receipts from passive sources (royalties, rents, dividends, interest, capital gain) over preceding 5 years
view.s1244.qual.issuee: You must be the ORIGINAL ISSUEE (acquired in exchange for money or property, NOT services)
view.s1244.qual.individual: Only individuals + partnerships qualify (not corporations, not trusts)
view.s1244.qual.no_swap: NOT acquired via tax-free reorg / contribution
view.s1244.label.corp_capital: Corp aggregate capital raised at issuance ($)
view.s1244.warning.fails: Does NOT qualify. Loss is capital, subject to $3,000/yr offset.
view.s280f.h1.title: // § 280F LUXURY AUTO DEPRECIATION
view.s280f.hint.intro: Caps depreciation on passenger autos + light trucks. Heavy SUVs (GVWR > 6,000 lbs) escape § 280F, get up to $30,500 § 179 + bonus. Personal use must be ≤ 50% to take bonus/§179. 2024 first-year limits: $20,400 with bonus, $12,400 without. Year 2: $19,800. Year 3: $11,900. Year 4+: $7,160/yr.
view.s280f.heavy.suv: Cadillac Escalade, Chevy Suburban / Tahoe, Ford Expedition, GMC Yukon
view.s280f.heavy.trucks: F-150 / F-250 / F-350, Silverado 1500+, Ram 1500+, Tundra
view.s280f.heavy.luxury: Range Rover, Mercedes G-Wagen, Lexus LX, BMW X7, Audi Q7/Q8
view.s280f.heavy.ev: Tesla Model X (most builds), Rivian R1T/R1S, F-150 Lightning
view.s280f.heavy.note: Always verify the SPECIFIC model + trim's GVWR on the manufacturer doorjamb sticker
view.s280f.recapture.body: If business use drops to ≤ 50% in any later year, § 280F(d)(1) requires recapture of all bonus + § 179 deducted in prior years — included as ordinary income. Keep contemporaneous mileage log (Form 4562 line 30).
view.s280f.warning.under50: Business use ≤ 50% — bonus depreciation + § 179 not available. Straight-line over 5 yrs only.
view.s197.h1.title: // § 197 INTANGIBLE AMORTIZATION
view.s197.hint.intro: Straight-line 15-year amortization on purchased intangibles. Covers goodwill, going concern, workforce in place, customer / supplier list, covenants not to compete, franchise / trademark / trade name, purchased patents / copyrights / software, license / permit / certificate. Self-created goodwill NOT subject to § 197 — capitalize as zero basis. 180-month line, mid-month convention.
view.s274.h1.title: // § 274 MEALS / GIFTS / ENTERTAINMENT
view.s274.hint.intro: Post-TCJA (2018+): business entertainment 100% DISALLOWED (sports tickets, golf, theater, concerts). Business meals 50% deductible (briefly 100% restaurants 2021-22 under CAA). Client gifts capped at $25/recipient/year (de minimis branded items < $4 with logo exempt).
view.s274.rule.meal_50: Business meal with client / prospect: 50% deductible
view.s274.rule.travel_meal: Travel meal (away from tax home): 50% deductible
view.s274.rule.gift_25: Client gift: $25/recipient/yr (per donor not per gift)
view.s274.rule.de_minimis: De minimis branded items (< $4, logo) exempt from $25 cap
view.s274.empty: No entries logged for this year.
view.solo.h1.title: // SOLO 401(k) CONTRIBUTION CALC
view.solo.hint.intro: 2024 limits: employee deferral $23,000 ($30,500 with 50+ catch-up, $34,250 with SECURE 2.0 60-63 super-catch-up), employer profit-share up to 25% W-2 comp or 20% SE net, combined cap $69,000 ($76,500 / $80,250 with catch-ups). MUST ESTABLISH BY DEC 31; can FUND through tax return due date (incl. extensions).
view.solo.h2.tradeoffs: Solo 401(k) vs SEP IRA vs SIMPLE IRA
view.solo.note.roth: Roth deferral: no year-1 tax saving on employee portion (you pay tax now); qualified withdrawals fully tax-free. Employer match always pre-tax (Roth match SECURE 2.0 optional).
view.grat.h1.title: // GRAT — GRANTOR RETAINED ANNUITY TRUST
view.grat.hint.intro: Estate-freezing technique. You contribute volatile / appreciating asset (pre-IPO stock, RSU shares, growth stock). Receive back an annuity over 2-10 yrs equal to principal + 7520 rate. Walton zeroed-out GRAT: gift value = $0. Excess appreciation passes to remainder beneficiaries (kids, trust) GIFT-TAX-FREE. Risk: grantor must SURVIVE the term or assets reverse to estate.
view.grat.mech.zero: Walton GRAT zeroes out the gift: annuity stream actuarially = principal × 7520
view.grat.mech.short: Short term (2-3 yrs) maximizes appreciation transfer + survives mortality risk
view.grat.mech.rolling: 'Rolling GRAT' strategy: chain 2-yr GRATs back-to-back, lock in gains as you go
view.grat.mech.grantor_trust: Grantor pays income tax on trust income — additional wealth transfer mechanism
view.grat.mech.swap_power: Substitution power lets grantor swap depreciated property out for cash
view.grat.mech.no_gst: GRATs DON'T allocate GST exemption efficiently (use SLAT / dynasty for grandchildren)
view.grat.mech.no_step_up: Remainder gets carryover basis — NO step-up at grantor death
view.grat.risk.mortality: Grantor death during term: all assets revert to estate
view.grat.risk.under_perform: Underperformance: annuity exhausts trust, nothing to remainder
view.grat.risk.bba_proposal: Biden / BBB proposals threatened to REQUIRE 10-yr minimum, eliminate zero-GRAT
view.grat.risk.audit: IRS scrutiny on hard-to-value assets (private stock, real estate)
view.s469.h1.title: // § 469 PASSIVE ACTIVITY + REP
view.s469.hint.intro: Passive losses only offset passive income — excess SUSPENDED indefinitely until activity disposition or future passive income absorbs. Real Estate Professional (REP): ≥ 750 hrs/yr in real estate trades AND > 50% of personal services in real estate. REP rentals lose passive presumption → potentially unlimited offset against ordinary income (still need material participation per activity, or aggregation election).
view.s469.label.hours_rentals: Your hours in real estate this year
view.s469.label.mp_only_one: You did substantially all the work?
view.s469.warning.self_rental: Self-rental trap: under § 469 net rental INCOME from property leased to your own active business is RECHARACTERIZED as non-passive — cannot absorb other passive losses. Net rental LOSS stays passive. Don't let your CPA put the property in the same entity.
view.s469.case.hakkak: Hakkak v. Comm'r (2020) — DENIED: contemporaneous log lacking, 'ballpark guesses'
view.s469.case.miller: Miller v. Comm'r (2010) — GRANTED: full-time builder, kept time log, 1000+ hrs
view.s469.case.bailey: Bailey v. Comm'r (2001) — DENIED: full-time IBM engineer, can't satisfy >50% test
view.s469.case.agarwal: Agarwal v. Comm'r (2009) — DENIED: physician spouse not REP, >50% in medicine
view.s469.case.lapid: Lapid v. Comm'r (2017) — DENIED: failed material participation per activity test
view.s469.aggregation.body: If you have multiple rentals, file an aggregation election treating ALL as ONE activity. Then 750-hr / 100-hr / material participation tests applied across aggregate, not per property. ELECTION IS IRREVOCABLE absent material change of facts. Attach statement to year-1 return. Without this, each property tested separately and material participation often fails.
tile.grat.label: GRAT
tile.grat.desc: Walton zeroed-out grantor retained annuity trust — appreciation transfer gift-free
view.sep.h1.title: // SEP IRA CONTRIBUTION CALC
view.sep.hint.intro: 2024 cap: $69,000. Employer contributes up to 25% of W-2 comp or ~20% of net SE earnings (after half-SE-tax). NO employee deferral. NO catch-up. Comp cap $345,000. Setup AND fund by tax return due date (incl. extensions). Easiest plan to run — single form 5305-SEP.
view.sep.cov.three_of_five: Must cover any employee who worked in 3 of last 5 yrs
view.sep.cov.wages_750: Must have earned ≥ $750 (2024) in current year
view.sep.cov.uniform: Contribution % must be UNIFORM across all eligible employees
view.sep.cov.no_skip: Cannot exclude long-term employees; can't favor highly compensated
view.sep.cov.same_for_owner: Owner gets SAME percentage rate as employees
view.sep.warning.employees: With eligible employees, SEP-IRA becomes EXPENSIVE — same % must go to them. Consider Solo 401(k) (no employees) or Safe Harbor 401(k) (lower employer cost via employee deferral structure).
view.s72t.h1.title: // § 72(t) SEPP CALCULATOR
view.s72t.hint.intro: Early IRA / 401(k) withdrawal before 59½ without 10% penalty via Substantially Equal Periodic Payments. THREE approved methods: RMD, Amortization, Annuitization. Must continue ≥ 5 years OR until 59½ (whichever later). MODIFY in those years and 10% penalty applies back to year 1 + interest. SECURE 2.0 floors rate at max(5%, 120% AFR).
view.s72t.note.amort: Balance amortized at rate over life expectancy
view.s72t.mod.amount_change: Changing the amount (except amort→RMD one-time switch)
view.s72t.mod.rollover: Rolling money IN OR OUT of the SEPP account
view.s72t.mod.account_zero: Account goes to zero (no longer 'substantially equal')
view.daf.h1.title: // DAF — DONOR ADVISED FUND
view.daf.hint.intro: Front-load charitable giving in high-income years. Contribute appreciated LT stock → FMV deduction (30% AGI cap) + avoid LTCG tax. DAF distributes to charity over time at your direction. Bunching strategy: stack 5 years of giving into one to exceed standard deduction, then itemize. Fidelity Charitable / Schwab Charitable / Vanguard Charitable: $5-25k min.
view.slat.h1.title: // SLAT — SPOUSAL LIFETIME ACCESS TRUST
view.slat.hint.intro: Lock in $13.61M ($27.22M MFJ) lifetime exemption before 2026 sunset to ~$7M ($14M MFJ). Gift to irrevocable trust for spouse's benefit — they have access during marriage. Each spouse can create one for the OTHER. Reciprocal-trust trap: trusts MUST differ in material terms (timing, beneficiaries, distribution standards) or IRS treats both as if grantor kept rights → assets back in estate.
view.slat.recip.different_beneficiaries: Different remainder beneficiaries (one excludes child, etc.)
view.slat.recip.different_powers: Different distribution standards (HEMS vs ascertainable vs discretionary)
view.slat.recip.estate_of_grace: Estate of Grace v. Comm'r (1969) is the leading case
view.slat.risk.divorce: DIVORCE: you lose indirect access via spouse; trust continues for ex-spouse
view.slat.risk.spouse_death: Spouse death before yours: loss of indirect access, but assets stay out of estate
view.slat.risk.no_step_up: No § 1014 step-up at death — beneficiary inherits carryover basis
view.slat.risk.grantor_trust: Most SLATs are grantor trusts — YOU pay the income tax (adds wealth transfer)
view.slat.risk.clawback_election: Clawback regs Rev. Proc. 2018-52: anti-clawback for completed gifts
view.slat.warning.sunset: POST-2026 sunset: lifetime exemption drops from $13.61M to ~$7M per spouse. Treasury anti-clawback regs (Rev. Proc. 2018-52) preserve completed pre-sunset gifts. If estate-exposed, complete SLATs BEFORE 2026.
view.s121.h1.title: // § 121 PRINCIPAL RESIDENCE EXCLUSION
view.s121.hint.intro: $250k single / $500k MFJ excluded from gain on sale of principal residence. Ownership test: ≥ 2 of last 5 yrs. Use test: ≥ 2 of last 5 yrs as principal residence. Once per 2 years. Post-2008 NON-QUALIFIED USE (rentals, vacation) allocated pro-rata. Job/health/unforeseen circumstance: partial exclusion (months ÷ 24).
view.s121.spec.widow: Surviving spouse: $500k MFJ exclusion preserved if sold within 2 years of spouse death
view.s121.spec.divorce: Divorce: time tacks via § 121(d)(3) for transferred-in spouse
view.s121.spec.disabled: Disabled person facility care counts as residence use
view.s121.spec.military: Military / Foreign Service / Intel: 10-yr suspended period
view.s121.spec.dep_recapture: § 1250 unrecaptured dep recapture EXCLUDED from § 121 — taxed at 25% max
view.s121.spec.no_capital_loss: Personal residence: § 165 NO loss deductible (only investment / rental)
tile.daf.label: DAF
tile.daf.desc: Donor Advised Fund — front-load FMV deduction + LTCG avoidance + 5-yr carryforward
tile.slat.label: SLAT
tile.slat.desc: Spousal Lifetime Access Trust — lock $13.6M exemption pre-2026 + reciprocal-trust trap
view.s1361.h1.title: // § 1361 S-CORP + REASONABLE COMP
view.s1361.hint.intro: Form 2553 election: 2 months 15 days into election year (Mar 15 for calendar). LATE election? Rev. Proc. 2013-30 auto-relief if reasonable cause + within 3-yr 75-day window. Reasonable Compensation: W-2 wages must be arms-length BEFORE distributions. Saves SE tax (15.3%) on profit ABOVE reasonable comp. Audit risk: Watson 8th Cir. lost $24k comp on $200k profit case.
view.s1361.elect.deadline: Form 2553 due by Mar 15 of effective year (calendar) OR within 2 mo 15 days of inception
view.s1361.elect.late: Late election: Rev. Proc. 2013-30 auto-relief if < 3 yr 75 days + reasonable cause
view.s1361.elect.consent: All shareholders must sign / spouse must consent if community property
view.s1361.elect.eligible: Eligible: 100 shareholder cap, 1 class of stock, no foreign owners, no C-corp/LLC owners
view.s1361.elect.trusts: Trust shareholders: ESBT or QSST election required
view.s1361.elect.tax_year: Calendar year required unless § 444 election + back-up payment
view.s1361.card.se_saved: SE tax saved vs Schedule C
view.s1361.case.watson: Watson, P.C. v. United States (8th Cir. 2012) — DENIED $24k on $200k+ profit; reclassified $91k as wages
view.s1361.case.sean: Sean McAlary Ltd. v. Comm'r (2013) — DENIED $24k for real estate agent; reclassified $83k
view.s1361.case.glass_blocks: Glass Blocks Unlimited v. Comm'r (2013) — DENIED $30k on $700k revenue
view.s1361.case.davis: Davis v. United States (1976, 11th Cir.) — established multi-factor framework
view.s1361.case.fact_sheet: IRS Fact Sheet 2008-25: 9 factors for reasonable comp determination
view.s162l.h1.title: // § 162(l) SE HEALTH INSURANCE
view.s162l.hint.intro: Above-the-line deduction (Schedule 1) for medical / dental / LTC premiums paid by self-employed, partners, and > 2% S-corp shareholders. Reduces AGI (not just Schedule A). LTC limited by age table. S-corp: premiums must be paid by corp + included in W-2 Box 1 wages + reported in Box 14 (not Box 3/5). DISALLOWED if eligible for spouse's subsidized employer plan.
view.s162l.s_corp.payor: Premiums must be paid by S-corp (not personally)
view.s162l.s_corp.w2: Must be added to W-2 Box 1 (taxable income) + Box 14 (informational)
view.s162l.s_corp.shareholder: Owner deducts on personal return Schedule 1 line 17
view.s162l.s_corp.reasonable_comp: Must have reasonable comp before § 162(l) deduction allowed
view.s162l.warning.spouse_plan: DISALLOWED: § 162(l)(2)(B) blocks the deduction in any month you're eligible for a subsidized employer plan via spouse. Pay premiums + take Schedule A 7.5% AGI floor instead.
view.s7872.h1.title: // § 7872 INTRA-FAMILY LOAN / AFR
view.s7872.hint.intro: Loan to family below the Applicable Federal Rate (AFR) → IRS imputes interest income to lender + may impute a gift. AFR is the IRS-published minimum monthly: short-term (≤3 yr), mid-term (3-9 yr), long-term (>9 yr). $10,000 gift loan safe harbor entirely exempt. $100,000 limit: imputed income capped at borrower's net investment income.
view.s7872.afr.irs: IRS publishes monthly via Rev. Rul. (typically third Friday of prior month)
view.s7872.afr.bracket: Short = ≤ 3 yr, mid = > 3 ≤ 9 yr, long = > 9 yr
view.s7872.afr.month_lock: Use rate from month loan is made (or lower of two prior months for term loan)
view.s7872.afr.fred: Bloomberg, FRED, IRS website all carry monthly AFR tables
view.crut.h1.title: // CRUT — CHARITABLE REMAINDER UNITRUST
view.crut.hint.intro: Donate appreciated asset → trust pays you fixed % of FMV revalued annually for life or up to 20-yr term. Remainder to charity. Income tax deduction = PV of charitable remainder. Min payout 5%, max 50%, remainder ≥ 10% test. Variants: standard (annual revalue), NICRUT (net income), NIMCRUT (makeup), FLIP-CRUT (triggers from NICRUT → standard). No immediate cap gains; tax stretched across distributions per 4-tier ordering rule.
view.crut.tier.ordinary: Tier 1: ordinary income (interest, dividends, ord biz)
view.crut.tier.cap_gain: Tier 2: capital gain (ST first, then LT — including pre-funding embedded gain)
view.ilit.h1.title: // ILIT — IRREV. LIFE INSURANCE TRUST
view.ilit.hint.intro: Holds life insurance outside estate. ILIT applies for / owns / names beneficiary — you can't have incidents of ownership. Pay premiums via Crummey letter gifts (each beneficiary gets 30-day withdrawal right → converts future interest into present interest, qualifying for $18k annual exclusion). 3-year lookback: existing policy transferred to ILIT within 3 yrs of death → insured-owned at death → in estate.
view.ilit.warning.lookback: § 2035 3-year rule: existing-policy transfer to ILIT counts as if you owned at death if you die within 3 years. Death benefit FULL VALUE back in estate. Better: have ILIT APPLY for and own a NEW policy from inception.
view.ilit.compl.no_ownership: Insured retains NO incidents of ownership (cash value access, beneficiary change, etc.)
view.ilit.compl.crummey_letter: Crummey letter to each beneficiary within reasonable time of premium gift
view.ilit.compl.5_5_rule: '5×5 power' limit: max(5%, $5,000) lapses avoid gift tax to grantor's other beneficiaries
view.ilit.compl.hanging_powers: 'Hanging Crummey powers' carry over excess → use to avoid 5×5 lapses
view.ilit.compl.split_premium: If MFJ split-gift, file 709 even if no tax due
view.ilit.compl.transfer_for_value: § 101(a)(2) transfer-for-value rule: don't sell policy to ILIT for cash
view.ilit.alt.spouse_owned: Spouse-owned policy (simpler, but in spouse's estate)
view.ilit.alt.private_split: Private split-dollar with ILIT (preserves cash value access)
tile.crut.label: CRUT
tile.crut.desc: Charitable Remainder Unitrust — 5-50% payout + 10% remainder test + 4-tier ordering
tile.ilit.label: ILIT
tile.ilit.desc: Irrev. Life Insurance Trust — Crummey letters + 3-yr lookback + 5×5 power
view.s168k.h1.title: // § 168(k) BONUS DEPRECIATION
view.s168k.hint.intro: TCJA phase-down: 100% (2017-22) → 80% (2023) → 60% (2024) → 40% (2025) → 20% (2026) → 0% (2027+). Property must be tangible, MACRS < 20 yr recovery, placed in service in the calendar year. Qualified Improvement Property (QIP) now eligible after CARES Act fix. Election OUT made class-by-class on timely return. Stacks under § 179 (which goes first).
view.s168.h1.title: // § 168 MACRS DEPRECIATION SCHEDULE
view.s168.hint.intro: Standard MACRS recovery periods: 3-yr (tools), 5-yr (computers, autos), 7-yr (office furniture), 15-yr (QIP, land improvements), 20-yr (farm buildings), 27.5-yr (residential rental, SL), 39-yr (non-residential real, SL). Half-year convention for most personal property; mid-quarter if > 40% Q4; mid-month for real property. QIP fixed by CARES Act 2020.
view.s263a.h1.title: // § 263A UNICAP + SMALL BIZ EXEMPTION
view.s263a.hint.intro: Producers + resellers must capitalize indirect costs into inventory and self-constructed assets — defers deduction until inventory sold or asset disposed. Small biz exemption: 3-yr avg gross receipts ≤ $30M (2024, indexed), no UNICAP required. TCJA raised threshold from $10M. Form 3115 method change required.
view.s263a.note.exempt_advice: Small biz exemption applies: expense indirect costs as incurred + simpler inventory accounting allowed (cash basis OR no inventory method per § 471(c)).
view.s263a.note.required: UNICAP required. Use Simplified Production Method (SPM) or Simplified Resale Method (SRM) absorption ratios. Form 3115 to change methods.
view.s6654.h1.title: // § 6654 ESTIMATED TAX SAFE HARBOR
view.s6654.hint.intro: Safe harbors avoid underpayment penalty: (1) 90% of current-year tax OR (2) 100% of prior-year tax (110% if prior AGI > $150k). Quarterly due: Apr 15, Jun 15, Sep 15, Jan 15. Withholding is treated as paid evenly across the year. Penalty = federal short-term AFR + 3% per quarter on each quarter's shortage. Farmers / fishermen: 66.67% safe harbor + Jan 15 single-date pay.
view.s6654.work.withhold_dec: Increase W-2 withholding in December — treated as paid evenly across year
view.s6654.work.annualized: Form 2210 Schedule AI: annualized income method for lumpy / Q4-loaded income
view.s6654.work.january: January 15 final due date — pay shortage then
view.s6654.work.farmer: Farmer / fisherman: pay 66.67% by Jan 15 OR full tax by Mar 1, no quarterly
view.s6654.work.first_year: First-year filer with no prior-year liability: no penalty
view.s911.h1.title: // § 911 FOREIGN EARNED INCOME EXCLUSION
view.s911.hint.intro: US citizens / residents working abroad exclude up to $126,500 (2024) of foreign earned income. Two tests: Bona Fide Resident (full calendar year + intent) OR Physical Presence (330 full days / any 12 mo). Stacks with § 911(c) Housing Cost exclusion (~$17k base, higher in HCOL cities). Self-employed get housing DEDUCTION not exclusion. Election made on Form 2555.
view.s911.note.election: Election on Form 2555 — once made, REVOKE requires IRS consent + 5-yr disqualification
view.s911.note.ftc_stacking: Below-excluded income still subject to stacking rule under § 911(f)
view.s911.note.fbar: Still must file FBAR + Form 8938 + FATCA + 1040
view.s911.note.se_tax: FEIE does NOT exempt SE tax — pay 15.3% on net SE earnings
view.s911.note.totalization: Totalization Agreement countries: exempt SE tax via foreign social security
view.s911.note.choose_method: Compare FEIE vs Foreign Tax Credit (Form 1116) — sometimes FTC alone superior
view.s1411.h1.title: // § 1411 NIIT 3.8% SURTAX
view.s1411.hint.intro: 3.8% surtax on net investment income when MAGI > $200k single / $250k MFJ / $125k MFS. Tax base = LESSER of (1) net investment income OR (2) MAGI excess over threshold. § 475(f) MTM ordinary EXCLUDED (huge trader benefit). Active S-corp earnings EXCLUDED. Retirement distributions EXCLUDED. Reported on Form 8960. Not indexed for inflation.
view.s1411.excl.active_kone: Active S-corp distributions / K-1 if material participation
view.s1411.excl.section_988: § 988 forex ordinary if held as trader
view.s1411.plan.475f: § 475(f) MTM election if TTS-qualified — escape NIIT entirely on trading
view.s1411.plan.material: Establish material participation in passive activities — recharacterize as active
view.s1411.plan.bunching: Bunch investment income into low-MAGI years
view.s1411.plan.roth: Roth conversions — distributions exempt forever after qualifying
view.s1411.plan.muni: Shift to municipal bonds — interest excluded
view.s1411.plan.qof: § 1400Z Opportunity Zone investment — defer + reduce gain
view.s280e.h1.title: // § 280E CANNABIS DISALLOWANCE
view.s280e.hint.intro: Schedule I / II trafficker businesses CANNOT deduct ordinary business expenses. Only COGS via § 471 escapes — direct + indirect production / acquisition costs. Result: effective tax rates can EXCEED 70%. Champ v. Comm'r (2007) carve-out: separate non-cannabis activity (caregiving services) keeps its own deductions. Olive v. Comm'r (9th Cir. 2015) tightened that exception.
view.s280e.cogs.indirect_production: Indirect production: utilities, depreciation, supervisor wages allocable to grow rooms
view.s280e.dis.rent: Dispensary rent (production area allocable to COGS)
view.s280e.case.champ: Champ v. Comm'r (2007) — separate caregiving activity escapes § 280E
view.s280e.case.olive: Olive v. Comm'r (9th Cir. 2015) — caregiving DOES NOT escape if too entwined
view.s280e.case.harborside: Harborside Health Center v. Comm'r (2018) — COGS strictly limited to § 471 production
view.s280e.case.alterman: Alterman v. Comm'r (2018) — denied § 471(c) attempt to recharacterize as inventory
view.s280e.case.alpenglow: Alpenglow Botanicals v. United States (10th Cir. 2018) — § 280E constitutionality upheld
view.s280e.note.future: Federal rescheduling (DEA Schedule I → III proposal pending) would END § 280E disallowance. Strong push from MORE Act and SAFE Banking Act.
view.s165g.h1.title: // § 165(g) + § 1234A WORTHLESS / TERMINATED
view.s165g.hint.intro: § 165(g): security becomes worthless in year → capital loss treated as sold for $0 on Dec 31. Hold period extended to year-end: > 1 yr held = LT. § 165(g)(3) affiliate exception: parent owning ≥ 80% takes ORDINARY loss. § 1234A: terminated rights, cancelled options, expired forward contracts → capital loss on TERMINATION date, preserving holding period.
view.s165g.empty: No worthless / terminated items for this year.
view.s165g.proof.no_liability: Delisting alone insufficient — need NO LIABILITY value remaining
view.s165g.proof.identifiable_event: 'Identifiable event' doctrine — bankruptcy filing OR cessation of trading
view.s165g.proof.documentation: Document: news, SEC filings, broker confirmation, expert opinion
view.s165g.proof.refund_claim: If wrong year identified: § 6511(d)(1) extends SOL to 7 yrs
view.s2010c.h1.title: // § 2010(c) DSUE PORTABILITY
view.s2010c.hint.intro: Surviving spouse claims Deceased Spousal Unused Exemption (DSUE). Election deadline: 9 months from death (15 with auto extension). Rev. Proc. 2022-32: simplified relief if not required to file — up to 5 years late. Without election, deceased spouse's unused exemption is FORFEITED. Combined exemption up to ~$27M (2024) without trust planning.
view.s2010c.warn.assumed_below: Assumed estate too small to need 706 → DSUE forfeited
view.s2010c.warn.late_deadline: 9-month deadline missed → use Rev. Proc. 2022-32 simplified relief (5 yr)
view.s2010c.warn.no_inflation: DSUE FROZEN — survivor exemption indexes, deceased's does not
view.s2010c.warn.last_remarriage: 'Last DSUE rule' — remarriage + death of new spouse without 706 → original DSUE lost
view.s2010c.warn.gst_separate: GST exemption is NEVER portable — separate planning needed
view.s2010c.warn.audit_open: Survivor's estate can re-open deceased's return to challenge DSUE valuation
view.s2010c.note.simplified: Past 15-mo deadline but within 5 yrs — file under Rev. Proc. 2022-32 simplified relief. Mark Form 706 with 'FILED PURSUANT TO REV. PROC. 2022-32' at top.
view.s174.h1.title: // § 174 R&D CAPITALIZATION
view.s174.hint.intro: TCJA 2017 forced mandatory capitalization of R&D effective tax years after 12/31/2021. US-based R&D: 5-year SL (60 months); foreign: 15-year SL (180 months). Mid-year convention: year-1 deduction is only 10% of US QREs (half of 1/5). Huge working-capital hit for tech / pharma / startups. § 41 R&D Credit unaffected. Repeal efforts ongoing.
view.s174.qual.wages: W-2 wages of researchers + supporting employees
view.s174.qual.contractors: Contractor R&D (65% allowed for credit; 100% capitalized)
view.s174.qual.supplies: Supplies consumed in R&D (chemicals, prototypes, sensors)
view.s174.qual.software: In-house software development costs (Rev. Proc. 2000-50 prior, now § 174)
view.s174.qual.patents: Patent attorney fees (acquisition costs, NOT defense)
view.s174.trans.first_year: First mandatory year: TY beginning after 12/31/2021
view.s174.trans.method_change: Automatic method change (DCN 265) — no IRS consent
view.s174.trans.repeal_efforts: Multiple bipartisan repeal bills (Tax Relief for American Families 2024)
view.s174.trans.retroactive: Likely retroactive if repealed — file protective extension
view.s174.trans.cash_impact: Industry impact: many startups forced into book/tax NOLs
view.s691.h1.title: // § 691 IRD INCOME IN RESPECT OF DECEDENT
view.s691.hint.intro: Income earned but not received before death. Beneficiary reports as ORDINARY income when received. NO § 1014 step-up in basis. § 691(c) deduction: recipient takes income tax deduction for estate tax attributable to IRD — prevents double taxation. Most common: traditional IRA / 401(k) balances, accrued interest, accounts receivable, NQ deferred comp, deferred IRC § 453 installment sale.
view.s691.plan.roth_convert: Pre-death Roth conversion: removes IRD treatment, gets future-tax-free
view.s691.plan.charity: Name charity as IRA beneficiary — charity avoids income tax
view.s691.plan.crt: Charitable Remainder Trust as IRA beneficiary — stretches income
view.s691.plan.stretch_secure: SECURE Act 2020 killed lifetime stretch — 10-yr cap for non-spouse
view.s691.plan.qpb: 'Eligible designated beneficiary' exceptions: spouse, minor child, disabled, < 10 yr younger
view.s691.plan.691c: § 691(c) deduction is a MISC itemized — suspended by TCJA through 2025?
view.s691.plan.nimcrut: NIMCRUT funded with IRA for stretch-like outcome
view.s25a.h1.title: // § 25A AOTC + LLC EDUCATION CREDITS
view.s25a.hint.intro: AOTC: $2,500/student/year — first 4 undergraduate years, ≥ half-time, no felony drug conviction. 40% refundable. LLC: $2,000 / RETURN (not per student) — 20% of first $10k. Unlimited years. Less generous but covers graduate / part-time. MAGI phase-outs: $80-90k single / $160-180k MFJ.
