view.s25a.label.name: Student name
view.s25a.th.name: Name
view.s25a.exp.books_aotc: AOTC ONLY: required books + supplies + equipment (whether or not from school)
view.s25a.exp.books_llc: LLC: required ONLY if purchased FROM school as condition of enrollment
view.s25a.exp.not_room: NOT room & board, transportation, insurance, medical, optional fees
view.s25a.exp.scholarship_offset: Reduce expenses by tax-free scholarships / grants / employer assistance
view.s25a.exp.529_no_double: 529 plan distributions: don't double-claim same dollars (electable allocation)
view.s221.h1.title: // § 221 STUDENT LOAN INTEREST
view.s221.hint.intro: Above-the-line deduction (Schedule 1) up to $2,500/year per RETURN. MAGI phase-out 2024: $80k-$95k single / $165k-$195k MFJ. MFS NOT allowed. Cannot be claimed if you're a DEPENDENT on someone else's return. Loan must be qualified — for taxpayer / spouse / dependent's education. Voluntary and capitalized interest both count.
view.s221.label.dependent: Claimed as dependent on another return?
view.s221.warning.ineligible: Not eligible: MFS filers + dependents on another return are disqualified.
view.s221.qual.education: Money used SOLELY to pay qualified education expenses
view.s221.qual.who: For taxpayer / spouse / dependent at time loan taken out
view.s221.qual.related: NOT from related person (parent), employer benefit, or qualified employer plan
view.s221.qual.timing: Loan must be primarily liable on (cosigners may share)
view.s221.qual.refinanced: Refinanced student loans: maintain qualified character if proceeds used only to pay original
view.s221.note.secure: Effective 2024: employers can MATCH employee student loan payments to 401(k) / 403(b) plan. Treated like deferral for match purposes — even if employee makes no plan contribution. Helps young workers debt-paydown + retirement save simultaneously.
view.crat.h1.title: // CRAT — CHARITABLE REMAINDER ANNUITY TRUST
view.crat.hint.intro: Pays grantor (or named beneficiary) FIXED dollar annuity (not %) each year. Min 5%, max 50% of initial FMV. Remainder ≥ 10% test. 5% probability-of-exhaustion test killed many CRATs post-2016. All four corners frozen at funding — no future revaluation. Distinct from CRUT (annual revalue). Best for bond / income asset where appreciation upside not needed.
view.crat.exhaustion.body: Rev. Rul. 77-374: if probability that the trust will run out of money BEFORE the charity receives anything exceeds 5%, the trust does not qualify. Causes: payout rate / § 7520 rate / life expectancy combination. Modern low rates + long expected lifetimes have made CRATs hard to qualify. Notice 2008-90 + Rev. Proc. 2016-42 'qualified contingency' clause lets CRAT include early-termination clause if probability would exceed 5%.
view.res.h1.title: // STATE RESIDENCY DAY-COUNT
view.res.hint.intro: Most states: > 183 days physical presence + domicile = statutory resident. NY / CA aggressive: any 'permanent place of abode' + 183 days = stat resident even if domiciled elsewhere. Traders relocating to TX / FL / NV / WA / TN must track meticulously. NY DTF audit rate historically ~12% for stat-resident claims; CA FTB high for > $1M income filers.
view.res.label.has_abode: Have permanent place of abode here?
view.res.dom.physical: Spend > 183 days in new state (track with calendar)
view.res.dom.drivers: Get new state driver's license + register vehicles
view.res.dom.voter: Register to vote + actually vote in new state
view.res.dom.bank: Move primary banking + brokerage to new state
view.res.dom.doctors: Change doctors / dentists / vets to new state
view.res.dom.mail: Update mailing address everywhere + USPS forwarding
view.res.dom.club: Cancel old state social club / gym / country club
view.res.dom.will: Update will / trust to reference new state
view.res.dom.affidavit: File NY IT-201/CA 540 with partial-year + statement of domicile change
view.res.dom.documentation: Keep AIRLINE TICKETS, hotel receipts, calendar exports — burden of proof on you
view.res.audit.items: Items near and dear (heirlooms, pets, valuables)
tile.crat.label: CRAT
tile.crat.desc: Charitable Remainder Annuity Trust — fixed $ + 5% exhaustion test + Notice 2008-90
view.s36b.h1.title: // § 36B ACA PREMIUM TAX CREDIT
view.s36b.hint.intro: Refundable credit to reduce monthly health insurance premiums on Healthcare.gov. Pre-IRA: available only 100-400% FPL (cliff). ARPA / IRA 2022-2025: extended to ALL incomes when premium > 8.5% of MAGI. Reconciled on Form 8962. Critical for self-employed traders — losing one year's APTC due to high gain costs thousands. 2026 sunset: 400% FPL cliff returns.
view.s36b.warning.repay: Advance PTC exceeds earned PTC → you OWE the difference back on Form 8962. Repayment caps apply < 400% FPL. ≥ 400% FPL (pre-IRA) = full repayment.
view.s36b.plan.solo_401k: Solo 401(k) max contribution to drop MAGI below cliff
view.s36b.plan.harvesting: Loss harvest to offset realized gains pre-year-end
view.s36b.plan.roth_timing: Time Roth conversions for low-income years
view.s36b.plan.advance: If you'll exceed advance estimate, ACT NOW to drop MAGI
view.s36b.plan.cap_repayment: Pre-ARPA: repayment cap 200% FPL — limit repayment risk
view.s36b.plan.cliff_2026: 2026 cliff returns: get below 400% FPL or lose entire subsidy
view.simple.h1.title: // SIMPLE IRA CONTRIBUTION CALC
view.simple.hint.intro: For employers with ≤ 100 employees. 2024 employee deferral: $16,000 ($19,500 with 50+ catch-up; $21,250 SECURE 2.0 60-63 super-catch-up). Employer chooses: 3% match OR 2% non-elective for ALL eligible. 25% early-withdrawal penalty (within 2 yrs of first contribution; 10% after). Must establish by Oct 1.
view.simple.warning.too_big: More than 100 employees — SIMPLE IRA disallowed. Consider Safe Harbor 401(k) instead.
view.simple.warning.two_year: Within 2 years of first contribution: 25% early-withdrawal penalty (vs normal 10%) AND distributions cannot be rolled to non-SIMPLE IRA. Wait 2 years before rollover.
view.db.h1.title: // DEFINED BENEFIT / CASH BALANCE
view.db.hint.intro: For high earners (> $300k). 2024 § 415(b) annual benefit cap: $275,000 at NRA. Annual contribution can EXCEED $250k for older participants. STACK with 401(k): $69k + $275k actuarially-determined annual contribution = $300k+ deductible/yr. Cash Balance: hybrid with notional account + interest credit rate. Requires actuary + Form 5500. Owner-employees 45+ benefit most.
view.db.h2.compare: Defined Benefit vs Solo 401(k) by age
view.db.cau.permanence: Must intend permanence (3-5+ yrs minimum, PBGC could investigate)
view.db.cau.employee_costs: Employees: must include eligible non-owner employees at coverage level
view.db.cau.funding: Minimum funding required even in down years
view.db.cau.overfunding: Reversion penalty 50% on overfunded amounts → use Qualified Replacement Plan
view.s213.h1.title: // § 213 MEDICAL EXPENSE DEDUCTION
view.s213.hint.intro: Itemize only. Deduct medical / dental expenses > 7.5% of AGI. Spouse + dependent expenses count. LTC services + LTC premiums (age-limited) qualify. Capital expenditures for medical necessity (wheelchair ramp, lift, custom kitchen) also count. NOT deductible: cosmetic, gym memberships, vitamins, HSA-paid amounts (no double-dip), employer-reimbursed.
view.s213.qual.doctors: Doctors, dentists, surgeons, chiropractors, psychiatrists, optometrists, podiatrists
view.s213.qual.capital: Capital expenditures (wheelchair ramp, stair lift, modified vehicle) — reduce by increase in property value
view.s213.qual.special_school: Special education for learning disabilities (medical necessity)
view.s6038.h1.title: // § 6038 FORM 5471 CFC REPORTING
view.s6038.hint.intro: US person owning ≥ 10% of a CFC (foreign corp where US owners hold > 50%) must file Form 5471 with their return. Penalty: $10,000 per year per CFC for non-filing, escalating $10k/month up to $50k. Continuing failure reduces foreign tax credit. Subpart F + GILTI income flows through annually regardless of cash distribution. 5 categories of filer by ownership / control. Common trader trap: foreign holding companies.
view.s6038.cat.1: US shareholder ≥ 10% in foreign passive investment company (§ 1297 PFIC overlap with 8621)
view.s6038.cat.2: US officer / director of foreign corp + US person owning ≥ 10%
view.s6038.cat.3: US person acquires ≥ 10% stock OR additional 5% during year
view.s6038.cat.5: US shareholder owning ≥ 10% of CFC at year end
view.s6038.label.name: Foreign corp name
view.s6038.th.name: Name
view.s6038.stream.body: If you missed Form 5471 filings: Streamlined Domestic Offshore Procedures may allow late filing with reduced/waived penalties IF non-willful. Required: 3 years amended returns + 6 years FBARs + 5% offshore-account penalty (Domestic version). Foreign Streamlined: no penalty if non-resident. Voluntary Disclosure Program if willful: penalty up to 50% of high balance.
view.s408d3.h1.title: // § 408(d)(3) BOBROW IRA ROLLOVER
view.s408d3.hint.intro: Bobrow v. Comm'r (2014): one indirect rollover per 12 months applies PER TAXPAYER across ALL your IRAs (Traditional + Roth + SEP + SIMPLE). Pre-Bobrow misreading allowed per-account. Second indirect rollover within 12 mo: amount taxable + 10% penalty if < 59½ + excess contribution + 6% excise. Trustee-to-trustee transfers do NOT count — unlimited. Roth conversions don't count.
view.s408d3.warning.violation: Multiple indirect rollovers within 12 months — second + later treated as TAXABLE DISTRIBUTION + possible 10% penalty + 6% excess contribution excise. File Form 5329 + consider self-certification.
view.s408d3.work.direct: Always do direct trustee-to-trustee transfer — unlimited per year
view.s408d3.work.conversions: Roth conversions don't count toward the limit
view.s408d3.work.60_days: If you do indirect: 60 days strict; banks must withhold 20% (less for IRA)
view.s408d3.work.self_certify: Late rollover relief: Rev. Proc. 2016-47 self-certification (11 reasons)
view.s408d3.work.private_letter: PLR for other reasons — $10k+ user fee
view.s408d3.work.spousal_separate: Spouse's IRA rollovers count separately (each spouse has own limit)
view.s162m.h1.title: // § 162(m) $1M EXEC COMP CAP
view.s162m.hint.intro: Public C-corps disallowed deduction for comp > $1M paid to covered employees: CEO, CFO, three highest-paid officers + any covered employee since 2017 (sticky list — once covered, always covered). TCJA 2017 eliminated performance-based exception. ARPA 2021 expanded to top 5 highest-paid (not just executives) starting 2027.
view.s162m.label.name: Name / role
view.s162m.label.title: Title
view.s162m.th.name: Name
view.s162m.th.title: Title
view.s162m.plan.deferred: Defer comp until post-separation year if person no longer in scope
view.s162m.plan.foreign_subs: Foreign sub of public US co: § 162(m) applies if subject to Securities Act reporting
view.s162m.plan.section_280g: Coordinate with § 280G golden parachute disallowance + 20% excise
view.s162m.plan.q1: SEC Form Summary Comp Table is § 162(m) starting point
view.s162m.plan.ipo: IPO transition relief: 3 yr post-IPO has reduced rules
view.s4975.h1.title: // § 4975 PROHIBITED TRANSACTIONS
view.s4975.hint.intro: 15% excise on amount involved (per year) + 100% if uncorrected within taxable period. IRA PT = automatic distribution of ENTIRE IRA on Jan 1 of year of PT — § 408(e)(2). Most common traps in self-directed IRAs investing in real estate, private business, crypto. Disqualified persons: IRA holder, spouse, ancestors, descendants, fiduciaries (50% owned entities).
view.s4975.label.dist_amount: Total IRA balance at PT date ($)
view.s4975.dq.lineal: Ancestors + descendants (parents, kids, grandkids) + their spouses
view.s4975.dq.50_owned: Entity 50%+ owned by combination of above
view.s4975.dq.not_siblings: NOT siblings, NOT in-laws beyond spouse
view.s4975.dq.fiduciary: Plan fiduciary, anyone with discretion over IRA assets
view.s4975.trap.live_in: Buying real estate IRA owns and living in it (use by you)
view.s4975.trap.fix_it: Personally doing repairs on IRA-owned property (services to IRA)
view.s4975.trap.lend_to_co: Lending IRA funds to your closely-held biz
view.s4975.trap.guarantee: Personally guaranteeing IRA loan (extension of credit)
view.s4975.trap.buy_from_self: Buying property already owned personally with IRA funds
view.s4975.trap.commission: Receiving real estate commission on IRA's property purchase
view.s4975.trap.rent_to_kid: Renting IRA-owned property to your child
view.s4975.trap.checkbook_warning: 'Checkbook IRA' / IRA-LLC: Swanson / Niemann ok IF structured right + no comingling
view.s4975.ex.pte_84_14: PTE 84-14 (QPAM) — independent qualified professional asset manager
view.s4975.ex.pte_77_4: PTE 77-4 — mutual fund investment by self-directed IRA
view.s4975.ex.pte_80_26: PTE 80-26 — short-term loans for ordinary operating expenses
view.s4975.ex.section_408: § 408 ROBS (Rollover for Business Startups) C-corp structure — narrow
view.s4980h.h1.title: // § 4980H ACA EMPLOYER MANDATE
view.s4980h.hint.intro: Applicable Large Employer (ALE) = 50+ FTE in prior calendar year. (a) Penalty: $2,970 × ALL FTEs minus 30 if ANY employee gets PTC and you offer to < 95% of FTEs. (b) Penalty: $4,460 per non-covered FTE that gets PTC when offered NOT minimum value / NOT affordable. Affordability: employee share ≤ 8.39% of household income (W-2 safe harbor, FPL safe harbor available). Form 1095-C / 1094-C required for each employee + transmittal.
view.s4980h.sh.w2: W-2 safe harbor: lowest plan ≤ 8.39% of Box 1 W-2 wages
view.s4980h.sh.choose: Choose safest based on workforce composition
view.s4980h.sh.combine: Different safe harbor per 'reasonable category' of employees allowed
view.s4980h.fte.measurement: Use look-back measurement (3-12 mo) + stability (6-12 mo) for variable hour
view.s4980h.fte.controlled: Controlled group / affiliated service group: aggregate FTEs across all entities
view.s4980h.fte.foreign: Foreign workers: excluded (no US source income)
view.tpr.h1.title: // § 263 + TPRs REPAIR VS IMPROVEMENT
view.tpr.hint.intro: Repairs deduct currently (§ 162). Improvements capitalize (§ 263). TPRs apply BAR test: Betterment, Adaptation, Restoration. Plus 3 safe harbors: De Minimis ($2.5k / $5k AFS), Routine Maintenance (10-yr cycle), Small Taxpayer Safe Harbor (≤ $10M gross + 2% basis OR $10k). Annual elections required.
view.tpr.bar.betterment: Betterment: ameliorates material defect from acquisition / addition / increase in capacity / productivity / efficiency / quality / strength
view.tpr.bar.adaptation: Adaptation: changes property to new / different use not consistent with intended use at acquisition
view.tpr.bar.restoration: Restoration: replaces major component / rebuilds to like-new / from disrepair / claimed casualty loss / replaces UoP that has substantially exhausted recovery period
view.tpr.bar.unit: Apply BAR per 'unit of property' — for buildings = 9 building systems (HVAC, plumbing, electrical, elevator, fire, security, gas, plumbing, structural)
view.tpr.elect.de_minimis: De minimis safe harbor: annual policy statement + written accounting policy + invoice-level expensing
view.tpr.elect.routine: Routine maintenance: business expectation that activity will recur within 10 yrs (buildings) or class life (personal)
view.tpr.elect.small_tp: Small taxpayer safe harbor: § 263(a) attach statement; building unadjusted basis ≤ $1M required
view.tpr.elect.partial_disp: Partial Disposition election (§ 1.168(i)-8): replace component, dispose of old basis
view.tpr.elect.materials: Materials & supplies (Reg § 1.162-3): de minimis $200 or 12-month rule
view.s6048.h1.title: // § 6048 FORM 3520 FOREIGN TRUST / GIFT
view.s6048.hint.intro: Three reportable events: (1) transfer to foreign trust; (2) distribution from foreign trust; (3) receipt of > $100,000 gift from foreign individual / estate, OR > $19,570 (2024) from foreign corp / partnership. Penalty: 35% of unreported amount (5%/month, min $10k). Form 3520-A annually by trustee. U.S. owner of foreign grantor trust reports trust income on 1040 even if no distribution.
view.s6048.excl.medical_education: Direct tuition / medical payments by foreign donor
view.s6048.excl.canadian_rrsp: Canadian RRSP / RRIF (Rev. Proc. 2014-55 auto exclusion)
view.s6048.excl.qualified_employee_trust: Qualified employee benefit trust (separate Form 8891 / FBAR)
view.s6048.relief.streamlined_dom: Streamlined Domestic Offshore (5% Title 26 miscellaneous penalty)
view.s6048.relief.delinquent: Delinquent International Information Return Submission Procedures (DIIRSP)
view.s6048.relief.precluded: Quiet disclosure / amended return — NOT penalty relief
view.s6038a.h1.title: // § 6038A FORM 5472 FOREIGN-OWNED US
view.s6038a.hint.intro: Required when (1) US corp with 25%+ foreign ownership, OR (2) foreign-owned single-member LLC (since 2017 disregarded entity rule). Reports related-party transactions in 11 categories. Penalty: $25,000 per failure (was $10k pre-2018) + $25k/month after 90 days. Common trap: foreign-owned US LLC holding US real estate. Pro-forma 1120 + Form 5472 required even for disregarded entity.
view.s6038a.rp.direct_25: Any 25% direct or indirect foreign shareholder
view.s6038a.rp.related_to_25: Any party related to 25% foreign shareholder under § 318 attribution
view.s6038a.rp.controlled: Any other foreign person controlled by 25% shareholder
view.s6038a.rp.cousins: Foreign brother-sister corps under common ownership
view.s6038a.rp.commonality: U.S. + foreign affiliates under common control
view.s6038a.dis.body: Pre-2017: foreign-owned SMLLC = no US tax / filing if no ETB. Treas. Reg. § 301.7701-2 (final 2017) treats disregarded entity as separate corp for § 6038A reporting. Now required to file pro-forma 1120 + Form 5472. Doing nothing = $25k+ penalties per year.
view.s6038a.warning.dis: Foreign-owned disregarded LLC (since 2017): MUST file pro-forma 1120 with Form 5472 attached. Common trap with foreign-owned single-member LLCs holding US real estate. Penalties stack year-over-year. No estate-tax / income filing history? Still required. File via 'Form 5472 only' return paperwork.
view.s7702a.h1.title: // § 7702A MEC 7-PAY TEST
view.s7702a.hint.intro: Life insurance contract failing the 7-pay test becomes a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC). MEC distributions / loans treated as TAXABLE INCOME first (LIFO) + 10% penalty if < 59½. Cum premiums > sum of 7 net level premiums in first 7 yrs → MEC. Material change (raise death benefit, certain option swaps) restarts 7-year window. Death benefit + cash-value-loan-payoff is tax-free for non-MEC contracts.
view.s7702a.label.basis: Basis in contract (cum premiums - prior dist) ($)
view.s7702a.ben.loans: Policy loans NOT taxable income (treated as borrowed)
view.s7702a.ben.fifo: Cash withdrawals: FIFO (basis first), then gains
view.s7702a.pen.lifo: Distributions / loans treated as INCOME FIRST (LIFO)
view.s7702a.pen.ord_income: Income portion taxed as ORDINARY (not LTCG)
view.s7702a.pen.10pct: 10% penalty under 59½ unless death / disability / SEPP
view.s7702a.pen.collateral: Pledge as loan collateral = deemed distribution
view.s7702a.use.high_premium: Maximize tax-deferred growth, pay all premiums upfront
view.s7702a.use.heirs: Plan to leave death benefit to heirs untaxed
view.s7702a.use.elderly: Elderly individuals using MEC as legacy vehicle
view.crypto_inc.h1.title: // CRYPTO STAKING + AIRDROP INCOME
view.crypto_inc.hint.intro: Rev. Rul. 2023-14: staking rewards = ORDINARY income at FMV when 'dominion and control' obtained (typically when received in wallet). Basis = FMV at receipt. Future sale: cap gain over basis. Rev. Rul. 2019-24: airdrops + hard forks = ORDINARY income at receipt. Jarrett v. United States (M.D. Tenn 2023): taxpayer argued staking ≠ income (new property created) — government settled, precedent unclear.
view.crypto_inc.empty: No events logged for this year.
view.crypto_inc.basis.body: Every staking / airdrop / mining receipt creates BASIS = FMV at receipt + ordinary income recognized. When you LATER sell those units, gain = sale proceeds − basis. Tax-software trap: CoinTracker / Koinly / Crypto.com Tax often miscalculate basis when staking auto-restakes. FIFO default; HIFO possible if specific-identification rules followed (Rev. Proc. 2019-19).
view.crypto_inc.wash.body: Current: § 1091 wash-sale rule applies only to 'stock or securities'. Crypto = property under Notice 2014-21, NOT a security. → Sell at loss + immediately rebuy with no disallowance. Multiple Build Back Better / Biden FY25 budget proposals to extend wash-sale to crypto — DID NOT PASS. Loophole alive for now.
view.s1042.h1.title: // § 1042 ESOP GAIN DEFERRAL
view.s1042.hint.intro: Sell qualified stock of closely-held domestic C-corp to ESOP that owns ≥ 30% post-sale. Reinvest proceeds in qualified replacement property (QRP) within 12 months (or 3 mo prior). Cap gain DEFERRED until QRP sold. Basis carries over. Estate planning hack: die holding QRP → step-up at death wipes out deferred gain (§ 1014). Massive succession + tax tool for retiring founders.
view.s1042.req.c_corp: Selling shareholder of domestic C-corp (not S-corp; S → § 1042 unavailable but S-ESOP has tax-free advantages)
view.s1042.req.esop_30pct: ESOP owns ≥ 30% of total outstanding stock immediately AFTER sale
view.s1042.req.qrp_window: Acquire QRP within 12 months after sale (or 3 mo before, with Notice of Election)
view.s1042.req.notice: File Notice of Election (§ 1042(c)(3)(B)) with return
view.s1042.req.esop_statement: ESOP files statement consenting to § 4978 excise on early disposition
view.s1042.req.no_family: Seller / family + 25% shareholders can't receive ESOP allocations for 10 yrs
view.s1042.qrp.qualified: Stock OR bonds of US OPERATING companies
view.s1042.qrp.50_pct_rule: Issuer must derive < 25% passive income and have ≥ 50% assets active
view.s1042.qrp.not_qualified: NOT: govt bonds, mutual funds, REITs, MLPs, S-corps, foreign corps
view.s1042.qrp.floating_rate: Common QRP: 'floating-rate notes' of operating cos (low risk + § 1042-qualified)
view.s1042.qrp.margin_loan: Can use margin loan against QRP to monetize without triggering gain
view.s1042.qrp.diversify: Limited diversification options — managed-account QRP common
view.s1259.h1.title: // § 1259 CONSTRUCTIVE SALE
view.s1259.hint.intro: Anti-short-against-the-box rule. If you hold an appreciated long position and enter into a hedging transaction that substantially eliminates risk + gain opportunity — treated as SOLD at FMV on that date. Cap gain recognized immediately. Defeats deferral strategies. Common triggers: short-against-the-box, equity swap pair, forward sale at fixed price, collar with overlap.
view.s1259.safe.close_by_jan30: Position closed within 30 days after year-end
view.s1259.safe.long_held_60: Long position held 60+ days after close of identifying transaction without diminished risk
view.s1259.safe.both_required: BOTH conditions required for safe-harbor protection
view.s1259.safe.atm_collar: ATM collars + protective puts generally don't trigger (gain opportunity remains)
view.s1259.safe.delta_hedge: Variable forward / VPF: usually safe IF retain delta > 0.20
view.s1259.work.vpf: Variable Prepaid Forward (VPF): give up upside in narrow band, keep some risk
view.s1259.work.collar: Wide-strike collar maintains real risk + gain opportunity
view.s1259.work.protective_put: Pure protective put: full upside retained, downside hedged
view.s1259.work.exchange_fund: Exchange fund: 7-year hold for diversification without constructive sale
view.s1259.work.margin_loan: Margin loan against unhedged position (no constructive sale)
view.pfic.h1.title: // § 1296 PFIC MTM ELECTION (FORM 8621)
view.pfic.hint.intro: PFIC = foreign corp where ≥ 75% income passive OR ≥ 50% assets generate passive. Default § 1291 regime: excess distributions (> 125% prior 3-yr avg) ordinary income + interest charge (compounding back to year 1). § 1296 MTM: annual ordinary income on gain (loss limited). § 1295 QEF: pass-through annual income, preserves cap gain character. Form 8621 annually. Common trap: foreign mutual funds, ETFs (e.g. European UCITS).
view.pfic.de_minimis.body: If aggregate PFIC value < $25,000 ($50k MFJ) at year-end AND no excess distributions: annual Form 8621 NOT required. However, MUST track + report sale gain. Below-threshold PFIC stocks still subject to default § 1291 regime on disposition.
view.pfic.trap.foreign_mutual: European UCITS, foreign mutual funds + ETFs (Vanguard / iShares foreign-domiciled)
view.pfic.trap.foreign_pension: Foreign pension plans (UK SIPP, Israeli Keren Pension)
view.pfic.trap.foreign_corp: Foreign holding cos / private investment cos
view.pfic.trap.startup: Foreign tech startup pre-revenue (no active business) → PFIC
view.pfic.trap.adr_safe: ADRs of foreign companies usually SAFE (active operating business)
view.s1245.plan.1031: § 1031 like-kind exchange DEFERS recapture (real property only post-TCJA)
view.s1245.plan.installment: § 453 installment sale: § 1245 recapture all RECOGNIZED in year of sale (no deferral)
view.s1245.plan.charitable: Gift of depreciated property: reduce basis by acc. depr; recipient inherits
view.s1245.plan.death: Step-up at death (§ 1014): recapture WIPED OUT — heir gets fresh basis
view.s1245.plan.cost_seg: Cost segregation: more depreciation now, more § 1245 recapture later — net positive
view.s351.note.disregarded: Single-member LLC = disregarded entity — no taxable event, owner directly holds property for tax purposes.
view.s351.boot.cash: Cash + non-stock property received = BOOT, gain recognized to extent of boot
view.s351.boot.liabilities: Liabilities assumed = BOOT for § 357(c) gain if liabilities > basis (corp only)
view.s351.boot.partnership_liability: Partnership liabilities: increase / decrease partner's basis under § 752
view.s351.boot.basis: Transferor basis in entity = property basis + gain - boot - liabilities
view.s351.boot.holding: Holding period of stock / interest TACKS
view.s172.h1.title: // § 172 NET OPERATING LOSS CARRYFORWARD
view.s172.hint.intro: Pre-2018 NOLs: 2-yr carryback + 20-yr forward, 100% offset. Post-2017 NOLs: NO carryback (CARES 5-yr was 2018-2020 special), INDEFINITE forward, limited to 80% of taxable income. § 461(l) Excess Business Loss limit layered on top — $305k single / $610k MFJ (2024). Disallowed amount becomes NOL.
view.s172.cares.tcja: TCJA 2017: post-2017 NOLs no carryback, indefinite forward, 80% cap
view.s172.cares.2018_2020: CARES Act 2020: TY 2018-2020 NOLs → 5-yr carryback + temporary 100% offset
view.s172.cares.post_2020: 2021+: Back to TCJA rules (no carryback, 80% limit)
view.s172.cares.farm_5yr: Farming NOLs: still 2-yr carryback option preserved
view.s172.cares.382: § 382: ownership change ≥ 50% in 3-yr period limits NOL usage
view.s172.ebl.body: Effective 2018 (originally TCJA, was suspended 2018-2020 then reactivated). Non-corp taxpayers: aggregate business loss exceeding $305k single / $610k MFJ (2024) is DISALLOWED. Excess becomes NOL carryforward. Inflation Reduction Act 2022 extended § 461(l) through 2028.
view.s172.plan.roth_recharacterize: Use NOL year to do large Roth conversion (absorbed at 0%)
view.s172.plan.harvest: Realize gains in NOL year to use up loss carryforward
view.s172.plan.s_corp_basis: S-corp basis must be tracked separately — limits flow-through loss usage
view.s172.plan.at_risk: § 465 at-risk + § 469 passive activity layered on top of EBL + NOL
view.s172.plan.election: Pre-2018 carryback waiver election irrevocable — choose wisely
view.s172.plan.amt_nol: AMT NOL has separate tracking — not unified with regular NOL
view.s1035.h1.title: // § 1035 INSURANCE / ANNUITY EXCHANGE
view.s1035.hint.intro: Tax-free replacement of insurance / annuity / endowment / LTC contracts. One-way street: annuity → life insurance NOT allowed; life → annuity OK. Basis carries over. Loss NEVER recognized. Pension Protection Act 2006: partial exchanges allowed but must follow Rev. Proc. 2011-38: 180-day rule + no distributions for 180 days from either contract.
view.s1035.warning.disallowed: Exchange NOT allowed under § 1035 — transaction treated as TAXABLE SURRENDER of old contract + purchase of new. Entire gain recognized.
view.s1035.boot.cash: Cash received = boot, gain to extent of gain
view.s1035.boot.loan: Loan extinguished by new carrier = boot (Estate of Halby; Greene)
view.s1035.boot.partial: Partial exchange + distribution within 180 days = taxable
view.s1035.boot.mec_carry: MEC contract: new contract IS ALSO MEC (no laundering)
view.s1035.boot.owner_assignee: Owner / assignee / insured must match (per Rev. Proc. 92-44)
view.s1035.use.cheaper: Move VA to lower-cost VA (M&E + admin saves $30-100k over 20 yrs)
view.s1035.use.no_lockout: Move past surrender-charge period to fresh investment options
view.s1035.use.guaranteed: Move to better income / death benefit / GMxB rider
view.s1035.use.term_to_whole: Convert term life to whole life
view.s1035.use.ltc: Move outdated LTC to modern hybrid LTC-life
view.s1035.use.consolidate: Consolidate multiple policies for simpler estate admin
view.s6707a.h1.title: // § 6707A REPORTABLE TRANSACTION
view.s6707a.hint.intro: 5 categories: Listed (most aggressive) / Confidential / Loss / Transaction-of-Interest / Contractual Protection. Penalty: 75% of decrease in tax claimed (min $10k / $50k entity, max $100k / $200k entity). Reportable on Form 8886. Strict liability — no reasonable cause defense.
view.s6707a.label.name: Transaction name
view.s6707a.th.name: Name
view.s6707a.material.body: Material Advisors who promote / draft / sell Reportable Transactions face their own penalties: § 6707: $50k flat (or $200k for Listed) for failing to file Form 8918. § 6708: $10k per day (max $50k) for not maintaining investor / advisee list. Form 13976 required to disclose advisor identity to investors.
view.s6707a.stack.7203: Criminal § 7203 willful failure to file (1-yr misdemeanor)
view.s280g.h1.title: // § 280G GOLDEN PARACHUTE EXCISE
view.s280g.hint.intro: Change-of-control comp ≥ 3× base amount (5-yr avg Box 1 W-2): entire amount over 1× base is 'excess parachute payment'. 20% excise on recipient + DISALLOWED deduction to corp on excess. Applies to 'disqualified individuals' — officers, > 1% shareholders, highly compensated (top 1%). Private cos: § 280G(b)(5) cleansing by 75%+ shareholder vote.
view.s280g.clean.smb: Must qualify as Small Business Corp (no public stock)
view.s280g.clean.adequate: Adequate disclosure of all material facts to shareholders
view.s280g.clean.75pct: Vote ≥ 75% of OUTSTANDING shares (not just present)
view.s280g.clean.exclusions: Disqualified persons + their relatives + control entities EXCLUDED from vote
view.s280g.clean.timing: Vote BEFORE payment + binding right to refuse
view.s280g.clean.tax_savings: If cleansed: no 20% excise + corp gets full deduction
view.s280g.work.gross_up: Cap on parachute at 2.99× base (avoid 3× threshold)
view.s280g.work.best_after_tax: 'Best-after-tax' cutback clause: cap at lower of 2.99× or threshold that maximizes net
view.s280g.work.reasonable: Allocate to reasonable post-acquisition comp / non-compete (not parachute)
view.s280g.work.vested: Pre-existing vested benefits = not parachute (must predate change of control)
view.s280g.work.cleansing_vote: Private co: shareholder cleansing vote (75% threshold)
view.s280g.work.gross_up_pay: Gross-up payment: corp pays excise tax — but gross-up itself is parachute
view.s280g.work.severance_unwind: Restructure severance to spread over post-CoC employment
view.s1374.h1.title: // § 1374 S-CORP BIG TAX
view.s1374.hint.intro: Former C-corp converting to S-corp: corporate-level 21% tax on gain accrued PRE-conversion when realized within 5-year recognition period. Limited to NUBIG (Net Unrealized Built-In Gain at conversion). BIG income flows to shareholders AFTER corp tax (double tax during 5-yr window). Reduces NUBIG dollar-for-dollar. Goal: hold appreciated assets ≥ 5 yrs post-conversion.
view.s1374.label.nubig_used: NUBIG already used in prior years ($)
view.s1374.card.subject: Subject to BIG
view.s1374.plan.5_yr: Hold appreciated assets ≥ 5 yrs post-conversion → BIG period expires
view.s1374.plan.appraisal: Pre-conversion appraisal: low FMV reduces NUBIG
view.s1374.plan.allocation: Allocate gain to assets WITHOUT built-in gain
view.s1374.plan.installment: § 453 installment sale spreads BIG over years (still within window though)
view.s1374.plan.like_kind: § 1031 exchange defers BIG (real estate post-TCJA only)
view.s1374.plan.charitable: Charitable contribution of appreciated property eliminates BIG
view.s1374.plan.short_recog: CARES Act / Bush Tax Relief: 5-yr recognition period (down from 10-yr)
view.s1374.plan.ti_limit: BIG limited to corp-level taxable income (recognition deferred if no income)
view.s1374.gotcha.passive: § 1375 passive investment income (PII) > 25% for 3 consecutive yrs → S-status revoked + 35% tax
view.s1374.gotcha.lifo: § 1363(d) LIFO recapture on C → S conversion (one-time pay over 4 yrs)
view.s1374.gotcha.aet: § 532 Accumulated Earnings Tax 20% (C-corp only) on undistributed earnings
view.s1374.gotcha.phc: § 541 Personal Holding Company 20% tax on undistributed personal holding income
view.s1374.gotcha.dpad: QBI § 199A may benefit from S vs C — analyze QBI limitations
view.s871m.h1.title: // § 871(m) DIVIDEND EQUIVALENT WITHHOLDING
view.s871m.hint.intro: Foreign person receiving 'dividend-equivalent payment' on US-equity-linked instrument (TRS, swap, options, securities lending) subject to 30% withholding (or treaty rate). Delta-1 instruments always covered. Non-delta-1 with delta > 0.80 = 'specified equity-linked instruments'. QDD regime for qualified dealers. Notice 2024-44 + Phase-in regs ongoing. Section 305(c) deemed distributions on convertibles also apply.
view.s871m.delta.delta_one: Delta-1 = 1:1 equity exposure (TRS, single-stock future, forwards)
view.s871m.delta.non_delta_one: Non-delta-1 with delta > 0.80 at issuance = covered
view.s871m.delta.combined: Combined instruments with related counterparties: aggregated for delta test
view.s871m.delta.deemed_div: Deemed dividend payment = notional × dividend yield × time
view.s871m.delta.ad_listed: ADR / listed company: equity-linked tests apply
view.s871m.qdd.body: Qualified Derivatives Dealer (QDD) — typically broker-dealer or affiliate — assumes primary withholding responsibility on derivatives positions. Eliminates cascading withholding through chain of intermediaries. Requires QI agreement + W-8IMY + annual compliance. Notice 2024-44 extended phase-in transition rules.
view.s1402.h1.title: // § 1402 SELF-EMPLOYMENT TAX
view.s1402.hint.intro: 15.3% on net SE × 0.9235: 12.4% SS + 2.9% Medicare. SS wage base 2024: $168,600; Medicare uncapped. Additional 0.9% Medicare over $200k single / $250k MFJ. Half-SE-tax deduction § 164(f) above-the-line. W-2 wages from same employer REDUCE SS-base SE tax dollar-for-dollar. Form SE attached to 1040.
view.s1402.h2.exclusions: Income NOT subject to SE tax
view.s1402.excl.investment: Investment income: dividends, interest, capital gains (incl. trader gains)
view.s1402.excl.s_corp_dist: S-corp distributions to shareholder-employees (above reasonable comp)
view.s1402.excl.tts_trader: § 475(f) trader: TTS trading income NOT SE (cap gains, not personal services)
view.s1402.opt.s_corp: S-corp election + reasonable comp: distributions escape SE tax
view.s1402.opt.qbi: QBI deduction § 199A still applies to SE income
view.s1402.opt.retirement: Solo 401(k) / SEP / SIMPLE max with QBI-friendly structure
view.s1402.opt.spouse_split: Spouse partnership splitting: each gets own SS-base wage cap
view.s1276.h1.title: // § 1276 MARKET DISCOUNT
view.s1276.hint.intro: Buy bond below face value: accrued discount = ORDINARY income, not cap gain. De minimis safe harbor: < 0.25% × years to maturity. Accrual method: ratable OR constant-yield. § 1278(b) election: include currently → reduce gain at sale. Tax-exempt muni bonds: market discount also ordinary income (federal), but not always state. Reported on 1099-OID or 1099-INT.
view.s1276.rel.oid: Original Issue Discount (§ 1271-1275): bonds issued below par; OID accrues as interest
view.s1276.rel.amortizable: Bond Premium § 171: paid > face = elect to amortize (offset interest)
view.s1276.rel.acquisition: § 1278(a)(2)(B) Acquisition Premium: amortize against OID accrual
view.s1276.rel.short_term: Short-term (≤ 1 yr maturity): § 1281 mandatory current accrual
view.s1276.rel.contingent: Contingent payment debt: Reg § 1.1275-4 OID-like accrual
view.s1233.h1.title: // § 1233 SHORT SALE HOLDING PERIOD
view.s1233.hint.intro: Short sale gain: holding period determined by stock HELD at time of short, not closing. Loss on short: long-term if > 1 yr; otherwise short-term. 'Short-against-the-box' or 'covered short': holding identical stock long before short → § 1233(b) suspends OR resets holding period of long. § 1259 constructive sale takes precedence for substantially-eliminated risk.
view.s1233.label.long_held_at_short: Held same stock long at short date?
view.s1233.label.long_more_year: Long stock held > 1 yr at short?
view.s1233.rule.gain_simple: Pure short with no long held: gain ALWAYS short-term (you owned the borrowed share < 1 yr)
view.s1233.rule.loss_holdings: Loss on close: looks at how long you owned the SOLD stock (the close buy)
view.s1233.rule.long_held_under_yr: Long held < 1 yr + short = long holding period RESTARTS at short close
view.s1233.rule.long_held_over_yr: Long held > 1 yr + short = LT character protected for long; short still ST
view.s1233.rule.box_disposes: Closing the short 'against the box' = sells the LONG (taxable disposition)
view.s1233.rule.identical: 'Substantially identical' = same stock, same class, similar economic exposure
view.s6166.h1.title: // § 6166 ESTATE TAX INSTALLMENT
view.s6166.hint.intro: Up to 14 years to pay federal estate tax on closely-held business interest. Years 1-4: interest-only at favorable 2% rate (on first $1.85M tax, 2024). Years 5-14: P+I. Closely-held = > 35% of adjusted gross estate must be active trade or business. Acceleration if 50% of business sold / undistributed income / late payment. Lien required; Form 706 election.
view.s6166.qual.aggregate: Aggregate test: combine multiple businesses (each ≥ 20% required)
view.s6166.qual.farm_business: Farms, ranches, family agricultural operations qualify
view.s6166.qual.holding_companies: Holding companies: must look through to operating sub
view.s6166.acc.50_pct: ≥ 50% of qualifying business disposed of or withdrawn
view.s6166.acc.distribution: Undistributed net income retained (any amount triggers under § 6166(g)(2))
view.s6166.acc.late: Late payment of installment after 6-month grace
view.s6166.acc.distribution_corp: Corporate redemptions to pay estate tax (§ 303 carve-out limited)
view.s4941.h1.title: // § 4941 PF SELF-DEALING
view.s4941.hint.intro: Private foundation can't transact with 'disqualified persons' (substantial contributor, manager, family, 35% owned entity). STRICT LIABILITY — no intent required. 10% on disqualified person + 5% on knowing manager INITIAL. 200% on DP + 50% on manager if not corrected within taxable period. Even 'fair price' transactions PROHIBITED. Form 4720 + Form 990-PF.
view.s4941.dq.substantial: Substantial contributor (≥ $5k AND > 2% of total contributions historically)
view.s4941.dq.family: Family member (spouse, ancestor, descendant + their spouses)
view.s4941.dq.35_owned: Entity 35%+ owned by combination of disqualified persons
view.s4941.ex.no_charge: Free use of property by foundation
view.s4941.ex.reasonable_comp: Reasonable comp for personal services NECESSARY to foundation
view.s4941.ex.incidental: Incidental benefit from charitable activities (e.g., banquet seat)
view.s4941.ex.bank_general: Bank deposit accounts at general commercial terms
view.s4941.ex.no_interest_loan: Interest-free / no-interest loans TO foundation (from DP)
view.s4941.rel.4945: § 4945: 20% on taxable expenditures (lobbying, political, individual grants)
view.s1092.h1.title: // § 1092 STRADDLE / MIXED-STRADDLE
view.s1092.hint.intro: Straddle = offsetting positions in actively traded property. Loss on one leg DEFERRED to extent of unrealized gain on offsetting leg. Holding period TOLLED during straddle. § 263(g): interest + carrying charges CAPITALIZED. § 1092(b)(2) Mixed-Straddle Election: identify positions to allow § 1256 60/40 treatment + non-§ 1256 leg. § 1233(d) Killer Straddle: short-against-the-box trap.
view.s1092.id.delta: Offsetting positions with substantial reduction in risk of loss
view.s1092.id.actively_traded: Actively traded personal property (stocks, options, futures, currencies)
view.s1092.id.presumption: Presumption: same-asset hedges (e.g., long stock + protective put)
view.s1092.id.qualified_covered: 'Qualified covered calls' (§ 1092(c)(4)) EXEMPT — OTM call against long stock
view.s1092.id.hedging: § 1221(a)(7) hedging transactions: ordinary character, exempt from § 1092
view.s1092.id.identified_straddle: 'Identified straddle' (§ 1092(a)(2)): elect within 30 days for special treatment
view.s1092.mixed.body: When one position is § 1256 (futures, broad-based index options) and another is not, taxpayer may elect to: (1) treat § 1256 position as non-§ 1256 (no 60/40 + no MTM), OR (2) net the positions identifying each pre-straddle. Annual election; identification within first day of straddle creation. Useful for tax-rate arbitrage between 60/40 (LT 23.8% blended max) vs full ordinary treatment.
view.s1092.rel.246: § 246(c) holding period requirement for QDI ÷ DRD
view.s6038b.h1.title: // § 6038B FORM 926 FOREIGN TRANSFER
view.s6038b.hint.intro: US person transferring > $100k cash OR any property to foreign corp must file Form 926. Penalty: 10% of FMV (max $100k per failure; uncapped if intentional). § 367(a): US-built appreciation on transferred property triggers gain (foreign corp can't be tax shelter). § 367(d): intangibles get special 'deemed royalty' treatment. § 367(b): outbound reorganizations / spin-offs.
view.s6038b.form.5472: Form 5472: foreign-owned US corp / disregarded entity (§ 6038A)
view.s2056.h1.title: // § 2056 MARITAL DEDUCTION + QTIP
view.s2056.hint.intro: Unlimited marital deduction for property passing to US-citizen surviving spouse. Non-citizen spouse: $185,000 (2024) annual exclusion + QDOT trust required for marital deduction. QTIP § 2056(b)(7): qualifying income-interest for life. First spouse uses exemption; second spouse gets income, NOT principal; remainder goes per first spouse's wishes. Common in blended families.
view.s2056.warning.non_citizen: Non-citizen spouse: marital deduction DENIED on outright transfers. Use QDOT trust. $185k (2024) annual exclusion for inter-spouse gifts during life; death-time transfers ONLY through QDOT.
view.s2056.qtip.income_interest: Surviving spouse must receive ALL income annually for life
view.s2056.qtip.no_others: No other person may receive income or principal during spouse's life
view.s2056.qtip.election: Executor must affirmatively elect QTIP on Form 706 within 9 mo + 6 mo extension
view.s2056.qtip.income_required: Income must be distributed at LEAST annually
view.s2056.qtip.spouse_estate: QTIP property INCLUDED in surviving spouse's estate at death (§ 2044)
view.s2056.qtip.no_gst: QTIP DOES NOT use GST exemption — separate election needed
view.s2056.qtip.reverse: Reverse QTIP election for GST: partial elect on portion for QTIP, reverse on rest
view.s2056.qtip.partial: Partial election allowed (only portion of trust qualifies)
view.s2056.qdot.us_trustee: At least one US-citizen / US-corp trustee required
view.s2056.qdot.security: Trust assets > $2M: security bond or escrow OR > 35% in US real property
view.s2056.qdot.distribution_tax: Distributions of principal trigger DEFERRED estate tax at first decedent's rate
view.s2056.qdot.naturalization: Surviving spouse naturalizes → QDOT requirement ends
view.s2056.qdot.hardship: Income distributions + § 2056A hardship distributions exempt from estate tax
view.s2056.qdot.formula: Marital deduction reduces estate to LOWER OF actual transfer or QDOT amount
view.s4940.h1.title: // § 4940 PF NET INVESTMENT INCOME
view.s4940.hint.intro: 1.39% flat tax on PF net investment income (interest, dividends, royalties, rents, net capital gains). Pre-2020 was 2% with reduced 1% rate available if distribution test met — SECURE Act eliminated 2-tier, made flat 1.39%. Reported on Form 990-PF Part XI. Net long-term capital losses do NOT offset other income (PF specific). Quarterly estimated tax payments required if > $500.
view.s4940.inc.cap_gain: Net long-term capital gains (cap losses do NOT offset NII)
view.s4940.inc.short_term: Short-term capital gains (treated as ordinary income)
view.s4940.ded.tax: State + local property taxes on investment real estate
view.s4940.ded.necessary: Other ordinary + necessary expenses related to income production
view.s4940.ded.no_charitable: NOT charitable program expenses (those go in Part I-A)
view.s4940.est.body: If § 4940 tax expected to exceed $500, quarterly estimated payments required by 5/15, 6/15, 9/15, 12/15. Form 990-W for computation. Safe harbor: 100% prior year OR 90% current year. Underpayment penalty under § 6655.
view.s7345.h1.title: // § 7345 PASSPORT REVOCATION
view.s7345.hint.intro: 'Seriously delinquent tax debt' = > $62,000 (2024, indexed) assessed federal tax + lien filed OR levy issued. IRS certifies to State Dept; passport revoked / denied. CP508C notice issued; right to reverse via payment / installment agreement / CDP hearing. Issued passports may be LIMITED to direct US return. Foreign / US-Mexican border crossings denied.
view.s7345.warning.act_now: ACT NOW: enter installment agreement / OIC / CDP hearing BEFORE IRS certifies to State Dept. Once certified (CP508C), passport revocation can be immediate on next application / renewal. 30-day reversal after qualifying action.
view.s7345.excl.identity: Identified by IRS as identity theft victim
view.s7345.rem.pay_full: Pay in full — IRS reverses within 30 days
view.s7345.rem.cdp_request: Request CDP hearing (Form 12153 within 30 days of notice)
view.s7345.rem.expedite: Imminent international travel: contact Taxpayer Advocate for expedited reversal
view.s7345.rem.court: § 7345(e) suit in district court / Tax Court (limited remedies)
view.s7345.rem.emergency: Emergency passport for direct US return: contact embassy/consulate
view.s1212.h1.title: // § 1212 CAP LOSS CARRYFORWARD
view.s1212.hint.intro: Individuals: $3,000/yr ordinary-income offset ($1,500 MFS); excess CARRIES FORWARD INDEFINITELY. Character (ST/LT) preserved. C-corps: 3-yr CARRYBACK + 5-yr CARRYFORWARD, ALL ST character. Death of taxpayer: unused carryforward LOST (doesn't pass to estate). § 475(f) trader MTM: ordinary loss instead — no $3k cap, no 5-yr limit (corp). Bankruptcy estate: uses Code § 1398 separate filing.
view.s1212.net.same_class: Net ST gains × ST losses; net LT gains × LT losses
view.s1212.net.cross_class: Net ST excess loss against LT gain (or vice versa)
view.s1212.net.ordinary_offset: Excess net loss offsets ordinary income up to $3k ($1,500 MFS)
view.s1212.net.character_preserved: Character preserved going forward (ST remains ST)
view.s1212.net.absorb_st_first: $3k absorbs ST loss FIRST (ST taxed at ordinary rate anyway)
view.s1212.spec.death: Death of taxpayer: carryforward LOST entirely (not estate, not heirs)
view.s1212.spec.divorce: Divorce: each spouse gets own share of joint carryforward (Rev. Rul. 71-382)
view.s1212.spec.amt: AMT capital loss carryforward separately tracked
view.s1212.spec.section_382: § 382 ownership change limits corp capital loss carryforward
view.s1212.spec.bankruptcy: Bankruptcy estate: § 1398 + estate uses individual's CF separately
view.s1212.spec.section_1296: PFIC § 1296 MTM losses limited to prior MTM gains; not regular cap loss
view.s1212.note.large_carry: Large carryforward: consider § 475(f) MTM election if TTS-qualified to convert to ordinary loss without $3k cap. Roth conversions in low-income years consume carryforward at favorable rates.
view.s4980d.h1.title: // § 4980D HRA / GROUP HEALTH EXCISE
view.s4980d.hint.intro: $100/day per affected individual = up to $36,500/employee/year for ACA group market reform violations. Common: pre-2020 HRA paying individual-market premiums (Notice 2013-54), fee-for-service HRAs, ICHRAs without proper notices / classes. QSEHRA (small employer) safe under Cures Act. Annual report on Form 8928 if violation.
view.s4980d.allowed.qsehra: QSEHRA: small employer (< 50 FTE), $5,850/$11,800 (2024) caps, reimburses individual market
view.s4980d.allowed.ichra: ICHRA: any employer, structured by class (full-time, part-time, etc.), requires individual mkt coverage
view.s4980d.allowed.excepted: Excepted Benefit HRA: $2,100/yr cap, supplemental to group coverage
view.s4980d.allowed.integrated: Integrated HRA: pairs with employer group plan
view.s4980d.allowed.retiree: Retiree-only HRA: exempt from § 4980D when only covering ex-employees
view.s4980d.allowed.fsa: Health FSA: limited annual carryover + 2.5-month grace period
view.s4980d.relief.500k_cap: Annual cap: $500k or 10% of group plan aggregate health expenses
view.s4980d.relief.unintentional: Unintentional failure + first 30 days: no penalty
view.s6663.h1.title: // § 6663 CIVIL FRAUD 75%
view.s6663.hint.intro: 75% of underpayment portion due to fraud. IRS proves by clear + convincing evidence. Once any fraud established, ENTIRE underpayment presumed fraudulent (rebuttable by taxpayer's evidence). No SOL limit (§ 6501(c)(1)). 0.5%/month interest. Civil vs criminal: § 6663 civil; § 7201 criminal (5-yr felony) — government usually wins civil first then indicts.
view.s6663.label.correctly: Correct tax that should have been reported ($)
view.s6663.badge.evasion: Repeated underpayment + filing failures over multiple years
view.s6663.badge.consciousness_guilt: 'Consciousness of guilt' behaviors (rescheduling, evading IRS contacts)
view.s6663.rel.6694: § 6694 Return preparer: $5k or 75% of preparer's fee for unreasonable position
view.s6663.rel.6651: § 6651 Failure to file: 5%/month up to 25% (or 15%/month if fraudulent)
view.s6694.h1.title: // § 6694 PREPARER PENALTY
view.s6694.hint.intro: (a) Unreasonable position: lesser of $1,000 OR 50% of preparer's fee. (b) Willful or reckless: lesser of $5,000 OR 75% of preparer's fee. Reasonable-basis standard: ~33% chance of being sustained. Tax-shelter / reportable transaction: more-likely-than-not standard (>50%). Disclosure (Form 8275 / Form 8275-R) on aggressive position avoids (a). Required Practitioner Registration (PTIN) annually.
view.s6694.rel.6695a: § 6695(a) Failure to furnish copy of return: $60/failure (max $30k)
view.s6694.rel.6695g: § 6695(g) Failure to comply with EITC due diligence: $635/failure (2024)
view.s6694.rel.6700: § 6700 Abusive tax shelter promoter: 50% of gross income from activity
view.s6694.rel.7216: § 7216 Unauthorized disclosure of taxpayer info: $1k + 1-yr prison
view.s6045b.h1.title: // § 6045B FORM 8937 COST BASIS
view.s6045b.hint.intro: Issuers must file Form 8937 within 45 days of any 'specified action affecting basis' — spinoffs, mergers, splits, mandatory exchanges. Public on issuer website. Brokers (§ 6045) report adjusted basis on 1099-B (covered stocks post-2011, mutual funds post-2012). Penalty: $310 per failure (2024); $630 if intentional. Annual cap: $3.78M. § 6045B-1(d) safe harbor: posting on website with 30-day notice to holders.
view.s6045b.label.notice: Notice provided to holders within 15 days?
view.s6045b.cov.mutual_fund: Mutual fund + DRIP shares acquired after 1/1/2012
view.s6045b.cov.dividends: Debt securities acquired after 1/1/2014 + complex debt after 1/1/2016
view.s6045b.cov.crypto_future: Crypto / digital assets: post-2025 reporting effective per IRA + IRS rules
view.s6045b.act.stock_split: Stock splits, reverse splits, stock dividends > 5%
view.s6045b.act.cash_in_lieu: Cash in lieu of fractional shares
view.s6045b.broker.cov_uncov: 'Covered' vs 'non-covered' securities — only covered have basis reported
view.s6045b.broker.transfer: Transferring broker must send Form 1099-B + transfer statement within 15 days
view.s6045b.broker.elect_default: Default cost method: FIFO; can elect HIFO / specific ID before sale
view.s6045b.broker.wash_sale: Brokers must report wash-sale adjustments to basis
view.s6045b.broker.short_sale: Short sale basis: reporting begins year position closes
view.s6045b.broker.constructive: § 1259 constructive sale: separate broker reporting category
view.s529.h1.title: // § 529 QUALIFIED TUITION PLAN
view.s529.hint.intro: Tax-free growth + tax-free withdrawals for qualified education expenses. 5-year super-funding: $90k single / $180k MFJ at once. State deductions vary — check residence-only vs any-plan. $10k/yr K-12 tuition qualified (since 2018). Post-2024 Roth IRA rollover up to $35k lifetime for accounts ≥ 15 yrs old.
view.s529.qual.tuition: Tuition + required fees at eligible institution
view.s529.qual.room_board: Room + board (if half-time student, not exceeding school allowance)
view.s529.qual.student_loan: Up to $10k lifetime to student loan repayment (since 2019)
view.s529.qual.elementary: NOTE: K-12 may NOT be state-qualified (only 30 states recognize)
view.s529.secure.body: Effective 2024: 529 account → Roth IRA rollover allowed when (1) account ≥ 15 yrs old, (2) within annual Roth contribution limit, (3) recipient must be 529 beneficiary, (4) cumulative $35,000 lifetime cap, (5) rolling over CONTRIBUTIONS (not earnings) made within 5 yrs prior NOT eligible. Eliminates over-funding concerns. Federal-only — state may not conform.
view.s530.h1.title: // § 530 COVERDELL ESA
view.s530.hint.intro: $2,000/yr per beneficiary cap (aggregate across all contributors). AGI phase-out: $95-110k single / $190-220k MFJ. Tax-free growth + tax-free withdrawals for qualified K-12 + higher-ed expenses. Contributor's AGI matters, NOT beneficiary's. Beneficiary must use by age 30 (or roll to family member). § 530 ESA + § 529 plan COMPLEMENTARY (use both); ESA covers broader K-12 expenses than 529.
view.s530.warning.18: Beneficiary age 18-30: contributions allowed only for special-needs beneficiaries. Must use balance by age 30 or transfer to family member.
view.s530.warning.30: Beneficiary age 30+: account must distribute within 30 days (taxable to extent of earnings + 10% penalty) OR transfer to family member < 30. Special-needs exception applies.
view.s530.qual.higher_ed: Higher ed: tuition / fees / books / room+board (similar to 529)
view.s401h.h1.title: // § 401(k) HARDSHIP WITHDRAWAL
view.s401h.hint.intro: 'Immediate + heavy financial need' + 'necessary' + 'no other resources'. Safe-harbor categories: medical, principal residence purchase, post-secondary education, prevent eviction / foreclosure, funeral, principal residence damage. SECURE 2.0 improvements: Roth source allowed + $1k emergency / yr + terminal illness + domestic abuse + disaster relief + employer match + own contributions all eligible.
view.s401h.ex.domestic_abuse: Domestic abuse victim (lesser of $10k or 50% balance)
view.s401h.doc.medical: Medical: itemized bills, doctor's letter, insurance EOBs
view.s401h.doc.home: Home purchase: P&S agreement, closing statement
view.s401h.doc.funeral: Funeral: itemized funeral bills + death certificate
view.s401h.doc.affirmation: Post-2020 SECURE Act: employee self-certification accepted instead of docs
view.s72p.h1.title: // § 72(p) 401(k) PLAN LOAN
view.s72p.hint.intro: Max: lesser of $50,000 OR 50% vested balance. 5-year max term (up to 30 yrs for principal residence). Quarterly payments minimum. Loan default = deemed distribution + 10% penalty if < 59½. SECURE 2.0: Roth source for loans + plan-to-plan rollover loans preserved. Hidden cost: opportunity cost (money out of market) + double taxation of interest (you repay with after-tax dollars, then pay tax again on withdrawal).
view.s72p.ben.no_credit: No credit check (loan from your own balance)
view.s72p.ben.low_rate: Interest typically lower than credit cards / personal loans
view.s72p.ben.interest_to_you: Interest goes BACK to your account (paying yourself)
view.s72p.ben.no_tax: Not a taxable distribution if repaid on time
view.s72p.ben.flexible: Can be re-paid early without penalty
view.s72p.risk.opportunity: Opportunity cost: money OUT of market while loan outstanding
view.s72p.risk.double_tax: Double-taxation: interest paid with after-tax dollars + taxed again on withdrawal
view.s72p.risk.job_loss: Job loss / separation triggers loan acceleration (60-90 days to repay)
view.s72p.risk.contributions: Suspend new contributions during loan period (some plans)
view.s72p.risk.market_timing: Borrowing in down market locks in losses
view.s72p.risk.balloon: Balloon payment at term-end if quarterly amounts insufficient
view.s401a9.h1.title: // § 401(a)(9) RMD CALCULATOR
view.s401a9.hint.intro: SECURE Act 2019 + SECURE 2.0 2022 changed RBD (Required Beginning Date): Born ≤ 6/30/1949: age 70½. Born 7/1/1949 → 12/31/1950: age 72. Born 1951-1959: age 73. Born 1960+: age 75. RMD = prior-year Dec 31 balance ÷ life-expectancy divisor (Uniform Lifetime Table). QCD up to $105,000 (2024) from IRA offsets RMD without inclusion in income. Penalty for missed RMD: 25% (10% if timely corrected).
view.s401a9.note.joint: Spouse > 10 yrs younger sole beneficiary: use Joint Life Expectancy Table (longer divisor → smaller RMD). Recalculate annually.
view.s401a9.qcd.no_agi: Excludes amount from AGI — better than itemized charitable deduction
view.s401a9.qcd.medicare: Lower AGI reduces Medicare IRMAA premiums (2-yr lookback)
view.s401a9.qcd.muni: Lower AGI keeps muni bond / Social Security from being taxed
view.s401a9.qcd.over_70: Available age 70½+ even if RMD not yet required
view.s401a9.qcd.cgi: Counts toward RMD if RMD age
view.s401a9.qcd.501c3: Direct transfer to 501(c)(3) public charity only (not DAF, foundation)
view.s401a9.qcd.cgi_2024: $105,000 limit 2024 (indexed); SECURE 2.0 split $50k one-time CRT / CGA
view.s401a9.qcd.spouse: Each spouse has separate limit if separate IRAs
view.s401a9.ex.roth_ira: Roth IRA: NO RMD during owner's life (SECURE 2.0 eliminated Roth 401(k) RMD too)
view.s401a9.ex.still_working: Still working + < 5% owner: 401(k) RMD deferred at current employer
view.s401a9.ex.first_year: First-year RMD can be delayed to April 1 of following year (but creates double RMD year)
view.s401a9.ex.qcd_offset: QCD up to $105k offsets RMD income inclusion
view.s401a9.ex.terminal: Terminal illness: SECURE 2.0 exception (lifetime expectancy < 7 yrs)
view.s401a9.ex.inherited: Inherited IRA: separate rules (10-yr rule + EDB exceptions)
view.s6015.h1.title: // § 6015 INNOCENT SPOUSE RELIEF
view.s6015.hint.intro: 3 forms: (b) Traditional Innocent Spouse, (c) Separation of Liability, (f) Equitable Relief. Relieves spouse of joint + several liability for tax on joint return attributable to other spouse. Form 8857. 2-year deadline for (b) + (c) from first IRS collection activity; (f) equitable within § 6502 CSED 10-yr period. Notice 2024-26 updated rules.
view.s6015.label.knew: Knew or had reason to know?
view.s6015.warning.not_joint: Innocent spouse relief only applies to joint returns. Use Form 1040-X to amend or consider Equitable Relief if Form 8857 still timely.
view.s6015.row.b_who: Joint return + understatement from spouse + no knowledge + inequitable to hold liable
view.s6015.row.c_who: No longer married / separated 12+ mo; allocate as if MFS
view.s6015.row.f_who: Catch-all when (b)/(c) unavailable; Rev. Proc. 2013-34 weighing factors
view.s6015.eq.legal_obligation: Legal obligation to pay (against if assumed)
view.s6015.injured.body: Injured spouse: wants own SHARE of joint refund preserved when IRS plans to offset for spouse's separate debt (child support, student loans, back taxes). Filed simultaneously with return or up to 3 yrs after. Innocent spouse: wants relief from joint liability already assessed. Different forms, different facts, different timing.
view.s6651.h1.title: // § 6651 FAILURE TO FILE / PAY
view.s6651.hint.intro: (a)(1) Failure to file: 5%/month of unpaid tax, max 25%. Min $485 (2024) if > 60 days late. (a)(2) Failure to pay: 0.5%/month, max 25%. Combined: FTF rate REDUCED to 4.5%/month when both apply (so combined never exceeds 5%/month). FRAUDULENT FTF: 15%/month, max 75%. First-Time Abatement available for compliant taxpayers (penalty once / 3 yrs).
view.s6651.rel.fta: First-Time Abatement: clean 3-yr record + currently filing/paying compliance
view.s6651.rel.reasonable: Reasonable cause: serious illness, natural disaster, records destroyed, IRS error
view.s6651.rel.disaster: Federally-declared disaster: automatic extension per IRS announcement
view.s6651.rel.ia_ftp: FTP rate halved (0.25%/mo) during installment agreement period
view.s6651.rel.no_fta_fraud: FTA + reasonable cause NOT available for fraudulent FTF
view.s6651.rel.ignorance: Ignorance of law NOT reasonable cause (United States v. Boyle 1985)
view.s6651.rel2.6651_d: § 6651(d): rate INCREASED to 1%/month after IRS issues 10-day notice + levy threat
view.s6651.rel2.6651_h: § 6651(h): rate REDUCED to 0.25%/month during installment agreement
view.s6651.rel2.6656: § 6656 Failure to deposit (employer trust fund taxes — 2-15% by lateness)
view.s6651.rel2.6672: § 6672 Trust Fund Recovery Penalty (TFRP) — 100% of unpaid trust fund taxes
view.s1014.h1.title: // § 1014 STEP-UP AT DEATH
view.s1014.hint.intro: Heir's basis = FMV at decedent's death (or alternate valuation 6 months later). Wipes built-in gain accrued during decedent's lifetime. § 1014(c) IRD exception: NO step-up for traditional IRA / 401(k) / annuity / NQ deferred comp. Joint property: only decedent's half steps up. Community property (AZ, CA, ID, LA, NV, NM, TX, WA, WI + AK opt-in): BOTH halves step up. Most powerful estate-planning rule.
view.s1014.warning.ird: § 1014(c) IRD exception: no basis step-up. Heir owes ordinary income tax on distributions. § 691(c) deduction available for estate tax attributable to IRD.
view.s1014.strat.hold: Hold appreciated assets in taxable estate until death (vs. selling pre-death)
view.s1014.strat.upstream: 'Upstream gifting' to elderly parents: they die, full step-up, you reinherit
view.s1014.strat.community_lookback: Community property: BOTH halves step up — vs JTWROS (only decedent's)
view.s1014.strat.section_2038: § 2038 retained powers: keep assets in estate intentionally for step-up
view.s1014.strat.section_2036: § 2036 retained life estate: includable in estate → full step-up
view.s1014.strat.idgt: IDGT: grantor trust during life, step-up if power swap exercised at death
view.s1014.strat.qtip: QTIP marital deduction property step-up at SECOND spouse's death
view.s1014.strat.roth_pre_death: Roth conversion before death = step-up wasted (already tax-free); for IRD assets, Roth convert PRE-death
view.s1014.strat.aging_parent_dollar: 'Aging parent dollar': gift cash to elderly parent + they buy appreciated asset → step-up at parent's death → reinherit
view.s23.h1.title: // § 23 ADOPTION CREDIT
view.s23.hint.intro: Up to $16,810 (2024) credit per child for qualified adoption expenses. MAGI phase-out: $252,150-$292,150 (2024). 5-yr carryforward (non-refundable). Special needs adoption: FULL credit regardless of expenses incurred. § 137: employer-paid adoption assistance up to $16,810 excluded from income (separate, NOT double-dip). Form 8839.
view.s23.qual.travel: Travel + lodging + meals (while traveling for adoption)
view.s23.qual.illegal: NOT: expenses violating state law / public policy
view.s23.qual.reimbursed: NOT: expenses reimbursed by employer / charity / gov't program
view.s23.sn.us_citizen: US citizen or resident at time adoption efforts began
view.s23.sn.state_determined: State determines child cannot return to parents' home
view.s23.sn.harder_to_place: State determines specific factor makes child harder to place
view.s23.sn.factors: Factors: ethnic background, age, sibling group, medical/emotional condition
view.s23.sn.tax_id: Use Adoption Taxpayer ID Number (ATIN) before SSN issued
view.s23.sn.title_iv_e: Title IV-E foster care payments don't reduce credit
view.s23.rel.ctc: § 24 Child Tax Credit: $2,000 per qualifying child < 17
view.s23.rel.actc: § 24 Additional CTC: refundable portion up to $1,700 (2024)
view.s23.rel.cdcc: § 21 Child + Dependent Care Credit: 20-35% on up to $3k/$6k expenses
view.eic.h1.title: // § 32 EARNED INCOME CREDIT
view.eic.hint.intro: REFUNDABLE credit for low-to-moderate income workers. 2024 max: $632 (no kids), $4,213 (1 child), $6,960 (2 children), $7,830 (3+ children). Investment income limit: $11,600 (2024) — exceeds disqualifies. No kids: age 25-64 required. Self-employed eligible. PATH Act: refunds held until mid-Feb. One of the most under-claimed credits (20% of eligible miss).
view.eic.qc.relationship: Relationship: child, stepchild, grandchild, sibling, niece/nephew, foster
view.eic.qc.age: Age: < 19 OR < 24 if full-time student OR any age if permanently disabled
view.eic.qc.residence: Residency: lived with you in US > half the year
view.eic.qc.joint_return: Not filing joint return (unless only for refund of withholding)
view.eic.qc.tiebreaker: Tiebreaker rules if multiple potentials (parent over non-parent)
view.eic.path.body: Refunds claiming EITC or ACTC held until mid-February to allow IRS verification (combats fraud). Even if filed Jan 15, refund issued ~Feb 27. Direct deposit available. Form 8867 due diligence required for preparers. EITC fraud carries 10-year ban (§ 32(k)).
view.eic.state.ca: California CalEITC: up to $3,529 + Young Child Tax Credit + Foster Youth credit
view.eic.state.ny: New York EITC: 30% of federal + NYC EITC 5%
view.eic.state.others: 31 states + DC + Puerto Rico have state EIC of various percentages
view.eic.state.refundable: State EIC may be refundable (most) or non-refundable
view.s4942.h1.title: // § 4942 PF UNDISTRIBUTED INCOME
view.s4942.hint.intro: PF must distribute 5% of average non-charitable-use asset value annually for charitable purposes. 30% initial excise on undistributed + 100% if not corrected within taxable period. Carryforward 5 yrs. Form 990-PF Part XII. Operating foundations exempt (substantially-all assets directly used in charitable activities).
view.s4942.qual.grants: Grants to public charities + foreign organizations (with expenditure responsibility)
view.s4942.qual.direct: Direct charitable activities (educational programs, research, museum operations)
view.s4942.qual.set_aside: Set-asides for specific future projects (5-yr limit + IRS approval)
view.s4942.qual.exempt_purpose: Acquisition of property for exempt purposes
view.s4942.qual.amounts_paid: Amounts paid OR set aside for charitable purposes
view.s4942.qual.foreign: Grants to foreign organizations with equivalency determination
view.s4942.qual.daf_no: Grants to DAFs DO count (since SECURE 2.0)
view.s4942.cf.fifo: FIFO usage: oldest carryover absorbed first
view.s4942.cf.set_aside: Set-aside elections require IRS approval + 5-yr limit
view.s4942.cf.unusual_grant: Unusual grant exception (Rev. Proc. 76-13) for one-time mega contributions
view.s4942.cf.transition: Newly-converted PF: 4-yr transition rule for full payout requirement
view.s4960.h1.title: // § 4960 TAX-EXEMPT $1M COMP
view.s4960.hint.intro: 21% excise on tax-exempt EMPLOYER for: (a) Annual comp > $1M to covered employee, OR (b) 'Excess parachute payment' (≥ 3× base × disqualified) similar to § 280G but on tax-exempt side. Covered employee = top-5 highest-paid for any year since 2017 (sticky list — once covered, always covered). Form 4720. Universities, hospitals, foundations, large nonprofits primary targets.
view.s4960.label.name: Name / role
view.s4960.th.name: Name
view.s4960.cov.related_orgs: Related tax-exempt orgs aggregated for determination
view.s4960.cov.licensed_pros: Medical / veterinary licensed professionals: medical-services portion EXCLUDED
view.s4960.cov.shareholders: Substantial shareholder of related taxable corp counted
view.s4960.cov.notice_2019: Notice 2019-9 + Notice 2021-78 + final regs 2021 for guidance
view.s4960.excl.admin: NOT administrative / teaching / research at hospital
view.s4960.excl.allocation: Allocate dual-purpose comp pro-rata across medical vs other
view.s6213.h1.title: // § 6213 NOTICE OF DEFICIENCY
view.s6213.hint.intro: IRS must issue 'Notice of Deficiency (90-day letter)' before assessment. 90 days to file Tax Court petition (150 days outside US). Petition SUSPENDS assessment + collection. Miss deadline = lose Tax Court rights. Alternative paths: pay + sue for refund (District Court / Court of Federal Claims) OR enter Appeals (administrative).
view.s6213.warning.urgent: URGENT: petition deadline approaching. File Tax Court petition NOW via TaxCourt.gov or pay deficiency + designate § 6603 deposit while pursuing claims.
view.s6213.strat.tax_court_pros: Tax Court PROS: don't pay first, suspend collection, expertise, small-case option
view.s6213.strat.district_pros: District Court PROS: jury option, better for 'ordinary law' issues
view.s6213.strat.precedent: Choose forum based on most favorable PRECEDENT (DC Circuit favors gov't)
view.s6213.strat.appeals_first: Always try IRS Appeals BEFORE Tax Court (no cost, can settle)
view.s6213.strat.statute_running: Petition timing = day 90 of notice (postmark counts for first-class mail)
view.s6213.strat.designated: Designated payment under § 6603 stops interest from running
view.s6213.strat.payment_during: Payment during petition recharacterizes as deposit, refunded if win
view.s6213.strat.last_known_address: Notice valid if mailed to 'last known address' — even if returned
view.s6321.h1.title: // § 6321 FEDERAL TAX LIEN
view.s6321.hint.intro: Federal tax lien arises AUTOMATICALLY at assessment for tax + penalty + interest. Does NOT require NFTL filing. NFTL (Notice of Federal Tax Lien) sets PUBLIC priority vs other secured creditors. Attaches to ALL property + rights to property (Drye doctrine). Affects credit score until 10-yr CSED expiration or release. § 6325 options: release, discharge (specific property), withdrawal (false), subordination.
view.s6321.opt.release: Release (§ 6325(a)): full payment OR CSED expired OR bond posted
view.s6321.opt.discharge: Discharge specific property (§ 6325(b)): pay equity attributable to that property
view.s6321.opt.subordination: Subordination (§ 6325(d)): IRS gives priority to another lender
view.s6321.opt.withdrawal: Withdrawal (§ 6323(j)): erroneous filing OR not in best interest OR direct debit agreement
view.s6321.opt.cdp: CDP hearing (§ 6320): right to challenge NFTL within 30 days of filing
view.s6321.opt.equivalent: Equivalent hearing: post-30-day window, less powerful
view.s6321.opt.fresh_start: 'Fresh Start' 2011: NFTL threshold raised to $10k; withdrawal after full pay
view.s6321.opt.compromise: Offer-in-Compromise § 7122 settles debt for less + lien release
view.s6321.pri.purchaser: 'Purchaser' / 'holder of security interest' before NFTL = priority
view.s6321.pri.45_day: 45-day window after NFTL: existing secured creditor still has priority
view.s6321.pri.super_priority: 'Super-priority' exceptions: § 6323(b) — wages, attorney fees, casualty insurance, etc.
view.s6321.pri.mechanics: Mechanics lien + materialman lien generally retain priority
view.s6321.pri.real_property: Real property: file with county recorder where situated
view.s6321.pri.personal: Personal property: file with state designation (UCC-like)
view.s6331.h1.title: // § 6331 LEVY / WAGE GARNISHMENT
view.s6331.hint.intro: Levy seizes property after notice + demand + Final Notice of Intent + 30 days. CDP right under § 6330: 30 days to request hearing → SUSPENDS collection. Exemptions: tools of trade ($5,540 2024), unemployment, child support, basic living standard. Continuous wage levy takes everything ABOVE exempt amount (Form 668-W). Bank levy is ONE-TIME (snapshot at receipt).
view.s6331.warning.cdp_urgent: URGENT: Final Notice (LT11) received. File Form 12153 within 30 days to request CDP hearing — preserves all collection alternatives + Tax Court review of any determination. Missing window means equivalent hearing only.
view.s6331.ex.fuel: $13,510 (2024) of fuel + provisions + furniture + personal effects
view.s6331.ex.basic_living: Basic living standard (per IRS exemption tables)
view.s6331.ex.social_security: Social Security: only 15% can be levied (FPLP automated levy)
view.s6331.res.cdp: CDP hearing within 30 days of Final Notice (Form 12153)
view.s6331.res.release: Request levy release: Form 911 or Taxpayer Advocate
view.s6331.res.equivalent: Equivalent hearing (post-30-day) Form 13753 — less powerful but available
view.s4943.h1.title: // § 4943 PF EXCESS BUSINESS HOLDINGS
view.s4943.hint.intro: PF + disqualified persons may hold up to 20% of voting stock (35% if 3rd-party effective control). De minimis: PF alone ≤ 2% always permitted. 5-yr divestiture period for gifts / bequests (extendable). 10% initial excise on excess + 200% if not corrected within taxable period. SECURE 2.0: 100% solely-owned biz holdings can qualify as Program-Related Investment (PRI).
view.s4943.ex.de_minimis: De minimis: PF alone ≤ 2% always allowed regardless of aggregate
view.s4943.ex.passive_income: Passive income business (rents, royalties, etc.) — different rules
view.s4943.ex.secure_2_0: SECURE 2.0 Newman exception: solely-owned biz qualifies as PRI if all profits charity
view.s4943.ex.unrelated_business: Unrelated trade or business: not exempt; subject to UBTI
view.s4943.disp.sell_market: Sell to non-DP in arm's-length market transaction
view.s4943.disp.pri_conversion: Re-characterize as PRI through governance change (Newman 2022)
view.s4943.disp.spin_off: Spin off the excess holdings to operating subsidiary
view.s4944.h1.title: // § 4944 PF JEOPARDIZING INVESTMENTS
view.s4944.hint.intro: PF managers must exercise ordinary business care + prudence — investments jeopardizing charitable purpose = excise. 10% initial on PF + 10% on manager (knowing). 25% PF + 5% additional manager if not corrected. § 4944(c) PRI EXEMPT — primary purpose charitable + no significant income purpose + no lobbying / political. Most foundations use modern portfolio theory.
view.s4944.pri.primary: Primary purpose: accomplish exempt purposes (charitable / educational / scientific)
view.s4944.pri.no_income: No SIGNIFICANT purpose to produce income
view.s4944.pri.no_political: No purpose to influence legislation / political campaigns
view.s4944.pri.examples: Examples: micro-loans, low-interest community development loans, charter school bonds
view.s4944.pri.counts_payout: PRIs COUNT toward § 4942 5% minimum payout requirement
view.s4944.pri.terms_loans: Below-market loans + equity in social enterprises common PRIs
view.s4944.prud.long_short: Long-term + short-term financial needs of PF
view.s4944.prud.uniform_act: Uniform Prudent Management of Institutional Funds Act (UPMIFA) — state law overlay
view.s4944.prud.review: Periodic portfolio review by qualified advisors
view.s4944.prud.no_per_se: NO per se prohibited investments under modern prudent investor rule
view.s4945.h1.title: // § 4945 PF TAXABLE EXPENDITURES
view.s4945.hint.intro: 20% initial PF + 5% manager (knowing) for: (1) lobbying / political, (2) grants to individuals without expenditure responsibility, (3) grants to non-public-charity orgs without expenditure responsibility, (4) non-charitable purpose. 100% PF + 50% manager if not corrected within taxable period. Manager penalty capped at $20,000 per expenditure.
view.s4945.er.pre_grant: Pre-grant inquiry: ensure grantee + funds will be used per grant terms
view.s4945.er.written: Written grant agreement specifying charitable use
view.s4945.er.records: PF retains records demonstrating proper use
view.s4945.er.recovery: Take appropriate steps if funds misused (refund / lawsuit)
view.s4945.er.cost_segregation: Grantee separately accounts for grant funds
view.s4945.er.report_to_irs: PF reports grants under expenditure responsibility on Form 990-PF Schedule O
view.s4945.indiv.qualified: Selected from qualified group sufficiently broad
view.s4945.indiv.purposes: For travel / study / similar charitable / scientific / educational purposes
view.s4945.indiv.advance: Pre-approval request to IRS prior to grant
view.s4945.indiv.report: Grantee reports per agreement; PF reviews
view.s4945.indiv.employer_relate: No relationship to PF / disqualified persons (anti-abuse)
view.s4945.indiv.alternative_er: Alternative: use expenditure responsibility instead of pre-approval
view.s4945.lob.no_propaganda: No carrying on propaganda / influencing legislation
view.s4945.lob.technical: Technical advice in response to written request from gov body OK
view.s4945.lob.public_charity: Grant to public charity for general support: not lobbying even if charity lobbies
view.s4945.lob.specific_project: Specific lobbying-purpose grants ARE taxable expenditures
view.s4945.lob.ballot_measures: Ballot measure advocacy MAY be lobbying (state-specific)
view.s6664.h1.title: // § 6664 REASONABLE CAUSE DEFENSE
view.s6664.hint.intro: Statutory defense against § 6662 accuracy + § 6663 civil fraud penalties. Requires (1) reasonable cause + (2) good faith. Burden on TAXPAYER. Factors: effort to assess proper liability, knowledge / experience, reliance on professional advice (with valid-reliance criteria). Doesn't apply to § 6707A reportable transaction penalty (strict liability).
view.s6664.h2.reliance_factors: Valid professional-reliance criteria (Neonatology Associates v. Comm'r)
view.s6664.rel.qualified: Adviser was a competent professional with sufficient expertise
view.s6664.rel.good_faith_reliance: Taxpayer actually relied in good faith on adviser's judgment
view.s6664.rel.independence: Adviser had no inherent conflict of interest / lack of independence
view.s6664.rel.discussion: Substantive discussion of facts + applicable law occurred
view.s6664.rel.specific_advice: Advice specific to taxpayer's facts (not generic)
view.s6664.rel.boilerplate: Boilerplate opinions / kit-style memoranda generally don't qualify
view.s6664.boyle.body: Reliance on agent for FILING DEADLINE = NOT reasonable cause (Boyle). However, reliance on advice for SUBSTANTIVE TAX positions CAN be reasonable cause if Neonatology factors met. Distinction: 'ministerial / clerical tasks' (taxpayer responsible) vs 'substantive judgment' (reliance OK). 7th Circuit in West v. Comm'r reaffirmed but with nuanced application.
view.s6664.fac.large_amount: Large dollar amount of underpayment relative to total income
view.s6664.fac.adverse_circumstances: Position taken in adverse-circumstance avoidance pattern
view.s6664.fac.expert_taxpayer: Taxpayer is professional / expert with knowledge
view.s6664.fac.willful_blindness: Willful blindness — reckless disregard for facts
view.s6664.fac.late_response: Late response to IRS inquiries / non-cooperation
view.s7430.h1.title: // § 7430 ATTORNEY FEES RECOVERY
view.s7430.hint.intro: Prevailing taxpayer can recover reasonable admin + litigation costs from gov't. Conditions: (1) substantially prevailed on amount or most significant issue, (2) net worth < $2M individual / $7M corp / 500 employees, (3) IRS position NOT substantially justified. Cap: $230/hr (2024, indexed). Qualified offer: recover even if didn't 'prevail' if IRS later got worse outcome.
view.s7430.costs.attorney: Attorney fees at lesser of actual rate or $230/hr (2024)
view.s7430.costs.special: Special factor enhancement: $230/hr can be exceeded for unusual specialization
view.s7430.costs.copying: Court costs + filing fees + copying / postage
view.s7430.costs.travel: Reasonable travel costs for taxpayer + counsel
view.s7430.costs.no_punitive: NO punitive damages — actual costs only
