view.s7430.costs.admin: Administrative costs from time IRS could have settled
view.s7430.qo.body: Taxpayer makes settlement offer in writing during admin process. If IRS rejects + later obtains a LESS favorable outcome (taxpayer wins more), § 7430 entitles taxpayer to costs from date of offer onwards REGARDLESS of substantially-justified question. Strong tool to flush out marginal cases + force IRS settlement.
view.s7430.sj.pierce: Pierce v. Underwood: 'reasonable basis in both law + fact'
view.s7430.sj.objective: Objective test from gov't perspective at time of position
view.s7430.sj.law_change: Subsequent change in law doesn't make prior position unjustified
view.s7430.sj.split_decisions: Cases where appellate courts split favor gov't (no clearly settled rule)
view.s7430.sj.factual_dispute: Factual disputes generally favor gov't if reasonable inferences possible
view.s7430.sj.taxpayer_burden: Burden ON taxpayer to prove NOT substantially justified
view.s7430.sj.amorphous: Standard is amorphous; success depends heavily on facts
view.s6502.h1.title: // § 6502 CSED 10-YR COLLECTION SOL
view.s6502.hint.intro: IRS must collect within 10 years of assessment. After CSED: debt legally UNCOLLECTIBLE (lien auto-releases). Suspensions extend CSED: OIC pending, CDP hearing, bankruptcy (+ 6 mo), military combat, IA pending, Innocent Spouse application (+ 60 days each). Verify dates via Form 4340 (account transcript). Misjudgment by IRS happens — fight for the original CSED.
view.s6502.toll.oic: § 7122 OIC pending: pendency + 30 days after rejection/withdrawal
view.s6502.toll.cdp: § 6320/§ 6330 CDP hearing: from CDP request through final determination + appeals
view.s6502.toll.ia: Installment agreement pending: from submission + 30 days after termination
view.s6502.toll.innocent_spouse: § 6015 Innocent Spouse election + 60 days after IRS final determination
view.s6502.toll.signed_extension: § 6502(a)(2) waiver extension signed by taxpayer (RARE; avoid)
view.s6502.strat.run_out_clock: Run out the clock: hold tight + comply with current filing requirements
view.s6502.strat.cnc: Currently Not Collectible (CNC) status preserves CSED running
view.s6502.strat.partial_pay: Partial-pay IA: CSED still runs, debt forgiven at CSED
view.s6502.strat.no_oic: DON'T file OIC just before CSED — extends collection time
view.s6502.strat.no_ia: DON'T enter IA in final year — extends CSED
view.s6502.strat.review_transcript: Review Form 4340 annually — IRS sometimes miscalculates CSED
view.s6502.strat.no_borrow: Don't borrow money to pay near-CSED debt
view.s6502.strat.fpa: File-then-pay: Get-out-of-jail-free if pay before CSED but it just expired
view.s7122.h1.title: // § 7122 OFFER IN COMPROMISE
view.s7122.hint.intro: IRS settles for less when full collection doubtful. 3 grounds: (1) Doubt as to Collectibility (DATC), (2) Doubt as to Liability (DATL), (3) Effective Tax Administration (ETA). Reasonable Collection Potential (RCP) = net equity + future income × 12 mo (lump) or 24 mo (periodic). $205 application fee + Form 656 + Form 433-A(OIC) / 433-B(OIC). Low-income waiver available.
view.s7122.po.lump_sum: Lump sum: 20% with offer + 5 payments within 5 months of acceptance
view.s7122.po.periodic: Periodic: initial + monthly payments DURING evaluation + after acceptance, 24 mo total
view.s7122.po.deferred: Deferred Payment Plan: pay over remaining CSED (rare, after IA failure)
view.s7122.po.application_fee: $205 application fee (waived if low-income or DATL)
view.s7122.po.during_eval: Payments during eval keep accruing — non-refundable if rejected
view.s7122.po.default: Default: full balance owed + add'l penalties
view.s7122.ale.food_clothing: Food + clothing + personal care + household supplies + miscellaneous
view.s7122.ale.transport: Transportation: ownership + operating costs by region
view.s6159.h1.title: // § 6159 INSTALLMENT AGREEMENT
view.s6159.hint.intro: Pay debt monthly. Guaranteed IA: < $10k, 36 mo, automatic. Streamlined: < $50k, 72 mo. Routine: < $250k (post-2020). Non-streamlined: > $50k requires Form 433-F. Partial Pay IA (PPIA): reduced payment runs out CSED; debt forgiven at CSED. Setup fee: $22-$178 (waived low-income). Failure to pay = default.
view.s6159.def.new_balance: Owed new tax balance + didn't pay
view.s6159.def.didnt_provide: Failed to provide requested financial info
view.s6159.def.estimated: Falls behind on estimated tax payments
view.s6159.def.csed_runs: IA running CSED restarts at termination
view.s7811.h1.title: // § 7811 TAXPAYER ASSISTANCE ORDER
view.s7811.hint.intro: Taxpayer Advocate Service (TAS) issues TAO ordering IRS action when 'significant hardship' exists. 4 criteria: (1) immediate threat of adverse action, (2) delay > 30 days, (3) cost (including professional fees), (4) irreparable injury / long-term adverse impact. Free service. Form 911 or 1-877-777-4778. TAS can override IRS Operations Manual.
view.s7811.note.file_now: File Form 911 (Application for Taxpayer Assistance Order) or call 1-877-777-4778. Free service. Acknowledgment within 1 business day; case assigned within 5 days. Bring all IRS correspondence + documentation of hardship.
view.s7811.tas.fix_processing: Fix processing errors + restore correct records
view.s7811.tas.systemic: Systemic Advocacy: address recurring IRS process failures
view.s7811.tas.research_change: Research + policy change recommendations via Annual Report to Congress
view.s7811.tas.legal_advice: NOT legal advice or tax planning
view.s7811.tao.compel: Compels IRS to act (release levy, issue refund, etc.) within specified time
view.s7811.tao.override: Can override IRM but NOT statutory mandates
view.s7811.tao.appeal: IRS can appeal to TAS Deputy Chief; TAO stays in effect during appeal
view.s7811.tao.can_recommend: Cannot recommend tax position; only procedural relief
view.s7811.tao.no_court: No automatic court enforcement (TAS is administrative)
view.s7811.tao.timeline: Acknowledgment within 1 business day; case manager within 5 days
view.s7811.tao.relief_types: Common relief: levy release within 24 hrs in true emergencies
view.s6724.h1.title: // § 6721/22/24 INFO RETURN PENALTIES
view.s6724.hint.intro: § 6721 Failure to file + § 6722 Failure to furnish payee stack. Penalties: $60/$130/$310 per return by tier (cured within 30 days / by Aug 1 / after). $630 intentional. Annual caps: $1.26M small biz / $3.78M large. § 6724 reasonable cause waiver + De Minimis safe harbor (10 or 0.5% of forms cured by Aug 1, whichever less).
view.s6724.de_minimis.body: 10 returns OR 0.5% of total, whichever is less, with INCORRECT information are PENALTY-FREE if cured by August 1. Excellent reason to do error-correcting Form W-2c / 1099-CORR by July 31. Doesn't apply to 'intentional disregard' failures.
view.s6724.rc.significant_mitigating: Significant mitigating factors despite normal business care
view.s6724.rc.events_beyond_control: Events beyond filer's control (records destroyed, key employee disabled, etc.)
view.s6724.rc.acted_quickly: Acted quickly to correct upon discovery
view.s6724.rc.no_warning: No advance IRS warning of the issue
view.s6724.rc.system_software: Software / system failure beyond filer's control
view.s6724.rel.6651: § 6651 Failure to file / pay TAX returns (separate from info returns)
view.s6724.rel.6672: § 6672 Trust Fund Recovery Penalty (TFRP): 100% on unpaid trust fund taxes
view.s6724.rel.6723: § 6723 Failure to comply with other info reporting ($60/failure)
view.s6724.rel.6722: § 6722 Failure to furnish CORRECT payee statement to recipient
view.s6724.rel.intentional: Intentional disregard penalty: $630/return or 10% of amount required
view.s6724.rel.electronic: Electronic filing requirement (≥ 10 returns 2024): $310/failure if paper instead
view.s6724.rel.payee_w9: Failure to obtain W-9 backup withholding $310/failure
view.ctc.h1.title: // § 24 CHILD TAX CREDIT
view.ctc.hint.intro: $2,000 per qualifying child < 17 (2024). Refundable portion (ACTC) up to $1,700. MAGI phase-out: $200k single / $400k MFJ. Reduces $50 per $1k over threshold. § 24(h)(4) Family Credit: $500 non-refundable for other dependents (parents, college kids, etc.). SSN required by due date of return.
view.ctc.warning.no_ssn: SSN required by return due date — children without SSN ineligible for full $2,000 CTC. Eligible for $500 Other Dependent Credit instead.
view.ctc.qc.relationship: Son, daughter, stepchild, foster, sibling, niece, nephew, grandchild
view.ctc.qc.residency: Lived with you > half the year (limited exceptions)
view.ctc.qc.support: Did not provide > half own support
view.ctc.qc.us_citizen: US citizen, national, or resident alien
view.ctc.qc.dependent: Claimed as dependent on your return
view.ctc.qc.no_joint: Not filing joint return (except for refund only)
view.ctc.qc.ssn: SSN issued before return due date
view.ctc.future.tcja_sunset: TCJA sunset 12/31/2025: $1,000 + lower phase-out, refundable changes
view.ctc.future.legislative: Multiple 2024 bills propose extending or increasing
view.ctc.future.2021_arpa: ARPA 2021 temporarily: $3,000/$3,600 monthly advance payments (didn't extend)
view.ctc.future.actc_increase: ACTC refundable cap increases by $100 each year through 2026
view.ctc.future.itin: ITIN children: NOT eligible for CTC; eligible for ODC
view.cdcc.h1.title: // § 21 CHILD & DEPENDENT CARE CREDIT
view.cdcc.hint.intro: 2024: 20-35% of $3k (1 child) / $6k (2+) qualifying expenses. Non-refundable. AGI > $43k: minimum 20% rate. Care must ENABLE taxpayer (+ spouse if MFJ) to work or job-search. ARPA 2021 boost expired (was refundable, up to $8k). § 129 Dependent Care FSA $5,000 alternative + can use both (offset rule). Form 2441.
view.cdcc.qi.child: Dependent child under age 13 when care provided
view.cdcc.qi.spouse: Spouse mentally / physically unable to care for self + lives with you > half year
view.cdcc.qi.dependent_disabled: Dependent mentally / physically unable to care for self + lives with you > half year
view.cdcc.qi.identifying: Identification # (SSN / ITIN / EIN) of care provider required
view.cdcc.qi.qualifying_provider: Provider must NOT be your spouse / parent of qualifying child / your other dependent
view.cdcc.qi.location: Care at provider's home OR yours; outside home OK
view.cdcc.qe.au_pair: Au pair, nanny (employer-employee, you must pay SS + Medicare)
view.cdcc.dcfsa.body: Employer Dependent Care FSA: pre-tax up to $5,000 single/MFJ ($2,500 MFS). Reduces salary subject to FICA + income tax. Coordination: § 21 expense cap REDUCED by FSA used (no double-dip). Strategy: if high earners + 2+ kids, max FSA first; remaining $1k+ expenses ($6k - $5k) for credit. Lower earners benefit more from § 21 (35% rate).
view.alimony.h1.title: // § 71 / § 1041 ALIMONY + TRANSFERS
view.alimony.hint.intro: TCJA 2018 ELIMINATED § 71 alimony income + § 215 alimony deduction for divorces / modifications AFTER 12/31/2018. Pre-2019 divorces: grandfathered unless modification explicitly removes old rules. § 1041: spousal property transfers NOT taxable (basis carries over). § 121: home sale exclusion preserved if used as residence 2 of last 5 yrs.
view.alimony.label.home_residence: Used home as principal residence 2 of last 5 yrs?
view.alimony.tcja.eliminated: Eliminated § 71 alimony income + § 215 deduction for post-2018 divorces
view.alimony.tcja.recipient_no_tax: Recipient no longer reports alimony as income (good for them)
view.alimony.tcja.payer_no_deduct: Payer cannot deduct alimony (bad for them)
view.alimony.tcja.pre_2019_grandfather: Pre-2019 divorces: grandfathered unless modification explicitly opts in to new rules
view.alimony.tcja.no_state: State income tax may differ (varies by state)
view.alimony.tcja.section_682: § 682 grantor trust alimony rules also repealed
view.alimony.s1041.no_gain: Transfer between spouses incident to divorce: NO gain / loss
view.alimony.s1041.appreciated: Transfer of appreciated property → recipient owes tax on sale (latent gain)
view.alimony.s1041.timing: Within 1 yr of divorce: automatic; up to 6 yrs if 'incident to divorce'
view.alimony.s1041.installment: § 453 installment sale to ex-spouse generally taxable to seller
view.alimony.s1041.qdros: QDROs distribute 401(k) tax-free if direct rollover
view.alimony.s1041.foreign: Non-citizen spouse: § 1041 doesn't apply → gain recognized
view.alimony.cs.body: Child support is NEVER taxable to recipient + NEVER deductible by payer (both pre- and post-TCJA). § 71(c) anti-recharacterization rules prevent disguising alimony as child support to evade. Watch for 'front-loading recapture' under § 71(f) — if amounts decrease > $15k/yr in first 3 years, payer recaptures.
view.s152.h1.title: // § 152 DEPENDENT QUALIFICATION
view.s152.hint.intro: Two categories: (1) Qualifying Child (QC) — relationship + age (< 19 or < 24 student or any age disabled) + residency > ½ yr + did not provide > ½ own support, (2) Qualifying Relative (QR) — relationship OR household member full year + gross income < $5,050 (2024) + support > ½. Multiple Support Agreement (Form 2120) if no one provides > 50%.
view.s152.tb.most_time: If both parents — parent with MORE residency time
view.s152.tb.highest_agi: Equal time — parent with higher AGI
view.s152.tb.no_parents: No parent claims — highest AGI taxpayer can claim
view.s152.tb.agreement: Multiple support agreement (Form 2120): > 10% support each, sign-off
view.s152.tb.divorced: Custodial parent default unless Form 8332 release signed
view.s152.qr.aunts_uncles: Aunt, uncle, niece, nephew (by blood ONLY, not in-law)
view.s152.qr.member: Any unrelated person who lives with you ENTIRE year
view.s152.qr.not_violation: Relationship not violation of local law (e.g., not bigamy)
view.s7508a.h1.title: // § 7508A DISASTER POSTPONEMENT
view.s7508a.hint.intro: IRS announces postponement after FEMA disaster declaration. Up to 1 year. Postpones filing, payment, refund claim deadlines. Also COVID-19 (§ 7508A(d) 2020-2021). Combat zones (§ 7508) separate provision. Automatic for taxpayers in affected county — no action required, but put disaster name on top of return. Late filing penalties + interest NOT charged during postponement.
view.s7508a.what.casualty_relief: § 165(i) election to claim casualty loss in prior year
view.s7508a.what.no_interest: No interest accrues during postponement on tax owed
view.s7508a.combat.body: Service in combat zone: 180-day extension + period of service + period in hospital. Spouses of military in combat zone: same extension. No interest charged. Direct contact: Form 9000 / IRS Combat Zone Hotline. Spouse + dependents file under combatant's protection.
view.s132.h1.title: // § 132 STATUTORY FRINGE BENEFITS
view.s132.hint.intro: 8 categories excluded from employee income: (a)(1) No-Additional-Cost, (a)(2) Qualified Employee Discount, (a)(3) Working Condition, (a)(4) De Minimis, (a)(5) Qualified Transportation ($315/mo 2024), (a)(6) Qualified Moving (military only), (a)(7) Retirement Planning, (a)(8) Athletic Facility. Non-discrim rules limit highly-compensated. TCJA 2018 eliminated employer deduction for qualified transportation.
view.s132.cat.no_add_cost: (a)(1) No-Additional-Cost: airline space-available, hotel rooms — no employer marginal cost
view.s132.cat.discount: (a)(2) Qualified Discount: ≤ employer gross profit % (or 20% for services)
view.s132.cat.working: (a)(3) Working Condition: laptop, magazine subscription, professional dues
view.s132.cat.de_minimis: (a)(4) De Minimis: occasional snacks, supper money, holiday gifts < $100
view.s132.cat.athletic: (a)(8) Athletic Facility: on-premises gym, mostly used by employees
view.s132.dm.flowers: Flowers / fruit for special events (achievement, illness)
view.s132.dm.gift_cards: CASH or CASH EQUIVALENT (gift cards) NEVER de minimis
view.s132.tr.no_deduct: Employer can't DEDUCT qualified transportation expense (TCJA repeal)
view.s132.tr.combine: Cannot combine: transit + parking allowed but separate limits
view.s132.tr.salary_reduction: Pre-tax salary reduction allowed (employee pays less FICA + income tax)
view.s127.h1.title: // § 127 EDUCATIONAL ASSISTANCE
view.s127.hint.intro: Employer can provide up to $5,250/year tax-free for qualifying education. Through 12/31/2025: includes employer payments toward student loans (SECURE 2.0 / CARES Act). Excludes sports, games, hobbies (unless job-related). Non-discrimination + written plan required. Above $5,250 generally taxable unless qualifies as 'working condition fringe' § 132(d).
view.s127.qual.tuition: Tuition + fees at any educational institution
view.s127.qual.related_no_required: Job-related NOT required (broader than § 132(d) working condition)
view.s127.qual.student_loan_2026: Student loan payments through 12/31/2025 (subject to congressional extension)
view.s127.qual.room_board_no: NOT room + board / transportation / personal use property
view.s127.nd.no_more_5pct: No more than 5% can go to > 5% owners + relatives
view.s127.nd.eligibility: Eligibility must not favor highly compensated
view.s127.nd.written_plan: Written plan required: must be reasonable + reduce taxes
view.s127.nd.notice: Reasonable notice to employees about plan benefits
view.s127.nd.no_choice: No choice between cash + benefit (or it becomes salary)
view.s127.nd.section_132_overlap: Above $5,250 can be excluded under § 132(d) working condition if job-related
view.s127.nd.section_117: § 117 qualified scholarship overlap allowed (tuition reduction for university employees)
view.s127.rel.117: § 117 Qualified Scholarship: tax-free if degree candidate + tuition portion
view.s127.rel.132d: § 132(d) Working Condition: job-related education (no $ limit, but must be necessary)
view.s127.rel.221: § 221 Student Loan Interest deduction: $2,500/yr cap, MAGI phase-out
view.s127.rel.25A: § 25A AOTC / LLC: education credits for taxpayer's own education
view.s125.h1.title: // § 125 CAFETERIA PLAN
view.s125.hint.intro: Allows employees to CHOOSE between cash + qualified benefits without constructive receipt. Common: pre-tax health insurance, Health FSA ($3,200/yr), Dependent Care FSA ($5,000), HSA contributions. Salary reduction reduces both income tax + FICA. Non-discrimination tests: eligibility, contribution + benefits, key employee concentration. Written plan + irrevocable elections (except qualifying status changes).
view.s125.qb.fsa: Health FSA: $3,200 (2024), $640 carryover, 2.5 mo grace period
view.s125.qb.401k: 401(k) contributions (pre-tax employee deferrals; usually separate plan but can interact)
view.s125.sc.employment: Change in employment status (you / spouse / dependent)
view.s125.sc.dependent: Dependent satisfies / ceases to satisfy eligibility
view.s125.sc.must_be_consistent: Change must be consistent with status event
view.s125.nd.eligibility: Eligibility: no group of HCEs treated more favorably for participation
view.s125.nd.contributions: Contributions + benefits: comparable terms for all participants
view.s125.nd.key_employee: Key employee concentration: ≤ 25% of nontaxable benefits go to key employees
view.s125.nd.simple_cafeteria: Simple cafeteria plan (≤ 100 employees): safe harbor passes all tests automatically
view.s125.nd.failed: Failed: HCEs lose tax-favored treatment for full year (recharacterized income)
view.s125.nd.testing_date: Annual testing on last day of plan year
view.s165c3.h1.title: // § 165(c)(3) CASUALTY LOSS
view.s165c3.hint.intro: TCJA 2018-2025: ONLY federally-declared disaster casualty losses allowed for non-business. Loss = lesser of (1) basis - insurance OR (2) decline in FMV. Reduce by (a) $100 per casualty + (b) 10% of AGI. § 165(i) election: claim loss in PRIOR year for faster refund (must file by amended return or original return within statute). Qualified disaster losses (special): $500 floor + no 10% AGI test.
view.s165c3.warning.tcja: TCJA 2018-2025: only federally-declared disaster casualty losses allowed for non-business property. Personal loss otherwise NOT deductible.
view.s165c3.qe.hurricane: Hurricane, tornado, flood, fire, earthquake (federally declared)
view.s165c3.qe.theft: Theft (post-TCJA: ONLY if federally declared disaster context)
view.s119.h1.title: // § 119 MEALS + LODGING FOR EMPLOYER CONVENIENCE
view.s119.hint.intro: Excluded from employee income if furnished: (1) on business premises + (2) for convenience of employer + (3) lodging requires 'as condition of employment'. TCJA: 50% disallowance of employer DEDUCTION 2018-2025; employee exclusion preserved. Common: hotels, hospital cafeteria, on-site housing for camp managers, lighthouse keepers.
view.s119.meals.hospital: Hospital cafeteria food for medical staff (Boyd Gaming Corp)
view.s119.meals.short_lunch: Short lunch period requires staying on premises
view.s119.meals.emergency: Available for emergency calls during meal period
view.s119.meals.full_field_staff: Meals furnished for substantial non-compensatory reason
view.s119.meals.taxable_to_employer: Pre-2026: 50% deduction limit for employer (down from 100%)
view.s119.lodging.condition: Employee must accept as a condition of employment
view.s119.lodging.duty: Required to be on call 24/7 at facility
view.s119.lodging.remote: Remote location with no reasonable alternative housing
view.s119.lodging.security: Security / on-site management (apartment manager, lighthouse, camp)
view.s119.lodging.company_subsidized: Subsidized housing without true requirement = taxable
view.s119.rel.119b: § 119(b)(3): faculty housing exception ≥ 5% FMV cost or rent
view.s119.rel.132e: § 132(e) De minimis: occasional supper money + coffee
view.s119.rel.7872: § 7872 Below-market loan rules for housing assistance
view.s25c.h1.title: // § 25C ENERGY EFFICIENT HOME
view.s25c.hint.intro: IRA 2022 expanded: 30% of qualified expenses. Annual caps: $1,200 general + $2,000 heat pumps / heat pump water heaters / biomass stoves. Specific caps: $600 windows, $500 doors total ($250 each), $150 home energy audit, $600 panel. Through 12/31/2032. Existing home + primary residence required. PIN reporting required 2025+ via Form 8908.
view.s25c.warning.eligibility: § 25C only applies to existing primary residences. New construction + secondary homes ineligible.
view.s25c.eff.windows: Windows / skylights: ENERGY STAR Most Efficient + meet IECC 2021
view.s25c.eff.ac: Central AC: ENERGY STAR Most Efficient
view.s25c.eff.heat_pump: Heat pump: ENERGY STAR Most Efficient
view.s25c.eff.panel: Panel: ≥ 200 amp + supports clean energy upgrade
view.s25c.eff.products_qualified: Manufacturer must certify (PIN to be required by IRS 2025+)
view.s25c.rel.25d: § 25D Residential Clean Energy: 30% solar / battery / geothermal (no cap)
view.s25c.rel.30d: § 30D Clean Vehicle Credit: up to $7,500 EVs
view.s25c.rel.30c: § 30C Alternative Fuel Refueling: 30% EV charger up to $1,000
view.s25c.rel.heera: HEEHRA + HOMES Rebates (DOE, separate state-administered programs)
view.s45l.h1.title: // § 45L NEW ENERGY EFFICIENT HOME
view.s45l.hint.intro: IRA 2022 redesigned: $2,500 ENERGY STAR single-family / $5,000 ZERH (Zero Energy Ready Home) / multifamily $500-$1,000 per unit ($2,500-$5,000 with prevailing wage + apprenticeship). Builder / eligible contractor claims. Through 12/31/2032. Certification by qualified rater. Form 8908.
view.s45l.who.builder: Eligible contractor: built / produced + sold or leased
view.s45l.who.cert_doc: Certification documentation retained for at least 3 yrs
view.s45l.who.lihtc: LIHTC interaction: § 45L credit may reduce eligible basis
view.s45l.zerh.body: DOE Zero Energy Ready Home: ~40-50% more efficient than typical new construction. Must meet ENERGY STAR + EPA Indoor airPLUS + WaterSense + EPA Renewable Energy Ready Home (RERH) checklist. Renewable energy system (solar) typically required. Targets net-zero energy use when paired with renewable system.
view.s179d.h1.title: // § 179D COMMERCIAL BUILDING ENERGY
view.s179d.hint.intro: IRA 2022 redesigned: $0.50-$5.65/sqft (2024) based on efficiency vs ASHRAE 90.1. Base $0.50/sqft + per-percentage-point lift over 25% ($0.02/sqft × pp). Prevailing wage + apprenticeship 5× multiplier. Allocation possible to designer (architect / engineer) for govt / non-profit buildings. Form 7205.
view.s179d.q.envelope: Building envelope: insulation, windows, doors, roof
view.s179d.q.commercial: Commercial buildings (4+ floors); 4+ story mixed use
view.s179d.q.govt: Government buildings (federal, state, local) — allocated to designer
view.s179d.q.tax_exempt: Non-profit / tax-exempt buildings (since IRA 2022) — allocated to designer
view.s179d.q.tribal: Tribal government / Alaska Native corp / Indian housing authority
view.s179d.designer.body: For government + non-profit buildings, building owner CANNOT claim § 179D (no tax liability). Owner can ALLOCATE deduction to qualifying designer (architect, engineer, contractor). Allocation by written certification. Designer treats as ordinary income recovery (basis reduction). One designer only per project; allocation does not affect cost basis to owner.
view.s179d.combo.bonus: § 168(k) Bonus depreciation: still allowed on basis after § 179D
view.s179d.combo.179: § 179 Expensing: separate from § 179D, can combine but reduces basis
view.s179d.combo.45L: § 45L New Energy Efficient Home: different — for residential / dwelling units
view.s179d.combo.6418: § 6418 Transferability: § 179D NOT in transferable credit list (deduction, not credit)
view.s179d.combo.itc: § 48 ITC: separate solar investment tax credit overlap allowed
view.s179d.combo.state: State + local utility energy rebates often stack
view.s86.h1.title: // § 86 SOCIAL SECURITY TAXABILITY
view.s86.hint.intro: 0%, 50%, or 85% of SS benefits taxable based on Provisional Income. Provisional Income = AGI + tax-exempt interest + 50% of SS benefits. Thresholds: $25k / $34k (single), $32k / $44k (MFJ). NOT indexed to inflation since 1983/1993 — highly criticized. Tier 1 RRSI treated identically; Tier 2 Railroad retirement taxed differently (more like pension).
view.s86.f.provisional: Provisional Income = AGI + tax-exempt interest + 50% of SS benefits
view.s86.f.first_tier: First tier: PI > first threshold → 50% of excess (up to 50% of SS) taxable
view.s86.f.second_tier: Second tier: PI > second threshold → 85% of excess + first tier amount; capped at 85% of SS
view.s86.f.mfs: MFS living with spouse: $0 thresholds → 85% always taxable
view.s86.f.foreign: Foreign-earned income excluded under § 911 still counts in AGI for § 86
view.s86.p.roth_conversion: Roth conversions in low-income years before SS starts
view.s86.p.delay: Delay SS to 70 (DRC 8% each year) — taxable but at higher amount
view.s86.p.qcd: QCD ($105k 2024) reduces RMD inclusion in AGI
view.s86.p.standard_deduction: Standard deduction doesn't reduce SS taxability (already below the line)
view.s86.p.muni_no_help: Muni bond interest INCLUDED in provisional income — doesn't help § 86
view.s86.p.no_indexing: No inflation indexing: 50 million seniors paying tax they didn't expect (1983 affected 10%, 2024 affects ~55%)
view.s86.p.spend_down_tax_deferred: Spend tax-deferred accounts before SS starts (lower AGI when SS adds in)
view.s86.p.realized_cap_gains: Manage realized capital gains around § 86 thresholds
view.s86.p.couple_strategy: Married couples: file MFJ (much higher thresholds vs MFS $0)
view.s86.state.no_tax: No state income tax states: WA, TX, FL, NV, AK, SD, WY, TN, NH
view.s86.state.exempt: Specifically exempt SS: AL, AZ, AR, CA, DE, GA, HI, ID, IL, IN, IA (post-2023), KY, LA, ME, MD, MA, MI, MS, NV (no tax), NJ, NY, NC, OH, OK, OR, PA, SC, VA, WI
view.s86.state.partial: Partial tax: CO, CT, KS, MN, MO (phased out), MT, NE (phased out), NM, RI, UT, VT, WV
view.s86.state.full: Full tax: federal only as of 2024 in remaining states
view.s86.state.changes: Recent trend: states phasing out SS taxability (NE + UT 2024)
view.s219.h1.title: // § 219 IRA DEDUCTION
view.s219.hint.intro: 2024: $7,000 base ($8,000 if 50+). Phase-out if active participant in employer plan: Single: $77k-$87k. MFJ (both active): $123k-$143k. MFJ (spouse active): $230k-$240k. No income limit if neither spouse covered by employer plan. Spousal IRA: working spouse covers non-working with own earned income. Roth (§ 408A) has different MAGI limits + no deduction.
view.s219.ap.401k: Eligible to participate in employer 401(k) / 403(b) / 457(b) — even if no contributions made
view.s219.ap.contributions: Received employer contributions during year (SEP, SIMPLE)
view.s219.ap.federal: Federal Civil Service Retirement System / FERS
view.s219.ap.exclusions: NOT: § 457(b) gov't only (after 2007), pure deferred comp, 401(k) you joined late in year
view.s219.nd.form_8606: File Form 8606 every year to track basis (lifetime!)
view.s219.nd.pro_rata: Backdoor Roth: pro-rata rule applies if pre-tax IRA balance exists
view.s219.nd.cost_basis: Future Roth conversion: pro-rata between basis + earnings
view.s219.nd.50_penalty: Form 8606 failure to file: $50 penalty per year (Form 8606 standalone if no return)
view.s219.nd.spousal_separate: Spousal basis tracked separately on each return
view.s219.dl.april_15: April 15 (or tax day) of following year for prior-year contribution
view.s219.dl.no_extension: Extension to file does NOT extend contribution deadline
view.s219.dl.recharacterization: Roth conversion CANNOT be recharacterized (TCJA repeal)
view.s219.dl.excess_removal: Excess contribution removal by Oct 15 (extended) avoids 6% penalty
view.s415.h1.title: // § 415 401(k) COMBINED LIMITS
view.s415.hint.intro: 2024 annual additions: $69,000 (employee + employer + after-tax) per individual per employer. § 415(b) Defined benefit: $275,000 annual benefit cap. Comp cap (§ 401(a)(17)): $345,000. HCE threshold (§ 414(q)): $155,000. 457(b) stacks on 401(k) limit (separate $23k). Multiple unrelated employers: separate $69k limits but combined employee deferral $23k.
view.s415.agg.controlled: Controlled group (parent-sub 80%+ OR brother-sister 80% identical owners): treated as ONE employer for § 415
view.s415.agg.affiliated: Affiliated service group: management or service relationship + ownership / common service
view.s415.agg.brother_sister: Brother-sister: 5 or fewer owners + each control 80% combined + 50% identical
view.s415.agg.unrelated: Unrelated employers: SEPARATE $69k limits (employee can stack but $23k 402(g) combined)
view.s415.agg.self_employed: Self-employed + W-2 job: separate; can max both but 402(g) deferral shared
view.s414v.h1.title: // § 414(v) CATCH-UP CONTRIBUTIONS
view.s414v.hint.intro: Age 50+: $7,500 catch-up (2024) on top of $23k base 401(k). SECURE 2.0 Super Catch-up 60-63: $11,250. IRA: $1,000. SECURE 2.0 Mandatory Roth Catch-up for high earners (> $145k 2023 wages) → catch-up MUST be Roth (effective 2026 after delay). Plan must offer Roth option to provide catch-up to high earners.
view.s414v.warning.blocked: High earner but employer plan doesn't offer Roth — NO catch-up available starting 2026 unless plan adds Roth option.
view.s414v.rm.must_be_roth: Catch-up MUST be Roth (post-tax) — no pre-tax option
view.s414v.rm.effective: Originally effective 2024; delayed by IRS Notice 2023-62 to 2026
view.s414v.rm.plan_offers: Plan must offer Roth option to provide catch-up to high earners
view.s414v.rm.no_option: No Roth option = no catch-up for high earners
view.s414v.rm.simple_ira: SIMPLE IRA: Roth option since SECURE 2.0
view.s414v.rm.wages_only: 'Wages' = FICA wages from § 3121(a); SE income excluded
view.s414v.rm.first_year: First year of employment: no Roth mandate (no prior-year wages)
view.s414v.strat.roth_for_high: High earners with Roth catch-up: locks in tax-free growth at peak earnings
view.s414v.strat.no_match: Catch-up usually NOT matched by employer
view.s414v.strat.full_calendar: Catch-up fills last; max base first
view.s414v.strat.spouse: Spousal IRA catch-up: each spouse $1,000 separately
view.s414v.strat.special_457b: § 457(b)(3) special last-3-year catch-up: 2× regular limit (use OR § 414(v) age 50, not both)
view.s481.cc.dcn123: DCN 123: § 461(h) economic performance recurring item exception
view.s481.cc.dcn235: DCN 235: De minimis tangible property election (§ 263(a))
view.s481.cc.dcn7_overall: DCN 7 overall method change automatic if < $25M gross receipts
view.s481.proc.timing: File Form 3115 with timely-filed return (incl. extensions) for year of change
view.s481.proc.duplicate: Send duplicate copy to IRS National Office Ogden, UT (since 2024)
view.s481.proc.signature: Officer / partner / sole owner signs Form 3115
view.s481.proc.non_automatic: Non-automatic: $11,500 user fee + advance consent (6 mo review)
view.s481.proc.under_examination: Under audit / examination: Form 3115 generally NOT permitted (limited exceptions)
view.s481.proc.notice_2022_36: Notice 2022-36 disaster relief: timely filing extension
view.s481.proc.consistency: Must use new method consistently going forward
view.s168g.h1.title: // § 168(g) ALTERNATIVE DEPRECIATION SYSTEM
view.s168g.hint.intro: Longer recovery periods + straight-line method. Required for: tax-exempt use property, listed property with ≤ 50% business use, certain imported property, § 263A(d)(3) post-2025 farming, BEAT property. ELECTION allowed for any class. No bonus depreciation on ADS property. Trade-off: ADS uses slower recovery but qualifies QIP for bonus retroactively (CARES Act fix).
view.s168g.req.tax_exempt: Tax-exempt use property (leased to govt / nonprofit / foreign person)
view.s168g.req.imported: Imported from foreign trade restriction country
view.s168g.req.passive_pal: § 469 passive activity property in certain cases
view.s168g.el.amt_minimization: Minimize AMT — straight-line method preferred for AMT
view.s168g.el.future_high_rate: Defer deduction to future higher-rate year
view.s168g.el.foreign_tax_credit: Maximize foreign tax credit using slower domestic depreciation
view.s168g.el.class_by_class: Election made class-by-class on Form 4562 line 20
view.s168g.el.no_bonus: No bonus depreciation on ADS property
view.s416.h1.title: // § 416 TOP-HEAVY PLAN
view.s416.hint.intro: Plan is top-heavy if KEY EMPLOYEES own > 60% of plan balance. Key employee: officer earning > $220k (2024), > 5% owner, OR > 1% owner earning > $150k. Top-heavy minimum: 3% non-key employee contribution. Faster vesting: 3-year cliff OR 2-6 year graded. Safe Harbor 401(k): deemed not top-heavy + skips ACP/ADP testing.
view.s416.ke.officers_count: Officers: only top 50 by salary count (capped)
view.s416.ke.former_keys: Former key employees still counted for 5-yr lookback
view.s416.v.2_6_graded: 2-6 year graded: 20% per year starting year 2 → 100% year 6
view.s416.v.faster_than_normal: Compare to 5-yr cliff OR 3-7 year graded for non-top-heavy
view.s416.v.match: Employer matching contributions: separate vesting (2-6 year graded since EGTRRA)
view.s416.v.safe_harbor: Safe Harbor: 100% immediate vesting of safe harbor contributions
view.s416.r.safe_harbor: Adopt Safe Harbor 401(k) — deemed not top-heavy
view.s416.r.qaca: QACA (Qualified Auto Contribution Arrangement) — also relief
view.s416.r.simple: SIMPLE plans: not subject to top-heavy
view.s416.r.401k_match_only: 401(k) with no employer contributions in top-heavy year
view.s416.r.aggregate: Aggregation rules: merge multiple plans to dilute key % below 60%
view.s416.r.distributions: Distributions to key employees count IF within last 5 years
view.s446.h1.title: // § 446 ACCOUNTING METHODS
view.s446.hint.intro: Two primary methods: Cash + Accrual. Hybrid permitted. Cash method generally available if avg gross receipts ≤ $30M (2024) and not 'tax shelter'. C-corps + partnerships with C-corp partner: generally must use accrual if > $30M. Inventory: § 471 generally requires accrual for inventory; small biz exception allows cash basis (§ 471(c)). Method change via Form 3115 + § 481(a) adjustment.
view.s446.cash.constructive: Constructive receipt: available without restriction = received (even if not deposited)
view.s446.cash.year_end_planning: Year-end planning: defer income / accelerate expenses
view.s446.cash.cap_assets: Capital purchases still depreciated, not expensed
view.s446.cash.prepaid: Prepaid expenses can NOT be deducted until benefit consumed (12-month rule limited exception)
view.s446.cash.eligibility: Eligible: sole prop, partnership, S-corp; C-corp + partnership-with-C-corp if ≤ $30M
view.s446.acc.income: Income when EARNED (all-events test + economic performance)
view.s446.acc.deductions: Deductions when LIABILITY fixed + amount determinable + economic performance
view.s446.acc.complex: More complex — A/R, A/P, accruals tracked
view.s446.acc.tax_shelter: Tax shelter must use accrual regardless of size
view.s446.hybrid.combine: Combine cash + accrual for different parts
view.s446.hybrid.common: Common: accrual for inventory + cash for everything else
view.s446.hybrid.clearly_reflects: IRS challenges if doesn't clearly reflect income
view.s446.hybrid.farming: Farming + ranching often hybrid (crop insurance + livestock)
view.s451.h1.title: // § 451 INCOME + ADVANCE PAYMENTS
view.s451.hint.intro: § 451(b) 'all events test': fixed right to receive + amount determinable + economic performance. § 451(c) (TCJA): advance payments deferred up to 1 year if also deferred for book purposes. Rev. Proc. 2004-34 + 2024 final regs: detailed advance payment rules for goods, services, IP licenses. Full inclusion election: include all advance payments in year-1.
view.s451.elig.occupancy: Occupancy of space (excl. real property rent)
view.s451.elig.eligible_loans: Loan commitments + standby letters of credit
view.s451.elig.eligible_transactions: Eligible transactions: prepayment for future delivery
view.s451.elig.NOT_rent: NOT eligible: rent for real property, royalties for resource extraction, financial instruments
view.s451.def.deferral_method: Deferral method: defer revenue NOT recognized for book in year of receipt
view.s451.def.full_inclusion: Full inclusion election: irrevocable, include all advance payments in year 1
view.s451.def.afs_method: AFS method: track recognition matching AFS treatment
view.s451.def.non_afs: Non-AFS method: recognize based on tax / book pattern
view.s451.def.cessation: Cessation of business: full inclusion in final year
view.s461.h1.title: // § 461 ECONOMIC PERFORMANCE
view.s461.hint.intro: All-events test: (1) all events fix liability, (2) amount determinable, (3) economic performance. Economic performance varies: services received, property used, payment for liability. § 461(h)(3) Recurring item exception: recurring liabilities accrue before EP if paid within 8.5 months after year-end. § 461(l) Excess Business Loss limit: $305k/$610k 2024.
view.s461.ep.use_property: Use of property: ratably over period of use
view.s461.ep.payment_liabilities: Payment-type liabilities (rebates, awards, insurance, workers comp): when PAID
view.s461.ep.taxes_assessed: Real property + payroll taxes: ratably accrued
view.s461.ep.interest: Interest: with passage of time (separate § 461 timing rules)
view.s461.re.paid_8_5: Liability paid within 8.5 months after year-end (or with extension)
view.s461.re.material: Either: not material OR matches income better in current year
view.s461.re.not_payment_liability: Doesn't apply to payment liabilities (rebates, prizes, workers comp, insurance, etc.)
view.s461.re.elect: Election made first year + consistent thereafter
view.s461.re.cash_method: Cash method always uses when-paid; this is accrual-method election
view.s461.ebl.non_corp: Non-corporate taxpayers only (corps + S-corps not subject)
view.s461.ebl.disallowed: Disallowed loss carries forward as NOL
view.s461.ebl.includes_wages: Wages / salary count as business income for EBL test
view.s461.ebl.no_passive: Stacks on top of § 469 passive activity + § 465 at-risk
view.s471.h1.title: // § 471 INVENTORY METHODS
view.s471.hint.intro: Required when production / purchase + sale of merchandise = income factor. Small business exemption: ≤ $30M avg gross receipts may use cash method + treat inventory as non-incidental supplies. Cost-flow methods: FIFO (default), LIFO (§ 472 election), Specific ID, Weighted Average. LIFO requires book conformity + LIFO recapture if S-corp converts from C.
view.s471.lifo.pros: Pros: lower COGS in rising prices = lower current taxes; better cash flow
view.s471.lifo.cons: Cons: lower reported earnings; book conformity rule (§ 472(c)); complex layers
view.s471.lifo.recapture: § 1363(d) LIFO recapture: 4-year recapture on C→S conversion
view.s471.lifo.repeal: Often repealed when prices flat / falling (LIFO liquidation triggers)
view.s471.lifo.dollar_value: Dollar-value LIFO: aggregates by index pool
view.s471.lifo.unicap: § 263A UNICAP rules add complexity for LIFO
view.s471.lifo.global_repeal: Global IFRS doesn't allow LIFO (some companies repeal US LIFO too)
view.s471.lifo.simplified: Simplified LIFO available for retailers (BLS index)
view.s471.sbe.cash_method: Can use cash method even with inventory
view.s471.sbe.non_incidental: Inventory treated as non-incidental materials and supplies
view.s471.sbe.deduct_when_paid: Deduct cost in year PAID + provided for sale/use
view.s471.sbe.no_unicap: No write-down or LCM required either
view.s471.sbe.elect: Election made on Form 3115 with § 481(a) adjustment
view.s482.h1.title: // § 482 TRANSFER PRICING
view.s482.hint.intro: IRS may allocate income / deductions among related parties to clearly reflect income. Standards: arm's-length transactions, comparability + best method analysis. Methods: CUP, RPM, CPM, PSM, TNMM. § 6662(e) penalty: 20% substantial / 40% gross misstatement if no contemporaneous documentation. Six principal documents required for protection. APA (Advance Pricing Agreement) provides certainty.
view.s482.doc.overview: Overview of business: organizational structure, ownership, operations, market
view.s482.doc.org_chart: Organization chart + comp data + related party identification
view.s482.doc.economic: Economic analysis: method selection + comparable selection
view.s482.doc.background: Background documents: contracts, transfer-pricing studies, agreements
view.s482.apa.body: Multi-year prospective agreement with IRS on transfer pricing methodology. Three types: Unilateral (IRS only), Bilateral (US + 1 foreign country), Multilateral (US + multiple). 5-year term typical, renewable. Strong protection from § 6662 penalties + foreign disputes (competent authority). User fee $113,500 (small business reduced).
view.s901.h1.title: // § 901 FOREIGN TAX CREDIT
view.s901.hint.intro: Direct credit for foreign income taxes PAID or ACCRUED. Election: credit vs deduction — credit usually wins (dollar-for-dollar reduction). § 904 limit: FTC ≤ (foreign source TI / worldwide TI) × US tax. Separate baskets: passive, GILTI, foreign branch, general, treaty resourced. Excess: carry back 1 year, forward 10 years.
view.s901.note.election: Annual election: credit OR deduction (not both same year)
view.s901.note.simplified: § 904(j) simplified election: ≤ $300 / $600 MFJ — no Form 1116 required
view.s901.note.cash_basis: Cash method: foreign tax credit when PAID
view.s901.note.accrual_basis: Accrual method: foreign tax credit when ACCRUED
view.s901.note.economic_benefit: Subsidies / cost-share denied credit (economic benefit rule)
view.s901.note.refund: § 905(c) redetermination required if foreign tax changes (refund / settlement)
view.s901.note.high_tax: § 954(b)(4) high-tax exception: GILTI / subF excluded if foreign rate ≥ 90% US rate
view.s901.note.creditable: Test 'creditable' tax: net gain on income basis + realization + cost recovery
view.s901.note.2022_regs: 2022 final regs tightened 'creditable' test — many DST + WHT regimes no longer creditable
view.s901.gilti_note: GILTI basket: only 80% of foreign tax creditable (20% haircut). Combined with § 250 50% GILTI deduction, foreign rate ≥ 13.125% generally fully shields US tax.
view.s951.h1.title: // § 951 SUBPART F (CFC ANTI-DEFERRAL)
view.s951.hint.intro: CFC test: > 50% US shareholders by vote OR value; US shareholder: 10%+ vote OR value. US shareholders include pro-rata subpart F income currently (no distribution needed). Categories: FBC Sales / Services / Income from oil + insurance + FPHC income. § 954(b)(4) high-tax exception: foreign rate ≥ 90% × US (≥ 18.9%) → exclude. De minimis < 5% / $1M lesser; full inclusion > 70%.
view.s951.cfc.threshold: CFC: > 50% combined US shareholder ownership (vote OR value), any time during year
view.s951.cfc.us_shareholder: US shareholder = 10%+ vote OR value (post-TCJA also value, not just vote)
view.s951.cfc.attribution: Constructive ownership rules: family, partnerships, trusts, corps
view.s951.cfc.10_percent: Sub-10% shareholders not US shareholders → escape currrent inclusion
view.s951.cfc.downward_attribution: 2017 TCJA removed § 958(b)(4): downward attribution from foreign now applies
view.s951.cfc.foreign_corp: Foreign corp must be incorporated outside US
view.s951.cat.fpcsi: FPHC income (§ 954(c)): interest, div, royalties, rents, capital gains, currency gains
view.s951.cat.fbc_sales: FBC Sales income (§ 954(d)): sales to / from related party + outside country of incorp
view.s951.cat.fbc_services: FBC Services income (§ 954(e)): services performed for related party outside country of incorp
view.s951.cat.insurance: Insurance income (§ 953): underwriting risk outside country of incorp
view.s951.s962_note: § 962 individual election: tax at 21% corporate rate + claim § 960 deemed paid credit (otherwise unavailable to individuals). Subsequent distribution still taxed as dividend minus already-included PTEP.
view.s951A.h1.title: // § 951A GILTI
view.s951A.hint.intro: GILTI = Net CFC Tested Income − NDTIR (Net Deemed Tangible Income Return = 10% × QBAI − interest expense). C-corp effective rate ~10.5% (21% × 50% § 250 deduction), rising to ~13.125% post-2025. § 960(d): 80% deemed paid credit. Individual: 37% w/o § 962; 21% with § 962 + FTC. High-tax election (≥ 18.9%): exclude.
view.s951A.mech.tested: Compute tested income for each CFC (gross less deductions less subF less ECI less high-tax excl.)
view.s951A.mech.aggregate: Aggregate net CFC tested income across all CFCs of US shareholder
view.s951A.mech.qbai: Compute NDTIR: 10% × QBAI − interest expense allocated to QBAI
view.s951A.mech.gilti: GILTI = Net CFC tested income − NDTIR (floor at zero)
view.s951A.mech.s250: C-corp: claim § 250 deduction (50% of GILTI through 2025, 37.5% after)
view.s951A.mech.s960: C-corp: claim § 960(d) deemed-paid credit (80% of allocated foreign tax)
view.s951A.mech.individual: Individual: full GILTI at 37% (no § 250, no § 960) unless § 962 elected
view.s951A.plan.qbai: Maximize QBAI: hold depreciable tangible property in CFCs
view.s951A.plan.subF: Convert tested income → subF income (high-tax excluded subF still gives PTI)
view.s951A.plan.high_tax: Make § 954(b)(4) high-tax election if effective rate ≥ 18.9%
view.s951A.plan.individual: Individual US shareholder: § 962 election or check-the-box
view.s951A.plan.bramcfc: Consolidated CFC group: tested losses offset tested income
view.s951A.plan.ctb: Check-the-box to disregarded: branch + Form 8858 reporting
view.s951A.plan.ftc_basket: GILTI sits in own FTC basket — no cross-crediting with general / passive
view.s951A.plan.expense_allocation: Reduce US-source expense allocation to GILTI basket → preserve FTC capacity
view.s951A.post_2025_note: Post-2025: § 250 GILTI deduction drops from 50% to 37.5% → effective rate rises from 10.5% to 13.125%. Plan accordingly for QBAI buildup and high-tax elections.
view.s250.h1.title: // § 250 FDII + GILTI DEDUCTION
view.s250.hint.intro: FDII = 37.5% deduction → ~13.125% effective rate (TCJA carrot to keep IP in US). GILTI = 50% deduction → ~10.5% effective rate (anti-deferral counterweight). Post-2025: FDII drops to 21.875% (16.4% effective); GILTI drops to 37.5% (13.125% effective). Limitation: deduction ≤ taxable income (no NOL). C-corps only (and § 962-electing individuals).
view.s250.fdii.dei: DEI = Deduction Eligible Income (gross income less subF, GILTI, dividends, etc.)
view.s250.fdii.fdii: FDII = (DEII / DEI) × DEII (Foreign-Derived Income from Eligible Use)
view.s250.fdii.deemed: Deemed Intangible Income = DEI − 10% × Domestic QBAI
view.s250.fdii.foreign_pct: Foreign-use fraction = sales to foreign / total sales (services or sales)
view.s250.fdii.documentation: Documentation required: sales contracts + delivery proof + foreign-use evidence
view.s250.fdii.related_party: Related-party sales to non-US end user qualifies if documented to ultimate use
view.s250.qual.goods: Tangible property sold for foreign use (manufactured + customized + warranty)
view.s250.qual.services: Services performed FOR foreign person (delivery point matters)
view.s250.qual.intangibles: Licenses + royalties on IP used outside US
view.s250.qual.related_us: NOT qualifying: sales to foreign related party for US-end use
view.s250.qual.commodities: Commodity trading + financial product trading excluded
view.s250.ti_cap_note: Taxable income limitation triggered: full uncapped deduction exceeds TI. Deduction reduced to prevent NOL creation. Unused portion does NOT carry forward — lost permanently.
view.s163j.h1.title: // § 163(j) BUSINESS INTEREST LIMIT
view.s163j.hint.intro: BIE deduction ≤ business interest INCOME + 30% × ATI + floor-plan financing. Pre-2022: ATI based on EBITDA (more generous). Post-2022: ATI based on EBIT (TCJA stricter — depreciation + amortization excluded). Small biz exception: ≤ $30M avg gross receipts (3-yr). Real property + farming trades may irrevocably elect out (cost: ADS depreciation). Disallowed interest carries forward indefinitely (corp + partnership pass-through with EBIE rules).
view.s163j.exempt.tax_shelter: Tax shelter NEVER qualifies regardless of size
view.s163j.exempt.real: Real property trade: irrevocable elect-out at cost of ADS depreciation (40-yr nonres)
view.s163j.exempt.farming: Farming business: irrevocable elect-out at cost of ADS depreciation
view.s163j.exempt.utility: Electing utility business: exempt for regulated rate base
view.s163j.exempt.consumer: Floor-plan financing (auto, boat, RV dealers): always fully deductible
view.s163j.exempt.relative: Partner BIE limit applied at partnership level (not partner level) — EBIE allocation
view.s163j.exempt.s_corp: S-corp limit applied at corporate level (no partner-style EBIE)
view.s163j.ati.post_2022: Post-2022: do NOT add back D + A (depreciation + amortization stays out of ATI)
view.s163j.elect_note: Irrevocable elect-out triggers ADS depreciation requirement (40-yr nonres real, 30-yr res real, longer farming class lives). Permanent — cannot reverse.
view.s59A.h1.title: // § 59A BEAT
view.s59A.hint.intro: BEAT = 10% (post-2025: 12.5%) × MTI − regular tax. Applies to 'applicable taxpayer': large corp + base-erosion pct ≥ 3% (2% for banks + securities dealers) + avg gross receipts ≥ $500M (3-yr). MTI = TI + base-erosion tax benefits (deductible payments to foreign related parties). Defends US base from offshore deduction shifting. Filed on Form 8991.
view.s59A.bep.related: Payments to FOREIGN RELATED PARTY (≥ 25% ownership)
view.s59A.bep.deductible: Must give rise to a DEDUCTION (interest, royalties, services)
view.s59A.bep.services_excluded: SCM (Services Cost Method) services without markup excluded
view.s59A.bep.tloan_relief: TLAC interest excluded for Globally Systemically Important Banks
view.s59A.app.corp: Corporation (not S-corp, not RIC, not REIT)
view.s59A.app.derivatives: FDAP withholding NOT enough to escape BEAT
view.s59A.note: BEAT triggered. Form 8991 required. Consider: SCM service restructuring, royalty rate adjustments, COGS recharacterization, qualified derivative payment election, intercompany loan refinancing to non-related sources.
view.s245A.h1.title: // § 245A 100% DRD (Participation)
view.s245A.hint.intro: 100% deduction for foreign-source portion of dividends from 10%-owned foreign corp ('specified 10% owned'). Holding period: 365 / 731 days (45 / 90 for preferred). Hybrid dividend rule (§ 245A(e)): deduction DENIED if foreign payor deducted it. § 1248 gain on CFC stock sale recharacterized to E&P → eligible for § 245A. Net effect: US C-corps largely exempt from tax on actual foreign dividends.
view.s245A.req.us_corp: Recipient must be US C-corp (no S-corp, no individual)
view.s245A.req.specified10: 'Specified 10% owned' foreign corp: 10%+ by vote OR value
view.s245A.req.holding: Holding period: 365 days within 731-day window (45 / 90 days for preferred)
view.s245A.req.not_pfic: Foreign payor cannot be PFIC for any year of US shareholder ownership
view.s245A.req.no_hybrid: Not a hybrid dividend (would create double non-tax)
view.s245A.req.no_ftc: No § 901 FTC + no § 902 indirect credit on excluded portion (logical — no income to credit against)
view.s245A.req.no_deduction: Expenses allocable to deductible dividend NOT deductible (no whipsaw)
view.s245A.s1248.basic: CFC stock sale: gain up to allocated E&P recharacterized as DIVIDEND under § 1248
view.s245A.s1248.deduction: Recharacterized portion eligible for § 245A 100% DRD (huge planning win)
view.s245A.s1248.requires: Still requires holding period + foreign source + not hybrid
view.s245A.s1248.deconsolidation: Trumps gain on CFC sale → enables tax-free repatriation via sale
view.s245A.s1248_note: § 1248 recharacterized gain captured under § 245A — full DRD on E&P portion. Net result: tax-free repatriation via CFC sale. Coordinate with § 367 outbound transfer rules.
view.s7874.h1.title: // § 7874 ANTI-INVERSION
view.s7874.hint.intro: Foreign acquirer of US corp treated as US corp ('surrogate foreign corporation') if former US shareholders own 80%+. 60-79%: stays foreign but loses tax benefits ('inversion gain' taxed at 35% — § 4985 also taxes officers + directors 20% excise). SBA exception: 25% employees + assets + income in foreign jurisdiction. 2016 regs: 3-year lookback on serial inversions.
view.s7874.label.us_pct: Former US shareholder % of foreign acquirer
view.s7874.sba.all_three: ALL THREE prongs required — not 2 of 3
view.s7874.sba.real_business: Real operating business, not shell company
view.s7874.sba.tax_residency: Tax residency in test country (not flag of convenience)
view.s7874.sba.bermuda_caps: Bermuda + Caymans: SBA almost impossible to satisfy due to no real economy
view.s7874.sba.ireland_potential: Ireland + Netherlands: real ops possible due to economic substance
view.s7874.hist.notice2014_52: Notice 2014-52 attacked cash-box mergers + stuffing transactions
view.s7874.hist.regs2016: 2016 final + temp regs: 3-year lookback on serial inversions, killed Pfizer-Allergan
view.s7874.hist.modern: Modern inversions rare due to combined § 245A repatriation + GILTI minimum
view.s7874.surrogate_note: Foreign acquirer treated as US corp for ALL purposes — worldwide income subject to US tax, no treaty benefits, dividends from foreign subs taxed without § 245A. Anti-inversion penalty.
view.s4501.h1.title: // § 4501 STOCK BUYBACK EXCISE
view.s4501.hint.intro: 1% excise tax on FMV of publicly traded corp stock REPURCHASED, effective after 12/31/2022 (IRA 2022). Net of issuances: NEW stock issued reduces base (compensation, M&A, conversions). Exceptions: dividend treatment, ESOP contributions, ≤ $1M de minimis, RIC/REIT, reorganizations. Pending proposal: rate increase to 4% under various legislative drafts. Filed on Form 7208.
view.s4501.cov.public: Covered: publicly traded US corp (also 'covered surrogate foreign corp' under § 7874)
view.s4501.cov.private: Excluded: privately held companies (no securities exchange listing)
view.s4501.cov.expat: Foreign acquirer's US subsidiary repurchases of foreign parent stock — included if reach $1M
view.s4501.cov.tender: Self-tender offers + market repurchases + accelerated share repurchases — all covered
view.s4501.cov.derivative: Substantially similar transactions via derivatives — covered (anti-abuse)
view.s4501.exc.dividend: Dividend treatment: reduces base by amount treated as dividend by recipient
view.s4501.exc.reorg: Reorgs § 368 (qualified): exception when no FMV exchange
view.s4501.exc.esop: Contributions to employer-sponsored ESOP: full offset
view.s4501.exc.bank_dealer: Bank / Securities Dealer in ordinary course
view.s4501.exc.de_minimis: $1M de minimis: full exemption when annual buybacks ≤ $1M
view.s4501.exc.netting: 'Netting rule': new issuances (compensation + acquisition) reduce base $-for-$
view.s4501.exc.spinoff: § 355 spinoffs: distributing corp stock not subject
view.s4501.exc.gh_compete: Non-deductible: § 162 deduction unavailable for excise tax
view.s4501.proposal_note: Proposed 4% rate scenario. Various Biden-Harris + congressional proposals quadruple the rate from 1% to 4%. Plan for higher rate by netting more issuances + ESOP contributions.
view.s877A.h1.title: // § 877A EXIT TAX
view.s877A.hint.intro: 'Covered expatriate' mark-to-market: deemed sale of all assets day before expatriation. Triggers (any one): $2M net worth, 5-yr avg tax > $201K (2024), failure to certify 5-yr compliance. Exemption: $866K (2024) of net gain. Special asset rules: deferred comp + IRA + grantor trust not MTM (withholding + future tax). Long-term resident (LPR 8 of 15 yrs) also subject.
view.s877A.h2.gift_tax: § 2801 Gift / inheritance tax on US recipients
view.s877A.cov.networth: Net worth ≥ $2M on date of expatriation
view.s877A.cov.any: ANY ONE triggers covered expatriate status
view.s877A.cov.dual: Dual-status exception: born outside US + dual nationality at birth + foreign country tax resident 10 of 15 yrs
view.s877A.cov.minor: Minor exception: relinquish before 18.5 + US resident < 10 yrs
view.s877A.spec.deferred: Deferred compensation: 30% withholding on future distributions OR W-8CE election to MTM
view.s877A.spec.ira: IRA / qualified plans: deemed distribution day before expatriation (current taxation)
view.s877A.spec.trust: Grantor trust portion: deemed distribution + applicable rate
view.s877A.spec.non_grantor: Non-grantor trust: 30% withholding on future distributions
view.s877A.spec.specified_tax_deferred: Specified tax-deferred (529, MSA, HSA): mark-to-market with inclusion in income
view.s877A.spec.eligible_deferred: Eligible deferred comp: foreign payor + irrevocable W-8CE
view.s877A.gift.recipient_tax: US recipient pays 40% gift / inheritance tax on transfers from covered expatriate
view.s877A.gift.lifetime: Lifetime gift exclusion does NOT apply
view.s877A.gift.permanent: Applies even decades after expatriation — permanent tax
view.s877A.cov_note: Covered expatriate: Form 8854 required for year of expatriation + 10 subsequent years. Deferral election § 877A(b) requires bond / adequate security + waiver of treaty benefits. § 2801 inheritance tax applies to US recipients of future gifts / bequests at 40%.
view.s1291.h1.title: // § 1291 PFIC EXCESS DIST.
view.s1291.hint.intro: PFIC test: 75% passive income OR 50% passive assets (FMV avg). Default regime: 'excess distributions' taxed at HIGHEST ORDINARY RATE + INTEREST CHARGE back to start of holding period. Excess distribution = current dist > 125% of prior 3-yr avg. Gain on sale = entirely excess (no LTCG rate). Avoid via QEF election (§ 1295 = current inclusion) or MTM (§ 1296 = mark-to-market). Form 8621.
view.s1291.test.income: Income test: ≥ 75% of gross income is passive (interest, div, royalties, rents, gains)
view.s1291.test.assets: Asset test: ≥ 50% of FMV avg assets produce passive income or held for production thereof
view.s1291.test.either: EITHER test triggers PFIC status (not both required)
view.s1291.test.startup: Start-up exception: first year of new corp may be exempt
view.s1291.test.once: 'Once a PFIC, always a PFIC' — taint persists in shareholder's hands across years
view.s1291.test.cfc_overlap: CFC overlap: CFC + PFIC overlap rules exclude CFC US shareholders from PFIC regime
view.s1291.test.foreign_fund: Foreign mutual funds + ETFs almost always PFICs
view.s1291.purge.deemed: 'Deemed sale' purging election: recognize gain currently at § 1291 rates → then QEF / MTM clean
view.s1291.purge.late: Late QEF election: also requires purging via deemed sale
view.s1291.purge.pre_pfic: Pre-PFIC years not counted for interest charge (gain allocated to PFIC years only)
view.s1291.purge.allocate: Allocation: gain pro-rated DAY-BY-DAY across holding period
view.s1291.purge.current: Portion allocated to CURRENT year taxed at current top ordinary, no interest
view.s1291.long_hold_note: Long PFIC holding period: interest charge compounds annually and can exceed the base tax for 10+ year holdings. Consider purging election (deemed sale at § 1291 rates) + QEF / MTM election going forward.
view.s1295.h1.title: // § 1295 PFIC QEF
view.s1295.hint.intro: QEF = annual current inclusion of pro-rata ORDINARY EARNINGS + NET CAPITAL GAIN of PFIC. Avoids § 1291 interest charge AND preserves CHARACTER (ordinary vs LTCG). Requires PFIC AIS (Annual Information Statement) from foreign corp. Election: Form 8621 with timely-filed return; first-year of shareholder ownership best. Late = combine with PURGING election.
view.s1295.mech.eligibility: Must elect for first year shareholder treats foreign corp as PFIC
view.s1295.mech.late: Late election available only with PURGING (deemed sale at § 1291 rates)
view.s1295.mech.character: Inclusion preserves character — ordinary earnings + LTCG separate inclusion lines
view.s1295.mech.basis: Basis += inclusions; basis -= subsequent distributions of PTI
view.s1295.mech.ptep: Previously taxed income (PTI) regime prevents double-taxation on later distributions
view.s1295.mech.timing: Inclusion in shareholder's year that ends with or within PFIC's taxable year
view.s1295.mech.deferral: Optional § 1294 deferral election: defer with interest charge until distribution
view.s1295.ais.required: Required from foreign corp for QEF election to be valid
view.s1295.ais.contents: Contents: ordinary earnings + net capital gain attributable to shareholder + distributions + adjustments
view.s1295.ais.consent: Must include consent to U.S. examination of corporate records
view.s1295.ais.protective: Protective Statement available if AIS not yet received — preserve QEF without AIS
view.s1295.ais.controlled: Controlled QEF: shareholder + family + related own > 50% — can compel AIS
view.s1295.ais.alternative: Alternative: shareholder may obtain via legal action if rights established
view.s1295.ais.no_ais: No AIS = no QEF eligibility = default § 1291 regime applies
view.s1295.not_eligible_note: Not eligible for QEF without PFIC AIS + first-year (or purging) election. Default § 1291 regime applies: highest ordinary rate + interest charge back to start.
view.s897.h1.title: // § 897 FIRPTA
view.s897.hint.intro: Foreign person's gain on USRPI (US Real Property Interest) treated as ECI — taxed at regular US rates (37% individual / 21% corporate). USRPI includes direct US real estate + interests in USRPHC (US Real Property Holding Corp = 50%+ FMV in USRPI for 5-yr testing period). Publicly traded exception: ≤ 5% owners. § 1445 withholding: 15% (10% personal residence ≤ $1M). Forms 8288, 8288-A, 8288-B.
view.s897.usrpi.direct: Direct interest in US real property (fee simple, leases, mineral rights, royalties)
view.s897.usrpi.usrphc: USRPHC stock: domestic corp w/ 50%+ FMV in USRPI during 5-yr testing
view.s897.usrpi.partnership: Partnership interest where partnership is USRPHC equivalent
view.s897.usrpi.publicly: Publicly traded USRPHC: 5%-or-less foreign holder exempt (Class B exception)
view.s897.usrpi.reit: REIT stock: special rules — domestic REITs USRPI but exceptions for public + small-holder
view.s897.usrpi.regular_trade: Inventory + securities held in regular trade — NOT USRPI
view.s897.usrpi.deemed_sale: Deemed sales: corp liquidation distributing USRPI triggers § 897(d)
view.s897.exc.personal: Personal residence ≤ $300K — full withholding exemption (buyer's affidavit)
view.s897.exc.publicly_traded: Publicly traded USRPHC + ≤ 5% ownership — exempt from § 897
view.s897.exc.cleansing: Cleansing rule § 897(c)(1)(B): no USRPI in last 5 yrs — escapes USRPHC
view.s897.exc.s355_reorg: § 355 distributions + § 897(e) nonrec. transfers may defer (not eliminate)
view.s897.exc.cnu_election: Form 8288-B: reduced withholding certificate based on actual tax
view.s897.refund_note: Withholding exceeds tax due — file Form 8288-B (Withholding Certificate) at or before closing for reduced rate, OR claim refund via Form 1040NR (individual) / 1120-F (corp).
view.s1445.h1.title: // § 1445 FIRPTA WITHHOLDING
view.s1445.hint.intro: Buyer (transferee) withholds 15% of amount realized on USRPI disposition by foreign person. 10% for personal residence ≤ $1M; 0% for personal use ≤ $300K (buyer affidavit required). Foreign seller claims credit on 1040NR / 1120-F. Reduced withholding certificate via Form 8288-B pre-closing. Penalties on buyer for failure to withhold. Forms 8288, 8288-A, 8288-B.
view.s1445.proc.closing: Buyer withholds at closing — deducts from sale proceeds delivered to seller
view.s1445.proc.remit: Remit to IRS within 20 days of closing with Form 8288
view.s1445.proc.statement: Provide Form 8288-A copy to seller — needed for refund claim
view.s1445.proc.8288b: Pre-closing Form 8288-B: reduce withholding to actual tax — IRS has 90 days to respond
view.s1445.proc.escrow: Common practice: escrow withholding until 8288-B determination
view.s1445.proc.refund: Seller refund: file 1040NR (individual) or 1120-F (corp) for actual tax + claim withholding credit
view.s1445.proc.penalty: Buyer penalty: tax liability + interest if fails to withhold
view.s1445.proc.agent: Settlement agent / escrow agent often handles mechanical withholding
view.s1445.b_note: Form 8288-B pending: IRS issues Withholding Certificate within 90 days; escrow held until determination. Reduce withholding to actual computed tax. Closing proceeds may be deposited in escrow while certificate pending.
view.s754.h1.title: // § 754 PARTNERSHIP STEP-UP
view.s754.hint.intro: § 743(b) adjustment on TRANSFER of partnership interest (sale, death). Steps up (or down) the buyer's INSIDE basis in partnership assets to match OUTSIDE basis paid. § 734(b) adjustment on partnership DISTRIBUTION when distributee's outside basis differs from inside basis of distributed property. § 754 election = one-time irrevocable (rev. w/ IRS consent). Mandatory § 743(b) if substantial built-in loss (> $250K). Partnership-level election affects ALL transfers in / out.
view.s754.alloc.classes: Split partnership assets into 'capital gain' and 'ordinary income' classes
view.s754.alloc.ordinary_first: Ordinary class allocated first to extent of unrealized OI; remainder to capital class
view.s754.alloc.within_class: Within class: by relative FMV of assets
view.s754.alloc.real_estate: Real estate: stepped-up basis becomes new depreciation schedule for buyer
view.s754.alloc.depreciable: Adjustment recovered over remaining life of asset
view.s754.alloc.no_increase: No increase in basis of partnership cash / receivables (already at FMV)
view.s754.trade.pro_step_up: PRO: avoid phantom income for buyer; align inside / outside basis after transfers
view.s754.trade.con_admin: CON: administrative burden (track adjustment per partner per asset)
view.s754.trade.con_step_down: CON: mandatory step-down on loss transfers; cannot opt out post-2017
view.s754.trade.permanent: PERMANENT: irrevocable except with IRS consent
view.s754.trade.real_estate: Real estate partnerships: § 754 nearly universal because step-up offsets depreciation recapture
view.s754.trade.future_transfers: Affects FUTURE transfers as well — both sales + deaths thereafter
view.s754.trade.ptp: Publicly traded partnership: § 754 elections nearly always in effect
view.s302.h1.title: // § 302 STOCK REDEMPTION
view.s302.hint.intro: Redemption = corporation buys back its own stock from shareholder. Sale treatment (gain / loss) OR § 301 dividend (ordinary income to extent of E&P). Sale tests § 302(b): (1) not essentially equivalent, (2) substantially disproportionate (post < 80% of pre + post < 50% total), (3) complete termination, (4) partial liquidation. § 318 attribution — family + entity + options. Waiver of family attribution with 10-year look-back agreement.
view.s302.attr.family: Family: spouse + children + grandchildren + parents (NOT siblings)
view.s302.attr.corporation: Corporation: 50%+ shareholder treated as owning corp's stock pro-rata
view.s302.attr.trust: Trust + estate: full attribution to beneficiaries by interest
view.s302.attr.options: Options: option to acquire treated as owned
view.s302.attr.waiver: Family attribution may be waived with 10-yr look-back agreement (§ 302(c))
view.s302.attr.cant_waive_entity: Cannot waive entity attribution (corp, partnership, trust)
view.s302.div.ordinary: Full amount taxed as DIVIDEND to extent of E&P (no basis recovery)
view.s302.div.qualified: May be qualified dividend if individual + 60-day holding + domestic
view.s302.div.basis_lost: Basis NOT recovered against dividend — added to remaining shares' basis
view.s302.div.foreign: Foreign shareholder: 30% FDAP withholding (or treaty rate)
view.s302.div.no_capital: No long-term capital gains rate, no loss recognition
view.s302.dividend_note: Dividend treatment: basis is NOT recovered against dividend amount. Instead added to remaining shares' basis (or lost if complete termination via constructive). This is the major punishment of dividend characterization.
view.s332.h1.title: // § 332 SUB LIQUIDATION (TAX-FREE)
view.s332.hint.intro: § 332: Parent corp recognizes NO gain / loss on liquidation of ≥ 80%-owned subsidiary. § 337: Sub recognizes NO gain / loss on distribution to ≥ 80% parent. § 334(b): Parent inherits SUB's INSIDE BASIS in assets — NO step-up. § 381: Parent inherits NOL, E&P, credits, methods. Requires PLAN of liquidation + complete within ONE TAX YEAR (or 3 years per IRS approval). Minority shareholders: § 331 normal taxable. Insolvent sub: § 332 does NOT apply (parent recognizes loss under § 165).
view.s332.label.outside: Parent outside basis in sub stock ($)
view.s332.req.ownership: Parent corp owns ≥ 80% of sub stock by vote AND value (§ 1504(a)(2))
view.s332.req.plan: Plan of liquidation adopted (formal corporate action)
view.s332.req.timing: Complete within 1 tax year of plan, OR up to 3 years w/ approval § 332(b)(3)
view.s332.req.full_payment: Property distributed in complete cancellation of stock
view.s332.req.cessation: Sub ceases to exist for tax purposes (often state-law dissolution)
view.s332.req.minority_taxable: Minority shareholders (< 20%) get § 331 fully taxable treatment
view.s332.req.foreign_sub: Foreign sub: § 367(b) regulations apply — may trigger inclusion of all E&P
view.s332.s381.nol: NOL: parent inherits, subject to § 382 limitation if ownership change in sub
view.s332.s381.exception: SRLY rules limit pre-acq NOL use against post-acq income (cons. ret. only)
view.s332.insol.no_332: § 332 NOT available if sub insolvent (no property distributed for stock)
view.s332.insol.s165: Parent recognizes worthless stock loss under § 165(g)(1) — ordinary if affiliated
view.s332.insol.no_337: Sub still recognizes gain / loss on asset distribution (§ 337 not avail)
view.s332.insol.no_381: NO § 381 carryover — sub attributes lost (NOL, E&P, credits)
view.s332.insol.timing: Worthlessness identified — usually formal dissolution or bankruptcy
view.s332.insol.tradeoff: Tradeoff: parent gets ordinary loss BUT sub's attributes lost forever
view.s332.minority_note: Minority shareholders (< 20%) DO NOT get § 332 — they recognize gain / loss under § 331 (sale or exchange treatment). Plan for separate tax computation at minority level.
view.s338.h1.title: // § 338 STOCK→ASSET ELECTION
view.s338.hint.intro: Stock purchase treated as ASSET PURCHASE for tax. § 338(g): unilateral by buyer (Buyer pays target's immediate gain tax with no offset to Seller). § 338(h)(10): joint election (Buyer + Seller) for S-corp OR consolidated-group target — gain recognized at SELLER level (one tax). Target gets step-up to AGUB (Aggregated Grossed-Up Basis). Tradeoff: immediate gain tax at acquisition vs step-up (depreciation / amortization) recovery. Filed on Form 8023.
view.s338.cls.cash: Class I: cash + bank deposits (no allocation)
view.s338.cls.actively_traded: Class II: actively traded securities + CDs
view.s338.cls.receivables: Class III: receivables, mortgages, credit card
view.s338.cls.intangible_non_goodwill: Class VI: intangibles not in Class VII (§ 197, customer lists)
view.s338.cls.goodwill: Class VII: goodwill + going concern value
view.s338.cls.residual: Allocation: by FMV within each class, residual to Class VI/VII (goodwill)
view.s338.win.high_intangibles: WIN: Target has significant intangibles → § 197 15-year amortization
view.s338.win.high_basis: WIN: Target has high inside basis already (small step-up cost)
view.s338.win.foreign_338g: § 338(g) wins for foreign target (gain often deferred / minimal foreign-tax burden)
view.s338.win.s_corp: § 338(h)(10) win for S-corp targets — single level gain
view.s338.loss.high_appreciation: LOSE: Target has high appreciated assets (huge gain tax)
view.s338.loss.nol_destruction: LOSE: Target has NOLs that would otherwise carry over (lost in election)
view.s338.loss.consolidated: LOSE: Acquirer can't use sub's losses against group (SRLY) anyway
view.s338.loss.qsst_disabled: LOSE: S-corp ESBT / QSST trust beneficiary may disqualify
view.s338.no_election_note: Election cost exceeds step-up benefit. Likely better off WITHOUT § 338 election — accept stock-purchase treatment, retain target's NOLs / E&P, no immediate gain tax.
view.s338.election_note: Election produces positive NPV. Coordinate § 1060 / § 338 residual allocation method to maximize Class VI/VII (15-yr § 197 amortization) over Class IV/V (longer depreciation).
view.s368.h1.title: // § 368 REORGANIZATIONS
view.s368.hint.intro: Seven types of tax-free reorganizations § 368(a)(1)(A)-(G). Common requirements: Continuity of Interest (40%+ stock consideration), Continuity of Business Enterprise, Business Purpose, Plan of Reorganization. Carry over: § 381 sub attributes (NOL, E&P, credits). § 382 limitation on NOL post-ownership-change. Boot = non-stock consideration recognized as gain (not loss). Form 8806 filed for B / C / D acquisitive reorgs.
view.s368.jd.coi: Continuity of Interest (COI): 40%+ stock consideration; measured at time of binding agreement (post-2005)
view.s368.jd.cobe: Continuity of Business Enterprise (COBE): continue HISTORIC business OR use HISTORIC assets in business
view.s368.jd.business_purpose: Business Purpose: corp-level (not solely tax-motivated). Gregory v. Helvering (1935)
view.s368.jd.plan: Plan of Reorganization: formal corporate action documenting steps
view.s368.jd.step_transaction: Step transaction: integrated steps treated as one (Smith v. Comm'r, Walden v. Comm'r)
view.s368.jd.substance_form: Substance over form: tax follows economic substance
view.s368.jd.economic_substance: § 7701(o) codified economic substance doctrine — meaningful change required + non-tax purpose
view.s368.s382.trigger: Ownership change: > 50 pct-point shift over 3-yr test period among 5% shareholders
view.s368.s382.limit: Annual NOL limit: target value × federal long-term tax-exempt rate (~4-5%)
view.s368.s382.bankruptcy_exception: § 382(l)(5): G reorg in bankruptcy — historic creditors as continuing shareholders
view.s368.s382.built_in_gain: RBIG (recognized built-in gain) adjustments increase limit during 5-yr period
view.s368.s382.notice2003_65: Notice 2003-65 + 2019 proposed regs detail built-in gain measurement
view.s368.no_qualify_note: Reorg requirements NOT met → TAXABLE transaction. Full target gain recognized at corporate level (20%+ LTCG) + shareholder level (sale treatment) → double tax.
view.s1248.h1.title: // § 1248 CFC STOCK SALE
view.s1248.hint.intro: US 10% shareholder selling CFC stock: gain up to allocated foreign E&P recharacterized as DIVIDEND. Post-2017 PTEP (previously taxed E&P from GILTI / Subpart F) excluded — already taxed. C-corp shareholder + § 245A 100% DRD: recharacterized portion exempt → tax-free repatriation via sale. Individual: qualified dividend rate (20%) if treaty country + holding period. § 1248(f) also reaches outbound transfers (§ 367(b)). Form 5471 Sch P + § 1248 worksheet.
view.s1248.app.shareholder: US 10% shareholder (vote OR value) sells CFC stock
view.s1248.app.cfc: Foreign corp was CFC at any time during 5-year period before sale
view.s1248.app.shareholder_during: Seller was US 10% shareholder at any time during 5-year period
view.s1248.app.gain: Recharacterize gain up to allocated E&P (current-yr inclusion + accumulated)
view.s1248.app.ptep: PTEP / PTI excluded — already-taxed amounts not re-taxed
view.s1248.app.controlled_chain: Sale of foreign corp holding CFCs: chain attribution to lower-tier CFC E&P
view.s1248.plan.s245a: C-corp seller + § 245A: 100% DRD on recharacterized portion → tax-free
view.s1248.plan.holding: Holding period: 365 days (§ 246(c) for § 245A) — plan acquisition timing
view.s1248.plan.subF_purification: Pre-sale 'purification': dividend out subF / GILTI accumulated E&P
view.s1248.plan.s367b: § 367(b) outbound: similar mechanism on outbound transfers
view.s1248.plan.high_tax: High-tax accumulated E&P: better creditability post-recharacterization
view.s1248.plan.individual_treaty: Individual: qualified dividend if treaty country + 60-day hold (otherwise ordinary)
view.s1248.plan.ptep_first: PTEP-first accounting: deplete PTEP before § 1248 recharacterization
view.s1248.s245a_note: § 245A 100% DRD applied to recharacterized portion. Result: tax-free repatriation of CFC E&P via stock sale. Remaining capital gain still taxed at 20%. Combined with high-tax GILTI exclusion + PTEP basis adjustments, this is the optimal exit path for US C-corps.
view.s355.h1.title: // § 355 SPIN-OFF / SPLIT-OFF
view.s355.hint.intro: Tax-free distribution of CONTROLLED CORP STOCK. Requirements: (1) 5-yr active business EACH side, (2) control (80%) distributed, (3) not a device for dividend, (4) business purpose, (5) continuity of interest (50%+ owners). Spin-off: pro-rata (shareholders keep both). Split-off: surrender Distributing stock. Split-up: Distributing liquidates → shareholders get Sub1 + Sub2. Anti-abuse: § 355(d) 50% acquisition triggers gain. § 355(e) Morris Trust.
view.s355.req.active: Active Trade or Business 5-year rule: § 355(b) — BOTH Distributing + Controlled engaged for 5 years
view.s355.req.control: Distribute Control: 80% vote AND 80% value of Controlled stock
view.s355.req.device: Not Used Principally as Device for distribution of E&P (§ 355(a)(1)(B))
view.s355.req.continuity: Continuity of Interest: 50%+ Distributing shareholders retain stock in spun-off
view.s355.req.cobe: Continuity of Business Enterprise: BOTH sides continue historic business
view.s355.req.no_355e: No § 355(e) 'Morris Trust' 50% acquisition of either D or C within 2-yr window
view.s355.case.ebay_paypal: eBay → PayPal (2015): clean Type D divisive
view.s355.case.morris_trust_origin: Morris Trust v. Commissioner (1966): merger-spin combo upheld → § 355(e) enacted 1997
view.s355.case.private_letter: PLR practice: 6-9 months pre-spin work; ruling cost $40-50K
view.s355.case.no_ruling: 'No-rule' areas: 2003 Rev. Proc. 2003-48 reduced rulings significantly
view.s355.morris_note: § 355(e) Morris Trust triggered: 50%+ acquisition of either D or C within 2-yr window (before / after) causes Distributing to recognize gain (not loss). Plan against post-spin acquisitions for at least 24 months to preserve tax-free status.
view.s311.h1.title: // § 311 CORP DISTRIBUTIONS
view.s311.hint.intro: § 311(a): Distributing corp generally recognizes NO gain / loss on non-liquidating distribution. § 311(b): EXCEPT corp recognizes GAIN on APPRECIATED property (FMV > basis). NO LOSS on depreciated property — asymmetric. Liabilities assumed: treated as cash; FMV = greater of FMV or liab.
