Shareholder side: § 301 dividend (ordinary) to extent of E&P, then return of capital, then capital gain. Liquidating: § 336 (general gain) or § 337 (≥ 80% sub).
view.s311.share.fmv: Amount received = FMV of distributed property + liab discharged
view.s311.share.dividend: Dividend (ordinary) to extent of E&P (current + accumulated)
view.s311.share.return_capital: Return of capital — reduces stock basis to zero
view.s311.share.gain: Remaining = capital gain (LTCG if stock held > 1 yr)
view.s311.share.basis: Shareholder takes FMV BASIS in distributed property (§ 301(d))
view.s311.share.qualified_div: Qualified dividend rate (20%) if individual + 60-day holding + domestic
view.s311.share.corp_drd: Corp shareholder: § 243 DRD (50/65/100%) may reduce dividend tax
view.s311.share.holding_period: Holding period for distributed property starts on distribution date
view.s311.corp.no_loss: § 311(a) general rule: NO gain / loss recognized
view.s311.corp.gain: § 311(b): GAIN recognized on appreciated property (deemed sale at FMV)
view.s311.corp.no_loss_b: § 311(b)(1): NO LOSS allowed on depreciated property — asymmetric
view.s311.corp.liab: If liab > FMV, treated as FMV = liab (Crane / § 7701(g))
view.s311.corp.character: Character of gain: same as if sold (ordinary, capital, depreciation recapture)
view.s311.corp.ep_reduction: E&P reduced by greater of FMV or liab (§ 312(b))
view.s311.corp.s355_distinction: § 355 spinoffs: different rules (§ 361 — corp side, § 355 — shareholder)
view.s311.plan.dist_loss_assets: Distribute LOSS property first (no loss recognized but reduce E&P / basis)
view.s311.plan.sell_gain_keep: Better: SELL gain assets first (recognize gain) → distribute cash
view.s311.plan.s_corp: S-corp pass-through: gain flows to shareholders (single tax) — better than C-corp
view.s311.plan.installment: § 453 installment method on sale → defer cash + gain
view.s311.plan.related_party: § 267 related party rules: loss disallowed even on outright sale
view.s311.plan.basis_step: Basis step-up to shareholder may offset corp gain economically
view.s311.no_loss_note: Asymmetric § 311(b): corp gain on appreciation recognized BUT corp loss on depreciation NEVER recognized. Distribute LOSS property to reduce E&P without tax cost; sell GAIN property to control timing.
view.s1366.h1.title: // § 1366 S-CORP PASS-THROUGH
view.s1366.hint.intro: Shareholder includes pro-rata share of S-corp income, loss, deduction, credit. Items keep CHARACTER — LTCG stays LTCG, qualified div stays qualified, § 1231 separately. Pass-through tracked daily — per-day allocation for mid-year ownership change or election. Loss limited to OUTSIDE BASIS (§ 1366(d)), then AT-RISK (§ 465), then PAL (§ 469). Carryforward indefinitely until basis restored. QBI § 199A 20% deduction available.
view.s1366.char.qdiv: Qualified dividends keep qualified character + 60-day holding
view.s1366.char.s1231: § 1231 gain / loss: net at shareholder level → cap gain if net gain, ordinary if net loss
view.s1366.char.charitable: Charitable contributions: subject to shareholder's AGI limits (60%, 30%, 20%)
view.s1366.char.tax_exempt: Tax-exempt interest: keeps exempt character (raises basis without inclusion)
view.s1366.char.salt: SALT taxes on K-1: subject to $10K cap (BBA-PTET workaround)
view.s1366.char.niit: § 1411 NIIT 3.8%: applies if shareholder is passive
view.s1366.char.dpad: § 199A QBI 20% deduction for active trade or business income
view.s1366.basis.increase: INCREASE basis: ordinary income + sep stated income + tax-exempt int + cap contributions
view.s1366.basis.decrease: DECREASE basis: distributions + losses + sep stated losses + non-deductible
view.s1366.basis.order: Order: increases first, then distributions, then losses
view.s1366.basis.stock_first: Stock basis used first for losses; then debt basis (from direct loans only)
view.s1366.basis.suspended: Loss in excess of basis SUSPENDED → carry forward indefinitely
view.s1366.basis.restored: Suspended loss claimed when basis restored (additional contribution / income)
view.s1366.basis.guarantee: Guarantee of S-corp debt does NOT create debt basis (need direct loan)
view.s1366.basis.restore_debt: Repaid debt basis: gain to extent basis restored if reduced
view.s1366.suspended_note: Suspended loss carries forward indefinitely until basis restored via additional capital contribution OR future S-corp income. Consider loan-from-shareholder (direct, not guarantee) to create debt basis and unlock loss.
view.s1368.h1.title: // § 1368 S-CORP DISTRIBUTIONS
view.s1368.hint.intro: S-corp without C-corp E&P: distribution reduces basis → return of capital, then capital gain. S-corp with C-corp E&P: AAA first (tax-free up to basis), then E&P (taxable dividend), then OAA / basis. AAA = Accumulated Adjustments Account. PTI = Previously Taxed Income (pre-1983 carryover). OAA = Other Adjustments Account (tax-exempt interest). Schedule M-2 of Form 1120-S.
view.s1368.h2.ordering: Standard ordering for S-corp w/ C-corp E&P
view.s1368.order.aaa: AAA: tax-free (reduces basis); excess over AAA → next tier
view.s1368.order.pti: PTI: tax-free (no basis effect — pre-1983 holdover)
view.s1368.order.ep: Accumulated E&P: TAXABLE DIVIDEND at qualified rate
view.s1368.order.basis: Stock basis: tax-free (reduces basis to zero)
view.s1368.order.cg: Excess: CAPITAL GAIN (LTCG if > 1 yr holding)
view.s1368.order.no_ep: If S-corp has NO C-corp E&P: skip dividend tier — pure basis recovery
view.s1368.order.modify: § 1368(e)(3) elections modify ordering at corporate level
view.s1368.aaa.add_income: Add: ordinary income + sep stated income + tax-exempt interest INCOME
view.s1368.aaa.subtract_loss: Subtract: ordinary loss + sep stated losses + nondeductible expenses
view.s1368.aaa.distributions_last: Distributions subtracted LAST (reduce AAA, not below zero)
view.s1368.aaa.tax_exempt_oaa: Tax-exempt interest goes to OAA (not AAA) — corrected by 2002 regs
view.s1368.aaa.can_be_negative: AAA can be NEGATIVE from losses (but distribution limited to current AAA)
view.s1368.aaa.net_negative: Net negative AAA distributions skipped — go to E&P tier
view.s1368.aaa.s382_ats: Coordinate with § 382 AAA limitation upon ownership change
view.s1368.elect.bypass: § 1368(e)(3) AAA bypass: distribute from E&P FIRST (use up old C-corp E&P)
view.s1368.elect.close_books: § 1377(a)(2) close books: avoid blending pre/post-event allocation
view.s1368.elect.qsst_esbt: QSST / ESBT trust beneficiary elections: affect distribution flow
view.s1368.elect.consent: Election requires consent of ALL affected shareholders (Form 1120-S box)
view.s1368.elect.bypass_rationale: AAA bypass valuable if shareholder is C-corp with DRD vs individual w/ QDR
view.s1368.elect.aaa_cleanup: Permanent cleanup: use bypass election to eliminate accumulated C-corp E&P
view.s1368.bypass_note: AAA bypass election in effect: distribute E&P first to permanently eliminate C-corp E&P. Valuable cleanup when shareholders prefer dividend treatment now (e.g., DRD-eligible corp shareholder) over future deferral.
view.s41.h1.title: // § 41 RESEARCH CREDIT
view.s41.hint.intro: Regular method: 20% × (Current QREs − Base Amount). Base = avg gross receipts × fixed base %. ASC § 41(c)(5): 14% × (Current QREs − 50% × avg prior 3 yrs). Payroll tax election: qualified small biz (≤ $5M current revenue + ≤ 5 yrs revenue) uses up to $500K credit against employer FICA (PATH Act 2015). QREs: wages + supplies + contract research (65%) + computer time-share. § 174: 2022+ mandatory 5-yr / 15-yr amortization replaces immediate expensing.
view.s41.tst.permitted_purpose: PERMITTED PURPOSE: new / improved business component (function, performance, reliability, quality)
view.s41.tst.tech_uncertainty: TECHNOLOGICAL UNCERTAINTY: develop info to eliminate uncertainty about capability / method / design
view.s41.tst.experimentation: PROCESS OF EXPERIMENTATION: evaluate alternatives via systematic process (hypothesis → test → analyze)
view.s41.tst.technological: TECHNOLOGICAL IN NATURE: rely on hard sciences (engineering, physics, biology, computer science, chemistry)
view.s41.tst.all_four: ALL FOUR required — fact-intensive determination, contemporaneous documentation critical
view.s41.tst.exclusions: Exclusions § 41(d)(4): post-commercial production, adaptation, duplication, surveys, foreign research, software for internal use (3-part test)
view.s41.tst.documentation: Document: project objectives, technical challenges, alternatives evaluated, test results, time tracking
view.s41.qre.wages: Wages: W-2 Box 1 of employees engaged in qualified research (direct + supervision + support)
view.s41.qre.supplies: Supplies: tangible property used in research (NOT capital assets > 1 yr)
view.s41.qre.contract: Contract research: 65% of payments to contractors (75% for qualified energy research consortium)
view.s41.qre.computer: Computer time-share: cost of cloud computing + hosted services for qualified research
view.s41.qre.no_capital: Cost of fixed assets NOT QRE — depreciation already deducted under § 168
view.s41.qre.no_overhead: Overhead, indirect costs, depreciation NOT QRE
view.s41.qre.no_funded: Funded research (paid by 3rd party) NOT QRE for performer
view.s41.qre.basic_research: Basic research: separate § 41(b)(3) treatment — credit at 20% direct
view.s41.s280c.basic: § 280C(c): deduction reduced by credit amount (no double benefit)
view.s41.s280c.reduced: § 280C(c)(2) reduced credit election: take 79% credit but no deduction reduction (21%-rate)
view.s41.s280c.coordination: Coordinate with § 174: § 280C applies to current QRE, but § 174 amortization spreads
view.s41.s280c.tcja: TCJA pre-2022: § 174 immediate expense; post-2022: forced amortization → credit value rises relative to deduction
view.s41.s280c.bill_pending: Various proposals (TCDTRA, etc.) would restore § 174 immediate expensing
view.s41.qsb_note: Qualified small business: up to $500K of credit applied against employer FICA (6.2% OASDI portion) via Form 8974 + Form 941 quarterly. Valuable for pre-revenue startups with no income tax liability. Carryforward 5 years.
view.s1033.h1.title: // § 1033 INVOLUNTARY CONVERSION
view.s1033.hint.intro: Defer gain if proceeds REINVESTED in qualified replacement property within 2-yr window (3-yr real property condemnation, 4-yr federally declared disaster). 'Same or similar use' test (property & business owner-user); 'like-kind' test (investor-lessor § 1033(g)). Basis of replacement = cost MINUS deferred gain. Holding period TACKS. § 121 may also apply for principal residence. § 165 alternative: deductible loss. Form 1040 + statement.
view.s1033.qual.owner_user: Owner-user: 'same or similar use' — stricter test, functional equivalence
view.s1033.qual.condemned_real: Condemned real property: like-kind real prop in trade / biz / investment qualifies
view.s1033.qual.related_party: § 1033(i) related party: limited to certain real property situations
view.s1033.qual.no_inventory: Inventory or stock in trade NOT eligible as replacement
view.s1033.qual.controlled_corp: § 1033(a)(2)(A) replacement via 80%+ controlled corp acquisition allowed
view.s1033.qual.gain_only: Gain ONLY — loss not deferred under § 1033 (deductible under § 165)
view.s1033.qual.election_timely: Election: by filing return for year of gain (extended return + amended w/in 3 yrs)
view.s1033.s121.exclusion: § 121: $250K / $500K MFJ exclusion on principal residence sale (2 of 5 yr ownership + use)
view.s1033.s121.combine: Casualty of home: § 121 exclusion FIRST, then § 1033 deferral on remaining gain
view.s1033.s121.partial: Partial use § 121: reduced exclusion + § 1033 on remainder
view.s1033.s121.example: Example: $700K proceeds, $150K basis = $550K gain; § 121 excludes $500K → $50K § 1033 deferral on remainder
view.s1033.s121.window: § 121 has no replacement window (used once per 2 yrs); § 1033 deferral additional
view.s1033.fail_note: Deferral failed — replacement outside window OR not 'same or similar' / 'like-kind'. Full gain recognized currently. Consider amended return + extended election if facts allow.
view.s1400z.h1.title: // § 1400Z-2 OPPORTUNITY ZONES
view.s1400z.hint.intro: Tier 1 — Deferral: defer capital gain by investing in QOF within 180 days of realization → defer until earlier of QOF sale or Dec 31 2026. Tier 2 — Step-up: 5-yr hold → 10% basis step-up (15% if pre-2020 + 7-yr hold). Tier 3 — Exclusion: 10-yr hold → BASIS STEP-UP TO FMV → EXCLUDE APPRECIATION on QOF investment. QOF: 90%+ of assets in QOZ Property. Form 8996 (QOF) + Form 8997 (investor). Designation through 2028.
view.s1400z.elig.capital: Capital gain only (short OR long term)
view.s1400z.elig.pass_through: Pass-through K-1 gain — partner / S-shareholder reinvests
view.s1400z.elig.180_day: 180-day window: starts at realization (for individual) or partnership year-end
view.s1400z.elig.not_unrealized: Unrealized gain NOT eligible — must be realized first
view.s1400z.elig.not_ordinary: Ordinary income gain NOT eligible (recapture, inventory, etc.)
view.s1400z.comp.90pct: Hold 90%+ assets in QOZ Property (semiannual test, average of 2 dates)
view.s1400z.comp.qoz_property: QOZ Property: QOZ Business Property OR equity in QOZ Business OR partnership int
view.s1400z.comp.qoz_business: QOZ Business: 70% of tangible property in QOZ + 50% of gross income from QOZ + < 5% nonqualified financial property
view.s1400z.comp.original_use: Original use in QOZ — OR substantial improvement (double basis within 30 months)
view.s1400z.comp.sin_business: Sin businesses excluded: golf, country clubs, massage parlors, hot tubs, sun tan, racetracks, gambling, liquor stores
view.s1400z.comp.5_pct_finance: < 5% Nonqualified financial property (excludes working capital ≤ 31 months under 'safe harbor')
view.s1400z.comp.86_month: 31-month 'working capital safe harbor' for QOZ Business — written plan + schedule
view.s1400z.exit.dec_2026: Dec 31 2026: forced inclusion event — pay deferred gain tax
view.s1400z.exit.qof_sale: Earlier QOF sale: inclusion at sale date
view.s1400z.exit.10yr_step_up: 10-yr+ hold: full exclusion on QOF appreciation (not original deferred gain)
view.s1400z.exit.partial_disposition: Partial QOF interest disposition: pro-rata inclusion
view.s1400z.exit.death_basis: Death of investor: § 1014 basis step-up may eliminate deferred gain
view.s1400z.exit.gift: Gift to family: inclusion event (taxable to donor)
view.s1400z.exit.s1031_no: § 1031 within QOF not allowed (TCJA removed personal property)
view.s1400z.exit.qof_fail: QOF fails 90% test: monthly penalty $10K min (Section 6700-style)
view.s1400z.tier3_note: Tier 3 unlocked: 10-yr hold provides BASIS STEP-UP TO FMV on QOF interest sale. Full appreciation excluded — including LTCG from QOF business operations. Combined with Tier 1 deferral + Tier 2 step-up, this is the largest tax shelter available.
view.s357.h1.title: // § 357 LIABILITIES IN § 351
view.s357.hint.intro: § 357(a): Liability assumption generally NOT boot (no immediate gain). § 357(b): Tax avoidance / no business purpose → ENTIRE liability treated as boot → gain to transferor. § 357(c): Liabilities EXCEED basis → gain to extent of excess. Basis impact: transferor's stock basis = property basis − liabilities; transferee corporation inherits transferor's basis in property. Coordinate with § 351 control test.
view.s357.label.other_basis: Transferor's basis in OTHER prop transferred ($)
view.s357.a.no_boot: Assumption of liabilities NOT treated as boot
view.s357.a.basis_reduce: Transferor's stock basis REDUCED by liabilities assumed
view.s357.a.example: Example: transfer property basis $100K, FMV $300K, liab $80K → no gain; stock basis = $20K
view.s357.a.bookkeeping: No current tax, but built-in gain preserved
view.s357.a.rationale: Economic substance: transferor's net economic position unchanged
view.s357.b.trigger: Tax avoidance motive OR no business purpose for transfer of liability
view.s357.b.all_boot: ENTIRE liability amount treated as boot → gain to extent of FMV − basis ratio
view.s357.b.intent: IRS often finds avoidance when liability incurred shortly before transfer
view.s357.b.refinance: Refinancing before incorporation: scrutinized as bootstrap
view.s357.b.cash_out: Transferor extracts cash via debt → § 357(b) more likely applies
view.s357.b.recent_debt: Recent debt + cash extraction = 'tax avoidance' presumption
view.s357.c.basic: Liabilities ASSUMED > TOTAL BASIS of property transferred → recognize gain
view.s357.c.character: Character: same as if property sold (capital, ordinary, recapture)
view.s357.c.s357c_3: § 357(c)(3): liabilities arising from deductible-when-paid items NOT counted (accounts payable)
view.s357.c.basis_floor: Transferor's stock basis cannot go below zero — § 357(c) prevents negative basis
view.s357.c.solution_boot: Workaround: take back NOTE / boot for excess (recognize gain anyway but more flexibility)
view.s357.c.s361_reorg: § 361 reorg context: § 357(c) does NOT apply to plain D reorgs
view.s357.s357c_note: § 357(c) gain triggered — liabilities exceeded basis. Workarounds: (1) contribute additional high-basis property to absorb excess, (2) transferor retains liability instead of letting corp assume it, (3) take back installment note to spread gain.
view.s305.h1.title: // § 305 STOCK DIVIDENDS
view.s305.hint.intro: § 305(a): Stock dividend = TAX-FREE; just splits ownership pro-rata. Holding period tacks; basis allocated by relative FMV. § 305(b) EXCEPTIONS (5 categories) trigger ORDINARY DIVIDEND treatment: (1) cash or stock option, (2) disproportionate distribution, (3) common + preferred to different shareholders, (4) preferred-stock-on-preferred, (5) convertible preferred. § 305(c): DEEMED dividends — conversion ratio adjustments, accrued PIK on preferred, arrearages.
view.s305.b1: Distribution in lieu of money: shareholder option of cash OR stock
view.s305.b2: Disproportionate distribution: some shareholders receive cash, others stock — increased proportionate interest
view.s305.b3: Common AND preferred distributions: common to some shareholders + preferred to others
view.s305.b4: Preferred stock on preferred: distribution of preferred on preferred (except antidilution adj)
view.s305.b5: Convertible preferred: where any shareholder will have increased proportionate interest
view.s305.c.conversion: Conversion ratio adjustment: increases conversion advantage of one class vs others
view.s305.c.pik_preferred: PIK preferred: accrual of dividends paid in additional preferred = deemed dist
view.s305.c.arrearages: Accrued / suspended dividend arrearages on preferred: treated as deemed dividends in some cases
view.s305.c.anti_dilution: Anti-dilution adjustments: safe harbor if proportionate to ALL stock
view.s305.c.warrants: Warrants + options: § 305(d) treats holders as shareholders for these rules
view.s305.c.amount: Amount: FMV of increased interest (use binomial / Black-Scholes for convertibles)
view.s305.c.timing: Timing: economic accrual — not just legal payment date
view.s305.c.reporting: Form 1099-DIV reporting + Form 8937 corporate action issuer reporting
view.s305.basis.relative: Allocate basis between old + new stock by RELATIVE FMV on distribution date
view.s305.basis.holding: Holding period of new stock TACKS to old stock
view.s305.basis.example: Example: $10K basis on 100 shares; 1-for-1 stock split; $5K basis on each lot
view.s305.basis.preferred_common: Preferred + common: allocate by FMV ratio (preferred at par or market)
view.s305.basis.tax_dividend: If § 305(b) makes it taxable: basis = FMV at distribution (full step-up)
view.s305.basis.short_term: Cash in lieu of fractional shares: reported on 1099-B as sale; partial gain
view.s305.basis.s306: § 306 'tainted stock': bailout-prevention rule on later sale of preferred
view.s305.basis.recordkeeping: Form 8937 + 1099-DIV box reporting for issuer + broker
view.s305.c_note: § 305(c) deemed dividend triggered. PIK preferred accruals + conversion ratio adjustments cause economic-substance taxation even without legal payment. Apply Form 8937 reporting + 1099-DIV broker-level adjustment.
view.s336.h1.title: // § 336 CORP LIQUIDATION
view.s336.hint.intro: § 336(a): Corp recognizes GAIN AND LOSS on liquidating distribution at FMV. Contrast § 311 (non-liquidating, gain ONLY). § 336(d)(1): NO LOSS on distribution of 'disqualified property' (contributed in § 351 / capital contrib within 2 yrs to related party). § 336(d)(2): 'Tax avoidance' loss reduced if basis > FMV. Shareholder side: § 331 — gain / loss on stock; FMV basis in distributed property. § 332 alternate: tax-free if 80%+ parent (§ 337 for sub).
view.s336.share.amount: Amount realized = FMV of property + cash + liability discharged (not assumed)
view.s336.share.gain_loss: Gain / loss = amount realized − stock basis (capital character)
view.s336.share.holding: Holding period: from original acquisition; LTCG if > 1 yr
view.s336.share.depreciation_recap: No depreciation recapture passes through to shareholder (corp recognizes at § 336)
view.s336.share.installment: § 453(h): installment note received in liquidation reported on installment method
view.s336.share.s453B_d: § 453B(d): NO recognition on dist of installment notes (deferred)
view.s336.share.minority: Minority shareholders (< 20%): always § 331 (even if 80%+ parent uses § 332)
view.s336.loss.s336d1: § 336(d)(1): NO LOSS on distribution of disqualified property to related party (§ 267)
view.s336.loss.disqualified: 'Disqualified property' = property contributed in § 351 / capital contrib + tax-avoidance purpose
view.s336.loss.s336d2: § 336(d)(2): 'tax avoidance' loss reduced if asset's basis > FMV at contribution date
view.s336.loss.related: Related party: § 267 — > 50% owner, family, controlled corps
view.s336.loss.allocation: Loss allocated pro-rata across distributee shareholders
view.s336.loss.disqual_period: 5-year period: contribution + tax avoidance presumed within 2 yrs
view.s336.loss.s336d3: § 336(d)(3): no loss to 80% parent under § 332 (general non-recognition)
view.s336.s336e.scope: Stock sale to UNRELATED party by single SELLER (not buyer) recharacterized as asset sale
view.s336.s336e.s_corp: S-corp target OR consolidated sub eligible (similar to § 338(h)(10))
view.s336.s336e.consent: Seller-side election; no buyer consent required
view.s336.s336e.basis_step: Buyer gets stepped-up basis to AGUB (similar to 338(h)(10))
view.s336.s336e.gain_seller: Gain recognized at seller / consolidated group level
view.s336.s336e.s_corp_basis: S-corp shareholders adjust basis for inside-built gain
view.s336.s336e.election: Election: attached to seller's tax return for year of sale
view.s336.s336e.use_case: Useful when buyer is reluctant to join § 338(h)(10) but seller wants asset-sale treatment
view.s336.disqualified_note: Loss DISALLOWED — § 336(d)(1) disqualified property + related-party distribution. Allocate loss-property to UNRELATED shareholders, OR sell BEFORE liquidation, OR avoid § 351 contributions within 2 yrs of planned liquidation.
view.s30D.h1.title: // § 30D CLEAN VEHICLE
view.s30D.hint.intro: Up to $7,500: $3,750 critical minerals + $3,750 battery components (IRA 2022). Income limit: $300K MFJ / $225K HoH / $150K single (MAGI, current OR prior year). MSRP cap: $80K SUVs/vans/trucks; $55K cars. Final assembly: MUST occur in NORTH AMERICA. Transfer at dealer (2024+): get $7,500 off price at purchase. Form 8936. Used EV: § 25E ≤ $4,000 / 30% (separate, lower limits).
view.s30D.elig.assembly: Final assembly: must occur in North America (FuelEconomy.gov verifies)
view.s30D.elig.foreign_entity: FEOC (Foreign Entity of Concern) restriction: starting 2024 batteries, 2025 critical minerals
view.s30D.elig.magi: MAGI limit: $300K MFJ / $225K HoH / $150K single (CURRENT or PRIOR yr — pick lower)
view.s30D.elig.battery_kwh: Battery capacity: 7 kWh min for plug-in hybrid (PHEV); BEV no min
view.s30D.elig.gross_vehicle: Gross Vehicle Weight Rating: ≤ 14,000 lbs (commercial trucks use § 45W)
view.s30D.tx.dealer: Buyer transfers credit to dealer at point of sale → discount $ off MSRP
view.s30D.tx.cash: Dealer receives advance payment from IRS (Form 8300)
view.s30D.tx.no_tax_owed: Buyer benefits even if no tax liability — non-refundable becomes refundable in practice
view.s30D.tx.income_excess: If MAGI exceeds limit retroactively: REPAY credit on next return
view.s30D.tx.dealer_must_register: Dealer registers via IRS Energy Credits Online (ECO) portal
view.s30D.tx.report_form_15400: Report on Form 8936 + 8936 Schedule A; dealer issues Time-of-Sale Report
view.s30D.tx.repayment_safe_harbor: No clawback if buyer not aware of income excess
view.s30D.tx.lease_loophole: § 45W lease 'loophole' (commercial credit) — dealer credit available even if vehicle non-qualifying for § 30D
view.s30D.fail_note: Not eligible. Common failures: MSRP exceeds cap, MAGI exceeds limit, vehicle assembled outside NA, missing critical minerals / battery components qualifications. Consider used EV (§ 25E) or commercial route (§ 45W) — different rules + no income / MSRP limit.
view.s45Q.h1.title: // § 45Q CARBON CAPTURE
view.s45Q.hint.intro: IRA 2022 expanded: $85/ton (geologic sequestration), $60/ton (EOR + utilization), $180/ton DAC (Direct Air Capture). Prevailing wage + apprenticeship multiplier 5×: ($17 / $12 / $36). 12-year credit period post-placed-in-service (vs prior 10). § 6417 direct pay for tax-exempts + 5-yr cap for taxable; § 6418 transferability allows credit sale to 3rd parties. Minimum threshold: 1K tons/yr (DAC), 12.5K (industrial), 18.75K (electricity).
view.s45Q.label.price: Market price per ton ($) for transfer
view.s45Q.thr.electricity: Electricity generation: 18,750 tons/year (post-IRA, lower than prior 500K)
view.s45Q.thr.placed: Placed in service before 2033 (originally) — IRA extended to 'construction begin' before Jan 1 2033
view.s45Q.thr.contract: Carbon Capture Equipment Contract: written, binding contract
view.s45Q.thr.epa: EPA Subpart RR reporting required for geologic sequestration
view.s45Q.thr.lca: Life Cycle Assessment required for utilization (net reduction certification)
view.s45Q.thr.recapture: Recapture: leakage in 3-yr period triggers credit recapture pro-rata
view.s45Q.mon.direct_pay_eligible: § 6417 direct pay: tax-exempt entities + states + tribal + electric coops fully eligible
view.s45Q.mon.direct_pay_taxable: Taxable entities: § 6417 elective ONLY for first 5 years (CCS, hydrogen, advanced manufacturing)
view.s45Q.mon.direct_pay_clawback: Direct pay clawback: 15% / yr if domestic content not met (CCS exception applies)
view.s45Q.mon.transfer_cash: § 6418 transferability: sell credit for CASH to unrelated buyer at any discount
view.s45Q.mon.transfer_no_recapture: Credit purchaser not subject to recapture if seller in good faith
view.s45Q.mon.market_pricing: Current market: 88-95¢ on $1 for verified projects
view.s45Q.mon.same_year: Election made each year; specified credit amount
view.s45Q.mon.same_or_better: Carbon offset markets bidding up — voluntary carbon markets often above tax credit
view.s45Q.bonus_note: 5× bonus multiplier active: prevailing wage + apprenticeship compliance unlocks full $85 / $60 / $180 rate vs $17 / $12 / $36 base. CRITICAL for project economics — documentation + Davis-Bacon Act compliance required throughout construction + 5-yr repair period post-PIS.
view.s48.h1.title: // § 48 INVESTMENT TAX CREDIT
view.s48.hint.intro: 30% credit on qualified energy property basis (solar, geothermal, fuel cell, wind ≤ 100kW, biogas, CHP, micro-turbine, etc.). 5× bonus structure: base 6% + 24% prevailing wage / apprenticeship multiplier. Adders: +10% domestic content + 10% energy community + 10% LMI bonus (low-income community). Post-2024: § 48E 'Clean Electricity ITC' replaces. § 6417 direct pay tax-exempt; § 6418 transferability taxable. Forms 3468 + 3800.
view.s48.bonus.base: BASE rate: 6% (without wage / apprentice OR ≥ 1 MW)
view.s48.bonus.full: FULL rate: 30% (with wage + apprentice OR under 1 MW)
view.s48.bonus.domestic: +10% Domestic Content: ≥ 40% steel + iron domestic; manuf'd products % varies
view.s48.bonus.energy: +10% Energy Community: brownfield / fossil-fuel community / unemployment census
view.s48.bonus.lmi: +10-20% LMI bonus: low-income census tract or affordable housing — competitive allocation
view.s48.bonus.allocation_required: LMI bonus requires PRE-APPROVAL via Treasury / DOE Capacity Allocation Program
view.s48.bonus.under_1mw: < 1 MW projects: full 30% rate WITHOUT wage / apprentice compliance required
view.s48.s48e.tech_neutral: Technology-neutral: any zero-emission electricity generation qualifies
view.s48.s48e.phase_in: Begins 2025; § 48 phases out for new projects
view.s48.s48e.phaseout: Phase-out: 100% credit until 2032; phases out as GHG goals met
view.s48.s48e.future: Long-term: extends until US grid hits 25% of 2022 emissions
view.s48.s48e.choice: Choose § 48 (legacy) OR § 48E (post-2024) — projects in service 2025+ default to § 48E
view.s48.s48e.45y_ptc: § 45Y is PTC counterpart (operating credit per kWh)
view.s48.rec.triggers: Triggers: sale, abandonment, theft, casualty, lease change, change of use
view.s48.rec.transferability: Transferability: recapture stays w/ ORIGINAL TAXPAYER (seller); buyer protected
view.s48.rec.casualty_exception: Casualty replacement: no recapture if rebuilt within rules
view.s48.rec.basis_reduce: Basis reduction: 50% of credit (separate basis reduction for adders)
view.s48.rec.recapture_form: Form 4255 to compute recapture if triggered
view.s48.rec.exit_planning: Hold 5+ yrs to avoid recapture — common in tax equity partnerships
view.s38.h1.title: // § 38 GENERAL BUSINESS CREDIT
view.s38.hint.intro: Aggregator of business credits: § 41 R&D + § 45 PTC + § 48 ITC + § 45Q + § 30D + § 45W + § 45L + § 45X + Empowerment Zone + WOTC + Disabled Access + many more. Limit: net regular tax above TMT (Tentative Minimum Tax) + 25% of next $25K. Carryback 1-yr, Carryforward 20-yr (§ 39). Forms 3800 + 8000-series source forms. § 6417 direct pay + § 6418 transferability for IRA 2022 specified credits (different rules per credit).
view.s38.comp.investment: Investment credit § 46: § 48 ITC, § 48C, § 48E, § 47 rehab, § 48D semi mfg
view.s38.comp.work_opportunity: § 51 Work Opportunity Tax Credit (WOTC): up to $9,600 per qualified hire
view.s38.comp.alcohol: § 40 Alcohol fuel mixtures + § 40A biodiesel + § 40B Sustainable Aviation Fuel
view.s38.comp.research: § 41 Research credit (incremental QRE method or ASC)
view.s38.comp.renewable: § 45 Renewable Electricity PTC + § 45Y Clean Electricity PTC
view.s38.comp.employer: § 45E Small Employer Pension Setup + § 45F Childcare + § 45R Health Insurance
view.s38.lim.basic: Limit = Net Tax (regular) above Tentative Minimum Tax (TMT)
view.s38.lim.25pct: + 25% of remaining tax above $25K (after TMT reached)
view.s38.lim.specified: 'Specified credits' (R&D portion, energy ITC, etc.) can offset AMT + TMT
view.s38.lim.eligible_small: Eligible small biz: R&D + energy + various credits can offset AMT entirely
view.s38.lim.carry_back: Unused: carry back 1 yr first, then 20-year carryforward
view.s38.lim.s39_expiration: § 39 carryforward expires after 20 yrs → permanent loss
view.s38.lim.pre_acquisition: § 39(d) pre-acquisition GBCs: SRLY-limited similar to NOL
view.s38.carry_note: Unused credit carries back 1 yr (file Form 1045 / 1139 quick refund) OR forward 20 yrs. For IRA 2022 specified credits: consider § 6417 direct pay (tax-exempts) or § 6418 transferability (taxables) to monetize immediately at 88-95¢ on $1.
view.s6050W.h1.title: // § 6050W 1099-K REPORTING
view.s6050W.hint.intro: PSE (Payment Settlement Entity) reports gross payments via Form 1099-K. ARPA 2021 threshold $600 — Notice 2023-74 + 2024-85 delayed phase-in: $20K + 200 txns (2023, 2024 baseline) → $5K (2024) → $2.5K (2025) → $600 (2026). Affects: PayPal, Venmo, Cash App, Etsy, eBay, Stripe, Square, Airbnb sellers. Gross only — no expense deduction on form. Reconcile on Schedule C / Form 8949.
view.s6050W.rec.schedule_c: Business income: Schedule C — gross 1099-K + COGS + expenses → net profit
view.s6050W.rec.personal_loss: Personal item LOSS (e.g., sold used couch): Schedule 1 → 'Other Income +X' then 'Other Adj -X'
view.s6050W.rec.personal_gain: Personal item GAIN: Schedule D — collectibles gain (Form 8949 capital gain)
view.s6050W.rec.friends: Friends + family Venmo: NOT income — but document and exclude on Schedule 1 adj
view.s6050W.rec.gig: Gig income: Schedule C if regular trade or Schedule SE for self-employment tax
view.s6050W.rec.airbnb: Airbnb / VRBO: Schedule E if not service business; Schedule C if substantial services
view.s6050W.rec.crypto: Crypto exchanges 1099-K: report on Form 8949 (specific identification by lot)
view.s6050W.rec.disagree: Disagree with PSE? Request CORR from PSE → Form 1040 reconciliation statement
view.s6050W.exc.personal: Personal transfers (gifts, splitting bills): NOT reportable; PSE must distinguish
view.s6050W.exc.under_threshold: Below threshold: PSE may STILL report (de minimis) — many file anyway
view.s6050W.exc.foreign: Foreign payees: not reportable (different reporting under FATCA / Ch 3 / 4)
view.s6050W.exc.s139_disaster: Disaster relief payments (§ 139): excluded from gross income, may still appear
view.s6050W.exc.charity: Charitable contributions: excluded from PSE reporting (donor letter substantiates)
view.s6050W.exc.tax_exempt: Tax-exempt entities: still receive 1099-K but typically excluded from UBI
view.s6050W.exc.refunds: Refunds + chargebacks: GROSS — taxpayer reconciles to net on Schedule C
view.s6050W.exc.combined: Aggregation: PSE aggregates ALL transactions under SAME TIN/account
view.s6050W.personal_note: Personal transfers (friends + family): NOT taxable income. Reconcile on Schedule 1 — report Other Income gross then reverse with Other Adjustment 'Form 1099-K personal transfers not reportable'. PSE should have flagged as 'Personal Payment' but often misclassified.
view.s45X.h1.title: // § 45X ADVANCED MFG CREDIT
view.s45X.hint.intro: Per-unit credit on US production of clean energy components: solar cells / modules / wafers, wind blades / nacelles / towers, battery cells / modules, inverters, critical minerals. Phases out: 100% through 2029, 75% 2030, 50% 2031, 25% 2032, 0% 2033 (critical minerals stay 100% PERMANENTLY). Sold to unrelated party. § 6417 direct pay (taxable 5-yr / nonprofit perm); § 6418 transferability major monetization. Form 7207.
view.s45X.rate.solar_module: Solar module: $0.07 / direct current watt of capacity
view.s45X.rate.battery_module: Battery module: $10 / kWh ($15 / kWh w/o cells incorporated)
view.s45X.rate.critical_minerals: Critical minerals: 10% of production costs (NEVER phases out)
view.s45X.rate.electrode: Electrode active materials: 10% of production costs
view.s45X.rate.applicable_critical: 39 applicable critical minerals (lithium, cobalt, nickel, manganese, graphite, etc.)
view.s45X.mon.taxable_5yr: Taxable C-corps: 5-year § 6417 direct pay window starting in production year (2023-2032)
view.s45X.mon.cash_market: Active credit market: 87-94¢ on $1 typical for verified projects
view.s45X.mon.passive_inv: Passive activity rules: § 469 may limit corp partners using PTCs
view.s45X.mon.feedstock_use: Self-use as feedstock: § 45X(c)(2)(C) integrated facility election lets internal use qualify
view.s45X.mon.foreign_entity: FEOC (Foreign Entity of Concern) excluded from claiming + supply chain restrictions
view.s45X.mon.cogs_allocation: COGS allocation reduces taxable income — credit + COGS combo is the planning sweet spot
view.s45X.mon.repurchase: Stockpile + later sale: credit recognized at sale, not production
view.s45X.minerals_note: Critical minerals NEVER phase out — 10% of production costs PERMANENTLY through full IRA horizon. Strategically prioritize lithium, cobalt, graphite, manganese, nickel production in US (39 applicable minerals). Combined with § 30D EV credit's mineral sourcing rules, this drives US battery supply chain re-shoring.
view.s45V.h1.title: // § 45V CLEAN HYDROGEN
view.s45V.hint.intro: Tiered by lifecycle GHG emissions: ≤ 0.45 kg CO2e/kg H2 = $3.00/kg; 0.45-1.5 = $1.00/kg; 1.5-2.5 = $0.75/kg; 2.5-4.0 = $0.60/kg. 10-year credit period from facility placed in service. Construction begin before 1/1/2033. 5× wage + apprenticeship multiplier — base rates 1/5 of above. Three pillars regulation (incrementality + temporal matching + deliverability) under 2024 final regs. § 6417 direct pay + § 6418 transferability. § 48 ITC election: may elect § 48 instead.
view.s45V.h2.rates: Credit by GHG intensity (with wage + apprentice 5×)
view.s45V.label.ghg: Lifecycle GHG kg CO2e / kg H2
view.s45V.path.electrolysis: Electrolysis (green): renewable electricity + water → H2 + O2; lowest GHG if renewable
view.s45V.path.smr_ccs: Steam Methane Reforming + CCS (blue): natural gas + CCS may qualify if 90%+ capture
view.s45V.path.biogas: Biogas reforming: anaerobic digestion of waste → H2; LCA varies widely
view.s45V.path.nuclear: Nuclear (pink/red): high-temp electrolysis or thermochemical from nuclear heat
view.s45V.path.methane_pyrolysis: Methane pyrolysis (turquoise): solid carbon byproduct; potentially low-LCA
view.s45V.path.gas_no_ccs: Conventional SMR (gray): > 9 kg CO2e/kg — NO § 45V credit
view.s45V.path.byproduct: Byproduct hydrogen: from chlor-alkali, steam reforming — may qualify partial
view.s45V.pillar.incrementality: INCREMENTALITY: electricity from new renewable source (≤ 36 months pre-production)
view.s45V.pillar.temporal: TEMPORAL MATCHING: hourly matching 2030+ (annual matching 2024-2029 phase-in)
view.s45V.pillar.deliverability: DELIVERABILITY: same EIA region or DOE-defined contiguous area
view.s45V.pillar.43_safe_harbor: Safe harbor: nuclear existing capacity (up to 200 MW per facility / 5% national)
view.s45V.pillar.exceptions: Carve-outs: hydropower long-term contracts + ERCOT / California state-specific
view.s45V.pillar.epc: EPCs (Energy Attribute Certificates) used to track renewable sourcing
view.s45V.pillar.eu_comparison: EU has similar 'additionality' standards under Renewable Energy Directive III
view.s45V.elect.choose: Annual election: choose PTC (§ 45V) OR one-time ITC (§ 48)
view.s45V.elect.itc_appeal: ITC better when: high capex, low utilization, or quick monetization preferred
view.s45V.elect.ptc_appeal: PTC better when: high utilization (8000+ hrs/yr), low capex relative to output
view.s45V.elect.itc_recapture: ITC recapture risk if facility sold w/in 5 yrs
view.s45V.elect.ptc_no_recapture: PTC: no recapture, but credit only earned in years producing qualifying H2
view.s45V.elect.tax_equity: Tax equity market: both monetizable via § 6418 transferability
view.s45V.elect.fundamental: Fundamental tradeoff: certainty (ITC) vs ongoing (PTC); break-even ~5-6 years
view.s45V.full_credit_note: Lowest tier achieved: full $3/kg PTC. Requires either truly renewable electrolysis with three pillars OR SMR + 95%+ CCS + appropriate LCA. Verify with verified third-party LCA + GREET (DOE) model. Maintain throughout 10-yr period — re-verify if pathways change.
view.s48C.h1.title: // § 48C ADVANCED ENERGY
view.s48C.hint.intro: Competitive allocation by DOE: 30% ITC (with wage + apprenticeship) on energy property + advanced manufacturing equipment. Reauthorized IRA 2022 with $10B (40% reserved for energy communities). Round 1: $4B (Mar 2023); Round 2: $6B (Jan 2024). Reserved for: clean energy manufacturing + critical minerals + industrial decarbonization. 2-yr placed-in-service after allocation. Forms 3468 + 3800 + DOE concept paper + full app. § 6418 transferability allowed; NO § 6417 direct pay for § 48C.
view.s48C.proc.encourage: Step 2: DOE 'Encouragement Letter' — proceed with full application
view.s48C.proc.full_app: Step 3: 30-page full application + technical details + commercial plan
view.s48C.proc.allocation: Step 4: DOE selects + Treasury allocates credit amount
view.s48C.proc.placed: Step 5: Place in service within 2 years of allocation (extension up to 1 yr)
view.s48C.proc.recapture: Step 7: 5-yr recapture period (sale, abandonment, change of use)
view.s48C.proc.evaluation_criteria: Evaluation: (1) commercial viability, (2) job creation, (3) GHG reduction, (4) supply chain diversification
view.s48C.round.r1_amount: Round 1: $4B allocated (Mar 2023); 80 projects selected from 250+ applicants
view.s48C.round.r2_energy: Round 2: $2.5B for energy communities + 80% domestic content bonus
view.s48C.round.r3_pending: Round 3: pending congressional authorization for additional funding
view.s48C.round.beats_45x: Stacking: § 48C + § 45X possible if separate qualifying expenditures (different basis)
view.s48C.round.timing: Timing: concept papers due ~Q1; full applications ~Q3; allocations ~Q4-Q1 next yr
view.s48C.round.success_rate: Award rate: ~30-40% of full applicants — competitive
view.s48C.no_alloc_note: NO § 48C credit without DOE allocation. Apply at energy.gov 48C-PORTAL: 8-page concept paper → encouragement letter → 30-page full application → allocation. Process takes 6-9 months. Consider § 48 ITC (automatic, no allocation) as alternative.
view.s25D.h1.title: // § 25D RESIDENTIAL CLEAN ENERGY
view.s25D.hint.intro: 30% credit on residential property: solar PV, solar thermal water, fuel cells, geothermal heat pump, small wind, battery storage ≥ 3 kWh. IRA 2022 extended through 2034 (26% in 2033, 22% in 2034); 30% through 2032. Battery storage added 2023 as standalone qualified (no solar pairing). Non-refundable but UNLIMITED carryforward (unused offsets future tax). Form 5695. Principal residence + 2nd home eligible; rentals NO. Includes labor + installation.
view.s25D.elig.solar_pv: Solar PV: panels, inverters, wiring, mounting, installation labor
view.s25D.elig.solar_thermal: Solar thermal water heating: NOT pool / hot tub heating
view.s25D.elig.battery_storage: Battery storage ≥ 3 kWh — added IRA 2022 as standalone (no solar pairing required)
view.s25D.elig.fuel_cell: Fuel cell: $500/0.5 kW capacity limit (max $1,667 / household per yr × 30% = $500)
view.s25D.elig.geothermal: Geothermal heat pump: Energy Star certified; loop + heat exchanger + pump
view.s25D.elig.wind: Small wind: ≤ 100 kW capacity, residential property
view.s25D.elig.labor: Labor + installation INCLUDED in credit basis
view.s25D.elig.roof: NOT eligible: structural roof reinforcement, general roof replacement (unless integrated PV)
view.s25D.elig.financing: Financing costs (interest) NOT in basis; deductible separately
view.s25D.opt.bundling: Bundle with state + utility rebates — § 25D applies to cost AFTER rebates (federal subtracts state)
view.s25D.opt.battery_alone: Standalone battery now qualifies — install without solar if grid backup desired
view.s25D.opt.tax_planning: Coordinate with income year to use credit (Roth conv, capital gains realization)
view.s25D.opt.no_lease: Solar lease / PPA: NOT eligible — only OWNED systems qualify
view.s25D.opt.battery_sizing: Battery 3+ kWh: avoid sub-3 kWh batteries (no credit)
view.s25D.opt.divided_ownership: Co-owners: each claims proportionate share based on % paid
view.s25D.opt.basis_reduce: Basis reduction: 50% — applies to depreciation IF business use portion
view.s25D.carry_note: Unused credit carries forward INDEFINITELY — Roth conversion, IRA distribution, or other taxable event can use credit. § 25D is non-refundable but never expires. Unlike § 38 GBC (20-yr limit), § 25D has no time limit on carryforward.
view.s1446F.h1.title: // § 1446(f) PS INT. WITHHOLDING
view.s1446F.hint.intro: Transferee withholds 10% of amount realized on sale of US trade/biz partnership interest by foreign person (TCJA 2018). ECI portion deemed sold by foreign partner → may be subject to higher US tax. Exceptions: seller certifies non-foreign (Form W-8 + W-9), seller certifies no ECI portion, partnership certifies low %, PTP exception, withholding certificate. Forms 8804 + 8805 + 8288 series. Coordinate with § 1445 FIRPTA (real estate).
view.s1446F.scope.transferee: TRANSFEREE (buyer of partnership interest) must withhold
view.s1446F.scope.foreign_seller: SELLER is foreign person (NRA, foreign corp, foreign partnership)
view.s1446F.scope.ecit: Partnership conducts US trade or business (creates ECI on portion)
view.s1446F.scope.10pct: Default rate: 10% of TOTAL amount realized
view.s1446F.scope.eci_only: Reduced rate available if seller certifies LIMITED ECI exposure
view.s1446F.scope.remit: Remit to IRS within 20 days of payment
view.s1446F.scope.penalty: Penalty: 100% of withholding amount + interest if failure
view.s1446F.scope.partnership_secondary: Partnership becomes secondarily liable if transferee fails
view.s1446F.eci.deemed: Foreign partner deemed to sell ECI assets pro-rata on sale of interest
view.s1446F.eci.rev_91_32: Reverses Rev. Rul. 91-32 (only US assets treated as sourced US) — TCJA codified deemed sale
view.s1446F.eci.character: Character: same as if assets directly sold (cap gain, ordinary, recapture)
view.s1446F.eci.computation: ECI gain = total gain × FMV of ECI assets / FMV of all assets
view.s1446F.eci.us_rate: Taxed at regular US rates (max 37% individual / 21% corp)
view.s1446F.eci.ftc: Foreign FTC may apply if foreign country also taxes the gain
view.s1446F.eci.s897_combined: Coordinate with § 897 FIRPTA — overlapping for partnerships with US real property
view.s1446F.ptp.broker: Broker (not buyer) handles withholding on sales of PTP units
view.s1446F.ptp.cure: No correction filed if broker withholds correctly per Notice 2018-29
view.s1446F.ptp.qualified: 'Qualified notice' required: PTP certifies % ECI to broker
view.s1446F.ptp.distributions: PTP distributions to foreign partners: § 1446(a) withholding (37% indiv / 21% corp)
view.s1446F.ptp.exemption: If PTP < 10% ECI: no § 1446(f) withholding on sales
view.s1446F.ptp.report: Form 1042-S issued by broker for withholding
view.s1446F.ptp.major_ptps: Major PTPs: Energy Transfer Partners, Enterprise Products, MPLX, etc.
view.s1446F.refund_note: Withholding exceeds actual tax liability. File for refund via Form 1040NR / 1120-F. Alternative: pre-closing withholding certificate (similar to § 1445 Form 8288-B) reduces withholding to actual tax. IRS has 90 days to respond — typically escrow held until issued.
view.s42.h1.title: // § 42 LIHTC
view.s42.hint.intro: 4% credit (bond-financed) OR 9% credit (competitive allocation by state HFA). 10-year credit period; 15-year compliance; 30+ year extended use agreement. Threshold tests: (1) 20-50 (≥ 20% units at ≤ 50% AMI), (2) 40-60 (≥ 40% at ≤ 60%), (3) post-2017 income averaging (≥ 40% at avg ≤ 60% AMI; units range 20-80%). Recapture 1/3 over 15 yrs on noncompliance. QCT / DDA bonus +30% qualified basis. Form 8609.
view.s42.str.9pct: 9% credit: NEW construction / substantial rehab (rehab ≥ $7K/unit + 10% basis)
view.s42.str.4pct: 4% credit: tax-exempt bond-financed (≥ 50% costs from bonds)
view.s42.str.15yr: 15-year compliance period: maintain affordability or recapture
view.s42.str.30yr: 30-year extended use: state agreement, includes early restrictions
view.s42.str.qap: State Qualified Allocation Plan (QAP): scoring for competitive 9%
view.s42.str.partner_partnership: Typical structure: 99.99% investor + 0.01% sponsor in Limited Partnership
view.s42.comp.next_available: Next Available Unit Rule (§ 42(g)(2)(D)): rent next available to qualified tenant if existing falls out of compliance
view.s42.comp.recertification: Annual tenant recertification — verify income still meets test
view.s42.comp.cure_period: 90-day cure period for noncompliance — fix before annual report
view.s42.comp.s42j: Annual Form 8609 + Schedule A by state HFA
view.s42.comp.recapture_pct: Recapture: 1/3 of credit claimed for each year out of compliance + interest
view.s42.comp.exception: Recapture exceptions: casualty, partner death, change of qualified basis (de minimis)
view.s42.comp.5_year: First 5 years: heavier penalties; years 11-15 lighter
view.s42.comp.exit_strategy: Exit strategy: year 15 dispose to sponsor / nonprofit + Right of First Refusal
view.s42.avg_note: Income averaging (post-2017 PATH Act): provides FLEXIBILITY to serve mixed-income populations. Average AMI ≤ 60% allows units up to 80% AMI to subsidize units at 20% AMI. Available for compliance period 2018+; required Treasury final regs Oct 2022.
view.s83.h1.title: // § 83 RESTRICTED PROPERTY
view.s83.hint.intro: Property received for services taxed at FMV WHEN VESTED (substantial risk of forfeiture lapses). § 83(b) election: tax at GRANT — locks in low FMV; starts capital gain clock. 30-day deadline; irrevocable. If forfeit BEFORE vest: no deduction for tax paid (cap loss only if § 83(b)). Common: founder stock, restricted stock awards (RSAs), profits interests (Rev. Proc. 93-27). RSUs: NOT § 83 (separate § 451 timing).
view.s83.adv.low_fmv: Lock in LOW grant FMV → ordinary income on $0-ish at grant (founder stock)
view.s83.adv.cap_gain: Starts capital gain holding period → LTCG (20%/15%/0%) on appreciation
view.s83.adv.shelter: Shelter all future appreciation as LTCG instead of ordinary on vest
view.s83.adv.amt_avoid: ISO context: avoid AMT preference item on exercise (combined w/ § 422)
view.s83.adv.partnership_capital: Profits interest: receive partnership interest, no ordinary income (Rev. Proc. 93-27 safe harbor)
view.s83.adv.estate_planning: Estate planning: lock in low basis NOW for gift / transfer at low value
view.s83.adv.public_company: Public company: low-vol stock = small risk; high-vol = significant risk if forfeit
view.s83.risk.tax_now: Tax PAID NOW even if stock never vests
view.s83.risk.no_refund: Forfeiture → NO refund of tax paid (sunk cost)
view.s83.risk.capital_loss: Forfeiture → only CAPITAL LOSS (limited $3K/yr against ordinary)
view.s83.risk.cash_flow: Cash flow: need cash to pay tax on illiquid stock
view.s83.risk.high_fmv: High FMV at grant: tax bill may exceed value
view.s83.risk.appraisal: 409A appraisal: PRE-FUNDING risk if FMV not defensible
view.s83.risk.30_days: 30-DAY deadline: certified mail + return receipt + email copy + accountant
view.s83.risk.late_election: Late election: NO RELIEF except mistake / duress; signed waiver insufficient
view.s83.mech.30_days: Within 30 days of GRANT (not vest) — postmark counts
view.s83.mech.statement: File: written statement (IRS sample) — name, SSN, property description, grant date, FMV, restrictions
view.s83.mech.copy_to_employer: Copy to: employer (W-2 reporting) + retain copy + attach to current-year return
view.s83.mech.payment: Pay tax at marginal rate × (FMV − price paid) × shares
view.s83.mech.cash_payment_option: Option: have employer withhold via additional W-2 wages + tax withholding
view.s83.mech.no_email: EMAIL only is NOT sufficient — must be physical mail (certified)
view.s83.mech.2015_reg_change: 2015 reg: no longer required to attach to current-year return — but recommended
view.s83.mech.address: IRS service center where you file 1040 (find at irs.gov)
view.s83.forfeit_note: Forfeited after § 83(b) election: ordinary income still recognized at grant. NO REFUND of tax paid. Only relief: capital loss equal to PRICE PAID (NOT tax basis) — limited to $3K/yr against ordinary. This is the major risk of § 83(b) — confirm vesting probability before electing.
view.s409A.h1.title: // § 409A NONQUAL DEFERRED COMP
view.s409A.hint.intro: Defers tax on comp not actually / constructively received. VIOLATION: ALL deferred amounts taxed in current year + 20% additional tax + underpayment interest at 1% above market rate. 6 permitted distribution events: separation, disability, death, time/schedule, change in control, unforeseeable emergency. Initial election: 30 days post-eligibility OR before year start. Subsequent deferrals: 1-yr advance + 5-yr push-out. Specified employee (public co): 6-month delay on separation pay. Form W-2 box 12 code Y/Z.
view.s409A.exc.short_term: Short-term deferral: paid within 2.5 months of vesting
view.s409A.exc.separation_safe_harbor: Separation pay: 2-yr cap + 2× FICA wages limit ($330K × 2 = $660K)
view.s409A.exc.stock_rights: Stock rights: at-the-money options + RSUs (settled when vest)
view.s409A.exc.contractor: Non-employee director / contractor with multiple unrelated customers
view.s409A.exc.welfare: Welfare benefit plans (group health, life, disability)
view.s409A.exc.foreign: Foreign plans of bona fide foreign nationals (limited carve-out)
view.s409A.dist.time: Specified time or schedule — must be objectively determinable
view.s409A.dist.cic: Change in control — § 409A specific definitions (not always M&A)
view.s409A.dist.emergency: Unforeseeable emergency — limited; no medical / casualty insurance
view.s409A.dist.specified_employee: SPECIFIED EMPLOYEE (public co): 6-month delay on separation distributions
view.s409A.viol.discount: Discounted stock options: strike < FMV = built-in deferred comp
view.s409A.viol.no_election: Failure to make timely initial election
view.s409A.viol.late_change: Late change of distribution timing without 1-yr advance + 5-yr push-out
view.s409A.viol.acceleration: Acceleration of distribution to event other than 6 permitted
view.s409A.viol.no_six_month: Specified employee separation pay distributed before 6-month delay
view.s409A.viol.cic_definition: Inaccurate change in control definition (must match § 409A regs)
view.s409A.viol.severance: Severance > safe harbor without 6-month delay (public co)
view.s409A.pen.all_deferred: ALL deferred amounts taxable in year of violation (not just current vested)
view.s409A.pen.interest: + Interest at underpayment rate + 1% from year of deferral
view.s409A.pen.california: California: 20% state + interest stacks on top (parallel state law)
view.s409A.pen.no_employee_relief: No relief for employee even if employer's fault
view.s409A.pen.correction: Notice 2010-6 + 2008-113: limited correction safe harbors (some violations)
view.s409A.pen.amended_return: Amended return required for corrected violations
view.s409A.pen.aggregation: Aggregation rules: all plans of same type aggregated for violation
view.s409A.violation_note: § 409A violation triggers ALL deferred amounts current taxation + 20% penalty + interest. Effective tax rate often EXCEEDS 60%. Consider Notice 2010-6 / 2008-113 correction safe harbors for operational failures. Document failure prevention via plan administration audit.
view.s457.h1.title: // § 457 GOVT / NONPROFIT DEFER COMP
view.s457.hint.intro: § 457(b) ELIGIBLE plan: $23,500 (2025) limit — SAME as § 401(k) but SEPARATE buckets, STACKABLE for combined $47K+ for nonprofit / gov't. Triple catch-up last 3-yr before normal retirement: up to 2× annual limit. § 457(f) INELIGIBLE: top-hat for nonprofits; no $$$ limit but VESTING (substantial risk of forfeiture) required for deferral. Gov't 457(b): rollover allowed; no § 72(t) early penalty. Tax-exempt 457(b): NO rollover; subject to CREDITORS of employer.
view.s457.stack.dual: Dual employee (gov't day job + nonprofit consulting): can defer FULL limit to BOTH
view.s457.stack.same_employer: Same employer: § 457(b) deferral SEPARATE from § 401(k) — both at full $23,500
view.s457.stack.catch_up: Catch-up available SEPARATELY on each — additional $7,500 (50+) or $11,250 (60-63)
view.s457.stack.no_double_catch: Triple OR age-50 catch-up — NOT BOTH in same year
view.s457.tcu.window: Last 3 years before normal retirement age (as defined in plan)
view.s457.tcu.calculation: Limit = MIN(annual limit, deferral underutilized in prior years)
view.s457.tcu.example: Example: 2025 limit $23,500; if underutilized $20K total in prior years → catch-up = $20K
view.s457.tcu.no_combine: Cannot combine with age 50+ catch-up in same year
view.s457.tcu.gov_only: Available in BOTH gov't and tax-exempt § 457(b)
view.s457.tcu.normal_retirement: Normal retirement age: per plan (typically 65, can be earlier)
view.s457.tcu.documentation: Document underutilized: track all prior years to plan year
view.s457.f_note: § 457(f) ineligible plan: tax at VEST not separation. Plan vesting carefully — accelerated vesting triggers tax even if no distribution. Also subject to § 409A (additional 20% + interest if violation). Common in nonprofit C-suite (university presidents, hospital CEOs, 501(c)(3)).
view.s30C.h1.title: // § 30C ALT. FUEL CHARGER
view.s30C.hint.intro: EV charger + alternative fuel infrastructure credit. Business: 30% (with wage/apprenticeship) up to $100K per item. Personal: 30% / $1,000 cap. IRA 2022 restriction: MUST be located in non-urban area OR low-income community census tract (8911 mapper). Construction begin before 1/1/2033; placed in service after 12/31/2022. § 6418 transferability for business; 20-yr carryforward. Form 8911.
view.s30C.elig.non_urban: Non-urban: NOT in Census Bureau urban area (cities + suburbs)
view.s30C.elig.lic: Low-income community: § 45D(e) Census tract — poverty rate ≥ 20% OR income ≤ 80% statewide
view.s30C.elig.either: EITHER non-urban OR low-income qualifies (not both required)
view.s30C.elig.address: Address-based: enter installation address to verify eligibility
view.s30C.elig.coordinates: Mapper uses geographic coordinates from US Census 2020 + BLS data
view.s30C.elig.50pct_residential: Residential property: 50%+ used for personal — separate $1K limit applies
view.s30C.elig.previously_eligible: Pre-2023: ALL locations eligible — IRA 2022 added geographic restriction
view.s30C.prop.ev_charger: EV chargers: Level 2 (residential), DC fast charging (commercial)
view.s30C.prop.bidirectional: V2G bidirectional charging: eligible if meets criteria
view.s30C.prop.cost_basis: Includes: equipment + installation labor + permits + ancillary costs
view.s30C.loc_note: Location FAIL: IRA 2022 restricts § 30C to non-urban areas + low-income communities only. Pre-2023 installations may qualify under older rules. Verify via 8911 mapper at irs.gov. Address-based lookup uses Census 2020 boundaries — borderline addresses may be eligible.
view.s45W.h1.title: // § 45W COMMERCIAL CLEAN VEHICLE
view.s45W.hint.intro: $7,500 (≤ 14,000 lbs GVWR) OR $40,000 (14K+ GVWR — heavy duty). LESSER of: (1) 15% basis (30% if BEV/FCV) OR (2) incremental cost over comparable ICE vehicle. NO income limit, NO MSRP cap, NO domestic assembly — the 'lease loophole' bypassing § 30D limits. Commercial / business use only. Tax-exempts: § 6417 direct pay; taxable: § 6418 transferability. Lease 'loophole': dealer claims § 45W → passes through as lease discount. Form 8936.
view.s45W.elig.qualifying: Qualifying vehicle: BEV / FCV / PHEV with battery ≥ 15 kWh (or 7 kWh light duty)
view.s45W.elig.taxpayer: Acquired by taxpayer for USE OR LEASE
view.s45W.elig.business: USED in TRADE OR BUSINESS (Schedule C, F, Form 1120, etc.)
view.s45W.elig.no_minerals: NO critical minerals + battery components requirements
view.s45W.elig.no_resale: NOT acquired for resale (dealer inventory excluded)
view.s45W.lease.mechanism: Mechanism: leasing company (dealer / captive finance) claims § 45W
view.s45W.lease.pass_through: Dealer passes credit to lessee via reduced monthly payment or down payment credit
view.s45W.lease.bypass_30d: Lessee bypasses ALL § 30D limits: income, MSRP, assembly, minerals/components
view.s45W.lease.kia_hyundai: Particularly used by Kia / Hyundai (assembled in Korea — failed § 30D NA test)
view.s45W.lease.high_income: Used by HIGH-INCOME consumers who fail § 30D MAGI limit
view.s45W.lease.lobby_concerns: UAW + congressional concerns; some attempted closure but unchanged
view.s45W.lease.audit_risk: Watch IRS audit — must be GENUINE lease (not disguised sale)
view.s45W.lease_note: Lease structure: dealer (lessor) claims § 45W → passes through as discount. Bypasses § 30D income / MSRP / NA-assembly limits. Major route for HIGH-INCOME consumers and foreign-built vehicles. Verify genuine lease structure (not disguised sale) to maintain legitimate § 45W eligibility.
view.s47.h1.title: // § 47 HISTORIC REHAB
view.s47.hint.intro: 20% credit on Qualified Rehabilitation Expenditures (QREs) for CERTIFIED HISTORIC structures. TCJA 2017: 10% credit (pre-1936 non-certified) ELIMINATED. 5-year vesting: claim 4% per year over 5 years (vs prior single-year). NPS / SHPO 3-part app: Part 1 evaluation of significance, Part 2 description of rehab, Part 3 certification. Substantial rehab test: QREs > greater of $5K or building basis. 5-yr recapture on sale or change of use. § 6418 transferability. Form 3468.
view.s47.qre.depreciable: Depreciable structural improvements: walls, floors, roofs, plumbing, electric, HVAC
view.s47.qre.interior: Interior finish work to historic standard
view.s47.qre.exterior: Exterior preservation to historic standard (per Secretary of Interior Standards)
view.s47.qre.no_acquisition: EXCLUDES: acquisition cost of building, land
view.s47.qre.no_landscape: EXCLUDES: landscaping, site work, demolition (with some exceptions)
view.s47.qre.no_developer_above: EXCLUDES: developer fees in excess of arm's-length
view.s47.process.part1: Part 1 — Evaluation of Significance: confirms certified historic structure
view.s47.process.part2: Part 2 — Description of Rehabilitation: review rehabilitation plans BEFORE work
view.s47.process.part3: Part 3 — Request for Certification of Completed Work: AFTER project finished
view.s47.process.shpo: SHPO (State Historic Preservation Officer): initial reviewer in most states
view.s47.process.nps: NPS (National Park Service): final approving authority
view.s47.process.fee: User fees: $0-$5,000+ depending on project size
view.s47.process.timeline: Typical timeline: 6-9 months pre-construction; 6 months post-completion
view.s47.process.appeal: Appeals: 30 days to NPS within Washington
view.s47.rec.triggers: Triggers: sale, change of use, demolition, change in historic character
view.s47.rec.exception: Casualty + restoration: no recapture if restored to historic standard
view.s47.rec.transfer_recapture: Transferability: recapture stays with ORIGINAL TAXPAYER
view.s47.rec.exempt_lease: Exempt lease > 50% disqualifies (must rent at market to non-exempt)
view.s47.rec.basis_reduction: Basis reduction: 100% of credit reduces building basis
view.s47.subst_note: Substantial rehabilitation test FAIL: QREs must exceed greater of $5K OR building basis (excluding land) over 24-60 month measuring period. Phased work over multiple years may require careful planning to meet test.
view.s51.h1.title: // § 51 WOTC
view.s51.hint.intro: Up to $2,400 per new hire from 9 targeted groups. $9,600 enhanced for qualified veterans with service-connected disability. 40% on first $6K wages (25% if < 400 hrs but ≥ 120); $0 if < 120 hrs. 50% on 2nd-year wages for long-term TANF. Form 8850 pre-screening within 28 days of hire + Form 5884 + Form 3800. Expired Dec 31 2025 — historically renewed; awaiting congressional extension.
view.s51.grp.tanf: TANF recipient (short-term 9 mo OR long-term 18 mo)
view.s51.grp.snap: SNAP recipient (food stamps for 3 of last 15 months)
view.s51.grp.unemployed: Long-term unemployed (27+ consecutive weeks; verified Form 9061)
view.s51.grp.dcr: Designated community resident (16-39 in empowerment zone)
view.s51.proc.prescreen: Pre-screening: Form 8850 SIGNED BY EMPLOYEE on day of job offer
view.s51.proc.28days: FILE Form 8850 + ETA 9061 with State Workforce Agency WITHIN 28 DAYS of hire
view.s51.proc.swa_review: SWA reviews + certifies (free) or rejects within 60-90 days
view.s51.proc.certified: Once certified, claim on Form 5884 + Form 3800 GBC
view.s51.proc.deny: Denial: no appeal, but may retry with different group
view.s51.proc.missed: Missed 28-day: NO CREDIT for that employee — strict deadline
view.s51.proc.documentation: Documentation: DD-214 (veteran), public assistance records, ICE arrest record
view.s51.proc.thirdparty: Third-party services: 0.5-2% of credit fee, manage 28-day deadline
view.s51.late_note: Missed 28-day Form 8850 deadline → NO CREDIT for this employee. Strict deadline, no retroactive cure. Set up dedicated WOTC process or use 3rd-party service (Walton, ADP, Equifax) to manage automatically on every hire.
view.s25E.h1.title: // § 25E USED CLEAN VEHICLE
view.s25E.hint.intro: 30% of sale price OR $4,000 — LOWER applies. Sale price ≤ $25,000. Vehicle must be at least 2 model years older than current. MAGI limit: $75K single / $112.5K HoH / $150K MFJ. First resale only (not flippers). Qualified dealer required (registered with IRS Energy Credits Online). Buyer transfers credit to dealer at POS. One-per-three-year limit for buyer. Form 8936. NO domestic assembly / minerals / battery components requirements — unlike § 30D.
view.s25E.elig.price: Sale price ≤ $25,000 (includes options, dealer prep, document fees)
view.s25E.elig.age: Model year at least 2 years older than current year
view.s25E.elig.first_resale: FIRST resale after original sale (not flippers / repeat sellers)
view.s25E.elig.dealer: Sold by IRS-registered dealer (NOT private party)
view.s25E.elig.battery_phev: PHEV: battery ≥ 7 kWh; BEV: no battery min stated; FCV: any
view.s25E.elig.no_personal_use_before: Not used by purchasing taxpayer before
view.s25E.elig.income: MAGI: $75K / $112.5K / $150K (CURRENT or PRIOR yr — pick lower)
view.s25E.elig.no_domestic: NO domestic assembly / minerals / battery components requirement
view.s25E.tx.discount: Discount applied at POS — dealer registers via IRS Energy Credits Online
view.s25E.tx.dealer_advance: Dealer receives advance payment from IRS (Form 8300)
view.s25E.tx.refundable_effect: Refundable effect: even if no tax liability, buyer gets discount
view.s25E.tx.repay_excess: Buyer repays on next return if MAGI exceeds limit
view.s25E.tx.no_clawback_safe_harbor: No clawback if buyer not aware of excess MAGI
view.s25E.tx.dealer_3rd_party: 3rd-party finance ok; dealer must process credit transfer
view.s25E.ex.prime: Used Prius Prime PHEV (2020, $18K): credit min($4,000, $5,400) = $4,000
view.s25E.ex.ford_lightning: Used Ford Lightning (2022, $30K): NO CREDIT — over $25K cap
view.s25E.ex.mach_e: Used Mach-E (2022, $26K): NO CREDIT — over $25K cap
view.s25E.ex.private_party: Private party sale (no dealer): NO CREDIT — must be qualified dealer
view.s25E.over_price_note: Sale price exceeds $25,000 cap. Negotiate price down or look at lower-priced inventory. For higher-priced new vehicles, consider § 30D (new) or § 45W (commercial / lease loophole) instead. § 25E is targeted at affordability for used EV market.
view.s460.h1.title: // § 460 LONG-TERM CONTRACTS
view.s460.hint.intro: PCM (Percentage-of-Completion) required for contracts NOT completed in same year started. Cost-to-cost method: revenue = costs incurred / total estimated costs × contract price. Exceptions: small contractor ≤ $30M avg gross receipts + home construction + ≤ 2-yr contracts → Completed-Contract Method (CCM) or cash. 10% method: defer all income/expense until 10% costs incurred. Look-back: Form 8697 — compute actual vs estimated profit; pay / receive interest on differences. § 460(e) certain residential 70/30 split.
view.s460.req.default: Default: PCM required for contracts not completed in same year started
view.s460.req.small_2yr: Small contractor (≤ $30M avg gross receipts) + ≤ 2-yr contract: CCM allowed
view.s460.req.contracts: Manufacturing contracts: include in PCM if unique items or > 12-mo production
view.s460.req.services_only: Services-only contracts: NOT § 460 (use general accounting methods)
view.s460.cost.percent: % Complete = Costs incurred to date / Total estimated costs
view.s460.cost.current: Current year revenue = Cumulative revenue − Prior years revenue
view.s460.cost.profit: Gross profit = Revenue recognized − Costs incurred this year
view.s460.cost.estimate_change: Estimate change: cumulative catch-up in year of change
view.s460.cost.loss_recognize: Anticipated loss: recognize FULLY in current year (no spreading)
view.s460.cost.excludes: Excludes: warranty + post-completion administrative costs from PCM
view.s460.cost.subcontracts: Subcontracts: prime contractor still uses PCM on subcontracted portion
view.s460.look.purpose: Purpose: prevent abuse of estimates to defer / accelerate tax
view.s460.look.compare: Compare actual final profit to estimated profit at each year
view.s460.look.under: Underpaid tax: pay interest TO IRS
view.s460.look.over: Overpaid tax: receive interest FROM IRS
view.s460.look.exception: Exception: small contracts ($1M cumulative, < 2-yr) — opt-out elective
view.s460.look.s460_h: § 460(h): Look-back interest method exemption for delayed reapplication
view.s460.ccm_note: CCM (Completed-Contract Method) elected: defer ALL revenue + costs until contract done. Benefit: avoid PCM cash flow on long projects with milestone delays. Cost: no early income; AMT-PCM may still apply for alternative minimum tax purposes.
view.s467.h1.title: // § 467 DEFERRED / STEPPED RENT
view.s467.hint.intro: Required for rental property agreements ≥ $250K total rent + deferred / front-loaded / stepped rents. Constant Rental Accrual (CRA): tax follows economic accrual, not cash payments. Time-value-of-money imputed interest applied. Disqualified leaseback OR lease term > 75% useful life triggers harsher CRA mandatory. Forms: Schedule E (real estate) / Schedule K (partnership) + § 467 statement. Aligns lessor + lessee timing.
view.s467.label.useful: Lease term as % of useful life
view.s467.app.increasing: Stepped rent: increasing in amount across periods
view.s467.app.prepaid: Prepaid rent: amount not for current period
view.s467.app.long_term: Long-term lease: > 75% of property useful life triggers CRA
view.s467.app.public_lease: Pub. lease (LPI under state law): § 467 may not apply
view.s467.cra.calc: Calculate: present-value level annuity over lease term, AFR applied
view.s467.cra.report: Report this LEVEL amount per year regardless of payments
view.s467.cra.imputed: Difference between CRA and cash payment = IMPUTED INTEREST (separate income / deduction)
view.s467.cra.lessor: Lessor: rent income at CRA; interest income on imputed
view.s467.cra.lessee: Lessee: rent expense at CRA; interest expense on imputed (subject to § 163(j))
view.s467.cra.gaap: GAAP straight-line lease accounting often aligns w/ CRA (with adjustments)
view.s467.cra.disqualified: 'Disqualified' lease (sale-leaseback / tax-avoidance): CRA forced even if not stepped
view.s467.ex.rent_increases: Annual 3% rent increases — typical stepped pattern (CPI escalation)
view.s467.ex.tenant_improvements: Tenant improvement allowance + free rent month: § 467 prepaid analysis
view.s467.ex.percentage_rent: Percentage rent (retail): § 467 if minimum + sales-based escalators
view.s467.ex.balloon: Balloon payment at end: CRA forces level accrual; large interest imputed
view.s467.ex.holiday_period: Initial rent holiday (60-90 days): defer + spread on CRA
view.s467.ex.master_lease: Master lease + ancillary: each component analyzed separately
view.s467.ex.cam_charges: CAM (Common Area Maintenance): typically excluded from § 467 (operating exp)
view.s467.ex.gross_up: Gross-up rent provisions: treated as additional rent obligation
view.s467.long_note: Long-term lease (> 75% useful life) OR sale-leaseback: CRA mandatory regardless of rent pattern. Plan for level accrual + imputed interest income / expense. Coordinate with § 163(j) interest limit on lessee side. AFR rate impacts deferral cost significantly.
view.s79.h1.title: // § 79 GROUP TERM LIFE
view.s79.hint.intro: First $50,000 of employer-provided group term life coverage = TAX-FREE. Coverage over $50K: IMPUTED INCOME based on IRS Uniform Premium Table I (Notice 2021-65). Formula: (Coverage − $50K) / 1,000 × IRS rate per $1K × months covered. Reported W-2 Box 12 code C. Subject to Soc Sec + Medicare (but NOT income tax withholding). Discrimination rules: if plan favors key employees, full coverage taxable to them (no $50K exclusion).
view.s79.h2.uniform: Table I — Uniform Premiums per $1K per month (Notice 2021-65)
view.s79.disc.test_e: Eligibility test: cannot favor key employees in coverage
view.s79.disc.test_b: Benefits test: cannot favor key employees in benefits
view.s79.disc.consequence: Discriminatory plan → KEY EMPLOYEES taxable on FULL coverage cost (no $50K excl)
view.s79.disc.actual_cost: Actual cost = GREATER of actual premium OR Table I uniform cost
view.s79.disc.cure: Cure: equalize coverage / benefits or carve out keys to separate plan
view.s79.spec.spousal: Spousal coverage ≤ $2,000: de minimis — no income
view.s79.spec.dependent: Dependent coverage ≤ $2,000: de minimis — no income
view.s79.spec.permanent: Permanent benefits (cash value): full value taxable in year of acquisition
view.s79.spec.retired: Retired employees: continued coverage may still be § 79 subject
view.s79.spec.disabled: Disabled employees: tax-free up to limit
view.s79.spec.beneficiary: Beneficiary received death benefit: tax-free under § 101(a)
view.s79.spec.carry_over: Coverage continuation: months past employment count
view.s79.spec.cafeteria: § 125 election to pay premiums pre-tax: reduces imputed income
view.s79.disc_note: Discriminatory plan + key employee: lose $50K exclusion → FULL coverage cost taxable (GREATER of actual premium or Table I uniform cost). Cure: carve out keys to separate plan OR equalize coverage / benefits across all employees.
view.s105.h1.title: // § 105 ACCIDENT + HEALTH
view.s105.hint.intro: § 105(a): Amounts under employer accident / health plan EXCLUDED from gross income. § 105(b): Medical care reimbursement — full exclusion. § 105(c): permanent loss of bodily function payments — excluded. § 105(h): SELF-INSURED medical plans — discriminatory amounts TAXABLE to highly compensated employees (HCE). § 162(l) SE health deduction: SE persons + 2% S-corp owners deduct ABOVE-THE-LINE. HRA / HSA / FSA use § 105 for tax-free reimbursement.
view.s105.sub.a: § 105(a): amounts under employer plan — EXCLUDED from gross income (default rule)
view.s105.sub.b: § 105(b): medical care reimbursement up to actual expenses — full exclusion
view.s105.sub.c: § 105(c): permanent loss / disfigurement payments — excluded (regardless of wages)
view.s105.sub.d: § 105(d): wage / salary replacement (sick pay) — INCLUDED in income
view.s105.sub.h: § 105(h): self-insured plans discrimination — HCE taxable on excess benefits
view.s105.sub.s125: § 125 cafeteria plan + § 105: pre-tax salary reduction → reimbursement → no income
view.s105.disc.eligibility: Eligibility test: 70%+ non-HCE OR 80%+ benefits non-HCE
view.s105.disc.benefits: Benefits test: same benefits available to all participants
view.s105.disc.violations: Violations: HCE taxable on 'excess reimbursements' + Soc Sec
view.s105.disc.cure: Cure: equalize benefits across HCE / non-HCE
view.s105.disc.formula: Excess = (HCE benefit − average non-HCE benefit) for that HCE
view.s105.disc.timing: Test applied on year-end basis (last day of year)
view.s105.disc.insurance_carrier_safe: Insured plans NOT subject to § 105(h) — bypass via insurance
view.s105.disc.s162m: Public companies: + § 162(m) $1M deduction limit on HCE comp
view.s105.l.who: Eligible: sole prop, partner, 2%+ S-corp owner, member of multimember LLC
view.s105.l.spouse_dependents: Includes premiums for self, spouse, dependents, kids < 27 yrs
view.s105.l.limit: Limited to earned income from business (cannot create loss)
view.s105.l.no_em_plan: NOT eligible if employee + spouse employee plan available
view.s105.l.s_corp_w2: S-corp owners: premiums must be ON W-2 (Box 1) to be deductible by shareholder
view.s105.l.no_se: Deduction does NOT reduce SE tax (separate computation)
view.s105.l.aca_marketplace: Marketplace coverage premiums qualify (must self-fund or have employer not offer)
view.s105.types.hra: HRA (Health Reimbursement Arrangement): employer-funded, reimburses medical
view.s105.types.qsehra: QSEHRA (Qualified Small Employer HRA): small biz ≤ 50 employees
view.s105.types.ichra: ICHRA (Individual Coverage HRA): premium reimbursement for individual plans
view.s105.types.hsa: HSA (Health Savings Account): individual + employer combined, requires HDHP
view.s105.types.fsa: FSA (Flexible Spending Account): salary reduction, use-it-or-lose-it
view.s105.types.dcfsa: Dependent Care FSA: $5K limit, child care + elderly care
view.s105.types.cobra: COBRA premium subsidy: § 6432 + ARPA temporary subsidies
view.s105.types.cba: Cafeteria Plan § 125: choice between cash + tax-free benefits
view.s105.disc_note: § 105(h) discrimination: excess reimbursements TAXABLE to HCE. Includes both regular + Social Security earnings. Cure: equalize benefits across HCE / non-HCE OR switch to INSURED plan (not subject to § 105(h)). Common issue in self-funded health plans of large employers.
view.s269.h1.title: // § 269 ANTI-ABUSE ACQUISITION
view.s269.hint.intro: IRS DISALLOWS deductions, credits, allowances from acquired corp if PRIMARY purpose = tax avoidance. § 269(a)(1): person acquires CONTROL (50%) of corp. § 269(a)(2): corp acquires PROPERTY w/ carryover basis. Burden: IRS (initially), then taxpayer to rebut. Overlap: § 382 NOL limit, § 384 SRLY, § 7701(o) economic substance. § 269 PRE-EMPTS even when § 382 also limits — covers SRLY-style and entity-level transactions broader than § 382 ownership change.
view.s269.test.tax_motive: Tax motive must be MORE THAN 50% of purpose (primary)
view.s269.test.business_purpose: Business purpose can SAVE acquisition even if tax savings significant
view.s269.test.shell_corps: Shell corps + dormant targets = high § 269 risk
view.s269.test.large_nol: Large NOL with no operations = clear § 269 territory
view.s269.test.bargain_purchase: Bargain purchase below FMV due to deferred tax assets = indicia of tax motive
view.s269.test.factual: Fact-intensive: written documents, internal memoranda, board minutes scrutinized
view.s269.coord.s382: § 382: mechanical limit on NOL usage post-ownership change (annual ATE × LT rate)
view.s269.coord.s384: § 384: SRLY rule limits pre-acquisition losses against post-acquisition built-in gains
view.s269.coord.s269_overlap: § 269 PRE-EMPTS even when § 382 also applies — broader anti-abuse
view.s269.coord.s7701o: § 7701(o) Economic Substance: codified — meaningful change required + non-tax purpose
view.s269.coord.s482: § 482 transfer pricing: parallel anti-abuse for related-party transactions
view.s269.coord.s367: § 367: outbound transfers anti-abuse for foreign acquisitions
view.s269.coord.s7704: § 7704 PTP rules: prevent corporate shell using partnership structure
view.s269.coord.s356: § 356 (boot in reorg) coordinates with § 269 in tax-free reorgs
view.s269.cons.disallow_nol: DISALLOW NOL carryover (in whole or part — discretion of Commissioner)
view.s269.cons.partial: Partial disallowance possible — depends on facts
view.s269.cons.discretion: Commissioner has broad discretion to disallow some but not all
view.s269.applies_note: § 269 likely applies: PRIMARY purpose tax avoidance + no business purpose. IRS may disallow NOL, credits, deductions in whole or part. Documents pre-acquisition: rebut with business purpose evidence (synergies, market expansion, talent acquisition). Pre-clearance: consider PLR before completing transaction.
view.s1239.h1.title: // § 1239 RELATED PARTY SALE
view.s1239.hint.intro: Gain on sale to RELATED PARTY = ORDINARY INCOME (not capital gain). Property must be DEPRECIABLE in hands of recipient (not seller). Related person: > 50% controlled entity, family members, partnerships, trusts, controlled corps. Purpose: prevents conversion of ordinary income (depreciation recapture eligible) to LTCG via sale to controlled buyer who then takes new depreciation basis. Always recharacterize as ordinary. Form 4797 + Schedule D.
view.s1239.label.capital: Held as capital asset by seller?
view.s1239.rel.corp_corp: Corporation + corporation: members of controlled group (> 50% common ownership)
view.s1239.rel.partnership_ind: Partnership + partner: any partner having any partnership interest
view.s1239.rel.trust_grantor: Trust + grantor: any trust where grantor / beneficiary related
