view.s1239.rel.family_def: Family: spouse, ancestors, lineal descendants, siblings (full / half blood)
view.s1239.rel.constructive_267: § 267 constructive ownership rules apply (family + entity + options)
view.s1239.ex.individual_corp: Individual sells equipment (capital asset to her) to her 100% S-corp → § 1239 ordinary
view.s1239.ex.corp_subsidiary: Parent sells building to 60% subsidiary → § 1239 ordinary on gain
view.s1239.ex.partnership_partner: Partnership sells truck to controlling partner → § 1239 ordinary
view.s1239.ex.family: Father sells rental property (depreciable in son's hands) to son → § 1239 ordinary
view.s1239.ex.no_depreciation: Sale of land (NOT depreciable) to controlled entity → § 1239 N/A — LTCG OK
view.s1239.ex.investment_to_personal: Sale to spouse for personal use (not depreciable) → § 1239 N/A
view.s1239.ex.s_corp_owners: Two S-corps with same 100% owner sell to each other → both § 1239 + § 267
view.s1239.ex.basis_to_buyer: Buyer's basis = purchase price (full step-up for depreciation purposes)
view.s1239.coord.s267: § 267 disallows LOSS on related-party sale (separate from § 1239 ordinary characterization)
view.s1239.coord.s1245: § 1245 recapture: ordinary up to depreciation; § 1239 stacks AFTER recapture
view.s1239.coord.s1250: § 1250 recapture (real estate): also ordinary; § 1239 stacks on top of any remaining cap gain
view.s1239.coord.s1031: § 1031 like-kind exchange: § 1239 holding period rules — must hold 2+ yrs after exchange to avoid
view.s1239.coord.installment: § 453 installment method: § 1239 applies to FULL gain even if reported via installment
view.s1239.coord.no_loss: § 1239 doesn't apply to LOSSES — losses limited / disallowed by § 267 instead
view.s1239.coord.character_election: No election to opt-out — § 1239 mandatory if conditions met
view.s1239.coord.s453_2yr: § 453 2-yr resale rule: related buyer can't resell within 2 yrs to outsiders without acceleration
view.s1239.applies_note: § 1239 forces ORDINARY rate (up to 37%) instead of LTCG (20%). Loss of preferential rate on gain. To avoid: sell to UNRELATED buyer (may include LLC where ownership reduced < 50%), OR distribute property to shareholder first then sale outside related-party rules.
view.s7701o.h1.title: // § 7701(o) ECONOMIC SUBSTANCE
view.s7701o.hint.intro: Economic substance doctrine CODIFIED 2010 (Health Care Act). Transaction has economic substance only if BOTH: (1) meaningful change in economic position + (2) substantial non-tax purpose. CONJUNCTIVE — both required. Violations: § 6662(b)(6) 20% accuracy penalty (40% if undisclosed); STRICT LIABILITY (no reasonable cause defense). Targets: tax shelters, listed transactions, abusive transactions. Pre-2010: common law 'two-prong' was disjunctive in some circuits, conjunctive in others.
view.s7701o.t.objective: OBJECTIVE prong: meaningful change in economic position — pre-tax profit potential
view.s7701o.t.subjective: SUBJECTIVE prong: substantial non-tax purpose (business / personal motive)
view.s7701o.t.both: BOTH required — pre-2010 some circuits used disjunctive (either / or)
view.s7701o.t.profit_substantial: Pre-tax profit must be SUBSTANTIAL relative to TAX BENEFIT
view.s7701o.t.financial_accounting: Financial accounting purpose alone DOES NOT count as substantial non-tax
view.s7701o.t.aggregate_or_separate: Aggregate or separately analyze — Commissioner discretion
view.s7701o.t.taxpayer_burden: Taxpayer burden of proof on both prongs
view.s7701o.t.relevant: Doctrine RELEVANT to transaction (not all transactions)
view.s7701o.pen.20pct: 20% on understatement attributable to economic substance violation
view.s7701o.pen.strict: STRICT LIABILITY: § 6664(c)(2) — no 'reasonable cause' defense for § 7701(o)
view.s7701o.pen.contrast: Contrast: regular § 6662 penalty has reasonable cause defense
view.s7701o.pen.disclosure: Disclosure via Form 8275: lowers penalty from 40% to 20%
view.s7701o.pen.qualified_opinion: Qualified opinion (more-likely-than-not) does NOT avoid § 7701(o) penalty
view.s7701o.pen.return_preparer: Return preparer penalty § 6694 applies for 'unreasonable position'
view.s7701o.pen.no_settlement: No settlement / amnesty programs for § 7701(o) violations historically
view.s7701o.case.gregory: Gregory v. Helvering (1935): foundation case — 'business purpose' required
view.s7701o.case.frank_lyon: Frank Lyon (1978): sale-leaseback substance over form
view.s7701o.case.compaq_castle: Compaq v. Comm'r (5th Cir. 2002): foreign tax credit shelter struck
view.s7701o.case.coltec: Coltec Industries (Fed. Cir. 2006): two-prong test articulated
view.s7701o.case.son_of_boss: 'Son of BOSS' shelters: 2000s wave struck down by economic substance doctrine
view.s7701o.case.castle_harbor: Castle Harbor (CA-3 2009): partnership tax shelter rebuffed
view.s7701o.case.salem_financial: Salem Financial (2014): UK / US dividend stripping shelter
view.s7701o.fail_note: Transaction FAILS economic substance: TAX BENEFITS DISALLOWED + 20-40% STRICT LIABILITY penalty (no reasonable cause defense). Disclosure (Form 8275) reduces penalty 40% → 20%. Verify BOTH prongs PRE-transaction with qualified tax opinion + contemporaneous documentation of business purpose.
view.s106.h1.title: // § 106 EMPLOYER HEALTH COVERAGE
view.s106.hint.intro: Employer contributions to accident / health plans EXCLUDED from employee gross income. Includes: health insurance premiums, HSA, HRA, vision / dental. 2%+ S-corp shareholders: NOT excluded — must INCLUDE on W-2 Box 1 (exception: can claim § 162(l) SE health deduction). § 125 cafeteria plan + § 106: pre-tax salary contribution. ACA mandate: employer-shared responsibility for > 50 FTE. NOT excluded: domestic partner coverage (unless dependent), wellness program incentives, gym memberships.
view.s106.scope.hsa: HSA contributions (§ 223): excluded from income + FICA
view.s106.scope.dental_vision: Dental + vision: included in 'accident / health' broadly
view.s106.scope.long_term_care: Long-term care insurance (§ 7702B): excluded up to age limits
view.s106.scope.cobra: Employer-paid COBRA premiums for terminated employees: excluded
view.s106.scope.same_sex: Same-sex spousal coverage: excluded post-Windsor (2013) / Obergefell (2015)
view.s106.scope.dp_excluded: Domestic partner (non-spouse): generally NOT § 106 unless tax dependent
view.s106.scope.opposite_sex_cohabitant: Opposite-sex cohabitant: not § 106; tax dependent test applies
view.s106.exc.2_pct: 2%+ S-corp shareholders: NOT § 106 — must include premiums on W-2 Box 1
view.s106.exc.partners: Partners + LLC members: NOT § 106 — must include premiums on Schedule K-1
view.s106.exc.s162l: BUT § 162(l) above-the-line deduction available to all SE persons + 2%+ S-corp
view.s106.exc.kids_27: PPACA: kids ≤ 27 covered tax-free under § 106 (even if not tax dependent)
view.s106.exc.dp_dependent: Domestic partner: § 106 if qualifies as § 152 tax dependent (income + support tests)
view.s106.exc.dp_imputed: Otherwise: domestic partner premiums → IMPUTED INCOME on W-2 Box 1 / FICA
view.s106.exc.retirees: Retiree health coverage: § 106 if structured as continuing employer obligation
view.s106.exc.wellness: Wellness program incentives (cash, gym): NOT § 106 — included as wages
view.s106.cad.tcja_repealed: 40% excise tax on high-cost employer plans REPEALED by Further Consolidated Appropriations Act 2020
view.s106.cad.was_2020: Was scheduled to take effect 2020 — never enforced
view.s106.cad.replaced: Replaced by: Medical Device Tax repeal + Cadillac tax repeal + Health Insurance Tax repeal
view.s106.cad.deduction_unlimited: Therefore: employer health deduction remains UNLIMITED (subject to § 162 reasonableness)
view.s106.cad.aca_state: Some state-level cost-sharing taxes remain (CA, NY, MA)
view.s106.cad.future_pdf: Future: § 105(h) discrimination + non-discrimination still apply for self-insured plans
view.s106.hsa.triple: Triple-tax-advantaged: contribution + growth + qualified medical withdrawal all tax-free
view.s106.hsa.hdhp_required: Requires Qualified High Deductible Plan (HDHP): 2025 min deductible $1,650/$3,300; max OOP $8,300/$16,600
view.s106.hsa.limit_2025: 2025 contribution limit: $4,300 individual / $8,550 family + $1,000 catch-up age 55+
view.s106.hsa.employer_employee: Employer + employee contributions COMBINED count to limit
view.s106.hsa.cafeteria: § 125 cafeteria pre-tax: salary reduction contribution to HSA
view.s106.hsa.investment: Investment growth: tax-free if used for qualified medical (Vanguard, Fidelity HSAs)
view.s106.hsa.retirement: Post-65: withdrawal for non-medical = INCOME (no penalty); for medical = tax-free
view.s106.hsa.medicare_no: Medicare enrollment ENDS HSA contributions (incl. spousal HSA)
view.s106.hsa.fsa_limit: FSA ≠ HSA: $3,300 FSA limit (2025); use-it-or-lose-it + carryover $660
view.s106.s_corp_note: 2%+ S-corp shareholder: § 106 EXCLUSION NOT AVAILABLE. Premiums INCLUDED on W-2 Box 1. BUT claim § 162(l) above-the-line deduction (Schedule 1) for SE health insurance — full income offset BUT no FICA reduction. Subject to earned-income limitation.
view.s1015.h1.title: // § 1015 GIFT BASIS
view.s1015.hint.intro: CARRYOVER basis: donee takes donor's adjusted basis. Holding period TACKS from donor's. Dual basis rule: if FMV at gift < donor's basis: GAIN uses donor's basis; LOSS uses FMV at gift. 'NO GAIN / NO LOSS' zone: between FMV at gift and donor's basis. § 1015(d)(6) gift tax adjustment: add portion of gift tax attributable to net appreciation. Spousal gift: § 1041 carryover basis + no gift tax (with elections). Compared to § 1014: death gives FULL STEP-UP to FMV (gift loses this).
view.s1015.h2.dual_basis: Dual basis rule (loss / gain different basis)
view.s1015.db.appreciation: If FMV at gift ≥ donor's basis: STANDARD carryover (single basis = donor's basis)
view.s1015.db.depreciation: If FMV at gift < donor's basis: DUAL BASIS applies
view.s1015.db.gain_basis: FOR GAIN: use donor's basis (no recognition of pre-gift loss)
view.s1015.db.loss_basis: FOR LOSS: use FMV at gift (no recognition of pre-gift depreciation)
view.s1015.db.zone: 'No-gain / no-loss zone': sale price between FMV at gift and donor's basis
view.s1015.db.example: Example: donor's basis $100K, FMV at gift $80K; sale at $90K = NO GAIN / NO LOSS
view.s1015.gtb.formula: Adjustment = gift tax × (net appreciation / net gift)
view.s1015.gtb.appreciation: Net appreciation = FMV at gift − donor's basis
view.s1015.gtb.net_gift: Net gift = FMV at gift − annual exclusion used ($19K 2025)
view.s1015.gtb.who_paid: Applied when DONOR PAID gift tax (not donee)
view.s1015.gtb.add_to_basis: Adjusted basis = donor's basis + gift tax adjustment
view.s1015.gtb.cannot_exceed_fmv: Adjusted basis cannot EXCEED FMV at gift (no further step-up)
view.s1015.gtb.example: Example: donor's basis $50K, FMV $200K, annual excl $19K, gift tax $30K. Adjustment = $30K × ($150K / $181K) = $24,862
view.s1015.plan.death_better_appreciated: DEATH is BETTER for highly appreciated assets (full step-up vs carryover)
view.s1015.plan.gift_better_depreciated: GIFT is BETTER for depreciating assets (no loss waste at carryover basis)
view.s1015.plan.annual_exclusion: Use annual exclusion ($19K 2025) for gift tax-free transfers
view.s1015.plan.gst: GST tax: separate exemption for generation-skipping transfers
view.s1015.plan.529: § 529 plans: special 5-yr forward gift treatment (5× annual exclusion)
view.s1015.plan.med_education: Direct medical / educational payments: NOT counted (no $19K limit)
view.s1015.plan.unified_credit: 2026 sunset: unified credit may drop ~$7M unless extended
view.s1015.no_gain_loss_note: Sale falls in 'no-gain / no-loss zone' between FMV at gift and donor's basis. No gain or loss recognized — donor's pre-gift unrealized loss is PERMANENTLY LOST. Plan accordingly: hold or sell at lower price for tax loss benefit.
view.s444.h1.title: // § 444 FISCAL YEAR ELECTION
view.s444.hint.intro: Partnerships, S-corps, and Personal Service Corps (PSCs) generally must use CALENDAR year (§ 441). § 444 election: up to 3-month deferral (e.g., FY ending Sep 30, Oct 31, Nov 30). Cost: § 7519 required payment (refundable deposit at termination). PSCs: alternative § 280H min salary requirement INSTEAD of § 7519. Natural business year 25%-test alternative: no § 7519 required if 25%+ of gross receipts in last 2 months. Form 8716.
view.s444.label.psc_min: PSC min salary paid in deferral ($)
view.s444.label.psc_comp: PSC owner comp pct of services %
view.s444.req.partnership: Partnership: tax year that results in least deferral to partners (usually calendar)
view.s444.req.estate: Estate: any year — typically fiscal in 1st year
view.s444.req.short_period: Initial / final year: short period return
view.s444.def.3_months: Maximum 3-month deferral allowed (Sep 30, Oct 31, Nov 30 for calendar required)
view.s444.def.alternative_2: Alternative: shorter than 3 months OK (Oct 31, Nov 30)
view.s444.def.partnership: Partnership: must elect by 5/15 of year of election (Form 8716 by 5/15)
view.s444.def.s_corp: S-corp: within 30 days after Form 2553 election effective date
view.s444.def.short_period_initial: Initial short period: from formation date to elected year-end
view.s444.def.natural_business: Natural Business Year (NBY) exception: no § 7519 required if 25%+ in last 2 months
view.s444.def.revocation: Revocation: Form 8716 to revert; effective next year
view.s444.s7519.formula: Required payment = deferral period income × highest rate × (deferred months / 12)
view.s444.s7519.deposit: Treated as DEPOSIT — refundable when entity terminates / changes year
view.s444.s7519.no_interest: NO interest paid on deposit (lost time value)
view.s444.s7519.recovery: Refund: filed within 2 yrs of termination event
view.s444.s7519.cumulative: Cumulative across years — adjusted based on net deferral each year
view.s444.s7519.example: Example: $1M deferral income, 37% rate, 3-month deferral = $1M × 37% × 25% = $92,500 required payment
view.s444.s280h.purpose: PSC instead of § 7519: minimum salary requirement to owner-employees
view.s444.s280h.formula: Owner salary during deferral months × 365/365 × deferral pct ≥ baseline
view.s444.s280h.fail: Failure: deduction limitation on excess of bonus paid during deferral period
view.s444.s280h.policy: Policy goal: ensure PSC owners receive consistent salary throughout fiscal year
view.s444.s280h.timing: Test applied at year-end based on annual salary pattern
view.s444.s280h.distribution_alternative: Cannot satisfy by distribution alone — must be wages
view.s444.s280h.cure: Pay year-end bonus to cure shortfall
view.s444.nby_note: Natural Business Year (NBY) exception: 25%+ of gross receipts in last 2 months of fiscal year permits SHORT FISCAL YEAR without § 7519 required payment. Common for seasonal businesses (Q4 retail, December construction). No deposit required = no time value lost.
view.s904.h1.title: // § 904 FTC LIMITATION
view.s904.hint.intro: Limit = (foreign source TI / worldwide TI) × US tax (pre-FTC). Separate baskets: passive, GILTI, foreign branch, general, treaty resourced. Excess: carry back 1 year, forward 10 years (WITHIN SAME BASKET). § 904(d) lookthrough rules: pass-through entity income flows by character. § 904(g) high-tax kickout: high-taxed passive income moves to GENERAL basket. § 904(j) de minimis: ≤ $300 / $600 MFJ — no Form 1116. § 904(b)(4): US-source income re-sourced (Worldwide). Form 1116 / 1118.
view.s904.exp.gross: Foreign-source TI starts with foreign GROSS income
view.s904.exp.allocate: Allocate + apportion US deductions to foreign source basis
view.s904.exp.interest: Interest expense: allocated based on FMV of assets (or tax book)
view.s904.exp.rd: R&D expense: 25% / 50% / 75% to source — taxpayer election
view.s904.exp.sga: SG&A: gross income, asset, sales — taxpayer choice
view.s904.exp.directly_traceable: Directly traceable: full allocation to source (charge-off)
view.s904.exp.cm_election: Cost method election vs FMV — locked for 5 yrs
view.s904.htk.trigger: Trigger: passive income's foreign effective rate > 90% × highest US rate
view.s904.htk.2025: 2025 example: 37% × 90% = 33.3% — passive income at higher foreign rate moves to general
view.s904.htk.purpose: Purpose: prevent loading general basket with low-tax passive income
view.s904.htk.kicks_general: Income kicks to GENERAL basket (better cross-crediting)
view.s904.htk.election: 2024 final regs: kick-out automatic; no election
view.s904.htk.consistency: Consistency: apply same way each year for similar income
view.s904.htk.cfc_overlap: CFC overlap: high-tax exception § 954(b)(4) for subpart F also relevant
view.s904.cf.forward_10: Then carry forward up to 10 years
view.s904.cf.same_basket: Must stay in SAME basket — no cross-basket use
view.s904.cf.character_preserve: Character preserved — passive carryforward only offsets passive
view.s904.cf.s382_overlap: § 382 ownership change can limit carryforward (analogous to NOL)
view.s904.cf.no_election: No election to opt out of carry-back (forced)
view.s904.expense_note: US expenses allocated to foreign source REDUCE the § 904 limit. Reducing expense allocation (consider direct-charge basis vs apportionment) preserves FTC capacity. § 904(b)(4) + 2020 final regs limit reallocation of US-source income. Compare R&D 25% vs 75% elections impact on basket allocation.
view.s7701b.h1.title: // § 7701(b) RESIDENT ALIEN
view.s7701b.hint.intro: US RESIDENT ALIEN if: (1) Green Card test (LPR status) OR (2) Substantial Presence Test (SPT). SPT: 31 days current year + 183 days weighted (current + 1/3 prior + 1/6 two prior). Closer connection exception (Form 8840): SPT met but closer to foreign country. Treaty tie-breaker: dual residents — treaty residence rules apply. Resident alien: taxed on WORLDWIDE income. Nonresident alien: US-source only.
view.s7701b.label.exempt: Exempt individual (J, F, M, Q visa)?
view.s7701b.spt.31_min: Step 1: Count current-year days — must be ≥ 31 (else NOT resident regardless of formula)
view.s7701b.spt.weighted: Step 2: Weighted formula: current + 1/3 prior + 1/6 two prior
view.s7701b.spt.183: Step 3: If weighted total ≥ 183 → RESIDENT ALIEN
view.s7701b.spt.day_count: Day count: any day physically in US (even partial day = full day)
view.s7701b.spt.transit: Transit through US (< 24 hrs): NOT counted
view.s7701b.spt.commuter: Daily commuters from Canada / Mexico: NOT counted
view.s7701b.spt.exempt_days: Exempt individual (F, J, M, Q visa) days don't count
view.s7701b.spt.medical: Medical condition keep days don't count if traveled w/ intent to leave
view.s7701b.exc.teacher_trainee: Teacher / trainee on J or Q visa (limited to 2 of past 6 yrs)
view.s7701b.exc.student: Student on F, J, M visa (limited to 5 calendar years)
view.s7701b.exc.athlete: Professional athlete competing in charitable sports event
view.s7701b.exc.dual_status: Dual-status year: residence may start / end mid-year
view.s7701b.cc.spt_met: Available when SPT met but tax home is foreign + closer connection
view.s7701b.cc.183_in_year: NOT available if 183+ days in current year alone
view.s7701b.cc.factors: Factors: family, home, personal belongings, social ties, voting
view.s7701b.cc.no_green: CANNOT apply if Green Card applied for or pending
view.s7701b.cc.timely: Must file Form 8840 timely with Form 1040NR
view.s7701b.cc.tax_home_foreign: Tax home: principal place of business / employment
view.s7701b.cc.dual_residence: Dual residence: invoke treaty tie-breaker article instead
view.s7701b.tt.dual_resident: Used when person is resident under BOTH US and foreign country domestic law
view.s7701b.tt.permanent_home: Test 1: Permanent home — country with permanent home wins
view.s7701b.tt.center_vital: Test 2: Center of vital interests — closer connections
view.s7701b.tt.mutual_agreement: Test 5: Mutual agreement between competent authorities
view.s7701b.tt.us_treaty_residency: Treaty residency: tax as nonresident in non-residence country
view.s7701b.tt.disclosure_8833: Must file Form 8833 to invoke treaty position
view.s7701b.resident_note: RESIDENT ALIEN: subject to US tax on WORLDWIDE income. File Form 1040. May elect treaty tie-breaker via Form 8833 if dual resident under treaty. Must comply with FBAR, Form 8938 if foreign assets > $10K / $50K thresholds. § 911 FEIE may apply if working abroad.
view.s7701b.nonresident_note: NONRESIDENT ALIEN: taxed only on US-source income + ECI. File Form 1040NR. 30% FDAP withholding on US-source passive income. Capital gains on non-real-estate generally exempt. § 871(m) dividend equivalent payments separately analyzed.
view.s1041.h1.title: // § 1041 SPOUSE / DIVORCE
view.s1041.hint.intro: Transfers between spouses + transfers incident to divorce (within 1 yr OR pursuant to divorce decree) = NO GAIN OR LOSS. Recipient takes CARRYOVER BASIS from transferor. Holding period TACKS. Trust for spouse also qualifies. EXCEPTION: § 1041 does NOT apply if recipient is nonresident alien spouse (NRA carve-out). Post-TCJA 2017: alimony NO LONGER deductible / includible (for divorces post-12/31/2018). QDRO (Qualified Domestic Relations Order) for retirement assets — separate rules.
view.s1041.scope.incident: 'Incident to divorce': within 1 yr OR pursuant to decree
view.s1041.scope.6_year: 6-year presumption: pursuant to decree if w/in 6 yrs
view.s1041.scope.beyond_6: Beyond 6 yrs: rebuttable presumption — must show divorce nexus
view.s1041.scope.trust: Transfer to trust for spouse's benefit: § 1041
view.s1041.scope.installment: Installment note: § 1041 applies to original transfer; § 453B(g) for note received
view.s1041.scope.assumption: Liabilities > basis (negative basis): § 1041 still applies (no recharacterization)
view.s1041.scope.nra_exception: NONRESIDENT ALIEN spouse: § 1041 does NOT apply (gain recognized)
view.s1041.alm.pre_2019: Pre-12/31/2018 divorces: alimony DEDUCTIBLE to payor; INCLUDIBLE to payee
view.s1041.alm.post_tcja: Post-12/31/2018: alimony NEITHER deductible nor includible
view.s1041.alm.modification: Modification of pre-2019 decree: keep old rules UNLESS new agreement specifies new
view.s1041.alm.child_support: Child support: NEVER deductible to payor / income to payee
view.s1041.alm.property_settlement: Property settlement (§ 1041): always tax-neutral (no income / deduction)
view.s1041.alm.lump_sum: Lump-sum payments: scrutinized — § 71(c) recharacterizes if disguised property settlement
view.s1041.alm.retiree: Retiree planning: marital settlement around retirement assets via QDRO is critical
view.s1041.alm.state_law: State law: not all states followed TCJA — some allow state-level deduction
view.s1041.spec.iras: IRAs: transfer pursuant to divorce decree NOT taxable; QDRO not for IRA (use trustee-to-trustee)
view.s1041.spec.401k: 401(k) / qualified plans: QDRO required — TAXABLE if not properly transferred
view.s1041.spec.business_interests: Business interests: pre-divorce valuation often required + tax-free shifts to spouse
view.s1041.spec.s_corp_stock: S-corp stock: § 1041 ok; recipient becomes shareholder for pass-through
view.s1041.spec.installment_note: Installment notes: § 453B(g) — recipient takes carryover basis + no gain on transfer
view.s1041.spec.options: Stock options / RSU: vested options to ex-spouse via § 1041; tax due at exercise by recipient
view.s1041.spec.deferred_comp: Deferred comp / pension splits: QDRO required to avoid acceleration; § 409A trap
view.s1041.basis.carryover: Recipient takes CARRYOVER basis = transferor's adjusted basis
view.s1041.basis.no_dual: NO dual basis (contrast § 1015 gift rule for depreciated property)
view.s1041.basis.gift_tax_inapplicable: NO gift tax adjustment (no gift tax paid since spousal transfer)
view.s1041.basis.liabilities: Liabilities assumed by recipient REDUCE basis (Crane rule)
view.s1041.basis.nondeductible: No transferor loss recognition even if FMV < basis
view.s1041.basis.later_sale: Later sale by recipient: gain / loss based on carryover basis
view.s1041.nra_note: NONRESIDENT ALIEN spouse: § 1041 does NOT apply. Transferor recognizes FULL gain/loss on transfer at FMV. Critical planning point — NRA spouse acquires basis = FMV but transferor pays tax now. Consider NRA election to be treated as US resident (§ 6013(g)) to access § 1041, but creates US-tax on worldwide income for NRA spouse.
view.s871a.h1.title: // § 871(a) NRA FDAP WITHHOLDING
view.s871a.hint.intro: 30% flat tax on US-source FDAP income to NRAs. Withholding agent must REMIT — Forms W-8BEN (NRA claim of treaty / status) + 1042-S (information return + 1042 aggregate). FDAP: Fixed/Determinable/Annual/Periodical — interest, dividends, rents, royalties, salaries (passive non-ECI). Treaty rates: often 0-15% (LOB + active trade tests). Exemptions: portfolio interest § 871(h), bank deposit interest § 871(i), capital gains (NRA-domiciled). § 871(m): dividend equivalents from total return swaps. § 1441 / § 1442 withholding mechanics.
view.s871a.fdap.rent_personal: Rent from personal property in US
view.s871a.fdap.compensation: Compensation for personal services performed in US
view.s871a.fdap.gambling: Gambling winnings (mostly 30% — exempt: blackjack, baccarat, craps, roulette, big-6 in casinos)
view.s871a.fdap.original_issue: OID accruals + capital gain dividends from RIC / REIT
view.s871a.fdap.scholarships: Scholarships in excess of qualified expenses
view.s871a.exem.portfolio: § 871(h) portfolio interest: registered debt + foreign payee + non-10% shareholder of issuer
view.s871a.exem.bank_dep: § 871(i) bank deposit interest: US bank deposits to foreign payee (since 2013)
view.s871a.exem.cap_gains: Capital gains: NRAs generally NOT taxed except (1) US real prop (§ 897 FIRPTA), (2) ECI, (3) 183-day rule for individuals
view.s871a.exem.original_issue_short: Original Issue Discount on short-term obligations (≤ 183 days)
view.s871a.exem.casino: Certain casino gambling: blackjack, baccarat, craps, roulette, big-6 wheel
view.s871a.wa.w8ben: Collect Form W-8BEN (individual) / W-8BEN-E (entity) / W-8ECI (ECI) / W-8IMY (intermediary)
view.s871a.wa.documentation: Document treaty claim before reduced rate applied
view.s871a.wa.expiration: W-8BEN valid 3 yrs from execution + indefinitely if facts unchanged
view.s871a.wa.fatca: FATCA Chapter 4: 30% withholding on payments to non-FATCA compliant foreign entities
view.s871a.wa.penalty: Penalty: 100% of withholding amount + interest if failure
view.s871a.no_w8_note: No W-8BEN on file → DEFAULT 30% withholding applied. NRA must file Form 1040NR + W-8BEN retroactively + claim refund. Withholding agent has no liability if W-8BEN obtained timely. Treaty rate not auto-applied — explicit documentation required.
view.s367d.h1.title: // § 367(d) IP OUTBOUND
view.s367d.hint.intro: US transferor of INTANGIBLE PROPERTY to foreign corp must include ANNUAL DEEMED ROYALTY over IP's useful life. TCJA 2017 expansion: 'intangible' now includes workforce in place, goodwill, going concern, customer lists. Royalty = arm's-length annual amount (parallel to § 482 transfer pricing). Cost Sharing Arrangement (CSA) § 482 alternative — buy-in + ongoing R&D shares. 2017 effective: applies post-Dec 31 2017 transfers. § 6038B reporting. Avoid: keep IP in US, or CSA, or royalty structure (not outbound transfer).
view.s367d.def.patents: Patents, inventions, formulas, processes, designs, patterns, knowhow
view.s367d.def.trademarks: Trademarks, trade names, brand names, franchises
view.s367d.def.licenses: Licenses, permits, other rights granted by governmental units
view.s367d.def.goodwill: Goodwill and going concern value — TCJA NEW
view.s367d.def.other: Methods of doing business, business records, operating systems
view.s367d.csa.purpose: Avoid § 367(d) by SHARING development costs of new IP
view.s367d.csa.entry_fee: Foreign sub pays BUY-IN to US sub for existing IP value
view.s367d.csa.ongoing: Ongoing R&D costs SHARED in proportion to expected benefits
view.s367d.csa.platform_contribution: Platform Contribution: existing IP buy-in determined via Income Method / CUT / DCM
view.s367d.csa.stock_based: Stock-based compensation MUST be included in cost base (Altera v. Comm'r)
view.s367d.csa.financial_substance: Each party must contribute substantial cost + receive substantial benefit
view.s367d.csa.amazon_altera: Tax Court precedent: Amazon (CSA cost methodology), Altera (SBC must be shared)
view.s367d.comp.useful_life: Useful life: estimated economic life of IP (typically 5-15 years)
view.s367d.comp.royalty: Annual royalty: arm's-length comparable (CUT, Profit Split, Income Method)
view.s367d.comp.amortization: Annual inclusion declines as IP's productive capacity decreases
view.s367d.comp.character: Character: ORDINARY royalty income (not capital gain)
view.s367d.comp.subF_overlap: Subject to subF / GILTI if held in CFC: avoid double-tax via § 78 gross-up
view.s367d.comp.transferee_basis: Transferee basis = FMV at transfer (capital expenditure)
view.s367d.comp.no_loss: No loss on transfer even if FMV < basis
view.s367d.comp.no_capital_gain: No capital gain even on appreciated IP at transfer (annual royalty instead)
view.s367d.coord.s367a: § 367(a)(1): tangible property outbound — gain recognition (vs § 367(d) ongoing royalty)
view.s367d.coord.s367a3: § 367(a)(3): active trade/business exception (TCJA removed for IP)
view.s367d.coord.s482: § 482 transfer pricing: same arm's-length standard applies on royalty determination
view.s367d.coord.s951a: § 951A GILTI: IP income now subject to GILTI inclusion if held in CFC
view.s367d.coord.s250: § 250 FDII: incentive to keep IP in US (13.125% effective rate)
view.s367d.coord.s936: § 936 possessions tax credit — repealed but historical context
view.s367d.coord.s367b: § 367(b) inbound regulations — exit-charge for CFC liquidations
view.s367d.coord.s956: § 956 investment in US property: keeps IP from US shareholder access
view.s367d.csa_note: CSA in place: § 367(d) deemed royalty AVOIDED. Buy-in payment + ongoing cost share. Critical: stock-based compensation MUST be included in cost base (Altera). Document IP development costs + benefit allocation contemporaneously. Tax Court has reviewed Amazon + Altera challenges to enforce arm's-length CSA pricing.
view.s165d.h1.title: // § 165(d) GAMBLING LOSSES
view.s165d.hint.intro: Gambling LOSSES deductible ONLY to extent of GAMBLING WINNINGS. Excess losses NOT deductible / no carry-forward. Amateurs: winnings → Schedule 1 income; losses → Schedule A itemized (only if itemizing). Professionals: Schedule C trade / business — gambling expenses + losses limited to winnings under TCJA 2017. Mayo v. Comm'r (2018) — pre-TCJA expenses + losses cap to winnings questioned; TCJA codified strict limit. Form W-2G: payer reports winnings ≥ $600/$1,200/$5,000 thresholds.
view.s165d.sess.purpose: Net within 'session' — discrete period (single day at casino)
view.s165d.sess.formula: Track buy-in + cash out per session
view.s165d.sess.report: Report only NET WIN per session (vs gross-up of every spin)
view.s165d.sess.poker: Poker / table games: per-table or per-tournament session
view.s165d.sess.sports: Sports betting: per-bet basis (no session netting)
view.s165d.sess.crypto: Crypto gambling: each bet separate (depending on platform)
view.s165d.sess.documentation: Documentation: time, date, location, amount, type, persons present (Rev. Proc. 77-29)
view.s165d.w2g.poker_tourn: Poker tournament: ≥ $5,000 reduced by entry fee (withholding 24% if > $5K)
view.s165d.w2g.cap: Backup withholding 24% if no TIN provided (vs default 24%)
view.s165d.w2g.copy_irs: Casino files copy with IRS — match to your return
view.s165d.rec.diary: Contemporaneous diary / log: date, location, type, amount, persons present
view.s165d.rec.receipts: Receipts: hotel, travel, parking, food (professional only)
view.s165d.rec.casino_card: Casino loyalty card statements (helpful but not authoritative)
view.s165d.rec.cohan: Cohan rule: courts may estimate if records inadequate (but disfavored)
view.s165d.rec.session_log: Session-based log preferred over per-bet detail
view.s165d.rec.audit_risk: Audit risk: high if winnings + losses don't square; IRS cross-checks W-2Gs
view.s165d.no_itemize_note: Standard deduction beats itemizing — gambling losses DISALLOWED (cannot reduce winnings). Full winnings taxable. Common trap: amateur gambler with high standard deduction loses ALL benefit of losses. Consider professional status if activity is consistent + profit-motive demonstrable (5 Groetzinger factors).
view.s1377.h1.title: // § 1377 S-CORP ALLOC + PTTP
view.s1377.hint.intro: § 1377(a)(1) DEFAULT: pro-rata DAILY allocation when shareholder changes mid-year. § 1377(a)(2) Closing-of-Books Election: avoid pro-rata — allocate based on ACTUAL income in pre / post-change periods. Requires CONSENT of ALL affected shareholders + corp. Useful when income lumpy / heavy in one period. § 1377(b) PTTP (Post-Termination Transition Period): 1 year + 120 days after S election terminates to distribute AAA tax-free. § 1377(a)(2) v. § 1362(e)(3): different elections — term vs change in shareholder.
view.s1377.ed.consent: CONSENT: ALL affected shareholders (departing + new + remaining) + corp
view.s1377.ed.timing: Made on Form 1120-S for year of change
view.s1377.ed.irrevocable: Once made for a year: irrevocable for that year
view.s1377.ed.partial: Can be made for specific change events, not all
view.s1377.ed.use_case_loss: Use case: heavy losses in first half (new partner takes hit; departing avoids)
view.s1377.ed.use_case_gain: Use case: heavy gain in second half (new partner reaps; departing avoids tax)
view.s1377.ed.flip_disadvantage: Disadvantage flips: useful for one party, harmful for other — consent reflects negotiation
view.s1377.pttp.window: Window: 1 YEAR after termination + 120 days for late challenges
view.s1377.pttp.distributions: Distributions during PTTP treated as AAA / PTI / OAA — tax-free to extent
view.s1377.pttp.s1368_ordering: Standard § 1368 ordering: AAA → PTI → OAA → basis → cap gain
view.s1377.pttp.purpose: Purpose: allow former S-corp shareholders to cash out without dividend tax
view.s1377.pttp.no_extension: Cannot extend PTTP beyond statutory window
view.s1377.pttp.aaa_freeze: AAA balance FREEZES at termination — no further income additions
view.s1377.pttp.attributes_after: Post-PTTP distributions: ordinary dividend (subject to qualified div rules)
view.s1377.pttp.elect_out: § 1371(e)(2) elect out: revoke step-up to FMV (rarely used)
view.s1377.election_note: Closing-books election reflects ACTUAL income pattern instead of arbitrary daily pro-rata. Valuable when income lumpy — e.g., legal practice w/ year-end fee receipts; construction contract billed at completion; SaaS w/ annual billing. Consent of all shareholders required — negotiation often results in side payments to compensate disadvantaged party.
view.s162f.h1.title: // § 162(f) FINES + PENALTIES
view.s162f.hint.intro: NO DEDUCTION for fines / similar penalties paid to government / governmental entity. TCJA 2017 expanded: all government, even foreign. Exceptions: (1) IDENTIFIED RESTITUTION (to victim), (2) IDENTIFIED COMPLIANCE costs. Settlement language CRITICAL: must specifically IDENTIFY which portion is restitution vs penalty. § 6050X reporting: government MUST report payments to IRS via Form 1098-F. § 162(g): 2/3 of antitrust treble damages also nondeductible.
view.s162f.scope.fine: Fines + penalties paid to government for violation of law
view.s162f.scope.civil: Civil penalties (DOJ, EPA, SEC, CFTC, FTC, state attorneys general)
view.s162f.scope.environmental: Environmental penalties (EPA Clean Air, Clean Water, RCRA)
view.s162f.scope.opioid: Opioid settlements (recent wave): mostly NONDEDUCTIBLE under § 162(f)
view.s162f.id.specific: Settlement / court order must SPECIFICALLY IDENTIFY portion as restitution / compliance
view.s162f.id.amount: Amount specifically identified (not generic / pro-rata after the fact)
view.s162f.id.allocation: Cannot ALLOCATE after the fact — must be PRE-SET in settlement document
view.s162f.id.regulation: Reg § 1.162-21 (2021 final regs) clarifies identification standard
view.s162f.id.allocation_rare: Specific dollar amount + clear restitution / compliance label required
view.s162f.id.reasonable_basis: 'Reasonable basis to expect' amounts identified would be paid
view.s162f.id.preparer_burden: Document settlement carefully — IRS examiners challenge allocations
view.s162f.f.gov_files: Government MUST file Form 1098-F if total ≥ $50K
view.s162f.f.copy_to_taxpayer: Copy to taxpayer; copy to IRS
view.s162f.f.boxes: Form has separate boxes: (a) penalty, (b) restitution, (c) compliance, (d) other
view.s162f.f.consistent: Taxpayer must REPORT CONSISTENTLY with 1098-F amounts
view.s162f.f.dispute: Dispute: file amended 1098-F or claim different on 1040 with explanation
view.s162f.f.no_form_no_deduction: No 1098-F received: presumption is no restitution / compliance identified
view.s162f.f.timing: Filed in year payment made (cash basis for both parties)
view.s162f.f.exception: Exception: settlement payments to private plaintiffs not subject to 1098-F
view.s162f.rel.c: § 162(c): illegal payments / kickbacks NONDEDUCTIBLE (foreign bribes too)
view.s162f.rel.k: § 162(k): healthcare premium for retirees subject to limits
view.s162f.rel.m: § 162(m): $1M deduction limit on public co exec comp
view.s162f.rel.s: Smith / Jenkins / Westmoreland cases established narrow restitution interpretation
view.s162f.no_form_note: No Form 1098-F received: IRS presumes ALL payment to government is nondeductible. Critical pre-settlement step: NEGOTIATE settlement language identifying specific dollar amounts as restitution / compliance. Post-settlement allocation usually too late. Consult tax counsel during settlement negotiations.
view.s162c.h1.title: // § 162(c) ILLEGAL PAYMENTS
view.s162c.hint.intro: NO DEDUCTION for amounts constituting illegal bribes, kickbacks, other illegal payments. § 162(c)(1): payments to government officials — federal, state, local, FOREIGN. § 162(c)(2): kickbacks to non-government persons / entities. § 162(c)(3): kickbacks tied to Medicare / Medicaid fraud. FCPA (Foreign Corrupt Practices Act) violations: nondeductible. Burden: taxpayer must show payment NOT illegal. Conviction: not required — IRS can disallow based on facts + law analysis.
view.s162c.sub.1: § 162(c)(1): payments to government officials (any govt) — NEVER deductible
view.s162c.sub.2_burden: Burden: taxpayer must prove payment is NOT illegal
view.s162c.sub.3_safe_harbor: Anti-kickback statute safe harbors: legitimate payment structures
view.s162c.sub.legal_referrals: Legal referrals (with Stark Law compliance): deductible
view.s162c.sub.proof: No conviction required — IRS analyzes legal substance
view.s162c.fcpa.basic: FCPA prohibits bribery of foreign officials to obtain / retain business
view.s162c.fcpa.payor: Applies to: US persons + entities + foreign issuers (15 U.S.C. § 78dd-1)
view.s162c.fcpa.cdr: Books + records provisions require accurate financial reporting
view.s162c.fcpa.facilitating: Facilitating ('grease') payments: limited exception under FCPA, STILL NONDEDUCTIBLE under § 162(c)
view.s162c.fcpa.uk_bribery: UK Bribery Act 2010: more strict (no facilitating payment exception)
view.s162c.fcpa.successor_liability: Successor liability: acquiring corp may inherit FCPA exposure
view.s162c.med.safe_harbors: Safe harbors: bona fide employee, GPO (Group Purchasing), space rental, equipment rental
view.s162c.med.intent: Intent: anything of value INTENDED to induce referrals
view.s162c.med.consulting_fees: 'Consulting fees' disguised as referral compensation: scrutinized
view.s162c.med.compliance_program: Compliance program: Office of Inspector General (OIG) recommendations
view.s162c.med.qui_tam: Qui tam (False Claims Act): whistleblower 15-30% reward share
view.s162c.cases.commissioner: Commissioner v. Tellier (1966): legal fees in defense of business deductible (NOT § 162(c))
view.s162c.cases.smith_wessel: Smith v. Commissioner: kickback definition fact-intensive
view.s162c.cases.alcan: Alcan Aluminium (1998): Canadian tax law factor in foreign legality
view.s162c.cases.dichter: Dichter v. Commissioner: legitimate vs disguised commissions
view.s162c.cases.siemens: Siemens FCPA $800M (2008): largest FCPA settlement — all nondeductible
view.s162c.cases.goldman_1mdb: Goldman Sachs / 1MDB $3.3B settlement (2020): partial restitution / penalty allocation
view.s162c.cases.hca: HCA $1.7B Medicare fraud settlement: anti-kickback dimension
view.s162c.illegal_note: Payment NONDEDUCTIBLE under § 162(c). Even without DOJ prosecution / conviction, IRS may disallow based on legal substance. FCPA + Anti-Kickback Statute violations particularly high risk. Consider self-disclosure to DOJ for cooperation credit + reduce penalty exposure. Sec/SEC + DOJ + IRS exchange information.
view.s1297.h1.title: // § 1297 PFIC DEFINITION
view.s1297.hint.intro: PFIC if EITHER: (1) 75%+ income passive OR (2) 50%+ assets produce passive income (or held for such). 'Once a PFIC, always a PFIC' in shareholder's hands until purged. Look-through rule: ≥ 25%-owned subsidiaries taxed on look-through basis. Startup exception § 1298(b)(2): first year of new corp may be exempt. CFC overlap: CFC US shareholders escape PFIC regime under § 1298(b)(8). Form 8621 annually for each PFIC.
view.s1297.tests.income: INCOME test: 75%+ of gross income is 'passive' (§ 1297(b))
view.s1297.tests.assets: ASSET test: 50%+ of FMV avg assets produce passive income / held for such
view.s1297.tests.either: EITHER triggers PFIC — not both required
view.s1297.tests.passive_def: Passive: interest, dividends, royalties, rents, net gains on personal property
view.s1297.tests.banking: Active banking exception: ≥ 4 financial professionals, regulatory oversight
view.s1297.tests.real_estate: Rental income: passive UNLESS active real estate trade or business
view.s1297.tests.commodities: Commodities trading: special active dealer exception
view.s1297.lt.threshold: ≥ 25% owned subsidiaries treated as look-through (income + asset basis)
view.s1297.lt.allocation: Pro-rata share of sub's income + assets allocated to parent
view.s1297.lt.measurement: Asset measurement: FMV at end of each quarter, averaged
view.s1297.lt.intermediate: Multi-tier: look-through cascades through chain of 25%+ owned subs
view.s1297.lt.passive_assets_active: Foreign sub's active assets cleanse parent's passive characterization
view.s1297.lt.dividend_excl: Dividends from sub: NOT counted as passive income (avoid double-counting)
view.s1297.lt.intercompany: Intercompany debt: special rules to prevent abuse
view.s1297.exc.startup: § 1298(b)(2) STARTUP: first year of new active business may escape PFIC if not PFIC in first 3 yrs
view.s1297.exc.cfc_overlap: § 1298(b)(8) CFC overlap: CFC US 10% shareholder NOT PFIC for that shareholder
view.s1297.exc.banking: § 1297(b)(2)(A) active banking: ≥ 4 financial professionals + customary loans + deposits
view.s1297.exc.related_party: Related-party rent / royalty: NOT passive (look-through to active business)
view.s1297.exc.foreign_personal: Foreign personal services: NOT passive if performed by service-providers
view.s1297.exc.commodities: Active commodities dealer: ≥ 75% income from regular trade dealer activity
view.s1297.exc.5pct_passive: 5% de minimis: ≤ 5% income test exception in cleansing years
view.s1297.cons.default: § 1291 default: top ordinary rate + interest charge on excess distributions / sale gain
view.s1297.cons.qef: § 1295 QEF election: annual current inclusion + preserve character
view.s1297.cons.mtm: § 1296 MTM: mark-to-market on tradable PFIC stock
view.s1297.cons.taint: 'Once PFIC, always PFIC' until purged via deemed sale
view.s1297.cons.attribution: Pass-through entities: PFIC status flows to ultimate US owner
view.s1297.cons.estate: Estate planning: gift / inheritance triggers § 1291 unless purged
view.s1297.cons.cross_border_planning: Cross-border M&A: pre-deal PFIC analysis critical
view.s1297.cfc_note: § 1298(b)(8) CFC OVERLAP exception: this US 10% shareholder of a CFC is NOT subject to PFIC regime. Subpart F + GILTI apply instead. Critical when foreign sub has dual character — must verify CFC status (50%+ US ownership). Form 5471 filing replaces / supplements Form 8621 PFIC reporting.
view.s6655.h1.title: // § 6655 CORP ESTIMATED TAX
view.s6655.hint.intro: Corp must pay estimated tax in 4 installments (4/15, 6/15, 9/15, 12/15) totaling 100% of expected tax. Underpayment penalty: federal short-term rate + 3% on shortfall (Form 2220). Safe harbors: (1) 100% of prior-year tax (large corp: 100% of CURRENT year), (2) annualized income method, (3) adjusted seasonal installment. Large corp: avg taxable income ≥ $1M in any of past 3 yrs — ONLY current-year safe harbor available. Form 1120-W worksheet (not filed).
view.s6655.sched.q1: Q1: April 15 — 25% of required annual payment
view.s6655.sched.equal: Equal installments OR annualized / seasonal methods
view.s6655.sched.weekend: If due date is weekend / holiday: next business day
view.s6655.sched.consolidated: Consolidated group: parent + all subs aggregate
view.s6655.ann.purpose: Purpose: avoid penalty when income SPIKED in later periods
view.s6655.ann.q2: Q2: annualize Jan-May × 12/5 → tax × 50%, minus Q1
view.s6655.ann.q3: Q3: annualize Jan-Aug × 12/8 → tax × 75%, minus Q1+Q2
view.s6655.ann.q4: Q4: annualize Jan-Nov × 12/11 → tax × 100%, minus Q1+Q2+Q3
view.s6655.ann.elections: 3 different election periods available (months may vary)
view.s6655.ann.recalculation: Each quarter rechecked — late-year underpayment may trigger earlier penalty
view.s6655.pen.from: Period: due date of installment to earlier of payment OR Form 1120 filing
view.s6655.pen.exception_first: First-year corp + small operations: no penalty under § 6655(g)
view.s6655.pen.reasonable_cause: Reasonable cause exception: rarely granted (Boyle case strict)
view.s6655.annualized_note: Annualized income method elected: payment requirements based on YTD income annualized quarterly, not flat 25%. Particularly valuable for cyclical businesses w/ Q4 spikes. Form 2220 Part III computation. Reduces Q1-Q3 underpayment but Q4 cumulative test catches up if not paid sufficiently.
view.s6700.h1.title: // § 6700 PROMOTER PENALTY
view.s6700.hint.intro: Penalty on PROMOTERS of abusive tax shelters who: (1) make false/fraudulent statements about tax benefits OR (2) provide GROSS VALUATION OVERSTATEMENTS. Penalty: GREATER of $1,000 per activity OR 100% of gross income derived from the activity. Separate from § 6701 aid + abet ($1,000 individuals; $10,000 corps). Listed Transactions + Reportable Transactions: disclosure requirements + heightened penalties. Forms 8918 + 8886.
view.s6700.scope.promoter: Promoter: organizes / participates in sale of plan / arrangement
view.s6700.scope.seller: Seller: who sells interests in the arrangement
view.s6700.scope.advisor: Material advisor (§ 6111): provides tax-related material aid
view.s6700.scope.knowledge_or_should: 'Knew or had reason to know' of false statement
view.s6700.scope.tax_benefits: 'Tax benefits' includes deductions, credits, exclusions, deferral
view.s6700.scope.compensation_link: Penalty applies to person who derives INCOME from promotion
view.s6700.lt.crispr: CRISPR / R&D credit abuse arrangements — recent
view.s6700.lt.son_of_boss: Son of BOSS / similar — Notice 99-59 (older but active enforcement)
view.s6700.rep.form_8886: Form 8886 — Reportable Transaction disclosure by taxpayer
view.s6700.rep.list: Maintained list of advisees (Material Advisor List § 6112)
view.s6700.rep.list_30day: Provide list to IRS within 20 days of request
view.s6700.rep.failure_disclosure: Failure to disclose: $50,000 per failure (more for listed)
view.s6700.rep.crossborder: FATCA + CRS reporting overlap for offshore arrangements
view.s6700.rep.reportable_transaction_ty: 5 reportable transaction categories: listed, confidential, contractual, loss, transaction of interest
view.s6700.rep.statute_listed: Listed transactions: statute of limitations re-opens for 1 yr after disclosure required
view.s6700.listed_note: LISTED TRANSACTION: heightened enforcement. § 6707 $50K failure-to-register + § 6707A $5-200K failure-to-disclose. Material advisor list § 6112 must be maintained + provided to IRS within 20 days. Statute of limitations re-opens 1 yr after disclosure required. DOJ injunction also available (§ 7408).
view.s6325.h1.title: // § 6325 LIEN RELEASE
view.s6325.hint.intro: Lien RELEASED when: liability satisfied, uncollectible, OR bond posted. § 6325(a)(1): full payment / accepted Offer in Compromise. § 6325(b)(1): partial release of specific property — value retained ≥ 2× liability. § 6325(b)(2): discharge if government's interest valueless. § 6325(b)(3): bond / cash deposit. § 6325(d): subordination for refinancing. § 6326: 5-day appeal of erroneous filing.
view.s6325.r.paid: Full payment of liability + interest + penalties
view.s6325.r.uncollectible: CNC (Currently Not Collectible) status + lien hindering collection
view.s6325.r.bonded: Bond posted equal to liability (Form 12153 if appealing)
view.s6325.r.timing: Release issued within 30 days of request / qualifying event
view.s6325.r.self_releasing: Self-releasing lien: certain liens release automatically (Form 668(Y))
view.s6325.r.refile: Refile possible if facts change OR new period within statute
view.s6325.d.b1: § 6325(b)(1): RETAIN value ≥ 2× liability — value safely covers exposure
view.s6325.d.b2: § 6325(b)(2): VALUELESS to government — no IRS interest in property
view.s6325.d.b3: § 6325(b)(3): BOND or cash deposit — substitute security
view.s6325.d.sale: Sale of property: IRS interest follows proceeds (Subordination)
view.s6325.d.refinance: Refi: § 6325(d) subordination allows new lien priority
view.s6325.d.process_time: Typical: 30-60 days to process discharge application
view.s6325.app.s6326: § 6326: 5 days to appeal erroneous filing — admin review
view.s6325.app.cdp: § 6320 CDP hearing: 30 days from Notice of Lien Filing
view.s6325.app.equivalent: Equivalent hearing (post-30-day): less powerful but available 1 yr
view.s6325.app.tax_court: Tax Court appeal after Final Notice of Determination
view.s6325.app.collateral: Collateral attack via refund suit limited
view.s6325.app.form_12277: Form 12277 — withdraw notice if benefits taxpayer (compliance / OIC / IA)
view.s6325.app.administrative: Administrative appeals: Office of Appeals review
view.s6325.app.tax_court_lien: Tax Court can issue determination on lien filing appropriate
view.s6325.coll.s6321: § 6321: tax lien — attaches to all property + rights
view.s6325.coll.s6322: § 6322: lien arises at time of assessment
view.s6325.coll.s6323: § 6323: notice of lien — priority + protection for certain creditors
view.s6325.delay_note: Request pending > 30 days. Contact IRS Centralized Lien Office (CLO) at 800-913-6050. Form 911 to Taxpayer Advocate Service if hardship. CDP hearing may be available if appeal rights not exhausted. Consider local TAS if facing financial harm.
view.s1298.h1.title: // § 1298 PFIC SPECIAL RULES
view.s1298.hint.intro: § 1298(a) Attribution: PFIC stock owned through partnerships, S-corps, trusts, estates flows to ultimate US owner. § 1298(b)(1) 'Once PFIC, always PFIC': taint persists in shareholder's hands; cured only by PURGING (deemed sale at § 1291 rates). § 1298(b)(2) Startup: first year of new corp may be exempt if not PFIC in first 3 years. § 1298(b)(8) CFC overlap: CFC US 10% shareholders ESCAPE PFIC regime. § 1298(f) Form 8621 reporting requirement.
view.s1298.att.partnership: Partnerships: pass through to partners pro-rata
view.s1298.att.trust: Trusts: pass to beneficiaries by interest
view.s1298.att.tiered: Tiered structure: cascade through all layers
view.s1298.att.grantor: Grantor trust: deemed owned by grantor for PFIC purposes
view.s1298.att.foreign_partnership: Foreign partnership: still PFIC pass-through to US owners
view.s1298.att.cooperative: Cooperative ownership rules: aggregate indirect interests
view.s1298.start.first_year: FIRST YEAR of new active business corp
view.s1298.start.test: Test: not PFIC in any of first 3 yrs
view.s1298.start.year_1: If passes 3-yr test → year 1 retroactively NOT PFIC
view.s1298.start.no_active_business: No active business → not eligible for startup exception
view.s1298.start.pre_operating: Pre-operating phase common (no income but high assets)
view.s1298.start.cash_intensive: Cash-intensive R&D stage: typically PFIC despite eventual active business
view.s1298.start.failed_acquirer: Failed acquirer: holding-co structure may inadvertently trigger
view.s1298.cfc.test: US 10% shareholder of CFC: NOT subject to PFIC regime
view.s1298.cfc.policy: Policy: subF + GILTI already address tax abuse; no double-rule
view.s1298.cfc.test_when_no_longer: If US ownership drops below 10% → PFIC regime triggers
view.s1298.cfc.dual_residency: Dual residency exception: treaty-resident considered foreign for PFIC
view.s1298.cfc.form_5471: Form 5471 reporting replaces Form 8621 PFIC reporting
view.s1298.cfc.smaller_shareholder: Other US shareholders (< 10%) still subject to PFIC
view.s1298.cfc.s962_election: § 962 election available for individual US 10% shareholders
view.s1298.taint.basic: § 1298(b)(1): foreign corp PFIC for any year in shareholder's hands = PFIC for ALL subsequent years
view.s1298.taint.corp_changes: Even if corp changes operations + ceases to be PFIC under tests, taint persists to that shareholder
view.s1298.taint.purge_deemed_sale: Purge via DEEMED SALE election: recognize gain currently at § 1291 rates → restart clean
view.s1298.taint.purge_qef_late: Late QEF election: also requires PURGING via deemed sale
view.s1298.taint.purge_mtm_late: MTM election (after PFIC status): apply purging gain recognition
view.s1298.taint.disposition_cleanses: Complete disposition (sale + 36 months pass): may cleanse
view.s1298.taint.death: Death of shareholder: § 1014 step-up + restart from heirs' perspective
view.s1298.taint.cleansing_election: § 1298(b)(1)(B) cleansing election: deemed sale at § 1291 rates → cleanse
view.s1298.rep.required: Any US person owning PFIC interest (direct or indirect) MUST file Form 8621
view.s1298.rep.threshold: Reportable: aggregate value > $25K ($50K MFJ) OR Pedigree Election Made
view.s1298.rep.failure: Failure to file: $10K minimum + 25% understatement penalty
view.s1298.rep.statute_extension: Statute of limitations extends 3 yrs after filing
view.s1298.rep.per_pfic: Form 8621 PER PFIC each year (not aggregate)
view.s1298.rep.partner_share: Partners + S-corp shareholders file own Form 8621
view.s1298.rep.trustees: Grantor trust trustees file on behalf of grantor
view.s1298.report_note: Form 8621 REQUIRED but NOT FILED. Penalty: $10K minimum + 25% understatement penalty + statute of limitations 3-yr extension. Streamlined Filing Compliance Procedures (SFCP) may apply for delinquent reporting. Foreign mutual funds + ETFs almost always PFICs → annual filing per fund.
view.s6072.h1.title: // § 6072 RETURN DUE DATES
view.s6072.hint.intro: Individual: April 15 (calendar). C-corp: 15th day of 4th month after year-end. Partnership + S-corp: 15th day of 3rd month after year-end (March 15 calendar). Trust / estate: 15th day of 4th month (April 15 calendar). Extension: 6-month automatic via Form 4868 (individual) / Form 7004 (entities). Extension to FILE — NOT to PAY. Combat zone: 6+ months. Abroad: June 15 automatic for individuals.
view.s6072.ext.individual: Individual: Form 4868 by April 15 → automatic 6-month extension to Oct 15
view.s6072.ext.entity: Entity: Form 7004 by original due date → automatic 6-month (5.5 for trust)
view.s6072.ext.pay_required: CRITICAL: extension to FILE only, NOT to pay — interest + penalty start from original due date
view.s6072.ext.abroad: Abroad: automatic 2-month extension to June 15 — file by then OR file 4868 for additional 4 months
view.s6072.ext.combat: Combat zone: extension to 180 days after leaving zone
view.s6072.ext.disaster: Federally declared disaster: IRS announces specific extended dates per region
view.s6072.ext.no_combo: Extension does NOT extend SE tax payment due date
view.s6072.late.6651a1: § 6651(a)(1) failure to file: 5% / month up to 25% of unpaid tax
view.s6072.late.6651a2: § 6651(a)(2) failure to pay: 0.5% / month up to 25% of unpaid tax
view.s6072.late.minimum: Minimum failure-to-file penalty: smaller of $485 (2024) or 100% of unpaid tax
view.s6072.late.interest: Interest at federal short-term rate + 3% (compounded daily)
view.s6072.late.reasonable_cause: Reasonable cause exception: Boyle case very strict (limited to extraordinary circumstances)
view.s6072.late.first_time: First Time Penalty Abatement (FTA): one-time relief if compliant 3 prior yrs
view.s6072.late.collection_alternative: Collection alternatives: installment agreement, OIC, CNC
view.s6072.unpaid_note: Unpaid balance + no extension: failure-to-file PLUS failure-to-pay penalties accrue from original due date. Combined penalty up to 47.5%. File ASAP even if can't pay. Form 9465 installment agreement available. First Time Penalty Abatement may waive if compliant 3 prior years.
view.s162a1.h1.title: // § 162(a)(1) REASONABLE COMP
view.s162a1.hint.intro: Deductible compensation must be REASONABLE in amount for services actually rendered. Excess: characterized as DISGUISED DIVIDEND (C-corp) → NOT DEDUCTIBLE distribution. Tests: Mayson Manufacturing 5-factor + Independent Investor Test (Exacto Spring) + Multi-Factor (most circuits). Disputes: closely-held corps, family businesses. S-corp REVERSE problem: owner-employees UNDERPAY salary to avoid SE tax → IRS recharacterizes distributions as wages.
view.s162a1.mayson.role: Employee's role: position, hours worked, duties
view.s162a1.mayson.external: External comparisons: similar positions in similar companies
view.s162a1.mayson.character: Character + condition of company: size, complexity, financial position
view.s162a1.mayson.conflict_interest: Conflict of interest: any inherent bias (owner-employee, family)
view.s162a1.mayson.internal_consistency: Internal consistency: comp pattern over time + similar employees
view.s162a1.iit.test: Test: would an independent investor in the corp be content with the return after compensation?
view.s162a1.iit.return: Look at: return on equity / assets after deducting compensation
view.s162a1.iit.industry: Compare to: industry benchmark return on capital
view.s162a1.iit.satisfied: If hypothetical investor would be content → comp REASONABLE
view.s162a1.iit.deficient: If hypothetical investor would be dissatisfied → comp EXCESSIVE
view.s162a1.iit.minority: Other circuits still use 5-factor (Tax Court general approach)
view.s162a1.iit.combined: Court often applies BOTH tests in combination
view.s162a1.scr.problem: S-corp owners pay themselves LOW salary + take large distributions to avoid SE/FICA
view.s162a1.scr.irs: IRS recharacterizes distribution as wages → adds back SE / FICA + penalties
view.s162a1.scr.test: Test: REASONABLE comp for services rendered
view.s162a1.scr.factors: Factors: training, experience, duties, time devoted, comparable positions, employee dividend history
view.s162a1.scr.cases: Watson v. Comm'r (2010): $24K salary on $371K K-1 distribution rejected — IRS adjusted to $90K
view.s162a1.scr.s262: IRS Fact Sheet 2008-25 + Notice 2018-2: documentation expectations
view.s162a1.scr.payroll_taxes: Reasonable comp study: PRO documents salary determination
view.s162a1.scr.flow_through: Recharacterization triggers: payroll taxes + Form 941 + W-2 amendments
view.s162a1.doc.compensation_study: Annual compensation study: benchmark to similar companies / positions
view.s162a1.doc.board_minutes: Board / shareholder minutes documenting comp decision + rationale
view.s162a1.doc.employment_agreement: Written employment agreement with specific salary / bonus provisions
view.s162a1.doc.performance_metrics: Performance metrics + bonus formulas in writing
view.s162a1.doc.contemporaneous: Decisions contemporaneous with payment (not after-the-fact rationalization)
view.s162a1.doc.bonus_clawback: Bonus clawback provisions strengthen reasonable comp argument
view.s162a1.doc.s_corp_compensation_stud: RCReports + Equilar + BLS data: industry benchmarks
view.s162a1.doc.salary_history: Maintain salary history showing pattern over years
view.s162a1.unreasonable_note: Compensation NOT reasonable: C-corp deduction disallowed for excess (DOUBLE TAX as dividend); S-corp reclassification triggers SE tax + payroll taxes + penalties. Obtain compensation study (RCReports, Equilar, BLS) + document board decision + maintain contemporaneous records.
view.s367a.h1.title: // § 367(a) OUTBOUND TANGIBLE
view.s367a.hint.intro: US transferor recognizes GAIN on outbound transfer of property to foreign corp. § 367(a)(1) general rule: foreign transferee treated as NOT a corp → no § 351 deferral. § 367(a)(3) ACTIVE business exception (HISTORICAL, TCJA 2017 NARROWED scope — for ACTIVE TRADE / BUSINESS US TANGIBLE property). Inventory + depreciable + installment obligations + receivables: NEVER qualify for exception. Compare § 367(d) IP outbound (annual deemed royalty). § 6038B reporting required.
view.s367a.basic.no_corp: Foreign transferee TREATED AS NOT A CORPORATION
view.s367a.basic.policy: Policy: prevent erosion of US tax base on built-in gain
view.s367a.basic.alternative: Alternative: § 367(d) IP / § 482 transfer pricing for ongoing royalties
view.s367a.basic.character: Character of gain: same as if sold at FMV (ordinary, capital, recapture)
view.s367a.basic.holding_period: Holding period: as if disposed of (resets in transferee's hands)
view.s367a.exc.active_pre_tcja: PRE-TCJA: active business exception EXEMPT property used in active foreign trade or business
view.s367a.exc.tcja_narrowed: TCJA 2017: REMOVED active business exception for IP, foreign goodwill, going concern
view.s367a.exc.remaining: REMAINING (post-TCJA): tangible property used in ACTIVE TRADE / BUSINESS outside US
view.s367a.exc.never_qualify: NEVER qualify: inventory, depreciable US-use property, installment notes, receivables, foreign currency obligations
view.s367a.exc.foreign_use: Foreign use: must be USED predominantly OUTSIDE US
view.s367a.exc.active_definition: Active TIB: § 1.367(a)-2 substantial managerial / operational activities
view.s367a.exc.recapture: Recapture upon disposition within 5 yrs (Gain Recognition Agreement)
view.s367a.exc.transferee_country: Transferee country: subject to subF, GILTI rules + ECI overlay
view.s367a.gra.purpose: 5-year deferral if transferor enters GRA + commits to recognize gain on triggering events
view.s367a.gra.triggering: Triggering events: sale of stock, dispositions of property, change in basis
view.s367a.gra.failure: Failure: full gain recognition retroactive + interest + penalties
view.s367a.gra.formal: Formal written agreement + IRS specific procedure
view.s367a.gra.s_corp: S-corp transferor: shareholders bear gain consequences
view.s367a.gra.6038b: Coordinate with § 6038B reporting (Form 926 — Return for U.S. Transferors of Property)
view.s367a.gra.lock_in: 5-year period locked in — cannot revoke unless replaced by new GRA
view.s367a.no_filing_note: § 6038B Form 926 REQUIRED for outbound transfers. Failure to file: $100,000 minimum penalty (or 10% of FMV if higher, capped at $100K). Statute of limitations remains open for 3 yrs after late filing. Penalty waived only for reasonable cause + GOOD FAITH effort.
view.s882.h1.title: // § 882 FOREIGN CORP ECI
view.s882.hint.intro: Foreign corp engaged in US trade / business: taxed at regular US corporate rates on ECI. § 882(a)(1): general rule — taxed AS IF a US corporation on ECI. § 882(c): deductions allowed only with TIMELY FILING of accurate Form 1120-F. § 882(d): election to treat US real property income as ECI. Treaty PE (Permanent Establishment) generally required if treaty country. FDAP separately withheld at 30% / treaty rate. § 884: branch profits tax (BPT) on dividend equivalent.
view.s882.eci.us_trade_business: Income effectively connected with US trade or business (USTB)
view.s882.eci.permanent_basis: Asset use test: assets used in conducting USTB
view.s882.eci.business_activities: Business activities test: substantial business activity in US giving rise to income
view.s882.eci.force_of_attraction: Force of attraction (limited): treaties often limit § 882 to permanent establishment
view.s882.eci.us_real_property: US real property: per § 897 FIRPTA — gain treated as ECI by election
view.s882.eci.dividends: Dividends + interest may be ECI if connected to USTB
view.s882.eci.s897: § 897 FIRPTA: NRA / foreign corp gain on USRPI = ECI
view.s882.eci.s1446f: § 1446(f): sale of US partnership interest = ECI portion deemed sold
view.s882.pe.threshold: Treaty country: § 882 only if treaty PE exists (OECD Article 5)
view.s882.pe.fixed_place: PE: fixed place of business through which business carried on
view.s882.pe.examples: Examples: branch office, factory, mine, well, warehouse, dependent agent
view.s882.pe.exclusions: Excl: independent agent, storage, display, processing by third party
view.s882.pe.services: Services PE: services performed > 183 days within 12-month period (US-CA, US-NL)
view.s882.pe.attributable: Income attributable to PE — arms-length business
view.s882.pe.agent: Agent acting on behalf (binding contracts) — creates PE
view.s882.ded.timely_filing: Deductions ALLOWED only if Form 1120-F filed within 18 months of original due date
view.s882.ded.protective_filing: Protective filing: Form 1120-F as 'Pro-forma' + statement of intent
view.s882.ded.charitable: Charitable: limited to 10% of taxable income before charitable deduction
view.s882.ded.s_corp_method: SE method: regulated investment company method (rare)
view.s882.ded.consolidated_return: Consolidated filing: with US affiliates if US sub member
view.s882.ded.s108_cod: § 108 COD income: complex application to foreign corp ECI
view.s882.rel.s884: § 884: branch profits tax — 30% on dividend equivalent amount
view.s882.rel.s897: § 897: FIRPTA real estate gain treated as ECI
view.s882.rel.s903_ftc: § 903 FTC: foreign tax credit available for foreign tax on ECI
view.s882.rel.s7701b: § 7701(b): does not apply to foreign corps (only individuals)
view.s882.rel.s1446a: § 1446(a): partnership ECI withholding on foreign partners
view.s882.rel.s367b: § 367(b): inbound rearrangements impact foreign corp tax
view.s882.deductions_lost_note: Deductions DENIED under § 882(c) — Form 1120-F not filed within 18 months. Taxed on GROSS ECI without any deductions. CRITICAL: file Form 1120-F (or protective Pro-forma) within 18 months even if uncertain about ECI. Reasonable cause exception very narrow.
view.s884.h1.title: // § 884 BRANCH PROFITS TAX
view.s884.hint.intro: 30% (or treaty rate) on Dividend Equivalent Amount (DEA) — branch profits NOT reinvested in US. DEA = ECI earnings × (1 − change in US-connected E&P kept in US) − after-tax ECI. Coordinate with § 882: ECI taxed at 21%, then DEA taxed at 30% → DOUBLE LAYER (similar to corp + dividend). Treaty reductions: 5%/15% typical (similar to dividends). § 884(f): branch interest at 30% similar to dividend equivalents. Form 1120-F + Form 8848.
view.s884.dea.step1: Step 1: After-tax ECI = ECI - § 882 tax (21% corp rate)
view.s884.dea.step2: Step 2: Increase / decrease in US-connected E&P (reinvested in US)
view.s884.dea.step3: Step 3: DEA = after-tax ECI − increase in US E&P + decrease
view.s884.dea.step4: Step 4: Apply 30% (or treaty rate) to DEA
view.s884.dea.s884_e: § 884(e): election to treat as accumulated branch profits
view.s884.dea.termination: § 884(d): election to terminate USTB → triggers final BPT on accumulated
view.s884.dea.minimum: Minimum: BPT applies even if no current-year ECI if accumulated US E&P
view.s884.dea.character: Character: treated as if dividend paid to foreign parent
view.s884.lob.purpose: Prevent treaty shopping (third-country residents claiming treaty benefits)
view.s884.lob.qualified_resident: 'Qualified Person' tests: publicly traded, ownership tests, active business
view.s884.lob.derivative_benefits: Derivative benefits: 95%/95% ownership by qualified residents of treaty countries
view.s884.lob.objective_tests: Objective tests: ownership, base erosion, active trade
view.s884.lob.us_treaties: All US treaties have LOB articles since US Model 2006
view.s884.lob.fdap_separate: FDAP withholding: separate W-8BEN-E + treaty claim
view.s884.bi.purpose: Prevent base erosion by paying interest to foreign parent / affiliates
view.s884.bi.rate: 30% on EXCESS interest paid to foreign related party
view.s884.bi.actually_paid: Tax on interest actually paid (not deemed)
view.s884.bi.excess: Excess interest = paid - allowable = subject to 30% BPT-like tax
view.s884.bi.treaty_reduction: Treaty rates apply similar to dividends
view.s884.bi.deduction_limits: § 163(j) limit may also apply to interest deduction
view.s884.no_treaty_note: Treaty BPT reduction NOT applied. Verify LOB qualification (Qualified Person tests): publicly traded, ownership / base erosion tests, or active trade. Form 8833 required to invoke treaty. Default 30% BPT applied. Common error: treaty residence does not equal LOB eligibility — foreign holding companies often fail LOB.
view.s6045.h1.title: // § 6045 BROKER REPORTING
view.s6045.hint.intro: Broker must report sales of securities + BASIS information to IRS + customer via Form 1099-B. Covered securities (since 2011): equities, mutual funds, options, futures, debt instruments. Basis methods: FIFO (default), LIFO, specific identification, average cost (mutual funds). Wash sale + dividend reinvestment + corporate action adjustments included. Box B: basis NOT reported to IRS (legacy / uncovered). § 6045(g): digital asset broker reporting (post-2023 Form 1099-DA).
view.s6045.cov.mutual_fund_2012: Mutual funds + ETFs: acquired AFTER 1/1/2012 — covered
view.s6045.cov.bonds_2014: Less complex bonds: acquired AFTER 1/1/2014 — covered
view.s6045.cov.complex_2016: Complex debt + options: acquired AFTER 1/1/2016 — covered
view.s6045.cov.digital_2025: Digital assets: brokers report effective 2025 (Form 1099-DA + Form 1099-DA-G)
view.s6045.cov.uncovered: Uncovered: pre-effective-date acquisitions OR transferred from non-broker
view.s6045.cov.box_a: Box A: short-term basis reported to IRS
view.s6045.cov.box_d: Box D: long-term basis reported to IRS
view.s6045.cov.box_b: Box B: short-term, basis NOT reported (taxpayer responsibility)
view.s6045.cov.box_e: Box E: long-term, basis NOT reported
view.s6045.bm.fifo_default: FIFO: oldest lots sold first — DEFAULT for stocks if no election
view.s6045.bm.lifo: LIFO: newest lots first (uncommon for equities, common bonds)
view.s6045.bm.specific_id: Specific identification: choose specific lot — REQUIRES BROKER CONFIRMATION at sale (settlement date)
view.s6045.bm.average_cost: Average cost: mutual funds only — historical default; locked once elected
view.s6045.bm.hifo: HIFO: tax-loss harvesting (highest basis first to maximize loss / minimize gain)
view.s6045.bm.tax_lot_optimization: Tax lot optimization: most brokers offer auto-HIFO / 'max tax loss' mode
view.s6045.bm.dividend_reinvestment: Dividend reinvestment: each share treated as separate lot
view.s6045.bm.election_method: Election made AT or BEFORE sale (per share lot) — broker confirms in writing
view.s6045.ws.30_days: Wash sale: buy 'substantially identical' within 30 days before / after sale at loss
view.s6045.ws.disallowed: Loss DISALLOWED + ADDED to basis of replacement shares
view.s6045.ws.holding_period: Holding period of replacement shares TACKS from disallowed sale
view.s6045.ws.same_broker_account: Within same account only — IRS Rev. Rul. 2008-5 (across accounts also)
view.s6045.ws.spouse_iras: Cross-account: IRA / 401(k) / spouse account also can trigger
view.s6045.ws.crypto: Crypto: NO wash sale rule currently (proposed legislation)
view.s6045.ws.options_replacement: Option grant to buy same security: triggers wash sale
view.s6045.ws.short_sales: Short sale closure triggers similar 30-day rule (Rev. Rul. 60-159)
view.s6045.recon.code: Code column: adjustments codes (W wash sale, B basis adj, T term adj, etc.)
view.s6045.recon.schedule_d: Schedule D: net gain / loss from Form 8949
view.s6045.recon.basis_difference: Basis difference from 1099-B: report adjustments + explanation
view.s6045.recon.cross_reference: Cross-reference: 1099-B box totals to Schedule D line totals
view.s6045.recon.estate_step_up: Inherited: § 1014 step-up to date-of-death FMV (often higher basis)
view.s6045.recon.gift: Gift: § 1015 carryover basis + dual basis rule
view.s6045.box_b_note: Box B (basis NOT reported to IRS): taxpayer must independently track + report basis. Common for: pre-2011 stock acquisitions, ESPP / RSU shares (often broker basis incorrect), inherited / gifted shares, transfers between brokers. Maintain personal records — IRS may audit if numbers diverge from broker reporting.
view.s263c.h1.title: // § 263(c) IDC ELECTION
view.s263c.hint.intro: Operators / working interest owners can ELECT to deduct Intangible Drilling Costs (IDC) CURRENTLY instead of capitalizing. IDCs: labor, fuel, repairs, hauling, supplies in drilling / development of wells. Independent producers + royalty owners: 100% deductible. Integrated oil cos: 70% deductible / 30% amortized 60 months. AMT preference: excess IDC over 65% of net oil + gas income. § 1254 recapture upon disposition. Reg § 1.612-4 election.
view.s263c.comp.labor: Labor: drilling crew, supervision, geological labor
view.s263c.comp.supplies: Supplies: drilling mud, fluids, chemicals, abrasives
view.s263c.comp.dry_hole: Dry hole costs: §263(c) currently deductible (or capitalize)
view.s263c.amt.preference: AMT preference item: excess of IDC deduction over IDC-amortized basis
view.s263c.amt.formula: Preference = current IDC deduction - (current IDC if amortized over 10 yrs)
view.s263c.amt.65pct: Adjustment limited to 65% of net oil + gas income for the year
view.s263c.amt.integrated: Integrated oil co: still subject to AMT preference
view.s263c.amt.regular_tax: Preference adds back to AMTI; not a tax — feeds into AMT calculation
view.s263c.amt.individual_corp: Both individual + corp AMT (TCJA repealed corp AMT 2018-2022; now CAMT)
view.s263c.amt.s56: § 56(g) related: passive activity loss disallowance for IDC
view.s263c.rec.purpose: Recapture intangible costs deducted on disposition (parallel § 1245 / § 1250)
view.s263c.rec.amount: Ordinary income up to amount of prior IDC + depletion deductions
view.s263c.rec.basis: Recapture basis: cumulative IDC + depletion above mineral basis
view.s263c.rec.disposition_types: Sale, exchange, abandonment, distribution all trigger
view.s263c.rec.holding_period_gain: Beyond recapture: § 1231 gain (capital) vs § 1245 if equipment recapture
view.s263c.rec.related_party: Related party: § 1239 may recharacterize as ordinary income (if depreciable to buyer)
view.s263c.rec.s291_corp: § 291 corporate cutback: 20% of S corp / C corp § 1254 recapture (parallel)
view.s263c.indep_note: Independent producer: 100% IDC current deduction + § 57(a)(2)(E) AMT exemption. Critical tax benefit for small / mid-size E&P companies. Limit: 75,000 bbl/day production. Combined with § 613A small producer depletion deduction (15%): substantial tax shelter for working interest investors.
view.s412.h1.title: // § 412 PENSION FUNDING
view.s412.hint.intro: DB plans must satisfy minimum funding to maintain qualified status under § 401(a). § 430 single-employer / § 431 multi-employer. Funding shortfall: triggers PBGC variable-rate premium + § 4971 excise tax (10% / 100% cumulative). Quarterly contributions required if shortfall + at-risk status. At-risk: < 80% / 70% funded → benefit restrictions + accelerated contributions. PBGC premium: $101 flat + 5.25% variable on shortfall (2025).
view.s412.h2.s4971: § 4971 excise tax for failure to fund
view.s412.mrc.target: Funding Target: PV of benefits accrued to date
view.s412.mrc.normal: Target Normal Cost: PV of benefits expected to accrue this year
view.s412.mrc.mrc: MRC = normal cost + shortfall amortization + at-risk add-on
view.s412.mrc.at_risk: At-risk: additional load factor on target normal cost
view.s412.mrc.quarterly: Quarterly contributions required if shortfall exists
view.s412.mrc.due_date: Quarterly due dates: 15 days after quarter end
view.s412.atr.test: Test: Funded Target % < 80% AND Funded % AT-RISK < 70%
view.s412.atr.computation: At-risk computation: accelerated assumptions + load factor
view.s412.atr.benefit_restrictions: § 436 benefit restrictions: no lump sums, no accruals if < 60% funded
view.s412.atr.notice: Notice to participants: required when in at-risk + benefit restrictions
view.s412.atr.continue: Continued at-risk: 3 consecutive years → 'at-risk' load factor permanently
view.s412.atr.exemption: Recent shortfall exemption: temporary relief in plan years 2014-2024 (extension acts)
view.s412.atr.transition: Transition: gradual rule application for newly at-risk plans
view.s412.pbgc.variable: Variable-rate premium: 5.25% × unfunded vested benefits (capped at $652/participant)
view.s412.pbgc.partition: SFA (Special Financial Assistance) Act 2021: $86B grant to underfunded multi-employer plans
view.s412.pbgc.form_5500: Form 5500 includes PBGC premium worksheet (Schedule SB)
view.s412.exc.tier_1: Tier 1: 10% of accumulated funding deficiency annually
view.s412.exc.tier_2: Tier 2: 100% if not corrected within taxable period
view.s412.exc.minimum: Reportable on Form 5330 separately from Form 5500
view.s412.exc.fiduciary: Fiduciary liability: trustees may be personally liable
view.s412.exc.contribution_deductibility: Tax deduction available for contributions (subject to § 404 limit)
view.s412.exc.waiver: § 412(c) waiver: temporary funding waiver (rare, limited duration)
view.s412.exc.cure_period: Cure period: usually 90 days to cure with payment
view.s412.exc.disqualification: Beyond cure: § 401(a) plan disqualification = all contributions taxable
view.s412.at_risk_note: AT-RISK STATUS: § 430 load factor adds 40% to target normal cost. § 436 benefit restrictions: no lump sums (< 80%); no future accruals (< 60%); 50% lump sum available at 60-80%. Notify participants. Address shortfall via: increase contributions, plan freezes, benefit formula reduction (no clawback of accrued).
view.s1362.h1.title: // § 1362 S-CORP ELECTION
view.s1362.hint.intro: Form 2553 filed by 15th day of 3rd month of tax year for election that year. ALL shareholders must consent in writing. Termination triggers: revocation, > 100 shareholders, ineligible shareholder, > 25% passive income (3 consecutive yrs with C-corp E&P). Late election relief: Rev. Proc. 2013-30 + Rev. Proc. 2022-19 — relief often available within 3 yrs 75 days. 5-year post-termination wait before re-electing (unless IRS consent). Inadvertent termination relief § 1362(f) — taxpayer + IRS in tandem.
view.s1362.label.classes: More than one class of stock?
view.s1362.mech.deadline: By 15th day of 3rd month of tax year (March 15 for calendar)
view.s1362.mech.late_75: 75-day late election available with reasonable cause
view.s1362.mech.signatures: ALL shareholders + spouses (community property states) must sign
view.s1362.mech.entity_class: Entity must be eligible S corp: domestic, ≤ 100 shareholders, no ineligible, 1 class of stock
view.s1362.mech.tax_year: Choose acceptable tax year (calendar OR § 444 fiscal w/ § 7519 deposit)
view.s1362.mech.confirmation: IRS confirmation: CP261 notice (acceptance) within 60 days
view.s1362.mech.no_form_filed: If no CP261: contact IRS — common error
view.s1362.mech.qsst_esbt: QSST / ESBT trust elections: required to be eligible shareholders
view.s1362.term.revocation: Revocation: majority shareholder consent + Form 2553A statement
view.s1362.term.ineligible: Ineligible shareholder (corp, partnership, NRA, non-grantor trust)
