view.s1362.term.classes: 2+ classes of stock (voting / non-voting OK; different distribution rights NOT)
view.s1362.term.passive_income: > 25% passive income 3 consecutive years + has C-corp E&P → automatic termination
view.s1362.term.inadvertent_relief: Inadvertent termination: § 1362(f) relief if cured promptly
view.s1362.term.5_year_wait: 5-year wait before re-election (IRS consent required to shorten)
view.s1362.lr.purpose: Cure missed election + missed terminating events without PLR
view.s1362.lr.scope: Scope: S-corp election, ESBT, QSST, QSub elections
view.s1362.lr.timing: Generally must request within 3 years 75 days of intended effective date
view.s1362.lr.requirements: Requirements: reasonable cause, return filed consistent w/ S, statement of facts
view.s1362.lr.late_filing: Late filing: attach Form 2553 to first late return + Rev. Proc. reference
view.s1362.lr.2022_19: Rev. Proc. 2022-19: updated guidance + expanded scope for common errors
view.s1362.lr.user_fee: No user fee — free relief if timely
view.s1362.lr.plr_alternative: PLR alternative: ~$30K+ fee + 6-9 months wait if outside automatic relief
view.s1362.es.qsst: Qualified Subchapter S Trust (QSST) — single beneficiary US person
view.s1362.es.esbt: Electing Small Business Trust (ESBT) — multi-beneficiary US persons
view.s1362.es.iras: IRA shareholders (post-2017 for bank S-corps only)
view.s1362.es.grantor_trust: Grantor trust (during grantor's lifetime + 2 yrs after death)
view.s1362.es.no_corp: NOT eligible: corp, partnership, foreign trust, NRA, multi-beneficiary trust w/o ESBT
view.s1362.terminated_note: S election TERMINATED automatically. 5-year wait before re-election (IRS consent required to shorten). Apply for inadvertent termination relief under § 1362(f) if facts permit — requires showing termination was inadvertent + cured promptly + reasonable. PLR application typically required. Tax cost: C-corp double tax until cured.
view.s414.h1.title: // § 414 AGGREGATED EMPLOYER
view.s414.hint.intro: § 414(b) controlled group: parent-subsidiary (80%+ ownership) OR brother-sister (5 or fewer common owners; 80%+ common + 50%+ identical). § 414(c): non-corporate trades / businesses under common control (LLCs, partnerships, sole props). § 414(m) affiliated service group: shared services + first-service org rules. Purpose: mandatory aggregation for benefit plan testing, retirement limits, ACA, deferred comp. Form 5500 + benefit coverage tests (§ 410(b), § 401(a)(4), § 416 top-heavy).
view.s414.ps.test: Test: at least one corp owns ≥ 80% of another → controlled group
view.s414.ps.chain: Chain: A owns 80% of B owns 80% of C → A, B, C aggregated
view.s414.ps.intermediary: Intermediary: stock of subs aggregated to identify common owner
view.s414.ps.measurement: Measurement: voting power AND value (both required)
view.s414.ps.exempt: Excluded: certain regulated investment companies, REITs, others
view.s414.ps.ss_employee: Direct ownership counts; constructive ownership often controlling
view.s414.bs.test_5: Test: 5 or fewer common shareholders own ≥ 80% of each corp
view.s414.bs.test_50: AND those 5 or fewer common shareholders have ≥ 50% IDENTICAL ownership
view.s414.bs.identical: Identical: smallest interest each owner has in any corp
view.s414.bs.example: Example: A owns 60% Corp 1 + 30% Corp 2; B owns 40% Corp 1 + 70% Corp 2 → not controlled (only 30% identical for A, 40% for B)
view.s414.bs.5_taken_with: 'Taken together w/ each other' 5 or fewer + 80% + 50% identical
view.s414.bs.individuals_estates_trusts: Individuals, estates, trusts only (not corporate owners)
view.s414.bs.spouse: Spouses: constructive ownership unless legally separated
view.s414.asg.purpose: Captures shared service arrangements not caught by controlled group
view.s414.asg.fso: First Service Org (FSO): performs services for / with B-org
view.s414.asg.a_org: A-Org: B-org provides services FOR FSO; B-org owners involved
view.s414.asg.b_org: B-Org: ≥ 10% owned by FSO owners; provides services TO FSO
view.s414.asg.management: Management company test: B-org primarily manages FSO
view.s414.asg.medical: Medical practices: separate professional corps + management LLC = ASG
view.s414.asg.law_firms: Law firms / accountants: similar professional service organization rules
view.s414.asg.complex: Complex test — IRS Form 5300 PLR for confirmation
view.s414.ap.coverage: § 410(b) plan coverage test (ratio percentage / average benefits)
view.s414.ap.compensation: § 401(a)(17) comp limit ($345K 2024) — per group not per entity
view.s414.ap.s415: § 415 retirement plan limit ($69K 2024 DC, $275K DB) — per group
view.s414.ap.s401_k: § 401(k) deferral limit ($23,500 2025) — per individual across all employers in group
view.s414.ap.aca_50: ACA: aggregate 50+ FTE employer mandate trigger
view.s414.ap.s409a: § 409A deferred comp: timing rules + 6-month delay for specified employees
view.s414.aggregated_note: AGGREGATED for § 414: benefit plan coverage testing must include ALL group employees. § 401(k) deferral limit shared across employers. § 415 retirement plan limit per group. Top-heavy testing across group. ACA 50+ FTE: aggregated. § 409A specified employee determined at group level. Form 5500 may need single filing or coordinated.
view.s472.h1.title: // § 472 LIFO INVENTORY METHOD
view.s472.hint.intro: LIFO election: identify ending inventory at COST of most recent acquisitions. During inflation: HIGHER COGS + LOWER taxable income vs FIFO. Book conformity rule (§ 472(c)): financial reporting MUST also use LIFO. Dollar-Value LIFO: aggregates similar items into pools by index. LIFO recapture (§ 1363(d)): 4-year recapture on C-corp to S-corp conversion. § 472(e): termination of LIFO method. Form 970 filed to elect. IFRS does NOT allow LIFO (global comparison).
view.s472.mech.layers: 'Layers': each year's purchases at year-end cost; oldest at bottom
view.s472.mech.cogs: COGS = most recent purchases (top of LIFO stack)
view.s472.mech.ending: Ending inventory: older layers preserved (potentially decades old)
view.s472.mech.specific_goods: Specific goods LIFO: track each item type separately
view.s472.mech.dollar_value: Dollar-Value LIFO: aggregate by FMV index (less complex tracking)
view.s472.mech.simplified_dollar: Simplified Dollar-Value LIFO: BLS / IPIC index allowed for smaller business
view.s472.mech.lifo_reserve: LIFO Reserve: difference between FIFO and LIFO inventory = deferred income
view.s472.mech.liquidation: LIFO liquidation: dipping into old layers releases low-cost inventory → income spike
view.s472.bc.basic: REQUIREMENT: use LIFO for FINANCIAL reporting too (GAAP / IFRS)
view.s472.bc.cumulative_only: Disclosure of LIFO Reserve in financial statements: REQUIRED + ALLOWED
view.s472.bc.cost_method: 'Cost method' — historical cost basis for LIFO
view.s472.bc.ifrs: IFRS conversion: IFRS PROHIBITS LIFO — forces LIFO termination if going IFRS
view.s472.bc.consequence: Violation: § 472(c) revokes LIFO election + retroactive FIFO
view.s472.bc.parent_sub: Consolidated reporting: each entity's election counts separately
view.s472.bc.terminate: Termination: § 472(e) — recapture LIFO reserve over 3-4 years (Form 3115 method change)
view.s472.rec.basic: C-corp using LIFO converting to S-corp: 4-yr recapture of LIFO Reserve
view.s472.rec.formula: Recapture amount = LIFO Reserve = FIFO basis - LIFO basis
view.s472.rec.payments: 25% recapture in C-corp final year + 25% × 3 yrs by S-corp
view.s472.rec.installments: Section 1378 installment provisions: pay over 4 yrs interest-free
view.s472.rec.aging_inventory: Common in long-established businesses with significant LIFO reserves
view.s472.rec.partial_election: Partial S-election (split between active + holding) may reduce trigger
view.s472.rec.recent_examples: Recent: oil + gas + heavy industry with old LIFO layers
view.s472.rec.estate_planning: Estate planning: consider LIFO recapture before S-election + heirs
view.s472.no_conformity_note: Book conformity rule VIOLATED — § 472(c) requires LIFO for financial reporting too. IRS may revoke LIFO election retroactively, requiring FIFO recomputation + interest + penalties on understated tax. Disclose LIFO Reserve in financial statement notes; don't show 'as-if FIFO' inventory on main balance sheet.
view.s901j.h1.title: // § 901(j) SANCTIONED FTC DENIAL
view.s901j.hint.intro: NO FTC ALLOWED for taxes paid to countries on State Department sanctioned list. Currently sanctioned (2024): Iran, North Korea, Syria, Cuba, Venezuela (partial), Russia (post-2022, but treaty suspended). FTC denial applies to ALL taxes (income, withholding, branch) to these countries. Conduit jurisdictions: routing through unsanctioned countries DOES NOT restore FTC if income clearly tied to sanctioned source. OFAC licenses: obtained licenses for certain activities don't restore FTC.
view.s901j.list.update_status: List updates via Executive Orders + State Department
view.s901j.scope.all_taxes: ALL taxes denied: income, withholding, VAT, customs duties, branch profits
view.s901j.scope.cfc_holding: CFCs in sanctioned country: subF + GILTI inclusions still apply
view.s901j.scope.no_credit: No FTC + no deduction (under § 901(j)) — pure tax loss
view.s901j.scope.section_960: § 960 deemed-paid credit: also denied for sanctioned country taxes
view.s901j.scope.no_basket: No special basket — denial is absolute
view.s901j.scope.subF_double_tax: Combined effect: subF inclusion + foreign tax + no FTC = double taxation
view.s901j.scope.deduction_alternative: May claim deduction (subject to limit) instead — limited utility
view.s901j.scope.permanent: Designation permanent until State Department removes from list
view.s901j.work.exit: Exit sanctioned country operations: divest before designation hardens
view.s901j.work.deduct_loss: Take deduction (not credit) — subject to deduction limits + at-risk rules
view.s901j.work.no_routing: Routing through 3rd country DOES NOT restore FTC for sanctioned-source income
view.s901j.work.cfc_basis: CFC basis: still recognize basis decrease per § 961(b) for sanctioned-tax payments
view.s901j.work.gilti_avoidance: GILTI: high-tax election § 954(b)(4) DOES NOT eliminate sanctioned country tax
view.s901j.work.exclusion: No exception for OFAC-licensed activities (e.g., medical / humanitarian)
view.s901j.work.exit_strategy: Pre-divestiture: claim historic FTC for years before designation
view.s901j.work.dual_resident: Dual treaty residency may shift source to non-sanctioned country (rare)
view.s901j.rem.process: Removal: Executive Order rescinding sanction → automatic § 901(j) removal
view.s901j.rem.historical: Historical removals: Libya (2006), Sudan (2017), various smaller adjustments
view.s901j.rem.transitional: Transitional rule: tax paid AFTER removal eligible for FTC
view.s901j.rem.prior_year_recovery: Cannot recover FTC for prior years (no retroactive)
view.s901j.rem.notice: IRS Notice often confirms FTC restored
view.s901j.rem.cuba_partial: Cuba: partial sanctions reductions in 2014-2016 + reversal 2017
view.s901j.rem.iran_jcpoa: Iran JCPOA period (2015-2017): partial relief; reverted with US withdrawal
view.s901j.rem.uncertainty: Political volatility: sanctions can change quickly with administration changes
view.s901j.denied_note: FTC DENIED under § 901(j) for sanctioned country. Foreign tax becomes pure cost — only 21% recovery via deduction. CFC + subF / GILTI inclusion still applies. Routing through unsanctioned 3rd country DOES NOT restore FTC. Consider divestiture before sanctions harden; lobby for treaty protection (unlikely for hostile states).
view.s248.h1.title: // § 248 ORGANIZATIONAL EXPENDITURES
view.s248.hint.intro: $5K immediate deduction in first tax year + remaining amortized over 180 months (15 yrs). Phaseout: $5K reduces $-for-$ when org exp > $50K → no immediate at $55K. Parallel: § 195 startup + § 709 partnership organizational. Eligible expenses: legal fees, accounting fees, state filing fees, incorporation costs. NOT eligible: stock issuance costs, transfer of assets, lobbying. Election: made on first return; no separate form (Reg § 1.248-1).
view.s248.eligible.legal: Legal fees: incorporation, drafting articles + bylaws, organizing meetings
view.s248.eligible.accounting: Accounting fees: setting up books + records + first audit
view.s248.eligible.state_filing: State filing fees: Articles of Incorporation, Statement of Information
view.s248.eligible.federal_filing: Federal filing fees: EIN, S-corp Form 2553, exempt org Form 1023
view.s248.eligible.organizational_meetin: Organizational meetings + temporary directors / officers compensation
view.s248.eligible.printing: Printing of stock certificates, corporate seals, minutes book
view.s248.eligible.registered_agent: Registered agent / resident agent setup fees
view.s248.ineligible.stock_issuance: Stock issuance costs: § 263(a) capital expenditure — reduce stock proceeds
view.s248.ineligible.transfer_assets: Transfer of assets to new corp: separate § 351 / § 1032 treatment
view.s248.elect.default: DEFAULT election: deemed elected if return reports per § 248 treatment
view.s248.elect.no_form: No separate election form — choice made on first return
view.s248.elect.amortization_begin: Amortization begins month business begins (active business start)
view.s248.elect.short_period: Short first year: prorated based on months
view.s248.elect.election_capitalize: Election to CAPITALIZE entire amount (no current deduction): irrevocable
view.s248.phaseout_note: Organizational expenditures EXCEED $55K phaseout — NO immediate $5K deduction. Entire amount must be amortized over 180 months. Common for sophisticated corporate structures with extensive legal, accounting, securities counsel work. Consider whether to fully capitalize to basis instead (election made on first return).
view.s6041.h1.title: // § 6041 INFO REPORTING (1099)
view.s6041.hint.intro: Payers must report $600+ payments to non-corp persons for services (NEC), rent, prizes, royalties (MISC). Threshold: $600 / yr per recipient. Form 1099-NEC (Box 1 nonemployee compensation) — since PATH Act 2020 separate from MISC. Form 1099-MISC (Boxes 1-14: rent, royalties, prizes, medical, attorney fees, etc.). Corporations exempt EXCEPT: attorneys, medical providers, federally-regulated. Backup withholding 24% if no TIN. Form 1096 transmittal.
view.s6041.exc.corp_default: Corporations generally NOT subject to § 6041 reporting
view.s6041.exc.attorneys: EXCEPT: attorney fees (always reportable, even to law firm corp)
view.s6041.exc.federal_govt: EXCEPT: federal executive agencies + federal contractors
view.s6041.exc.s_corp: S-corps + LLCs (electing corp status): generally treated as corps
view.s6041.exc.llc: LLCs: depends on TIN type (corp = exempt; partnership = reportable)
view.s6041.bw.rate: Rate: 24% on REPORTABLE PAYMENT (since 2018 TCJA reduction from 28%)
view.s6041.bw.triggers: Triggers: (1) no TIN provided, (2) incorrect TIN, (3) IRS notification (B-Notice)
view.s6041.bw.w9: Solution: get Form W-9 (Request for Taxpayer Identification Number)
view.s6041.bw.b_notice: B-Notice / C-Notice: IRS sends to payer when discrepancy detected
view.s6041.bw.recipient_credit: Recipient credits BW against income tax on 1040 / 1120
view.s6041.bw.report_box4: Report on Form 1099 Box 4 (Federal Income Tax Withheld)
view.s6041.bw.remit_945: Remit via Form 945 (Annual Return of Withheld Federal Income Tax)
view.s6041.bw.penalty: Payer penalty: 100% of unwithheld amount + interest if failure
view.s6041.dl.misc_recipient: 1099-MISC: to recipient by January 31 (Boxes 8, 10 — by February 15)
view.s6041.dl.efile_threshold: E-file mandatory: 10+ returns aggregate (post-2024 IRS regs)
view.s6041.dl.extension: Extension: Form 8809 (30 days automatic, may extend 30 more)
view.s6041.dl.correction: Correction: file CORR 1099 promptly when error discovered
view.s6041.pen.tier1: Tier 1: $60 per form if corrected within 30 days (max $230K)
view.s6041.pen.tier2: Tier 2: $120 per form if corrected by August 1 (max $683K)
view.s6041.pen.tier3: Tier 3: $330 per form if NOT corrected by August 1 (max $1.37M)
view.s6041.pen.intentional: Intentional disregard: $660 per form + UNLIMITED (no cap)
view.s6041.pen.small_biz: Small biz ($5M or less): reduced caps ($79K / $239K / $479K)
view.s6041.pen.s6722: § 6722 statement to payee: similar penalties for failure to furnish
view.s6041.pen.reasonable_cause: Reasonable cause exception: Boyle case strict (Form 1099 disputes rare)
view.s6041.pen.de_minimis: De minimis: 10 or less incorrect forms (or 1% of total) — no penalty
view.s6041.bw_note: BACKUP WITHHOLDING REQUIRED — no W-9 / TIN from recipient. Withhold 24% from each payment + remit via Form 945 + report Box 4 of 1099. Recipient claims credit on income tax return. Failure to withhold: payer personally liable for entire amount. Obtain W-9 IMMEDIATELY before next payment.
view.s6049.h1.title: // § 6049 INTEREST REPORTING (1099-INT)
view.s6049.hint.intro: Payers must report $10+ interest paid + $600+ in trade/business interest. Form 1099-INT: Box 1 (interest income), Box 2 (early withdrawal penalty), Box 3 (US gov interest), Box 4 (federal withholding), Box 8 (tax-exempt interest), Box 9 (private activity bond interest — AMT prefer item). Form 1099-OID separate for original issue discount. Foreign payees: Form 1042-S separate regime (with W-8BEN). Bank deposit interest to NRAs: § 871(i) exemption from withholding (since 2013).
view.s6049.box.2: Box 2: early withdrawal penalty (deductible on Schedule 1)
view.s6049.box.9: Box 9: specified private activity bond interest (AMT preference)
view.s6049.box.14: Box 14: tax-exempt + tax credit bond CUSIP
view.s6049.exem.tin: Less than $10: no reporting (Box 1 + Box 3 combined)
view.s6049.exem.muni_exempt: Tax-exempt entities: NOT EXEMPT from reporting (but they still get 1099-INT)
view.s6049.exem.bank_dep_nra: Bank deposits to NRAs: § 871(i) exemption from 30% withholding (since 2013); still reported via 1042-S
view.s6049.exem.portfolio_int: § 871(h) portfolio interest: 0% withholding for foreign payees
view.s6049.exem.under_10: Threshold: Box 1 + Box 3 combined ≥ $10 → must file
view.s6049.te.federal: Federal: municipal bond interest (Box 8) excluded from gross income
view.s6049.te.amt_pab: AMT preference: private activity bond interest (Box 9) — adds back for AMT
view.s6049.te.state_in_state: In-state municipal bonds: usually state tax exempt too
view.s6049.te.state_out_state: Out-of-state municipal: federal exempt + state TAXABLE (most states)
view.s6049.te.treasury_state: US Treasury / agency: state tax exempt (Box 3)
view.s6049.te.aotc: Tax-exempt interest affects: Social Security taxation, AOTC phaseout, IRA contribution limits
view.s6049.te.modified_agi: Modified AGI for ACA premium credit: includes tax-exempt interest
view.s6049.te.alternative_minimum: Pre-2018 individual AMT preference; post-2025 sunset uncertain
view.s6049.oid.separate: OID accrual reported on separate Form 1099-OID, not 1099-INT
view.s6049.oid.box1: 1099-OID Box 1: OID amount accrued in current year
view.s6049.oid.zero_coupon: Zero-coupon bonds: large OID accrual + no cash interest payments
view.s6049.oid.taxpayer_pays: Taxpayer pays tax on accrual EVEN WITHOUT cash interest (zero-coupon problem)
view.s6049.oid.tax_exempt: Tax-exempt municipal OID: excluded similar to regular muni interest
view.s6049.oid.short_term: Short-term obligations ≤ 1 year: no OID reporting
view.s6049.oid.de_minimis: De minimis OID: 0.25% × years to maturity → not reportable
view.s6049.pab_amt_note: Private Activity Bond (Box 9): federal tax-exempt for regular tax BUT AMT preference item adds back to AMTI. Watch out: high-income taxpayers w/ tax-exempt PAB income may unexpectedly hit AMT. Consider swapping to non-PAB muni bonds if AMT triggers consistently.
view.s6051.h1.title: // § 6051 W-2 WAGE STATEMENTS
view.s6051.hint.intro: Employers furnish W-2 Wage + Tax Statement to each employee + Social Security Administration. Box 1: federal wages (taxable). Box 3 + 5: Social Security + Medicare wages. Box 12: codes for retirement (D, E, G), HSA (W), dep care (DD), group life > $50K (C), etc. Form W-3 transmittal. Due January 31 (employee + SSA, regardless of paper/electronic). SSA matching: wages cross-check vs SSA records.
view.s6051.label.s_corp_premium: S-corp health premium added to Box 1 ($)
view.s6051.box.1: Box 1: Wages, tips, other compensation (subject to federal income tax)
view.s6051.box.3: Box 3: Social Security wages (up to wage base $176,100 for 2025)
view.s6051.box.12: Box 12: Various codes (see list — most common)
view.s6051.box.13: Box 13: Statutory employee / retirement plan / third-party sick pay
view.s6051.pre.401k: 401(k) / 403(b) elective deferrals: reduce Box 1 but NOT Box 3/5 (FICA still applies)
view.s6051.pre.health: § 125 health insurance premiums: reduce Box 1 + Box 3 + Box 5 (full pre-tax)
view.s6051.pre.hsa: HSA contributions: reduce Box 1 + Box 3 + Box 5 (full pre-tax)
view.s6051.pre.dep_care: Dependent care FSA: reduces Box 1 + Box 3 + Box 5 ($5K limit 2025)
view.s6051.pre.transit: Transit / parking: reduces Box 1 + Box 3 + Box 5 ($325/mo each 2025)
view.s6051.pre.health_pre_tax_vs_post: After-tax health insurance: does NOT reduce — paid from net pay
view.s6051.pre.imputed_income: Imputed income (group life > $50K): ADDS to Box 1 + Box 3 + Box 5
view.s6051.scorp.health_taxable: Health insurance premiums INCLUDED in Box 1 (TAXABLE wages)
view.s6051.scorp.no_fica: Health premiums NOT in Box 3/5 (NO FICA on this portion)
view.s6051.scorp.deduction: Owner takes § 162(l) above-the-line deduction on personal return
view.s6051.scorp.box_14: Optionally disclosed in Box 14 with code 'S CORP HEALTH'
view.s6051.scorp.k1_split: Net effect: tax-free for FICA but income tax-free via § 162(l)
view.s6051.scorp.hsa: HSA: similar rule — included in Box 1, but excluded from Box 3/5 if HDHP
view.s6051.scorp.life_insurance: Group term life: similar inclusion rules
view.s6051.dp.ssa: Due to SSA: January 31 (paper + electronic) since 2017 PATH Act
view.s6051.dp.w3: Form W-3 transmittal: submit with paper W-2; not required for SSA electronic
view.s6051.dp.s6722: § 6722 penalty: failure to furnish to employee — same tiers
view.s6051.dp.extension: Extension: Form 8809 (30-day automatic; only available if hardship)
view.s6051.scorp_note: 2%+ S-corp owner: health insurance premiums INCLUDED in Box 1 wages. NO FICA on health portion (Box 3/5 reduced). Owner takes § 162(l) above-the-line deduction on Form 1040. Net result: full income deductibility WITHOUT FICA. Box 14 disclosure recommended: 'S CORP HEALTH $X' for clarity.
view.s1273.h1.title: // § 1273 ORIGINAL ISSUE DISCOUNT
view.s1273.hint.intro: OID = Stated Redemption Price at Maturity − Issue Price. Accrued ratably over life of bond → INCLUDED IN INCOME each year (even WITHOUT cash receipt — zero coupon problem). De minimis test: OID < 0.25% × years to maturity → not OID. Constant yield method standard (compound basis, like loan amortization). Form 1099-OID reporting if accrual ≥ $10. Tax-exempt municipal OID: still accrued but tax-free. § 1278 market discount separate concept (post-issuance).
view.s1273.comp.step1: Step 1: Find Yield to Maturity (YTM) that makes PV of cash flows = issue price
view.s1273.comp.step2: Step 2: Period accrual = (adjusted issue price × YTM) − stated interest
view.s1273.comp.step3: Step 3: Adjusted issue price t+1 = adjusted issue price t + OID accrual
view.s1273.comp.step4: Step 4: Cumulative accrual = sum of all period accruals
view.s1273.comp.step5: Step 5: Holder's basis = original cost + cumulative OID accrual
view.s1273.comp.zero_coupon: Zero coupon: stated interest = 0; all accrual is OID
view.s1273.comp.daily: Daily accrual: prorate the period accrual
view.s1273.comp.partial_year: Partial-year holding: prorate based on days held
view.s1273.dm.formula: De minimis = stated redemption × 0.25% × years to maturity
view.s1273.dm.example: Example: $1,000 par × 0.25% × 10 yrs = $25 de minimis threshold
view.s1273.dm.below: If actual OID < de minimis → NOT treated as OID
view.s1273.dm.character: Below-threshold: gain on sale = capital gain (not ordinary OID)
view.s1273.dm.purpose: Purpose: prevent OID complexity on small discounts
view.s1273.dm.practical: Practical: short-maturity bonds rarely have significant OID
view.s1273.spec.tax_exempt: Tax-exempt OID: accrue normally but income excluded (Box 11 of 1099-OID)
view.s1273.spec.short_term: Short-term obligations ≤ 1 yr: no OID accrual; gain at sale = ordinary
view.s1273.spec.treasury_oid: US Treasury OID: state tax exempt (Box 8 of 1099-OID)
view.s1273.spec.zero_coupon_strips: Zero-coupon Treasury STRIPS: substantial annual OID accrual problem
view.s1273.spec.high_yield_oid: High Yield OID Discount Obligations § 163(e)(5): payer deduction limited
view.s1273.spec.aha: Applicable High-Yield Discount Obligation: > 5 yrs maturity + > AFR + 5%
view.s1273.spec.modification: § 1.1001-3: significant modification of debt = deemed new instrument with new OID
view.s1273.spec.distress: Distressed debt: deep discount may be OID, not market discount
view.s1273.zero_coupon_note: ZERO COUPON BOND: substantial annual OID accrual without cash receipts. Pay tax NOW on income you HAVEN'T received. Common problem for retail investors. Consider holding zero-coupon bonds in tax-deferred accounts (IRA, 401(k)) OR tax-exempt accounts (Roth IRA). Tax-exempt municipal zero-coupons (e.g., Build America Bonds, BABs): solved this issue.
view.s7702.h1.title: // § 7702 LIFE INSURANCE CONTRACT
view.s7702.hint.intro: Defines what qualifies as life insurance for tax purposes (cash value buildup tax-deferred). Must satisfy: (1) CVAT (Cash Value Accumulation Test) OR (2) GPT (Guideline Premium Test) + Cash Value Corridor. Failure: cash value taxed as ordinary income each year (tax-deferred status lost). § 7702A — MEC (Modified Endowment Contract): separate test prevents abuse via large premiums. 2021 update: interest rate floor lowered from 4% to 2% (Consolidated Appropriations Act 2021).
view.s7702.cvat.principle: Cash value at any time MAY NOT exceed net single premium for death benefit at that point
view.s7702.cvat.formula: Net single premium based on minimum mortality (CSO tables) + minimum interest rate
view.s7702.cvat.mortality: Mortality: 2001 Commissioners' Standard Ordinary (CSO) — replaced 1980 CSO
view.s7702.cvat.advantage: CVAT advantage: more cash value buildup permitted (less restrictive)
view.s7702.cvat.disadvantage: CVAT disadvantage: must constantly increase death benefit or contract becomes non-qualifying
view.s7702.cvat.simplification: Calculation: per $1,000 of death benefit × insured's age (CSO table)
view.s7702.cvat.common: Common for: whole life, single premium policies
view.s7702.gpt.test: Sum of premiums paid cannot exceed guideline single + guideline level premium limits
view.s7702.gpt.single: Guideline single premium: actuarial computation based on CSO + interest rate
view.s7702.gpt.level: Guideline level premium: amortized over insured's lifetime
view.s7702.gpt.cvct: PLUS Cash Value Corridor Test: cash value ≤ % of death benefit
view.s7702.gpt.corridor_ages: Corridor %: 250% at age 40, declines to 100% at age 95
view.s7702.gpt.common: Common for: universal life, variable life
view.s7702.gpt.advantage: GPT advantage: lower required death benefit relative to cash value
view.s7702.gpt.disadvantage: GPT disadvantage: less premium flexibility / lower cash accumulation
view.s7702.c.lower_floor: Interest rate floor lowered from 4% to 2% (60% of long-term AFR)
view.s7702.c.effect: Effect: ALLOWS HIGHER CASH VALUE relative to death benefit
view.s7702.c.products: Products developed: high cash value whole life designed under new rules
view.s7702.c.transition: Transition: existing policies grandfathered; new policies use new rules
view.s7702.c.industry: Industry impact: more flexible accumulation products available
view.s7702.c.future_review: Future: rates re-evaluated periodically based on AFR (no Congressional action)
view.s7702.fail.taxable: Failure: cash value increase taxed as ORDINARY INCOME each year
view.s7702.fail.no_death_benefit: Death benefit may still be tax-free under § 101
view.s7702.fail.no_loans_tax_free: Loans against cash value: now taxable (instead of tax-free)
view.s7702.fail.cure: Cure: adjust death benefit OR refund excess premiums
view.s7702.fail.mec: MEC § 7702A: distributions taxed FIFO income-first + 10% penalty < age 59.5
view.s7702.fail.investor_owned_life: Investor-owned life insurance (IOLI): § 264 disallows interest deduction
view.s7702.fail.charitable: Charitable: contribution of policy may be subject to § 170 limitations
view.s7702.fail_note: POLICY FAILED § 7702. Cash value buildup TAXED as ordinary income each year. Loans against cash value: TAXABLE. Death benefit may still be tax-free under § 101(a). CURE: increase death benefit (raise corridor) OR refund excess premiums + interest. Insurance company should self-monitor + warn before failure. Consult tax + insurance counsel before policy redesign.
view.s6033.h1.title: // § 6033 EXEMPT ORG FILING
view.s6033.hint.intro: 501(c) orgs must file annual return: Form 990, 990-EZ, 990-N, or 990-PF. Form selection based on gross receipts + total assets. Form 990-N (e-Postcard): ≤ $50K gross receipts. Form 990-EZ: < $200K receipts + < $500K assets. Form 990 (full): ≥ $200K OR ≥ $500K. Form 990-PF: ALL private foundations. Failure 3 consecutive years: AUTOMATIC LOSS of exempt status (§ 6033(j) — Pension Protection Act 2006). Public inspection: Forms 990 publicly available.
view.s6033.dl.standard: Standard deadline: 15th day of 5th month after fiscal year end (May 15 for calendar)
view.s6033.dl.extension: Form 8868 extension: 6-month automatic extension to November 15 (calendar)
view.s6033.dl.990n: Form 990-N: filed online via IRS e-Postcard portal (free)
view.s6033.dl.late_penalty: Late penalty: $20/day (up to $11K) or $100/day for large orgs (up to $56K)
view.s6033.dl.3_consecutive: 3 consecutive years failure: AUTOMATIC LOSS of exempt status
view.s6033.dl.reinstatement: Reinstatement via Form 1023 + Rev. Proc. 2014-11 streamlined process
view.s6033.dl.public_inspection: Public inspection: Forms 990 + 1023 available 3 yrs free at IRS
view.s6033.exc.religious_orders: Religious orders / mission societies: NOT required to file
view.s6033.exc.govt_instrumentalities: Governmental units / instrumentalities: NOT required to file
view.s6033.exc.exclusively_religious: Exclusively religious instructional orgs: NOT required to file
view.s6033.exc.us_possessions: US possession affiliated orgs: limited filing
view.s6033.exc.unincorporated: Unincorporated 501(c)(3) under religious affiliation: may not need to file
view.s6033.exc.affiliate_of_church: Integrated auxiliary of church (e.g., school): NOT required to file (Form 4720 exception)
view.s6033.exc.below_minimum: Below $50K + first year: may still file Form 990-N for transparency
view.s6033.sb.purpose: Discloses substantial contributors (≥ $5K AND ≥ 2% of total)
view.s6033.sb.501c3: 501(c)(3) public charity: only IRS sees names + addresses (post-2018 Rev. Proc. 2018-38)
view.s6033.sb.501c4: 501(c)(4) + (5) + (6): NOT required (donor anonymity post-2018 Rev. Proc.)
view.s6033.sb.501c3_pi: Public inspection: amounts only (not donor names) for 501(c)(3) public charities
view.s6033.sb.pf: Private foundations: full disclosure even publicly (different rule)
view.s6033.sb.amat_v_irs: Americans for Prosperity v. Bonta (2021): SCOTUS struck down CA/NY donor disclosure
view.s6033.sb.state_rules: State-level: NY + CA stopped requiring after AFP v. Bonta
view.s6033.sb.federal_continue: Federal disclosure to IRS (not public): continues regardless
view.s6033.revoked_note: AUTOMATIC REVOCATION: 3 consecutive years of failure to file. Exempt status LOST. Apply for reinstatement via Form 1023 + Rev. Proc. 2014-11 (streamlined available if eligible). Retroactive reinstatement possible if applied within 15 months of revocation notice. Public IRS list 'Auto Revocation' shows revoked orgs.
view.s7491.h1.title: // § 7491 BURDEN OF PROOF
view.s7491.hint.intro: IRS Restructuring + Reform Act 1998: shifted burden to IRS in certain cases. Burden shifts if: (1) taxpayer satisfies RECORD-KEEPING, (2) COOPERATES with IRS, (3) provides reasonable case to IRS administratively. EXCEPTIONS: criminal cases, foreign-source income, partnership audit issues, controlled foreign corp issues. § 7491(c): IRS bears burden on PENALTY for $1M+ individual income (and certain dollar-defined penalties). § 7491(a)(2)(A): individual + net worth requirements for full shift.
view.s7491.req.records: RECORD-KEEPING: produced contemporaneous records adequate to substantiate position
view.s7491.req.cooperation: COOPERATION: with reasonable IRS information requests + substantiation
view.s7491.req.administrative: ADMINISTRATIVE: provided reasonable case to IRS BEFORE Tax Court (not just first there)
view.s7491.req.factual_issue: FACTUAL ISSUE: applies to factual issues, not legal questions
view.s7491.req.individual_corp: INDIVIDUAL or CORP: limited to individuals + small corps (≤ $7M net worth + ≤ 500 employees)
view.s7491.req.cooperative_meaning: 'Cooperative' = same as discovery + reasonable response time
view.s7491.exc.cfc: Controlled Foreign Corp / subpart F issues
view.s7491.exc.civil_fraud: § 6663 civil fraud (75% penalty) — IRS always has burden
view.s7491.exc.refund_suit: Refund suit (district court / claims court) — burden on plaintiff
view.s7491.exc.injunction: Injunction proceedings against IRS — different standards
view.s7491.exc.summary: Summary judgment: burden under FRCP 56 standards
view.s7491.c.basic: IRS bears burden of production on penalties / additions to tax
view.s7491.c.individual: Individual: applies when penalty ≥ $1M income year
view.s7491.c.s6664: § 6664(c) reasonable cause exception: taxpayer still bears burden on cure
view.s7491.c.fraud_penalty: § 6663 fraud: IRS has burden of PROOF (not just production)
view.s7491.c.s6651: § 6651 failure to file / pay: IRS production only
view.s7491.c.s7491c_2: § 7491(c)(2): supervisor approval requirement for penalty assertion
view.s7491.c.graev_chai: Graev v. Comm'r (2017) + Chai v. Comm'r (2017): super approval = invalidating defective penalties
view.s7491.c.cure_window: Reasonable cause: taxpayer + facts; IRS gets to rebut
view.s7491.fees.purpose: Recover attorney's fees if IRS position NOT SUBSTANTIALLY JUSTIFIED
view.s7491.fees.prevailing: Taxpayer must PREVAIL on substantive issues
view.s7491.fees.tax_court: Tax Court awards fees only after final disposition
view.s7491.fees.section_6402: Coordinate with § 6402 set-off + refund offset rules
view.s7491.shifted_note: BURDEN SHIFTED to IRS. Strong tactical advantage in Tax Court. Affidavits + business records become powerful evidence. IRS examiner now must prove deficiency rather than taxpayer disproving it. Combine with § 7430 attorney's fees recovery if IRS position not substantially justified.
view.s483.h1.title: // § 483 IMPUTED INTEREST
view.s483.hint.intro: Installment sales with inadequate interest: portion of each payment recharacterized as INTEREST. Test rate: AFR (Applicable Federal Rate) based on term + compounding period. Below-AFR loans: portion of each payment = interest (not capital gain). § 7872 below-market loans + § 1274 debt instruments coordinate. 3 AFR rates: short-term (≤ 3 yrs), mid-term (3-9 yrs), long-term (> 9 yrs). De minimis: ≤ $10K sales without imputed interest.
view.s483.afr.short: Short-term AFR: applies to debt ≤ 3 years (90-day Treasury)
view.s483.afr.mid: Mid-term AFR: applies to debt > 3 to ≤ 9 years (3-9 year Treasury)
view.s483.afr.long: Long-term AFR: applies to debt > 9 years (long bond yields)
view.s483.afr.published: Published monthly in Rev. Rul. (e.g., Rev. Rul. 2024-X)
view.s483.afr.applicable_rate: 'Applicable rate' used to test adequate interest
view.s483.imp.test: Test: stated rate ≥ AFR → adequate interest; no imputation
view.s483.imp.below: If stated rate < AFR: portion of each payment = imputed interest
view.s483.imp.formula: Imputed interest = AFR × outstanding balance per period
view.s483.imp.allocation: Allocate to interest first, then to principal recovery
view.s483.imp.character: Interest portion: ORDINARY INCOME for seller, deductible (§ 163) for buyer
view.s483.imp.principal: Principal portion: capital gain (per § 453 installment method)
view.s483.imp.buyer_basis: Buyer's basis = principal portion (NOT total payments)
view.s483.imp.section_1274: § 1274 may apply instead for 'publicly traded' debt instruments
view.s483.exc.de_minimis: De minimis: aggregate ≤ $10K sales of property in a year
view.s483.exc.small_personal: Small personal use property: ≤ $250K sales (e.g., cars)
view.s483.exc.publicly_traded: Publicly traded property: § 1274 may apply instead
view.s483.exc.cash_method: Cash method seller + small amounts: may opt out
view.s483.coord.s453: § 453 installment method: imputed interest part NOT subject to installment
view.s483.coord.s7872: § 7872 below-market loans: similar imputation for non-sale loans
view.s483.coord.s1274: § 1274 debt instruments: applies to PUBLICLY TRADED debt (not § 483)
view.s483.coord.s7702: § 7702 life insurance contracts: separate test, not § 483
view.s483.coord.s1239: § 1239 related party: combine with imputed interest analysis
view.s483.coord.s453_b_g: § 453B(g): transferred installment notes to spouse — tax-free under § 1041
view.s483.coord.estate: Estate planning: low-AFR sale to grantor trust (private annuity sale)
view.s483.coord.s2032a: § 2032A estate freezes use installment notes with AFR
view.s483.related_note: BELOW-AFR + RELATED PARTY: § 483 imputed interest combined w/ § 7872 below-market loan rules + § 1239 related party rules. IRS scrutiny on private installment sales among family. Document at-least-AFR stated rate. Estate planning structure (intra-family sales) requires careful AFR documentation + annual interest reporting.
view.s4973.h1.title: // § 4973 EXCESS IRA / HSA
view.s4973.hint.intro: 6% excise tax per year on excess contributions to IRAs, HSAs, Coverdell ESAs, Archer MSAs. CUMULATIVE: tax continues EACH year until excess removed + earnings withdrawn. Timely correction (by tax-filing deadline + extensions): avoids penalty + earnings withdrawn as ordinary income. Late correction: 6% excise on excess + earnings stay in account taxable later. Form 5329 Part III for IRAs, Part VII for HSAs, etc. Limit 2024: $7K IRA + $1K catch-up; $8,300 HSA family.
view.s4973.lim.ira_trad: Traditional + Roth IRA: $7,000 (combined; age 50+ add $1,000 catch-up = $8,000)
view.s4973.lim.ira_phase_roth: Roth IRA income limit: $146-$161K single / $230-$240K MFJ phase-out
view.s4973.lim.coverdell: Coverdell ESA: $2,000 per beneficiary (single $95-$110K income phase-out)
view.s4973.lim.spousal: Spousal IRA: non-working spouse may contribute up to $7K if combined earned income
view.s4973.corr.timely_withdraw: Timely withdrawal: by tax-filing deadline + extensions (typically Oct 15)
view.s4973.corr.must_remove_earnings: MUST REMOVE ALSO: net income attributable to excess (NIA)
view.s4973.corr.formula: NIA = excess × (adj. earnings / total balance) — Reg § 1.408-11
view.s4973.corr.recharacterize: Recharacterize traditional ↔ Roth: by Oct 15 (post-2017 TCJA limited)
view.s4973.corr.absorb: Absorb in next year: count toward NEXT year's contribution (limited use)
view.s4973.corr.cumulative: Cumulative: 6% × YEAR after year on uncorrected excess
view.s4973.corr.deemed_distribution: Late correction: excess remains; future withdrawal = ordinary income
view.s4973.corr.form_5329: Form 5329: file with 1040 to report excess + pay excise
view.s4973.nia.formula: NIA = Excess × [(Adj Cl Bal - Adj Op Bal) / Adj Op Bal]
view.s4973.nia.op_bal: Adj Op Bal = Account balance BEFORE excess + other contributions during period
view.s4973.nia.cl_bal: Adj Cl Bal = Account balance AFTER excess + distributions removed
view.s4973.nia.positive: If NIA positive: earnings on excess included in income (year of distribution)
view.s4973.nia.negative: If NIA negative: loss on excess (basis adjustment only)
view.s4973.nia.daily: Daily NIA computation often required for trustee tracking
view.s4973.nia.broker: Most brokers calculate NIA automatically when excess withdrawal requested
view.s4973.nia.10pct: Premature distribution under 59.5: 10% additional § 72(t) on NIA
view.s4973.cause.income_limit: Roth IRA income limit exceeded (high earner contributions)
view.s4973.cause.hsa_partial: HSA partial year (eligibility changes mid-year + full contribution made)
view.s4973.cause.spousal_earned: Spousal IRA without sufficient earned income
view.s4973.cause.late_recharacterization: Late recharacterization (post-TCJA elimination of Roth → Traditional)
view.s4973.cause.foreign_excluded: Foreign earned income excluded (no qualifying income for IRA)
view.s4973.correct_now_note: CORRECTION WINDOW OPEN — days remaining. Contact IRA / HSA custodian to request 'Return of Excess Contribution' by tax-filing deadline (typically Oct 15). MUST also remove NIA (net income attributable). Avoid 6% excise tax permanently if completed timely. Form 5329 Part III to report.
view.s4973.late_note: LATE CORRECTION: 6% excise tax accrues EACH YEAR until excess removed. Either: (1) take distribution now (taxable income) OR (2) absorb in future year's contribution limit if eligible. Note: distribution counts toward income limits + may trigger § 72(t) 10% if under age 59.5. Continued failure = 6% perpetual + cumulative.
view.s6695.h1.title: // § 6695 PREPARER PENALTIES
view.s6695.hint.intro: Various FLAT PENALTIES for procedural failures by tax return preparers. § 6695(a): failure to furnish copy of return to taxpayer — $60 / return. § 6695(b): failure to sign as preparer — $60. § 6695(c): failure to maintain list of returns prepared — $60. § 6695(g): failure to perform due diligence on EITC, CTC, AOTC, HoH — $635 / failure (2024). Form 8867 required for each refundable credit return. Annual penalty caps by failure type. Reasonable cause exception limited.
view.s6695.h2.s6695g_diligence: § 6695(g) — EITC + CTC + AOTC + HoH due diligence
view.s6695.gg.scope: Refundable credits: EITC, Child Tax Credit (refundable), American Opportunity Tax Credit, Head of Household status
view.s6695.gg.form_8867: Form 8867 (Paid Preparer's Due Diligence Checklist) required EACH RETURN
view.s6695.gg.records: Retain ALL records 3 years from filing date
view.s6695.gg.questions: Required: ask appropriate questions + verify taxpayer eligibility
view.s6695.gg.documentation: Document: SSN cards, dependent records, qualifying expense receipts
view.s6695.gg.cant_solely_rely: Cannot rely solely on taxpayer's word — verify documentation
view.s6695.gg.penalty_per_credit: PENALTY PER CREDIT per return — multiple credits → multiple penalties
view.s6695.gg.cumulative: EITC + CTC + AOTC + HoH on same return = up to $635 × 4 = $2,540 per return
view.s6695.rc.standard: Limited reasonable cause exception (Boyle case strict)
view.s6695.rc.reliance: Reliance on substantial authority + adequate disclosure
view.s6695.rc.factors: Factors: experience, complexity of return, supervisor approval
view.s6695.rc.no_reliance: Cannot rely on taxpayer's representations without verification
view.s6695.rc.documentation: Document reasonable steps taken to verify
view.s6695.rc.first_time: First-time penalty: harder to abate (no automatic abatement)
view.s6695.rc.appeals: Appeals: petition Tax Court within 30 days of denial
view.s6695.c230.fees: § 10.27 fees: no contingent fees on original returns
view.s6695.dd_note: § 6695(g) due diligence FAILURES. Required: Form 8867 each return + retain 3-year records. Penalty PER CREDIT per return — EITC + CTC + AOTC + HoH on same return = up to $635 × 4 = $2,540 per return. IRS preparer compliance unit (CDX) audits high-volume EITC preparers. Implement systematic checklist + documentation procedures.
view.s4958.h1.title: // § 4958 INTERMEDIATE SANCTIONS
view.s4958.hint.intro: 25%/200% excise on excess benefit + 10% OM penalty + § 4960 21% exec comp + rebuttable presumption (independent board + comparability + documentation).
view.s102.h1.title: // § 102 GIFT / INHERITANCE EXCLUSION
view.s102.hint.intro: § 102(a) exclusion. § 102(c) employer→employee presumption of compensation. Duberstein detached + disinterested generosity. § 1015 carryover / § 1014 step-up.
view.s362.h1.title: // § 362 CORPORATE BASIS ON TAX-FREE TRANSFER
view.s362.hint.intro: § 362(a) carryover + § 362(b) reorg + § 362(e)(2) NBIL anti-loss reduction OR election to reduce outside basis. § 1223(2) holding period tacking.
view.s6330.h1.title: // § 6330 CDP LEVY HEARING
view.s6330.hint.intro: 30-day notice before levy. Form 12153 within 30 days. Independent Appeals officer. Tax Court jurisdiction. Levy suspended + § 6502 collection SOL tolled.
view.s6404.h1.title: // § 6404 ABATEMENT OF TAX / INTEREST / PENALTIES
view.s6404.hint.intro: § 6404(e) IRS delay 18+ mo + § 6404(f) erroneous written advice + § 6404(g) 36-mo notice failure suspension. Form 843. First-Time Penalty Abatement (FTA) IRM.
view.s6672.h1.title: // § 6672 TRUST FUND RECOVERY PENALTY
view.s6672.hint.intro: 100% personal penalty on responsible person who willfully fails to pay over trust fund taxes (withheld income tax + employee FICA). Joint and several liability + corp veil pierced.
view.s6662.h1.title: // § 6662 ACCURACY-RELATED PENALTY (20%/40%)
view.s6662.hint.intro: 20% on negligence + substantial understatement + valuation misstatement. 40% gross valuation + foreign asset undisclosed. § 6664 reasonable cause defense limited for tax shelters.
view.s67.h1.title: // § 67 2% MISC ITEMIZED FLOOR + TCJA SUSPENSION
view.s67.hint.intro: Pre-TCJA: 2% AGI floor. § 67(g) TCJA SUSPENDS misc itemized 2018-2025. § 67(b) exceptions (gambling/IRD/impairment) + § 67(e) estate/trust above-line preserved.
view.s421.h1.title: // § 421 QUALIFIED STOCK OPTIONS (ISO + ESPP)
view.s421.hint.intro: § 422 ISO no exercise income + 2yr/1yr holding for LTCG + AMT trap. § 423 ESPP 15% max + lookback. § 6039 Form 3921/3922 required. § 421(b) disqualifying disposition.
view.s989.h1.title: // § 989 FUNCTIONAL CURRENCY + QBU
view.s989.hint.intro: Functional currency = primary economic environment. QBU = separate clearly identified books + trade or business. § 987 2024 final regs for QBU remittance gain/loss.
view.s475.h1.title: // § 475 TRADER MARK-TO-MARKET ELECTION
view.s475.hint.intro: § 475(f) qualified trader MTM election: gain/loss ordinary + NO § 1091 wash sale + Schedule C above-line expenses. Election due April 15 unextended.
view.s988.h1.title: // § 988 FOREIGN CURRENCY TRANSACTIONS
view.s988.hint.intro: Ordinary character on nonfunctional currency transactions. § 988(a)(1)(B) election: capital. § 1256 futures preserve 60/40 MTM. § 988(e) $200 personal de minimis.
view.s303.h1.title: // § 303 ESTATE TAX REDEMPTION
view.s303.hint.intro: Closely-held stock redemption tax-free up to estate tax + funeral + admin expenses. Requires stock > 35% adjusted gross estate. Pairs with § 6166 installment.
view.s129.h1.title: // § 129 DEPENDENT CARE ASSISTANCE PROGRAM
view.s129.hint.intro: Employer DCAP up to $5,000 ($2,500 MFS) excluded from wages. § 125 cafeteria plan. § 21 credit coordination — no double dipping.
view.s134.h1.title: // § 134 MILITARY BENEFITS EXCLUSION
view.s134.hint.intro: Qualified military benefits + § 112 combat zone pay exclusion (enlisted full / officer capped). § 121(d)(9) 10-yr home sale ext + § 7508 SOL ext.
view.s1296.h1.title: // § 1296 PFIC MARK-TO-MARKET ELECTION
view.s1296.hint.intro: Annual MTM election for marketable PFIC stock. Gain ordinary, loss limited to unreversed inclusions. Alternative to § 1291 punitive + § 1295 QEF.
view.s1059.h1.title: // § 1059 EXTRAORDINARY DIVIDEND BASIS REDUCTION
view.s1059.hint.intro: Corp shareholder reduces basis by non-taxed portion (DRD) of extraordinary dividend. 5% common / 10% preferred + 85-day aggregation + 2-yr holding.
view.s304.h1.title: // § 304 RELATED CORPORATION REDEMPTION
view.s304.hint.intro: Anti-abuse: sale of stock to related corp treated as redemption + § 301 distribution. § 304(a)(1) brother-sister + § 304(a)(2) parent-sub.
view.s6112.h1.title: // § 6112 MATERIAL ADVISOR LIST MAINTENANCE
view.s6112.hint.intro: Material advisor must maintain advisee list 7 years + produce within 20 business days of IRS request. § 6708 $10K/day penalty with NO CAP.
view.s6601.h1.title: // § 6601 INTEREST ON UNDERPAYMENTS
view.s6601.hint.intro: Daily compound interest from due date. § 6621(a)(2) ST + 3pp underpayment / § 6621(c) hot corp ≥ $100K = ST + 5pp. § 6404(e) IRS-delay abatement.
view.s269a.h1.title: // § 269A PERSONAL SERVICE CORPORATION
view.s269a.hint.intro: IRS may reallocate income, deductions, credits between PSC + owner-employees if substantially all services to one entity AND principal purpose tax avoidance.
view.s318.h1.title: // § 318 CONSTRUCTIVE OWNERSHIP
view.s318.hint.intro: 5 attribution rules across family + entities + options. Used by § 302/§ 304 redemptions + § 1361 S-corp count + § 4975 prohibited transactions.
view.s481.h1.title: // § 481 ACCOUNTING METHOD CHANGE ADJUSTMENT
view.s481.hint.intro: Cumulative single-year catch-up when method changes. 4-yr spread unfavorable / 1-yr favorable. Form 3115 + Rev Proc 2022-14 automatic.
view.s6221.h1.title: // § 6221 BBA PARTNERSHIP AUDIT REGIME
view.s6221.hint.intro: Post-2017 default: entity-level audit + 37% imputed underpayment. § 6221(b) election out for ≤ 100 eligible partners. § 6226 push-out election within 45 days.
view.s956.h1.title: // § 956 CFC INVESTMENT IN US PROPERTY
view.s956.hint.intro: US shareholder must include lesser of CFC's increase in US property or E&P. § 245A hybrid relief (Reg § 1.956-1(a)(2) 2019 final) reduces inclusion.
view.s382.h1.title: // § 382 NOL LIMITATION (OWNERSHIP CHANGE)
view.s382.hint.intro: Limits NOL utilization after ownership change to annual limit = FMV × LT tax-exempt rate. § 382(h) RBIG/RBIL 5-yr. § 382(c) COBE 2-yr or NOLs eliminated.
view.s1234.h1.title: // § 1234 OPTIONS CHARACTER
view.s1234.hint.intro: Holder gain/loss on capital underlying = capital. Writer: sale = STCG; LAPSE = ordinary. § 1256 60/40 + MTM for non-equity options + broad index.
view.s32.h1.title: // § 32 EARNED INCOME TAX CREDIT
view.s32.hint.intro: Refundable credit for low-income workers. 2024 max $7,830 (3+ kids). § 32(d) $11,600 investment income cliff. SSN required.
view.s707.h1.title: // § 707 PARTNER-PARTNERSHIP TRANSACTIONS
view.s707.hint.intro: § 707(a)(2)(B) disguised sale 2-yr presumption. § 707(b) related party loss disallowance + gain ordinary. § 707(c) guaranteed payments + SE tax.
view.s736.h1.title: // § 736 RETIRING / DECEASED PARTNER PAYMENTS
view.s736.hint.intro: § 736(b) capital for partnership property. § 736(a) ordinary (distributive share or guaranteed payment). § 736(b)(2) service PS exception for unrealized receivables + unstated goodwill.
view.s1058.h1.title: // § 1058 SECURITIES LOAN NONRECOGNITION
view.s1058.hint.intro: Securities loan = nonrecognition if identical securities returned + substitute payments + at-will + economic ownership retained. Substitute payments ordinary not qualified.
view.s1250.h1.title: // § 1250 REAL PROPERTY RECAPTURE
view.s1250.hint.intro: Recaptures excess over straight-line as ordinary. Post-1986 SL only → effectively zero. Unrecaptured § 1250 gain taxed at 25% max. § 291 adds 20% for C-corps.
view.s1231.h1.title: // § 1231 BUSINESS PROPERTY (HOTCHPOT)
view.s1231.hint.intro: Net § 1231 gain = LTCG. Net § 1231 loss = ordinary. § 1231(c) 5-yr look-back recaptures gain as ordinary up to cumulative losses. § 1245/§ 1250 applied first.
view.s871.h1.title: // § 871 NONRESIDENT ALIEN TAX
view.s871.hint.intro: 30% FDAP withholding on US-source passive + graduated ECI on US TB income. § 871(m) dividend equivalents on equity derivatives. Portfolio interest exempt.
view.s731.h1.title: // § 731 PARTNERSHIP DISTRIBUTIONS
view.s731.hint.intro: Gain if cash distributed > outside basis. Loss only on liquidating distribution of cash + receivables + inventory. § 731(c) marketable securities treated as money.
view.s6011.h1.title: // § 6011 REPORTABLE TRANSACTION DISCLOSURE (Form 8886)
view.s6011.hint.intro: Form 8886 reports participation in 5 categories of reportable transactions. § 6707A penalty up to $200K for listed transactions. § 6501(c)(10) extends SOL.
view.s1245.h1.title: // § 1245 DEPRECIATION RECAPTURE
view.s1245.hint.intro: Recharacterizes gain as ordinary income to extent of accumulated depreciation. Recapture = lesser of (gain) OR (depreciation). Excess = § 1231 capital gain.
view.s164.h1.title: // § 164 TAXES DEDUCTION + SALT CAP
view.s164.hint.intro: $10K SALT cap (2018-2025, MFS $5K) on state/local income/sales + property tax. Business + rental property tax NOT capped. PTET workaround in 36 states.
view.s24.h1.title: // § 24 CHILD TAX CREDIT
view.s24.hint.intro: 2024 CTC $2,000/child under 17, $1,700 refundable ACTC. $500 ODC for other deps. MAGI phase $200K/$400K. SSN required. Sunsets to $1K post-2025.
view.s6045a.h1.title: // § 6045A COVERED SECURITY TRANSFER REPORTING
view.s6045a.hint.intro: Transferring broker must furnish basis statement to receiving broker within 15 days. Covered security phase-in 2011-2016. § 6722 penalty $60-$630/form.
view.s743.h1.title: // § 743 PARTNERSHIP BASIS ADJUSTMENT
view.s743.hint.intro: § 743(b) aligns transferee inside basis with outside basis when § 754 election OR substantial built-in loss > $250K. § 755 allocates between property classes.
view.s1202.h1.title: // § 1202 QSBS GAIN EXCLUSION
view.s1202.hint.intro: Up to 100% exclusion of gain on QSBS held > 5 years. Cap: greater of $10M or 10× basis per issuer. C-corp + ≤ $50M gross assets + 80% active qualified business.
view.s170.h1.title: // § 170 CHARITABLE CONTRIBUTIONS
view.s170.hint.intro: 60% AGI cash to public (2018-2025) / 30% cash to PF / 30% LTCG to public / 20% LTCG to PF. 5-year carryover. Substantiation $250+ requires written acknowledgment.
view.s351.h1.title: // § 351 TAX-FREE TRANSFER TO CONTROLLED CORP
view.s351.hint.intro: Nonrecognition on transfer of property for stock when transferors collectively control ≥ 80%. Boot recognized up to gain. § 358 / § 362 carryover basis.
view.s6038d.h1.title: // § 6038D FORM 8938 SPECIFIED FOREIGN ASSETS
view.s6038d.hint.intro: Form 8938 reports SFFA when value exceeds threshold. $50K-$600K depending on status + abroad. $10K base + $10K/30-day continuation. § 6662(j) 40% accuracy.
view.s752.h1.title: // § 752 PARTNERSHIP LIABILITIES + BASIS
view.s752.hint.intro: § 752(a) liability increase = deemed contribution + § 752(b) decrease = deemed distribution. Recourse via constructive liquidation. Nonrecourse via 3-tier allocation.
view.s199a.h1.title: // § 199A QBI DEDUCTION
view.s199a.hint.intro: Up to 20% deduction on QBI from pass-through entities. 2024 thresholds: $241,950 single / $483,900 MFJ. SSTB completely phased out above upper threshold. Sunsets Dec 31, 2025.
view.s461l.h1.title: // § 461(l) EXCESS BUSINESS LOSS
view.s461l.hint.intro: Non-corporate excess business loss limit: $305K single / $610K MFJ (2024). Excess = NOL carryforward. Extended through 2028 by OBBBA.
view.s165.h1.title: // § 165 LOSSES
view.s165.hint.intro: 4 categories: trade/business, profit transaction, personal casualty (federally declared disasters only post-TCJA), wagering. Worthless securities + Ponzi safe harbor.
view.s408a.h1.title: // § 408A ROTH IRA
view.s408a.hint.intro: After-tax contributions + tax-free qualified distributions. 2024 limits $7K / $8K catch-up. MAGI phase-out $146-161K single / $230-240K MFJ. Backdoor + mega backdoor.
view.s962.h1.title: // § 962 ELECTION (CORPORATE RATES ON CFC)
view.s962.hint.intro: Individual CFC shareholder elects corporate 21% rate on subpart F + GILTI. Unlocks § 250 50% GILTI deduction + § 960 indirect FTC. Trade-off: later dividend taxable again.
view.s280c.h1.title: // § 280C CREDITS DEDUCTION DISALLOWANCE
view.s280c.hint.intro: § 280C(a) wage credits reduce § 162 deduction. § 280C(c) R&D credit: reduce § 174 basis OR elect reduced 79% credit. Post-2022 § 174 capitalization changes the calculus.
view.s6111.h1.title: // § 6111 MATERIAL ADVISOR DISCLOSURE
view.s6111.hint.intro: Material advisor of reportable transaction must file Form 8918. § 6707 penalty: $50K (other) OR greater of $200K OR 75% gross income (listed/intentional). § 6112 list maintenance $10K/day.
view.s6111.cat.confidential: Confidential — fee paid + restriction on disclosure
view.s6111.cat.contractual: Contractual protection — refund if no tax benefit
view.s6111.cat.toi: Transactions of Interest — IRS designates as warranting scrutiny
view.s6111.thresh.listed_natural: Listed transaction, natural person: $50,000 gross income
view.s6111.thresh.expectation: Reasonably expects to receive — not actually received
view.s6111.thresh.cumulative: Cumulative across multiple participants in same transaction
view.s6111.tbl.listed_tp: 75% of decrease in tax / $200K cap / $100K min
view.s6111.tbl.other_tp: 75% of decrease in tax / $50K cap / $10K min
view.s6111.list.required: Material advisor must maintain list of advisees + transactions
view.s6111.list.contents: List: name, address, TIN, amount invested, copy of advice, all fees
view.s6111.list.retention: 7-year retention period from date last became material advisor
view.s6111.list.production: Must produce within 20 business days of IRS written request
view.s6111.list.penalty_clock: $10,000/day starts on day 21 — continues until full production
view.s6111.list.no_cap: NO cap on total — can exceed $200K easily
view.s6111.list.privilege: Attorney-client privilege NOT general defense — § 7525 narrow
view.s6111.coord.taxpayer: Taxpayer separately files Form 8886 (§ 6011) attached to return
view.s6111.coord.eited_taxpayer: Even if advisor fails to file 8918, taxpayer still required 8886
view.s6111.coord.sol_exception: § 6501(c)(10) extended SOL: 1 year after Form 8886 filed
view.s6111.coord.list_extension: List request triggers attorney-supervisor review at IRS national office
view.s6111.coord.injunction: § 7408 injunction available against repeat material advisor
view.s6111.coord.disclosure_taxpayer: Strategy disclosure may trigger client wave of audit + § 6662
view.s4972.h1.title: // § 4972 NONDEDUCTIBLE PLAN CONTRIBUTION
view.s4972.hint.intro: 10% excise on nondeductible contributions to qualified plans. § 404 limit. Cumulative excise each year excess remains.
view.s4972.cum.annual: 10% applied EACH year excess remains undistributed
view.s4972.cum.compound: Compound effect: $100K excess for 3 years = $30K total excise
view.s4972.cum.deemed: Excess deemed reduced by current-year contribution shortfall
view.s4972.cum.distribution: Distribution of excess = withdrawal subject to income tax + § 72 early
view.s4972.cum.return: Trustee return of excess by Apr 15 next year avoids further excise
view.s4972.cum.allocation: Multiple-employer plans: excise on employer making excess contribution
view.s4972.coord.s404: § 404(a) deduction limit (25% comp DC, actuarial DB) is the bright line
view.s4972.coord.s415: § 415(c) annual addition limit DIFFERENT from § 404 deduction limit
view.s4972.coord.dc_db: DC + DB combined: § 404(a)(7) lesser of 25% comp OR aggregate deduction
view.s4972.coord.s4979: § 4979 10% excise on excess elective deferrals (HCE refund)
view.s4972.coord.epcrs: EPCRS self-correction + voluntary correction + audit closing agreement
view.s6039.h1.title: // § 6039 ISO/ESPP INFORMATION REPORTING
view.s6039.hint.intro: Form 3921 (ISO) + Form 3922 (ESPP). Furnish to employee by Jan 31, file with IRS by Feb 28 paper / Mar 31 e-file. § 6721/6722 penalties up to $3.78M.
view.s6039.iso.both: BOTH must be satisfied for qualifying disposition
view.s6039.iso.tax_qual: Tax treatment: 100% long-term capital gain on sale
view.s6039.iso.disqual: Disqualifying disposition: ordinary income (FMV-strike) up to gain
view.s6039.iso.amt_trap: AMT trap: § 56(b)(3) — spread at exercise included in AMTI
view.s6039.iso.amt_basis: AMT basis adjustment: increase by spread → reduces future AMT gain
view.s6039.iso.amt_credit: § 53 minimum tax credit allows future recovery of AMT paid
view.s6039.iso.s100k: $100K rule: first $100K ISO grant per year eligible (excess = NSO)
view.s6039.espp.discount: 15% maximum discount on lower of: FMV at grant OR FMV at purchase
view.s6039.espp.grant_holding: 2 years from beginning of offering period
view.s6039.espp.qualifying_treatment: Qualifying disposition: discount = ordinary income up to gain, balance = LTCG
view.s6039.espp.disqualifying_treatment: Disqualifying disposition: discount + spread = ordinary income
view.s6039.espp.s25k: $25,000 limit per offering period (FMV at grant basis)
view.s6039.espp.tax_form: W-2 reports compensation income on qualifying disposition
view.s6039.espp.lookback: Lookback feature: discount based on lower of two prices
view.s6039.espp.eligible: All 5%+ owner OR HCE-excluded employees may be excluded
view.s6039.tbl.aug1: Filed after 30 days but by Aug 1
view.s6039.tbl.aftaug1: Filed after Aug 1 or not at all
view.s894.h1.title: // § 894 TREATY POSITION (Form 8833)
view.s894.hint.intro: Form 8833 disclosure required under § 6114 when treaty position reduces US tax by $10K+ (non-individual) or any amount where treaty waived withholding (individual). § 6712 $1K/$10K penalty.
view.s894.thresh.exempt_pe: Treaty article exempts US trade or business profits (no PE)
view.s894.thresh.no_disclosure: Routine treaty rate withholding (1441) — generally no disclosure
view.s894.lob.derivative: Derivative benefits test (US treaty country resident)
view.s894.tie.permanent_home: Permanent home available in only one contracting state
view.s894.tie.center_vital: Center of vital interests (closer personal + economic relations)
view.s894.tie.lcus: Long-term green-card holder rule: 10/15-year US tax resident
view.s894.tie.s7701b_e: § 7701(b)(6) treaty election to be taxed as nonresident
view.s894.over.hybrid: § 894(c) hybrid entity: payment to person not eligible
view.s894.over.beneficial: Beneficial ownership test essential — not pass-through
view.s894.over.s954_d: § 954(d)(4) hybrid mismatch rules subpart F coordination
view.s894.over.beat: § 59A BEAT applies despite treaty (10% base erosion threshold)
view.s894.over.savings_clause: Savings clause: US can tax US citizens regardless of treaty
view.s894.over.lwc: Last-in-time rule: later statute overrides treaty (Cook v. United States)
view.s2518.h1.title: // § 2518 QUALIFIED DISCLAIMER
view.s2518.hint.intro: Qualified disclaimer = refusal to accept inheritance. 5 reqs: written + 9 months + unconditional + no benefit + passes without disclaimant direction. NOT a gift under § 2511.
view.s2518.five.written: WRITTEN refusal — signed by disclaimant or legal rep
view.s2518.five.timing: 9 months from later of: (a) date of transfer creating interest, OR (b) age 21 for minor
view.s2518.five.unconditional: UNCONDITIONAL + irrevocable — no conditions, no power to revoke
view.s2518.five.passes: Passes WITHOUT disclaimant direction (governing instrument controls)
view.s2518.special.spouse_redirect: § 2518(b)(4)(A) — surviving spouse may disclaim AND interest passes to spouse
view.s2518.special.spouse_exception: Otherwise, disclaimed interest CANNOT pass to disclaimant
view.s2518.special.partial_undivided: Partial disclaimer: must be UNDIVIDED interest (severable share)
view.s2518.special.formula: Formula clause disclaimer permitted (e.g., applicable exclusion)
view.s2518.special.ira_separate: IRA / retirement: each beneficiary disclaims separately
view.s2518.special.fmv_date: FMV at date of disclaimer (not date of original transfer)
view.s2518.special.power_of_appointment: General power of appointment may be disclaimed
view.s2518.uc.bypass_trust: Disclaimer to bypass trust funding (reduces surviving spouse's estate)
view.s2518.uc.skip_generation: Disclaimer to skip generation (children disclaim → grandchildren — GST issues)
view.s2518.uc.poor_planning: Post-mortem correction of pre-mortem planning errors
view.s2518.uc.creditor: Creditor protection: disclaimed property never owned by disclaimant
view.s2518.uc.elderly: Elderly child / financially independent — disclaim in favor of next gen
view.s2518.uc.qtip_election: Disclaim to enable QTIP election + reverse direction
view.s2518.fail.deemed_gift: Treated as accepted + new gift from disclaimant to next beneficiary
view.s2518.fail.gift_tax: § 2511 gift tax applies — file Form 709 + use lifetime exemption
view.s2518.fail.cost_basis: Disclaimant becomes property owner — § 1014 step-up still applies
view.s2518.fail.state_law_only: State-law disclaimer may still effective for state purposes
view.s2518.fail.late_filing: 9-month deadline is strict — no equitable tolling
view.s2518.fail.partial_consequences: Partial disclaimer with conditions = total failure (not partial)
view.s2518.state.udpia: Uniform Disclaimer of Property Interests Act (UDPIA, 2002)
view.s2518.state.adoption: Adopted in 40+ states (CA, NY, IL, TX vary)
view.s2518.state.no_time_limit: UDPIA: NO state law time limit (federal 9-month controls for tax)
view.s2518.state.unborn: Unborn beneficiary: virtual representation may apply
view.s2518.failed_note: Disclaimer FAILS § 2518 — treated as accepted + gift from disclaimant. Triggers § 2511 gift tax. State-law disclaimer may still effective for non-tax purposes.
view.s4974.h1.title: // § 4974 RMD UNDERDISTRIBUTION
view.s4974.hint.intro: 25% excise tax (post-SECURE 2.0) on shortfall between Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) and actual. Reduced to 10% if corrected within 2-year window (post-SECURE 2.0). Apply to: 401(k), 403(b), 457(b), Traditional IRAs (NOT Roth while owner alive). RBD: April 1 of year after age 73 (SECURE 2.0, rising to 75 in 2033). First-year delay: can defer first RMD to April 1 of next year (but then take 2 in that year). Form 5329 Part IX reports.
view.s4974.rbd.first_year: First-year delay: April 1 of year AFTER reaching RBD age
view.s4974.rbd.deferral_strategy: Deferral strategy: take 1st RMD by April 1, take 2nd in same year
view.s4974.rbd.tax_implications: Both RMDs in same year may push income into higher tax bracket
view.s4974.rbd.employer_plans_delay: Employer plans: RBD = later of age 73 OR retirement (5%+ owners: age 73 regardless)
view.s4974.calc.balance: Account balance: December 31 of PRIOR year
view.s4974.calc.formula: RMD = Prior year-end balance / Distribution period divisor
view.s4974.calc.aggregation_ira: Aggregation: multiple IRAs total RMD; satisfy from any one
view.s4974.calc.beneficiary: Beneficiary RMDs: single life table (more aggressive)
view.s4974.calc.spousal_delay: Surviving spouse: may delay until spouse's own RBD
view.s4974.qcd.purpose: Direct IRA → 501(c)(3) charity transfer counts as RMD
view.s4974.qcd.no_double: Not taxable + does NOT count as itemized deduction (vs cash gift)
view.s4974.qcd.tax_efficient: Tax-efficient: avoids inclusion in AGI (lowers Medicare premiums, SS taxation)
view.s4974.qcd.coordination: Cannot send to donor-advised fund or supporting organization
view.s4974.qcd.reportable: Reported on 1099-R Box 1 + adjustment in Form 1040 Line 4b
view.s4974.corr.10pct: SECURE 2.0: 10% (reduced from 25%) if corrected within 2-year window
view.s4974.corr.calculation: 2-year window: from year RMD shortfall first occurred
view.s4974.corr.waiver: Reasonable cause waiver: still available; reasonable explanation + documentation
view.s4974.corr.illness: Reasonable cause examples: illness, financial advisor error, IRA custodian error
view.s4974.corr.late: Late even with waiver: penalty + amended return + Form 5329
view.s4974.corr.administrative: IRS administrative relief: typically waived if cured promptly + reasonable
view.s4974.shortfall_note: RMD SHORTFALL: 25% (or 10% if within 2-yr window) excise tax applies. Take missed distribution IMMEDIATELY + file Form 5329 Part IX. SECURE 2.0 introduced 2-year cure window for reduced 10% rate. Reasonable cause waiver still available — typically illness, error by IRA custodian / advisor. Avoid: setup automatic year-end RMD via brokerage.
view.settings.hint.crt_scanlines_neon_bo: CRT scanlines, neon-border pulse, and dark/light theme are toggled from the buttons in the topbar. Color scheme switches the whole HUD palette — picks below.
view.settings.hint.commission_rates_fill:
Commission rates fill in only when the broker file omits fees (fee = 0).
                Mirrors TraderVue's "manual rate" behavior — won't double-count.
view.settings.hint.share_this_with_someo: Share this with someone if they want to mentor you.
view.settings.th.name: Name
view.setups_by_setup.empty_wlr_chart: No tagged trades to chart wins/losses.
view.setups_by_setup.hint.risk_amount_en: Risk amount enables R-multiple. Net P&L > 0 = win, < 0 = loss, == 0 = scratch. Untagged trades ("-") are sent to the backend but excluded from any setup bucket — matches backend "no setup tag → not in the catalog".
view.short_interest.h2.watchlist_ranking: Watchlist ranking (sorted by short % of float)
view.short_interest.h2.dtc_chart: Days-to-cover ranking (squeeze potential vs raw short %)
view.short_interest.hint.dtc_chart: Top 20 by short_ratio (shares short ÷ avg daily volume). Orthogonal to short % of float: a 30 %-short name with 1 day-to-cover unwinds easily; a 5 %-short name with 10 days-to-cover is squeeze-prone because shorts can't exit on a single high-volume day.
view.short_interest.h2.finra_reg_sho_dai: FINRA Reg SHO daily short volume (last 30 sessions)
view.short_interest.hint.yahoo_defaultke:
Yahoo defaultKeyStatistics for shares short / float % / days-to-cover,
            FINRA Reg SHO daily short-volume aggregated across market centers.
view.short_interest.hint.no_finra_data_f: No FINRA data — file may be embargoed or pending.
view.signal_decomposition.h2.energy_char: Component energy share (% of total Σx²)
view.signal_decomposition.hint.energy: Each component's sum-of-squares divided by total component energy. Reveals which IMF / wavelet level / SSA eigentriple holds the bulk of the signal vs which carry mostly noise — orthogonal to the per-component subplots above.
view.signal_decomposition.empty_energy_c: No components to chart energy share.
view.signal_decomposition.hint.each_subp:
Each subplot is one component. For EMD: high-freq IMFs first, residual last.
                For Wavelet: detail levels first, smooth approximation last. For SSA: trend
                on top, noise residual below.
view.spread_tracker.hint.regime_cuts_at_:
Regime cuts at 5 / 25 / 100 bps. Wide → use limit orders.
                Pathological → feed broken or illiquid name; sit out.
view.spread_tracker.hint.cyan_spread_bps:
Cyan = spread bps. Dashed reference lines: green = 5 bps (tight),
                yellow = 25 bps (normal cap), red = 100 bps (pathological cliff).
view.spread_tracker.hint.just_so_you_see:
Just so you see if the spread blew out alongside a real
                price move (newsy event) vs. a pure feed glitch (mid steady).
view.squeeze_alerts.empty_sym_chart: No per-symbol event data to chart.
view.squeeze_alerts.btn.load_demo_3_symb: Load demo (3 symbols, 60 ticks each, 30 min span)
view.squeeze_alerts.hint.browser_autopla:
Browser autoplay policy: audio context unlocks after your first
                click anywhere on the page. If "Test bell" fails silently, click any button on
                the page first then retry.
view.squeeze_alerts.hint.all_settings_pe:
All settings persist in localStorage. Watchlist gates which
                symbols can fire — leave blank to alert on every symbol that breaches.
view.stop_loss_best_of.hint.9_candidates:
9 candidates by default: None / $1 / $2 / 0.5% / 1% / 2% / 1×ATR / 2×ATR / 3×ATR.
                Each is simulated against all trades; results ranked by total realized P&L.
view.algo.hint.stdout_multi: One pane per strategy + a global firehose. Each pane keeps its own 200-line buffer.
view.algo.hint.intro: Five strategy families: momentum, mean reversion, opening range breakout, Donchian trend, Bollinger squeeze. Native bracket orders with strategy-specific stops / take-profits. 1% risk per trade, capped by max position % of equity. New strategies are paper-locked for 30 days.
view.algo.empty: No strategies yet — click "New strategy" to get started.
view.algo.th.name: Name
view.algo.label.name: Name
view.algo.hint.no_accounts: No broker accounts found. Go to Settings → Accounts to add one before saving the strategy.
view.algo.hint.no_algo_accounts: No accounts on algo-supported brokers. Supported today: Alpaca. Go to Settings → Accounts and add an Alpaca account, or pick a strategy that uses internal_sim.
view.algo.h2.tape: Raw tape — live trades off the Alpaca WS
view.algo.hint.tape: Every trade frame the live-tick worker parses off the WS. Per-symbol coalesce of 250ms so a chatty pair doesn't flood the pane. Empty pane = the WS isn't producing trades for any subscribed symbol right now.
view.algo.opt.strat_orb: Opening Range Breakout (OR high break + RVOL)
view.algo.opt.strat_bb_squeeze: Bollinger Squeeze (BBW bottom decile + band break)
view.algo.hint.execution_safety: In-app sim fills at last known price (zero-risk). Broker paper uses the broker's sandbox. Live trades real money and requires the 30-day paper-lock to expire.
view.algo.hint.broker_status: Adapter status: alpaca wired; tradier scaffolded (next); ibkr / td / tastytrade scaffolded — strategy saves and runs, but orders return integration_pending until the per-broker adapter ships.
view.algo.confirm.engage_kill: Engage kill switch? Engine will refuse all new orders and cancel working orders.
view.algo.confirm.release_kill: Release kill switch? Strategy will resume placing orders on the next signal.
view.algo.confirm.delete: Delete "{{name}}"? The strategy is hidden from the list but its runs, orders, and fills stay in the database (recoverable via SQL).
view.strategy_alerts.empty_truth_chart: No rules to chart truth distribution.
view.strategy_alerts.tip.name: Human-readable rule name shown in the alerts table
view.strategy_alerts.tip.template: Pick a canned AST shape; selecting one overwrites the JSON editor
view.strategy_alerts.tip.create: Persist the rule and start evaluating it on the 60s server tick
view.strategy_alerts.tip.ast: Rule AST as JSON — leaf or and/or/not over price/RSI/SMA/EMA/quote
view.strategy_alerts.tip.evaluate_now: Re-evaluate every rule immediately instead of waiting for the next 60s tick
view.strategy_alerts.hint.compound_and_o:
Compound AND/OR/NOT rules over price + RSI/SMA/EMA + change-pct +
            pct-of-high + ^VIX / ^CPC / ^ADD quote leaves. Server evaluates every 60s and fires
            on the false→true edge so a persistent condition won't spam. Connected webhooks
            fan-out on every fire. AST is JSON; pick a template to start.
view.strategy_alerts.th.name: Name
view.stress_test.h2.curve_chart: P&L vs price shock (per IV shock)
view.stress_test.empty_chart: Run a stress test to chart the curves.
view.stress_test.h2.vega_chart: Portfolio vega ($) vs price shock (per IV shock)
view.stress_test.hint.vega_chart: Orthogonal to the P&L curve: how the portfolio's dollar vega shifts across the same shock grid. Reveals whether the position becomes more or less vol-sensitive in the tails — critical for sizing under a vol regime change.
view.stress_test.empty_vega_chart: Run a stress test to chart the vega curves.
view.stress_test.hint.negative_price_sho:
Negative price-shock = downside; negative IV-shock = vol crush.
                Worst-case + best-case cells highlighted in the heatmap below.
view.stress_test.hint.each_cell_portfoli:
Each cell = portfolio P&L under that shock pair (with time-decay
                applied). Gold border = worst-case cell. Cyan border = best-case cell.
                Hover any cell for full greeks under that shock.
view.tags.hint.color_chart: How many tags share each color value. Reveals palette monotony — if most tags pile up on one or two colors, visual distinguishability in the trades table suffers. Orthogonal to per-tag name length.
view.tape.hint.news_chart: How many news items each symbol generated in this refresh. Orthogonal to price action: a flat-tape symbol can be the day's news leader; a big mover can have zero news (technical-only move).
view.tape.hint.auto_refreshing_news_sect: Auto-refreshing news + sector snapshot from your watchlist universe. Updates every 30 seconds.
view.tape.hint.no_news_in_this_universe_: No news in this universe yet.
view.tape.hint.add_symbols_to_a_watchlis: Add symbols to a watchlist first.
view.tape_replay.empty_range_chart: No bar range data to chart.
view.tape_replay.empty_chart: No bar volume data to chart.
view.tape_replay.h2.what_i_d_do_differen: What I'd do differently (client-side, this session only)
view.tape_replay.hint.re_watch_the_exact:
Re-watch the exact bar sequence around any of your closed trades
            with execution markers fading in at their fill timestamps. Auto-picks bar interval
            from hold duration (1m/5m/1h/1d). Use the replay to ask "what would I do differently"
            and drop client-side journal notes at the cursor.
view.tape_replay.hint.no_closed_trades_o: No closed trades on this account.
view.tape_replay.hint.no_bars_cached_for: No bars cached for this trade window.
view.tax_lots.hint.no_account_selected_c: No account selected. Create one on the Accounts tab first.
view.tax_lots.hint.pick_a_year_method_an: Pick a year + method and run.
view.tax_lots.hint.no_closed_lots_in_thi: No closed lots in this year.
view.tax_lots.hint.holding_period_clock_:
Holding-period clock on open lots is current as of now — closing
                these positions today would realize at their displayed term.
view.tax_workshop.empty_subs_chart: Run the subscription detector to populate the chart.
view.tax_workshop.empty_chart: Run the quarterly forecast to populate the chart.
view.tax_workshop.hint.add_trips_irs_sta: Add trips. IRS standard rate is applied per trip date (handles 2022 mid-year split).
view.tax_workshop.hint.scans_your_import: Scans your imported transactions for monthly/quarterly/annual charges with stable amounts. Largest annual leak first.
view.three_bar_reversal.hint.delta_chart: Signed pattern magnitude per detected event. Reveals which reversals had strong follow-through (large signed delta) vs marginal (close to zero) — orthogonal to the time-series chart above. Yellow dashed = zero (no net move).
view.three_bar_reversal.empty_delta_char: No reversal events to chart delta.
view.three_bar_reversal.btn.load_demo_14: Load demo (14 bars, bull + bear pattern)
view.three_bar_reversal.hint.cyan_close_:
Cyan = close. Green dot = bullish reversal (placed below the
                third bar's low). Red dot = bearish reversal (placed above the third bar's high).
view.time_in_force.h2.age_chart: Order age (days since placed) vs cutoffs
view.top_signals.tip.watchlist: Symbol universe to score; blank unions every watchlist you own
view.top_signals.tip.limit: Max rows per side; ranks by score descending
view.top_signals.tip.refresh: Re-score the universe and rebuild both BUY and SELL lists
view.top_signals.hint.highest_and_lowest: Highest- and lowest-scoring symbols across your watchlists (StockInvest-style ranking).
view.trade_compare.h2.normalized_p_l_ove: Normalized P/L overlay (% return vs entry)
view.trade_compare.hint.hold_chart: Each trade's open-to-close hold in hours. Orthogonal to MFE/MAE: a small-MFE trade may have been a short scalp; a large-MFE trade may have been a multi-day swing. Reveals duration vs excursion at a glance.
view.trade_detail.h2.excursion_chart: MFE vs MAE vs Net P&L
