tile.multichart.label: Multi-Chart
tile.multichart.desc: 4-up multi-timeframe grid — shared symbol, per-pane indicators
view.multichart.h1.title: // MULTI-CHART
view.multichart.hint.intro: Four synchronized views of one symbol at different timeframes. Right-click any pane to add indicators — they persist when you change the symbol.
component.grid.hint: Each pane has its own timeframe + indicators. Changing the symbol reloads every pane but keeps timeframes and indicators.
view.s408a.qual.5year: 5-YEAR rule: from January 1 of year of first contribution
view.s408a.qual.conversion_separate: Each Roth conversion: separate 5-year clock (penalty avoidance only)
view.s408a.qual.age_595: Age 59½ — OR — death — OR — disability — OR — first-time home
view.s408a.qual.firsttime: First-time home: $10,000 lifetime limit (NOT first-EVER but no home in 2 yrs)
view.s408a.qual.ordering: Ordering rule: contributions FIRST, then conversions (oldest first), then earnings
view.s408a.qual.earnings: Earnings: tax + 10% § 72(t) penalty if not qualified
view.s408a.bd.step1: Step 1: Make NONDEDUCTIBLE contribution to traditional IRA ($7,000 limit)
view.s408a.bd.step2: Step 2: CONVERT immediately to Roth IRA
view.s408a.bd.no_phaseout: NO MAGI phase-out for nondeductible contribution OR conversion
view.s408a.bd.prorata: § 408(d)(2) PRO-RATA rule: ALL IRAs (pre + after-tax) aggregated
view.s408a.bd.prorata_formula: Taxable % = pre-tax balance / total IRA balance
view.s408a.bd.pretax_workaround: Workaround: roll pre-tax IRAs to 401(k) before conversion (if employer permits)
view.s408a.bd.spousal: Spouse can do separately — $7K each ($14K combined)
view.s408a.bd.step_transaction: Step transaction doctrine: IRS announced will NOT challenge
view.s408a.mega.matching_reduces: Employer matching contributions REDUCE available after-tax space
view.s408a.mega.in_service: In-service conversion to Roth 401(k) OR rollover to Roth IRA
view.s408a.mega.plan_must_allow: Plan must SPECIFICALLY permit (most do not)
view.s408a.mega.no_pretax_dilution: After-tax separate accounting — pro-rata NOT diluted by pre-tax
view.s408a.mega.deemed_irrev: Deemed irrevocable: cannot recharacterize after 2018 TCJA
view.s408a.secure.no_rmd_owner: NO RMD during owner's lifetime (vs Traditional)
view.s408a.secure.eligible_dl: Eligible designated beneficiary: stretch over life expectancy
view.s408a.secure.spouse: Spouse: treat as own + recalculate as owner
view.s408a.secure.minor_child: Minor child: stretch until age 21, then 10-year clock
view.s408a.secure.disabled: Disabled / chronically ill: stretch over life expectancy
view.s408a.secure.10yr_no_rmd: 10-year rule: NO annual RMD — just full distribution by year 10
view.s408a.secure.reg_2024: Reg § 1.401(a)(9) final rules 2024: annual RMD required for non-EDBs of pre-RBD owner
view.s67.susp.legal_taxable: Legal fees in connection with TAXABLE income
view.s67.susp.safe: Safe deposit box rental for taxable securities
view.s67.susp.appraisal: Appraisal fees (charitable contribution-related: still requires appraisal but fee not deductible)
view.s67.susp.iif_estate: Estate tax deduction allocated to income in respect of decedent (IRD) — § 691(c)
view.s67.h2.not_suspended: § 67(b) deductions NOT subject to 2% floor (still allowed)
view.s67.ns.gambling: Gambling losses to extent of winnings (§ 165(d))
view.s67.ns.iif: Estate tax deduction on IRD — § 691(c) (the FULL deduction, not the portion above 2%)
view.s67.ns.s170_charitable: § 170 charitable contributions (subject to AGI %, NOT 2% misc floor)
view.s67.above.s162_se: § 162 Self-employed Schedule C — fully deductible above-line
view.s67.above.s62_a_15: § 62(a)(15) — attorney fees for civil rights / qui tam recoveries
view.s67.s67e.fiduciary: Fiduciary fees + administration expenses UNIQUE to estate/trust: ABOVE-LINE
view.s67.s67e.unique: Test: would an INDIVIDUAL have incurred this expense?
view.s67.s67e.knight: Knight v. Comm. (SCOTUS 2008) — investment advisor fees subject to 2% floor
view.s67.s67e.reg_2014: Reg § 1.67-4 (2014) — clarifies post-Knight: bundled fee unbundling required
view.s67.s67e.notice_2018_61: Notice 2018-61 — § 67(g) does NOT suspend § 67(e) deductions for estates/trusts
view.s67.s67e.beneficiaries: Excess deductions on termination: pass to beneficiaries (post-2017 fix per § 67(g))
view.s67.s67e.bundled: Bundled fees: portion attributable to investment advice (§ 212) NOT deductible
view.s67.plan.s475: Securities traders: § 475(f) MTM election converts § 212 expenses to § 162 (above-line)
view.s67.plan.employer: Employees: get expenses REIMBURSED by employer (accountable plan) — avoids § 67(g)
view.s67.plan.accountable: Accountable plan: substantiate + return excess + business-purpose
view.s67.plan.s125: Cafeteria plan / DCAP / HSA — exclude expenses from income
view.s67.plan.work_home_office: Self-employment / S-corp salary mix: convert to deductible above-line
view.s67.plan.qualified_disaster: Casualty losses: only federally declared disasters deductible
view.s67.plan.s162: S-corp: pay business expenses + reimburse via accountable plan
view.s67.plan.investment_management: Investment management fees: NOT deductible UNTIL § 67(g) sunsets
view.s67.plan.sunset_2026: Plan for sunset Dec 31, 2025 → 2% floor returns 2026
view.s67.plan.tax_court: Tax court appeals: § 67(g) suspension argument upheld (limited exceptions)
view.s32.req.earned_income: Must have EARNED income (wages, SE, statutory employee, etc.)
view.s32.req.agi_limit: AGI below threshold (varies by # kids + filing status)
view.s32.req.ssn: Valid SSN for self + spouse + each qualifying child
view.s32.req.us_citizen: US citizen / resident alien full year
view.s32.req.not_qc: Cannot be qualifying child of another
view.s32.req.not_dependent: Cannot be claimed as dependent of another
view.s32.req.mfs: MFS: limited eligibility per § 32(d)(2) — must meet separation tests
view.s32.disq.cliff: CLIFF — exceed by $1, lose entire credit
view.s32.disq.gross: Gross investment income — losses do NOT offset (per § 32(i)(1))
view.s32.disq.s32_i_2: § 32(i)(2) — other investment income at IRS discretion
view.s32.qc.relationship: Son, daughter, stepchild, foster child, sibling, descendant
view.s32.qc.age: Under 19 OR under 24 + full-time student OR any age if permanently disabled
view.s32.qc.residency: Lived with taxpayer > 6 months in US
view.s32.qc.joint_return: Cannot file joint return (except for refund only)
view.s32.qc.ssn_required: Each child MUST have valid SSN (NOT ITIN) for full EITC w/ kids
view.s32.qc.s32_c_1_e: § 32(c)(1)(E) qualifying child test similar to § 152 but for EITC
view.s32.ban.2yr: § 32(k) 2-year ban for reckless disregard / improper claim
view.s32.ban.recertification: Form 8862 required to recertify after disallowance
view.s32.ban.paid_preparer: Paid preparer § 6695(g) due diligence — Form 8867 ($635 per violation 2024)
view.s32.ban.s7430: Wins by taxpayer against IRS: may collect attorney fees § 7430
view.s32.ban.audit_rate: EITC audit rate ~1% — highest of all taxpayer groups (despite low compliance)
view.s32.ban.s6213_b: § 6213(b) summary assessment authority for EITC disallowance
view.s32.ban.improper_payment: Improper payment rate ~25% — among IRS Top 10 highest
view.s743.adj.formula: Adjustment = Transferee outside basis − Transferee's share of inside basis
view.s743.adj.positive: Positive adjustment (step-up): transferee paid premium for PS interest — depreciation step-up
view.s743.adj.negative: Negative adjustment (step-down): transferee paid discount — reduces depreciation
view.s743.adj.personal: Personal to transferee — does NOT affect other partners
view.s743.adj.s743b_inside_basis: "Transferee's share of inside basis" = transferee's share of common basis
view.s743.adj.future_dep: Future depreciation: transferee gets ADDITIONAL deduction or REDUCED deduction
view.s743.adj.future_gain: Future gain on sale: transferee recognizes LESS gain or MORE loss
view.s743.adj.allocated: Allocated under § 755 first to property class then within class
view.s743.alloc.classes: Two classes: (1) ordinary income property + (2) capital gain property
view.s743.alloc.between: Allocate § 743(b) adj between classes proportional to net unrealized gain/loss in each class
view.s743.alloc.within: Within class: residual method — FMV minus AB minus prior § 743(b) adjustments
view.s743.alloc.s197_residual: § 197 intangibles residual — goodwill last after specifically identified assets
view.s743.alloc.s751_first: § 751 hot assets receive allocation FIRST in ordinary class
view.s743.alloc.alternative: Alternative methods only with IRS consent (rare)
view.s743.alloc.no_below_zero: Adjustment may NOT reduce basis of property below $0 (Reg § 1.755-1(b)(3))
view.s743.alloc.anti_stuffing: Anti-stuffing rules: protect against artificially shifting basis
view.s743.s754.timing: Made on partnership return for year of transfer / distribution
view.s743.s754.permanent: PERMANENT — binding for that year + all subsequent years
view.s743.s754.revocation: Revocation requires IRS consent (limited grounds in Reg § 1.754-1(c))
view.s743.s754.accounting: Partnership must track basis adjustments per partner ($K admin burden)
view.s743.s754.partners_taxable: Election generally taxable for incoming partner (step-up benefit)
view.s743.s754.no_revisit: Cannot re-elect — once revoked, requires another transfer to make new election
view.s743.s754.tax_matters: Tax Matters Partner / partnership rep must sign
view.s743.s754.k1: Form K-1 must reflect partner's § 743(b) adjustment annually
view.s743.sbil.250k: "Substantial built-in loss" = adjusted basis exceeds FMV by > $250,000
view.s743.sbil.tcja: TCJA 2017 added: pre-TCJA only required § 754 election
view.s743.sbil.transferee_loss: Or transferee would be allocated loss > $250K had PS sold all assets
view.s743.sbil.s743_d_3: § 743(d)(3): each partner's loss allocation tested separately for transferee
view.s743.sbil.s743_d_3_b: Substantial built-in loss exists even if § 754 election NOT in place
view.s743.rel.s734: § 734(b) — basis adjustment on distribution (analogous to § 743(b) for distributions)
view.s743.rel.s732: § 732 — basis of distributed property to recipient partner
view.s743.rel.s732_e: § 732(e) — mandatory basis reduction for built-in loss property distribution
view.s743.rel.s731: § 731 — recognition of gain on distribution exceeding outside basis
view.s743.rel.s751: § 751 — unrealized receivables + inventory items (hot assets) — ordinary income classification
view.s743.rel.s197: § 197 — 15-year amortization of acquired intangibles (frequent target of § 743(b))
view.s743.rel.s708: § 708 — partnership termination (no longer triggers technical termination post-TCJA)
view.s743.rel.s7704: § 7704 PTP — special rules for publicly traded partnerships
view.s4958.dp.substantial: PERSON WITH SUBSTANTIAL INFLUENCE: officer, director, key employee, etc.
view.s4958.dp.former: FORMER DP for 5 years after substantial influence ends
view.s4958.dp.family: FAMILY MEMBERS: spouse, ancestors, descendants, siblings + spouses
view.s4958.dp.factors: Factors: voting power, founding role, financial contributions, structure of authority
view.s4958.dp.daf_donor: DAF: donor + family members are DPs to fund
view.s4958.dp.501c3_excluded: EXCLUDED: § 501(c)(3) public charity (org itself), employees making < $130K (2024 indexed), volunteers
view.s4958.dp.regs: Reg § 53.4958-3 factors test for substantial influence
view.s4958.ebt.formula: Excess benefit = VALUE PROVIDED TO DP minus CONSIDERATION DP gave to org
view.s4958.ebt.compensation: Compensation: salary + bonuses + perks + retirement + § 79 life insurance
view.s4958.ebt.s4958_c_2: § 4958(c)(2) automatic excess benefit — DAF grants to DP — ENTIRE grant excess
view.s4958.ebt.economic_benefit: "Economic benefit" includes loans, rentals, sales (FMV vs paid)
view.s4958.ebt.scholarship: Scholarship to DP: typically excess benefit
view.s4958.ebt.partial_release: Forgiveness of debt, payment of personal expenses
view.s4958.ebt.timing: Timing: when economic benefit becomes ascertainable + non-refundable
view.s4958.rebut.independent: Independent board members approved transaction (no conflicts)
view.s4958.rebut.comparable: Comparability data: comparable orgs + arms-length salary surveys
view.s4958.rebut.contemporaneous: Contemporaneous documentation: minutes, comp committee, salary survey data
view.s4958.rebut.satisfaction: Satisfying all 3 → presumption of reasonableness (burden shifts to IRS)
view.s4958.rebut.benchmark: Benchmark data: 3+ similar orgs + similar geographic + sector
view.s4958.rebut.no_safe_harbor: Without safe harbor: facts & circumstances burden on DP/org
view.s4958.corr.repay: DP repays excess to organization (with interest)
view.s4958.corr.timing: Within taxable period = ending 90 days after FIRST notice of deficiency
view.s4958.corr.interest: Interest at § 6621 underpayment rate from date of excess
view.s4958.corr.s4961_abatement: § 4961 — IRS may abate § 4958(b) 200% on timely correction
view.s4958.corr.s4958_d: § 4958(d) — extends correction period during deficiency review
view.s4958.corr.no_double_recovery: DP cannot deduct repayment under § 165 (loss) — adjusts compensation
view.s4958.s4960.tcja: TCJA-added: 21% excise on excess executive comp by tax-exempt orgs
view.s4958.s4960.scope: Applies to: § 501(a) exempt orgs, applicable § 521 cooperatives, etc.
view.s4958.s4960.over_1m: Compensation > $1,000,000 to top-5 highest-paid → 21% on excess
view.s4958.s4960.parachute: Excess parachute payment: 3× base + 21% excise on excess
view.s4958.s4960.deductibility: NOT deductible (no impact — org is exempt)
view.s4958.s4960.regs_2020: Final regs T.D. 9938 (2020): scope, computation, related party rules
view.s4958.s4960.medical_exception: Medical services exception: doctors, vets, etc. — not counted
view.s4958.s4960.top_5: Top 5: aggregated current + 1 prior year HCEs
view.s421.tbl.disq_treatment: Spread = ordinary up to actual gain (§ 421(b))
view.s421.iso.written_plan: Written plan approved by shareholders within 12 months of adoption
view.s421.iso.10_year_grant: Granted within 10 years of plan adoption or approval
view.s421.iso.5pct_110pct: 5%+ owners: exercise price ≥ 110% FMV + 5-year exercise period
view.s421.iso.employee: Granted to employee at grant + employed within 3 months pre-exercise
view.s421.iso.s422_d_100k: § 422(d) $100K annual limit: first $100K of FMV per calendar year
view.s421.iso.disqualifying_2_1: Disqualifying: sale before 2-yr grant OR 1-yr exercise
view.s421.espp.written_plan: Written plan approved by shareholders within 12 months
view.s421.espp.15pct: Maximum 15% discount on LOWER of FMV at grant or FMV at purchase
view.s421.espp.lookback: Lookback feature: discount applied to LOWER of grant or purchase FMV
view.s421.espp.broad_based: Broad-based: all employees (limited exclusions: HCEs, < 2 yrs service, < 20 hrs/week)
view.s421.espp.same_rights: Same rights + privileges across employees (capped per amount)
view.s421.espp.25k_limit: $25,000 annual purchase limit (FMV at grant — not purchase)
view.s421.espp.qualifying_2_1: Qualifying: 2 yrs from BEGINNING of offering + 1 yr from purchase
view.s421.espp.tax_qualifying: Qualifying: ordinary income = lesser of (discount) OR (gain); rest = LTCG
view.s421.espp.disqualifying: Disqualifying: full spread (FMV at purchase − purchase price) = ordinary
view.s421.amt.preference: Spread at exercise = AMT preference item (§ 56(b)(3))
view.s421.amt.regular_no: Regular tax: NO ordinary income at exercise (§ 421(a))
view.s421.amt.amt_yes: AMT: ADD spread to AMTI — may trigger AMT liability
view.s421.amt.amt_basis: AMT basis in stock = exercise price + spread
view.s421.amt.sale_year_recovery: Subsequent sale: AMT basis reduces AMT gain (vs regular)
view.s421.amt.s53_credit: § 53 minimum tax credit recovers AMT paid in future years
view.s421.amt.unrefundable: Pre-TCJA: § 53 credit also refundable; post-TCJA non-refundable
view.s421.amt.early_exercise: Early exercise + § 83(b) election: starts holding clock + minimizes spread
view.s421.amt.disqualifying_amt: Disqualifying disposition same year: AVOIDS AMT (ordinary income for both)
view.s421.dis.trigger: Sale before 2-yr grant OR 1-yr exercise holding
view.s421.dis.ordinary: ORDINARY income = LESSER of (FMV at exercise − strike) OR (sale price − strike)
view.s421.dis.no_amt: NO AMT preference (already ordinary on disqualifying)
view.s421.dis.w2_v: W-2 Box 12 Code V (NSO) or no separate code for ISO disqualifying
view.s421.dis.basis: Basis becomes exercise price + ordinary income
view.s421.dis.capital: Remaining gain = capital (LTCG if held > 1 yr from exercise)
view.s421.dis.same_year_avoid_amt: Disqualifying same calendar year: AVOIDS AMT trap
view.s421.rep.f3921: Form 3921 — ISO exercise. Employer files + employee receives Jan 31
view.s421.rep.f3922: Form 3922 — ESPP transfer of legal title. Employer files + employee receives Jan 31
view.s421.rep.basis_tracking: 3921/3922 critical for tracking basis + holding period
view.s421.rep.w2_v: W-2 Box 12 Code V — NSO exercise spread (ordinary income)
view.s421.rep.no_iso_code: ISO qualifying disposition: NO W-2 code (capital gain only)
view.s421.rep.disqualifying_box1: ISO disqualifying same year: included in Box 1 wages
view.s736.bif.s736b_property: § 736(b) — payments FOR partnership property: capital treatment under § 731/§ 741
view.s736.bif.s736a_not_property: § 736(a) — payments NOT for property: ordinary treatment
view.s736.bif.s736a_1: § 736(a)(1): based on PS INCOME = distributive share (reduces other partners' share)
view.s736.bif.partnership_agreement: Partnership agreement controls — taxpayer can specify allocation
view.s736.bif.economic_substance: Must have economic substance — court will not respect blatant tax-motivated allocation
view.s736.bif.bona_fide: Bona fide allocation: facts & circumstances + reasonable
view.s736.b.gain_on_excess: Gain = LESSER of (payment) OR (payment over outside basis)
view.s736.b.loss_liquidation: Loss only if liquidating + cash/receivables/inventory only
view.s736.b.no_ps_deduction: PS does NOT deduct § 736(b) payments — capital character
view.s736.a.distributive: If varies with PS income → distributive share (reduces other partners')
view.s736.a.guaranteed: If fixed amount → § 707(c) guaranteed payment (PS deducts ordinary)
view.s736.a.se_tax: Subject to SE tax (if partner was general partner)
view.s736.a.no_capital_gain: NOT capital gain — even if would be capital if § 736(b)
view.s736.a.installment: Installment payments: PS deducts in year accrued/paid
view.s736.a.spread_dedn: PS gets ordinary deduction spread across payment periods
view.s736.a.partner_recognizes: Partner recognizes ordinary as RECEIVED (cash method) — installment method does NOT apply
view.s736.a.s451: § 451 timing — accrual at fixed/determinable + economic performance
view.s736.b2.purpose: Applies to GENERAL PARTNER in SERVICE PARTNERSHIP (law, accounting, etc.)
view.s736.b2.scope: Treats payments for unrealized receivables + unstated goodwill as § 736(a)
view.s736.b2.unrealized: Unrealized receivables: accrued but unbilled fees
view.s736.b2.goodwill: "Unstated goodwill" — only counted as § 736(a) if NOT specified in PS agreement
view.s736.b2.if_specified: If PS agreement DOES specify goodwill payments: treated as § 736(b)
view.s736.b2.limited_partner: Limited partner NOT subject to § 736(b)(2) — all property payments are § 736(b)
view.s736.b2.s197: § 197 intangibles — generally retiring partner's share of acquired goodwill
view.s736.b2.law_firm: Law/CPA firm example: retiring partner gets ordinary on uncollected fees + unstated goodwill
view.s736.alloc.optimization: Optimize: balance partner's character + PS deduction
view.s736.alloc.partner_low_bracket: Partner low tax bracket: prefer § 736(a) (ordinary at low rate + PS deducts)
view.s736.alloc.partner_high_bracket: Partner high bracket + remaining partners low: prefer § 736(b) (capital at lower partner rate)
view.s736.alloc.cap_intensive_default: Capital partnership: all § 736(b) by default — limited flexibility
view.s736.alloc.service_default: Service partnership: § 736(b)(2) restricts — unrealized receivables ALWAYS § 736(a)
view.s736.alloc.deal_term: Deal term: allocation in retirement agreement
view.s736.alloc.installment: Installment notes: § 453 capital gain (§ 736(b)) + PS deduction over period (§ 736(a))
view.s461l.stack.cumulative: Cumulative: must apply ALL hurdles before claiming loss
view.s461l.stack.carry_forward: Suspended losses carry to next year + apply same stack
view.s461l.mech.aggregate: Aggregate ALL trades/businesses (sole prop + S-corp + partnership + farm)
view.s461l.mech.threshold_aboveline: Compute below threshold first, then apply
view.s461l.mech.gross_plus: Threshold = gross business income + business gains + threshold amount
view.s461l.mech.wages_excluded: Wages NOT included in gross business income for non-employees
view.s461l.mech.excess_nol: Excess business loss = NOL under § 172 — carries forward 100% indefinite
view.s461l.mech.fpr_excluded: Foreign pass-through entities included (§ 875 trade or business)
view.s461l.mech.s199a_separate: § 199A QBI deduction computed separately (not netted at § 461(l))
view.s461l.exc.trust: Trusts and estates: § 461(l) applies (per § 642(g) NOL coordination)
view.s461l.exc.real_estate: Real estate professionals: passive vs active matters under § 469 first
view.s461l.exc.farmer_5yr: Farmers: 5-year NOL carryback election under § 172(b)(1)(B)(ii) for farming loss
view.s461l.exc.s108_3: Cancellation of debt income (§ 108): NOT business income for § 461(l)
view.s461l.exc.partial_year: First year as taxpayer: full threshold available (no prorating)
view.s461l.exc.mfs: MFS: each spouse gets ½ MFJ threshold ($305K each)
view.s461l.exc.both_spouses: MFJ: both spouses' businesses aggregated to single $610K
view.s461l.excess_note: Excess of $${excess_loss.toLocaleString()} carries forward as § 172 NOL indefinitely + 80% income limit (post-TCJA).
view.s6011.cat.listed: Listed transactions — IRS-designated abusive (Notice 2009-7 etc., basket option contracts, Roth 401(k) etc.)
view.s6011.cat.confidential: Confidential — fee paid + restriction on disclosure to others (Reg § 1.6011-4(b)(3))
view.s6011.cat.contractual: Contractual protection — refund/reduction if no tax benefit (Reg § 1.6011-4(b)(4))
view.s6011.cat.loss: Loss transactions — $10M C-corp / $2M individual / $5M partnership / $50M for 5+ year (Reg § 1.6011-4(b)(5))
view.s6011.cat.toi: Transactions of Interest — IRS designates as worthy of scrutiny (notice / rev. proc.)
view.s6011.cat.s6707a_listed: Listed: 75% of decrease in tax / $200K cap / $100K min individual
view.s6011.f8886.attached: Attached to return for year of participation
view.s6011.f8886.first_year: Plus separate copy sent to OTSA (Office of Tax Shelter Analysis) for FIRST year
view.s6011.f8886.each_year: Filed EACH YEAR taxpayer continues to participate
view.s6011.f8886.tax_benefit: Reports: category, transaction description, parties, projected tax benefit, fees paid
view.s6011.f8886.s6707a: § 6707A penalty: separate from accuracy-related, separate from interest
view.s6011.f8886.s6011_g: § 6011(g) failure to file = automatic § 6707A penalty (no reasonable cause for listed)
view.s6011.f8886.s6664_d: § 6664(d) — reasonable cause defense LIMITED for listed transactions
view.s6011.f8886.advance_disclosure: Advance disclosure: file ahead of return — avoid some penalties
view.s6011.sol.purpose: Extends 3-year ASED to ONE year after Form 8886 filed (if late)
view.s6011.sol.unlimited: If never filed: ASED essentially UNLIMITED for that item
view.s6011.sol.disclosure_protects: Timely Form 8886 disclosure: SOL runs normally from filing of underlying return
view.s6011.sol.s6501_a: § 6501(a) general 3-yr SOL starts from later of due date or actual filing
view.s6011.sol.s6501_e: § 6501(e) 6-yr SOL on 25%+ income omissions still applies
view.s6011.sol.s6664_b: § 6664(b) penalty SOL 3 years after disclosed transaction return filed
view.s6011.rel.s6112: § 6112 — material advisor list maintenance ($10K/day after 20 day request)
view.s6011.rel.s6707: § 6707 — material advisor penalty for failure to disclose
view.s6011.rel.s6701: § 6701 — aiding understatement of liability ($1K/$10K per occurrence)
view.s6011.rel.circular_230: Circular 230 § 10.51 — practitioner discipline for false/fraudulent advice
view.s6011.rel.s7525: § 7525 — federally authorized tax practitioner privilege LIMITED for tax shelters
view.s129.q.work_related: Must be WORK-RELATED (enable taxpayer + spouse to work)
view.s129.q.under_13: Child under 13 at time of care
view.s129.q.live_together: Qualifying individual must live with taxpayer > ½ year
view.s129.q.in_home: In-home care: babysitter, nanny, day care provider
view.s129.q.day_camp: Day camp: yes (residential overnight camp: no)
view.s129.q.preschool: Preschool / nursery school (educational excluded if kindergarten or higher)
view.s129.q.school_tuition: School tuition for kindergarten + above: NOT qualifying
view.s129.s21.no_double_dip: NO double-dipping: § 21 credit-eligible expenses REDUCED by DCAP exclusion
view.s129.s21.limits: § 21 limits: $3,000 (1 dep) / $6,000 (2+ deps) — much lower than § 129 $5K
view.s129.s21.rates: § 21 credit rate: 20-35% of expenses (sliding with AGI)
view.s129.s21.arpa: ARPA 2021 expanded § 21 to refundable + larger — expired
view.s129.s21.dcap_first: Strategy: § 129 DCAP exclusion first (saves 22-37% income + FICA ~7.65%), then § 21 credit on remainder
view.s129.s21.low_income: Low-income: § 21 credit better (35%); but DCAP saves FICA too
view.s129.s21.split_optimization: Split optimization: DCAP up to limit + § 21 credit on excess
view.s129.nd.eligibility: § 129(d)(2) eligibility: not in favor of HCEs / 5% owners
view.s129.nd.benefits_55_5: § 129(d)(3): benefits to 5% owners cannot exceed 25% of total
view.s129.nd.utilization: § 129(d)(5): average benefits to non-HCEs ≥ 55% of HCE average
view.s129.nd.reasonable: § 129(d)(4): reasonable communication to all eligible employees
view.s129.nd.safe_harbor: § 129(d)(6): safe harbor for plans satisfying ratio + average benefits
view.s129.nd.failure: Failure: HCE benefits become TAXABLE (non-HCEs preserved exclusion)
view.s129.nd.s125_separate: § 125 cafeteria plan nondiscrimination separate (key employee + average benefit)
view.s129.nd.testing_year: Annual testing — failure assessed at YEAR END
view.s129.fsa.s125: § 125 cafeteria plan election irrevocable for year
view.s129.fsa.reimbursement: Reimbursement only after expense incurred + receipts submitted
view.s129.fsa.qualifying_event: Mid-year change: only with qualifying life event (birth, marriage, divorce)
view.s129.fsa.cares_act_2020: CARES Act 2020 + ARPA 2021 temporary flexibility (carryover allowed for 2020/2021)
view.s129.fsa.no_run_out: No "run-out" period in following year for dep care (vs health FSA)
view.s129.fsa.employer_match: Some employers contribute additional dep care benefit on top
view.s752.rec.step1: Assume all partnership liabilities become due + payable at FMV
view.s752.rec.step2: Partnership sells all assets for $0 → losses charged to capital accounts
view.s752.rec.step3: Partner's deficit in capital account = obligation to restore (if any)
view.s752.rec.step4: Partner bears EROL (economic risk of loss) = recourse share
view.s752.rec.deficit_restoration: DRO (deficit restoration obligation) = mandatory if § 704(b) safe harbor used
view.s752.rec.guarantee: Partner guarantee = EROL only if NO reimbursement right
view.s752.rec.indemnity: Indemnity vs guarantee — indemnity transfers EROL between partners
view.s752.nr.t1: Tier 1: partnership minimum gain (PMG) — partner's share per § 704(b)
view.s752.nr.t2: Tier 2: § 704(c) minimum gain — partner's share of built-in gain on contributed property
view.s752.nr.t3: Tier 3: remainder allocated by profit-sharing % OR other reasonable method
view.s752.nr.t3_alt: Tier 3 alternative methods: SIBR (significant item method), liquidation method, profit method
view.s752.nr.pmg_def: PMG = excess of NR debt over book basis of secured property
view.s752.nr.s704_c_min_gain: § 704(c) min gain = built-in gain reduced by depreciation
view.s752.nr.consistent: Method must be CONSISTENT year-to-year unless reasonable cause
view.s752.nr.exculpatory: Exculpatory liability: lender recourse to partnership but NOT individual partner
view.s752.atrisk.qnr: EXCEPTION: § 465(b)(6) qualified nonrecourse financing in real estate
view.s752.atrisk.qnr_definition: QNR = secured by real property + held by activity of holding real property + lender unrelated
view.s752.atrisk.recourse_general: Recourse: generally at-risk to extent of personal liability
view.s752.atrisk.suspended: Disallowed losses suspended + carry forward indefinitely
view.s752.atrisk.recapture: § 465(e) recapture if at-risk falls below $0 (e.g., refinance NR)
view.s752.spec.contribution_basis: Contributed property with liab > basis: § 357(c) does NOT apply (partnership uses § 752)
view.s752.spec.disguised_sale: § 707(a)(2)(B) disguised sale: contribution + distribution within 2 years
view.s752.spec.s704c: § 704(c) built-in gain forced allocation on contribution
view.s752.spec.gracias_share: "Gracias" share method — pro-rata by profit share for tier 3
view.s752.spec.partnership_distribution: Partnership-level liability decrease may trigger § 752(b) to all partners
view.s752.spec.related_party: § 752(b)(2) related-party transfer: 90% test for liability attribution
view.s752.spec.tiered_partnerships: Tiered partnerships: look through upper-tier to lower partner
view.s752.spec.dre: Disregarded entity (DRE): owner deemed to bear EROL
view.s475.qt.substantial: SUBSTANTIAL activity — typically 720+ trades / year + 4+ trades/day average
view.s475.qt.regular_frequent: Regular + frequent — trade most days the market is open (200+ trading days)
view.s475.qt.short_holding: Short avg holding period (days to weeks, NOT months)
view.s475.qt.daily_swings: Profit motivation: daily price swings, NOT long-term appreciation + dividends
view.s475.qt.facts_circumstances: Facts & circumstances test — NO bright lines
view.s475.qt.endicott_factors: Endicott v. Comm. 145 T.C. 11 (2015): 4 factors weight
view.s475.qt.mayer: Mayer v. Comm. T.C. Memo 1994-209: original 4-factor framework
view.s475.qt.partnership_trader: Partnership trader: each partner's election may differ
view.s475.elect.timing: Election due by April 15 (unextended) of FIRST year for which effective
view.s475.elect.attached: Statement attached to timely filed return for PRIOR year
view.s475.elect.s475_f_2_b: § 475(f)(2)(B) — election applies starting NEXT taxable year
view.s475.elect.f3115_year_after: Form 3115 filed in YEAR AFTER election (change in accounting method)
view.s475.elect.s481_a: § 481(a) adjustment: MTM all open positions at FMV (favorable + unfavorable)
view.s475.elect.identification: § 475(c)(2)(C): securities held for investment must be IDENTIFIED on same day
view.s475.elect.id_failure: Failed identification → MTM applies to ALL securities
view.s475.elect.revocation: Revocation: Rev. Proc. 2017-30 — automatic with timely Form 3115
view.s475.elect.binding: Binding for that year + all subsequent years (until revoked)
view.s475.ben.no_capital_loss_limit: + NO $3,000 capital loss limitation (ordinary loss fully deductible)
view.s475.ben.s212_above_line: + Above-line Schedule C deductions (no § 67(g) 2% AGI floor)
view.s475.ben.s162: + § 162 ordinary & necessary business expense full deductibility
view.s475.ben.s199a_nope: + § 199A QBI — generally NOT eligible (specified service trade — investment management)
view.s475.ben.s481_a_payment: - § 481(a) catch-up adjustment may be large + payable in year of change
view.s475.ben.no_qualified_dividend: - Dividends on held positions still qualified — separately reported
view.s475.ben.unrealized_loss: - Year-end MTM forces unrealized loss recognition (cash flow + tax)
view.s475.ben.iso_disqualifying: - ISO exercise + sale subject to MTM if held in trading account
view.s475.id.purpose: Permits dealer/trader to hold INVESTMENT securities separately
view.s475.id.same_day: Identification ON SAME DAY of acquisition (Reg § 1.475(c)-2)
view.s475.id.separate_account: Best practice: separate "investment account" (NOT MTM) from "trading account" (MTM)
view.s475.id.late_id: Late identification → security MTM applied; subsequent sale = ordinary
view.s475.id.no_id: No identification: all securities MTM, ordinary
view.s475.id.brokerage: Multiple brokerage accounts can serve as natural separation
view.s871.fdap.gain_securities: Gain on US securities IF 183+ days physical presence § 871(a)(2)
view.s871.fdap.no_se: No social security or Medicare tax (NRA)
view.s871.fdap.withheld: Withheld at source by payor (Form 1042 / 1042-S reporting)
view.s871.ex.portfolio: § 871(h) Portfolio interest exemption: bonds issued post-July 1984 to non-bank NRA
view.s871.ex.bank: § 871(i)(2) Bank deposit interest: US bank deposits exempt (most countries)
view.s871.ex.os_securities: § 871(k) Original issue discount on short-term (≤ 183 days) obligations
view.s871.ex.short_term_gain: Short-term securities gains (NOT covered by 30% unless 183+ days)
view.s871.ex.s897: FIRPTA real estate: § 897(a) treats as ECI regardless
view.s871.ex.scholarship: Scholarship + grant from US source: § 117 partial exclusion
view.s871.eci.brackets: Graduated brackets (10%-37%) — same as US persons
view.s871.eci.s_864c: § 864(c) attribution: income "effectively connected" with US TB
view.s871.eci.personal_services: Personal services in US = US TB (§ 864(b))
view.s871.eci.merchandising: Merchandising activity in US = US TB
view.s871.eci.dealer: Trading securities for OWN account by dealer = US TB
view.s871.eci.investor: Trading securities by investor (not dealer) NOT US TB (Reg § 1.864-2(c)(2))
view.s871.eci.s897_real: § 897(a) US real property gain → automatic ECI treatment
view.s871.eci.s871d: § 871(d) election to treat real property rents as ECI (net basis)
view.s871.m.purpose: Anti-avoidance: treats notional dividends in equity derivatives as US-source dividends
view.s871.m.delta: Delta-1 instruments (futures, options w/ delta ≥ 0.8): subject
view.s871.m.complex_contracts: Complex contracts (variable rate, multi-leg): tested under "substantial equivalence"
view.s871.m.30pct: 30% withholding (or treaty rate) on notional dividend portion
view.s871.m.notional: Notional principal amount × actual underlying dividend
view.s871.m.qualified_index: QDD (qualified derivatives dealer) safe harbor: post-2017 limited relief
view.s871.m.no_treaty_relief: Substitute payments NOT eligible for treaty benefits (most treaties)
view.s871.sp.closer_connection: § 7701(b)(3)(B) closer connection exception: < 183 days current + tax home in foreign country
view.s871.sp.medical_exception: Medical condition exception: emergency medical treatment
view.s871.sp.tax_treaty: Treaty tie-breaker can override substantial presence (Form 8833)
view.s2055.h1.title: // § 2055 ESTATE CHARITABLE DED
view.s2055.crt.crat: CRAT — fixed annual payment (annuity); payout 5% min - 50% max of initial value
view.s2055.crt.crut: CRUT — variable annual payment % of annual value; same payout limits
view.s2055.crt.charitable_remainder: Charitable remainder: ≥ 10% of fair market value at inception
view.s2055.crt.income: Income beneficiaries: get annuity / unitrust payments during term
view.s2055.crt.term: Term: lifetime + ≤ 20 years OR fixed term ≤ 20 years
view.s2055.crt.benefits: Benefits: estate deduction + tax-free trust growth + income for life
view.s2055.crt.s664: § 664 requirements: written trust + qualifying charitable beneficiary
view.s2055.crt.flip_clut: FLIP CLUT: starts as straight CLUT, "flips" to CRUT on specific event
view.s2055.clt.opposite: Opposite of CRT: charity gets income FIRST, family gets remainder
view.s2055.clt.clut: CLUT — variable annual % of trust value
view.s2055.clt.income_tax_treatment: Income tax: grantor CLT vs non-grantor CLT (different income recognition)
view.s2055.clt.estate_tax: Estate tax deduction: equal to PV of charitable lead at IRS 7520 rate
view.s2055.clt.zero_out: "Zero-out" technique: payments to charity = PV of property → no remainder taxable
view.s2055.clt.low_rate_benefit: Low 7520 rate benefits remainder beneficiary (more accumulates)
view.s2055.clt.gst_planning: GST planning: zero-out CLT may avoid GST on remainder to grandchildren
view.s2055.coord.same_charity: Same charity may receive estate (§ 2055) + lifetime (§ 170) gifts
view.s2055.coord.public_60pct: Public charity: 60% AGI cap for cash; 30% for appreciated property
view.s2055.coord.5yr_carryover: § 170 5-year carryover for amounts above AGI limit
view.s2055.coord.crt_lifetime: CRT during life: § 170 deduction = PV of remainder + § 2055 at death if applicable
view.s2055.coord.daf: Donor-Advised Fund: § 170 deduction immediate; estate deduction at death if balance remains
view.s2055.coord.pf_self_dealing: Private foundation: § 4941 self-dealing rules + § 4942 distribution requirement
view.s2055.coord.qcd: QCD (§ 408(d)(8)): IRA → charity directly ($105K limit 2024) — separate income tax benefit
view.s2055.si.requirements: Split-interest: combo of charitable + non-charitable beneficiaries
view.s2055.si.specific_forms: Only certain forms qualify for deduction: CRAT, CRUT, CLT, PIF, qualified PRI
view.s2055.si.valuation: Charitable portion valued under § 7520 / Reg 20.2031-7 actuarial tables
view.s2055.si.special_factors: Special factors: payment timing, asset volatility, term length
view.s2055.si.deeded: Deeded interest in real property: limited; partial gift rules
view.s2055.si.qualified_pri: Qualified Personal Residence Interest (QPRI): limited estate deduction
view.s2055.si.non_qualifying: Non-qualifying split-interest: NO deduction (charitable portion lost)
view.s2055.split_note: Split-interest trust: only charitable portion (PV of remainder for CRT or PV of lead for CLT) deductible under § 2055. Actuarial computation under § 7520 rate. Monitor low rate environment for CLT planning (more residual to family); high rate environment for CRT (more charitable remainder).
view.s511.h1.title: // § 511 UBIT
view.s511.test.trade: TRADE OR BUSINESS: activity for production of income from sale of goods / performance of services
view.s511.test.regular: REGULARLY CARRIED ON: continuous or recurring (vs occasional / one-time)
view.s511.test.unrelated: NOT SUBSTANTIALLY RELATED: causal connection to exempt purpose required (not just income generation)
view.s511.test.related: If substantially related (gift shop in museum) → NOT UBTI
view.s511.test.aggregation: Aggregation: multiple unrelated activities considered separately (post-TCJA siloing)
view.s511.test.fragmentation: Fragmentation: bundled activities may be evaluated separately
view.s511.test.s512a6: § 512(a)(6): separate computation per "trade or business" — defined per § 1.512(a)-6
view.s511.exc.volunteer: Volunteer labor: substantially all (85%+) by volunteers (§ 513(a)(1))
view.s511.exc.convenience: Convenience for members / students / employees (§ 513(a)(2)) — cafeteria, bookstore
view.s511.exc.donated: Donated inventory: 100% inventory donated (§ 513(a)(3)) — thrift store
view.s511.exc.bingo: Bingo / qualifying gaming (§ 513(f)) — primary purpose religious / charitable
view.s511.exc.publication: Distribution of low-cost items (§ 513(h)) — items ≤ $13.20 (2024 indexed)
view.s511.exc.fundraising: Fundraising events: not regularly carried on if annual / occasional
view.s511.exc.mailings: Exchanges + rentals of mailing lists: NOT UBI (§ 513(h)(B))
view.s511.exc.research: Research income for federal / academic purposes: § 512(b)(8) exception
view.s511.passive.rents_personal: Personal property rents: ≤ 10% with real property OK; otherwise UBI
view.s511.passive.research_grants: Research grants from US gov / academic (§ 512(b)(7)-(9))
view.s511.passive.debt_financed: EXCEPTION — DEBT-FINANCED PROPERTY (§ 514): UBI to extent of debt %
view.s511.passive.controlled_org: Controlled organization income (§ 512(b)(13)): from > 50% owned sub may be UBI
view.s511.silo.purpose: Prevent net loss in one UBI activity from offsetting income in another
view.s511.silo.per_business: Compute UBTI separately for EACH trade or business
view.s511.silo.naics: Trade or business defined by NAICS 2-digit code (Reg § 1.512(a)-6)
view.s511.silo.aggregate: Within same NAICS: aggregate income + expenses
view.s511.silo.investment_silos: Investment partnership K-1s: aggregate by "facts + circumstances"
view.s511.silo.nol_per: NOLs: tracked per silo; cannot cross-silo
view.s511.silo.qualified_partnership: "Qualifying partnership interest" (QPI): ≤ 2% direct OR ≤ 20% with no control = grouped
view.s511.silo.s990t_columns: Form 990-T: separate columns per silo with own loss carryover
view.s511.tax_note: UBIT TRIGGERED — file Form 990-T. Significant UBI may trigger IRS scrutiny of overall 501(c)(3) classification (if UBI is "substantial" portion of activity). Risk of revocation if UBI dominates. Consider spinning UBI activity into for-profit sub or partnership. § 512(a)(6) siloing limits cross-activity loss netting (TCJA 2017).
view.s707.a.purpose: Partner transacts with partnership AS IF NOT a partner
view.s707.a.services: Services: compensation income to partner, deduction by PS
view.s707.a.s707_a_2_a: § 707(a)(2)(A) — distinction between bona fide allocation/distribution + § 707(a) transaction
view.s707.a.related_party: Related party transactions: arm's length scrutiny + § 482-like
view.s707.dis.purpose: Anti-abuse: cannot avoid sale treatment via contribution + distribution
view.s707.dis.2year: 2-YEAR presumption: contribution + distribution within 2 years = sale
view.s707.dis.factors: Factors: timing, conditioning, certainty, entrepreneurial risk, related parties
view.s707.dis.outside_2year: Outside 2 years: presumption REVERSED — not disguised sale unless facts indicate
view.s707.dis.assumed_liability: Assumed liability counted as part of "distribution" (Reg § 1.707-5)
view.s707.dis.safe_harbors: Safe harbors: operating distributions, reimbursements (Rev. Proc. 2017-31)
view.s707.b.loss: § 707(b)(1) — LOSS on sale partner ↔ controlled PS: DISALLOWED entirely
view.s707.b.gain: § 707(b)(2) — GAIN on sale between commonly-controlled PSs: ORDINARY (recharacterization)
view.s707.b.s267: Compare § 267: similar disallowance for related individuals + entities
view.s707.b.disallow_carryover: Disallowed loss: lost to seller; basis NOT increased to buyer either
view.s707.b.s267_d: § 267(d) subsequent gain: buyer offsets prior disallowed loss when buyer sells
view.s707.b.brother_sister: "Brother-sister" entities: both controlled by same partner = § 707(b)(2) applies
view.s707.c.fixed: Fixed payment determined WITHOUT regard to partnership income
view.s707.c.like_compensation: Treated as § 162 compensation (deductible by PS, ordinary to partner)
view.s707.c.timing: Timing: deducted by PS when accrued/paid (Rev. Rul. 2007-40)
view.s707.c.se_tax: Subject to SE tax (§ 1402(a)(13) general partner exception does NOT apply)
view.s707.c.no_partner_status: Does NOT confer partner status if not otherwise partner
view.s707.c.priority_distribution: Distinguish from PRIORITY DISTRIBUTION (varies with PS income) — § 704(b) allocation
view.s707.c.preferred_return: Preferred return on capital: may be guaranteed payment OR § 704(b) allocation
view.s707.c.documentation: Documentation in partnership agreement key for characterization
view.s707.c.s162_ordinary: PS deducts as § 162 ordinary expense unless capital expenditure
view.s707.safe.operating_dist: Operating distributions: not deemed sale if pursuant to bona fide PS agreement
view.s707.safe.reimbursement: Reimbursement of preformation costs: within 2 years OK if < 20% FMV at contribution
view.s707.safe.preferred_return: Preferred return: safe harbor for 150% of AFR — not deemed disguised sale
view.s707.safe.cash_flow: Cash flow distributions: based on partnership income — safe
view.s707.safe.qualified_liab: Qualified liabilities (Reg § 1.707-5(a)(6)) — not counted as distribution
view.s707.safe.s737_seven_year: § 737 7-yr lookback parallel for built-in gain protection
view.s707.safe.s704_c_7yr: § 704(c)(1)(B) 7-year built-in gain recognition on contributed property
view.s304.bs.transferor: Seller owns ≥ 50% of BOTH issuing + acquiring corps
view.s304.bs.transfer: Seller transfers issuing-corp stock to acquiring corp for cash + property
view.s304.bs.deemed: DEEMED REDEMPTION by issuing corp + § 351 contribution to acquiring
view.s304.bs.s301: § 301 distribution treatment applied: 1st acquiring E&P, then issuing E&P
view.s304.bs.basis_to_acquiring: Acquiring's basis in issuing stock = seller's basis in issuing stock
view.s304.bs.purpose: Purpose: prevent disguising dividend as sale (between commonly-controlled corps)
view.s304.bs.s304_b_2: § 304(b)(2): combined E&P rule — pool both corps' E&P
view.s304.ps.transfer: Shareholder transfers PARENT stock to SUBSIDIARY for cash
view.s304.ps.deemed: DEEMED REDEMPTION by parent of its own stock + § 351 contribution to subsidiary
view.s304.ps.purpose: Eliminates "earnings stripping" via downstream stock sales
view.s304.ps.allocation: E&P: parent's E&P utilized first (only parent's)
view.s304.ps.s304_a_1_a: § 304(a)(2)(A) — must transfer parent stock to subsidiary
view.s304.ps.parent_controls: Parent must control subsidiary (50%+ vote OR value)
view.s304.ord.1: First: § 301(c)(1) DIVIDEND up to ACQUIRING corp E&P
view.s304.ord.2: Second: § 301(c)(1) DIVIDEND up to ISSUING corp E&P (Brother-sister only)
view.s304.ord.3: Third: § 301(c)(2) BASIS RECOVERY (reduces basis in acquiring corp stock)
view.s304.ord.s956_loss: If parent/issuing has no E&P: basis recovery + capital gain (escape dividend)
view.s304.ord.s243_drd: If buyer is corp: § 243 DRD potentially available + § 246A debt-financed limitations
view.s304.ord.s1059: § 1059 may apply to corp seller (extraordinary dividend)
view.s304.esc.s304_b_3: § 304(b)(3): if § 302(b)(1)-(4) test met → SALE/EXCHANGE treatment (capital gain only)
view.s304.esc.substantial: Substantial disproportionate redemption: < 50% AND < 80% × prior ownership
view.s304.esc.s318_first: § 318 attribution applied FIRST to determine "ownership reduction"
view.s304.esc.attribution_blocks: Practical: § 318 attribution often blocks § 302 escape (related parties)
view.s304.fb.purpose: Anti-abuse: foreign acquiring corp used to repatriate CFC earnings without § 956 inclusion
view.s304.fb.s304_b_5: § 304(b)(5) — special rules when acquiring corp is FOREIGN
view.s304.fb.s956: § 956: 50%+ owned CFC stock transferred → still potential § 956 inclusion
view.s304.fb.s245a: § 245A 100% DRD potentially available if recharacterized dividend qualifies
view.s304.fb.s959: § 959 PTI rules — distributions out of previously taxed income preserved
view.s304.fb.notice_88_38: Notice 88-38 + subsequent guidance on foreign blockers
view.s304.fb.s7701_l: § 7701(l) anti-conduit rules may apply if treaty country blocker used
view.s988.cov.iv: § 988(c)(1)(B)(iv) — accrued items + similar in nonfunctional currency
view.s988.cov.s988_c_2: § 988(c)(2) — currency swap (treated as series of forward contracts)
view.s988.cov.notional_principal: Notional principal contracts (§ 1.446-3) — generally NOT § 988 unless currency-denominated
view.s988.cov.s_988_c_d_i: § 988(c)(1)(D)(i) — debt subject to original holding period
view.s988.char.scope: Election scope: forward/futures/options NOT subject to § 1256
view.s988.char.id_required: Identification required: by close of day acquired
view.s988.char.binding: Election BINDING — separately for each transaction
view.s988.char.s988_e_personal: § 988(e) personal transactions: capital character + $200 de minimis
view.s988.char.s988_d_hedge: § 988(d) hedging: character matches underlying hedged item
view.s988.s1256.fx_futures: Major currency futures (CME EUR/USD, JPY/USD) — § 1256 (regulated futures)
view.s988.s1256.forex_etf: Forex ETF (e.g., FXE) — generally § 988 ordinary, may elect § 1256 in some cases
view.s988.s1256.spot_fx: Spot FX: ALWAYS § 988 — ordinary (unless cap election + identification)
view.s988.s1256.retail_forex: Retail forex traders: complex — see Notice 2007-71, IRS Memo TAM 9809001
view.s988.s987.qbu_def: Qualified Business Unit (QBU): separate + clearly identified set of books in foreign currency
view.s988.s987.functional: Functional currency: currency of economic environment of operations
view.s988.s987.us_default: US person functional currency: USD by default
view.s988.s987.translation: QBU income translated to USD using weighted-average exchange rate
view.s988.s987.remittance: Remittance from QBU to US owner: § 987 gain/loss recognized
view.s988.s987.section_987_final_regs: § 987 final regs (2024): mark-to-market QBU equity + recognize FX gain/loss
view.s988.s987.deferral: Deferral periods for non-economic FX volatility
view.s988.s987.s988_coordination: § 988 transactions WITHIN QBU translated, not separately reported
view.s988.s987.foreign_branch: Foreign branch: typically a QBU for § 987
view.s988.s987.dre: Disregarded entity: aggregated with owner's QBU
view.s988.spec.personal_de_minimis: Personal transactions: $200 de minimis exclusion (per transaction)
view.s988.spec.travel: Travel + meals abroad: typically below $200 threshold per transaction
view.s988.spec.large_personal: Large personal FX transaction: capital character (under § 988(e))
view.s988.spec.foreign_real_estate: Foreign real estate sale: separate § 988 + § 1031 analysis
view.s988.spec.foreign_mortgage: Foreign currency mortgage: § 988(b) — capital on principal + ordinary on interest
view.s988.spec.crypto: Cryptocurrency: NOT § 988 currency (Notice 2014-21 — property, not currency)
view.s988.spec.s988_d_failure: Failed hedge identification: ordinary character preserved (no character matching)
view.s988.spec.f8865_reporting: Form 8865 reports foreign partnership FX transactions
view.s102.dub.test: "Detached and disinterested generosity" — out of love, affection, respect, charity
view.s102.dub.constraining: NOT constraining force / moral duty / business expectation
view.s102.dub.intent: Donor's intent CONTROLS (objective evidence of motivation)
view.s102.dub.context: Context: family vs business; surprise vs anticipated; quid-pro-quo
view.s102.dub.consideration: Past or implied consideration defeats gift status
view.s102.dub.tips: Tips: NOT gifts (Olk v. United States) — even if voluntary
view.s102.dub.crowdfunding: Crowdfunding / GoFundMe: typically gift if non-business + no return expected
view.s102.dub.windfall: Windfall + lottery: NOT gift (winner has earned via game)
view.s102.s102c.purpose: Anti-abuse: prevents disguising compensation as gift
view.s102.s102c.applies: Transfer from EMPLOYER to EMPLOYEE (current or former) presumed comp
view.s102.s102c.s132_b: § 132(b) de minimis fringe benefits — separately excluded
view.s102.s102c.bona_fide_owner: Owner-employee from personal funds: may rebut presumption (facts & circumstances)
view.s102.s102c.business_purpose: Business purpose evidence: pay-for-performance vs gift on personal occasion
view.s102.s102c.death_benefit: Employer death benefit to family: § 102(c) presumed but rebuttal possible (§ 101(b))
view.s102.tbl.no_cash: NO cash, cash equivalent, gift card, stocks
view.s102.tbl.length_safety: Length of service / safety achievement only
view.s102.s1015.dual_basis: § 1015(a) dual basis: for LOSS purposes = LESSER of donor basis OR FMV at gift
view.s102.s1015.holding_tacks: Holding period TACKS — donee holds since donor's acquisition
view.s102.s1015.gift_tax_adj: § 1015(d) basis adjustment: + portion of gift tax paid attributable to net appreciation
view.s102.s1015.formula: Adjustment = gift tax × (net appreciation / FMV at gift)
view.s102.s1015.bargain_sale: Bargain sale: basis allocation between sale portion + gift portion
view.s102.s1015.s2701_2704: § 2701-2704 — special valuation rules for family transfers (estate planning)
view.s102.s1015.f709: Form 709 — gift tax return (annual exclusion + lifetime exemption)
view.s102.s1014.general: Inherited property: basis = FMV at decedent's DATE OF DEATH
view.s102.s1014.alt: § 2032 alternate valuation date: 6 months after death (if elected at estate level)
view.s102.s1014.unrealized_gain_extingui: Unrealized gain at death: ELIMINATED — major estate planning benefit
view.s102.s1014.community_property: Community property double step-up at first death (§ 1014(b)(6))
view.s102.s1014.s1014_e: § 1014(e) — gift within 1 year of death + back to donor: NO step-up
view.s102.s1014.s691_c: § 691(c) estate tax deduction on IRD — offsets income tax on IRD
view.s102.s1014.s2056_marital: § 2056 marital deduction unlimited — no estate tax + step-up preserved
view.s102.s1014.beneficiary_step_up: Inherited Roth IRA: still tax-free + 10-year rule (post-SECURE)
view.s102.spec.tips: Tips: § 61 ordinary income (NOT gift) — even when voluntary
view.s102.spec.scholarship: § 117 scholarship: tax-free for qualified educational expenses
view.s102.spec.crowdfunding: Crowdfunding (GoFundMe etc.): facts & circumstances — typically gift if donative intent
view.s102.spec.support_payments: Support payments to family: typically gift (§ 102(a)) — not income
view.s102.spec.s74_b_charitable_exceptio: § 74(b) — Nobel Prize/similar: excluded if assigned to charity
view.s102.spec.political: Political gifts to campaigns: tax-free to candidate (§ 527)
view.s102.spec.kickback_disguised: "Kickbacks" disguised as gifts: § 162(c) — not gift, illegal payment
view.s102.spec.bribery: Bribes: § 162(c) NOT deductible — NOT gift to recipient (illegal)
view.s382.oc.threshold: "Ownership change" = > 50 PERCENTAGE POINT increase in stock owned by 5%+ shareholders
view.s382.oc.testing: Testing period: 3 years ending on potential change date
view.s382.oc.shareholder: "5% shareholder" includes both direct (named) + indirect (via aggregation)
view.s382.oc.public_group: "Public group" = aggregation of all < 5% shareholders (treated as single 5% shareholder)
view.s382.oc.equity_structure: Test based on lower of: voting power OR value
view.s382.oc.options: Stock options + convertible debt: treated as ownership if "in the money"
view.s382.oc.equity_split: Equity restructuring + recapitalization may trigger ownership change
view.s382.lim.formula: Annual limit = FMV of loss corp (immediately before change) × LT tax-exempt rate
view.s382.lim.rate: LT tax-exempt rate: highest of 3 prior months (published monthly by IRS)
view.s382.lim.carryforward: Unused limit: carries forward to next year (cumulative)
view.s382.lim.s382_b_2: § 382(b)(2): in change year, prorate based on days in year
view.s382.lim.s382_e_anti_stuffing: § 382(e) anti-stuffing: redemptions/contributions in 2-year period before change reduce FMV
view.s382.lim.zero_fmv: If FMV at change is ~$0 (bankruptcy etc.): annual limit = $0, NOLs functionally lost
view.s382.rb.purpose: 5-year window: identifies built-in gains/losses at time of change
view.s382.rb.rbig: RBIG: recognized built-in gain — ADDS to annual limit when recognized
view.s382.rb.rbil: RBIL: recognized built-in loss — subject to § 382 limit (does NOT escape)
view.s382.rb.threshold: NUBIG/NUBIL threshold: lesser of $10M OR 15% of value of asset at change
view.s382.rb.below_threshold: If below threshold: no RBIG/RBIL adjustments
view.s382.rb.s338_approach: § 338 approach: hypothetical sale at change FMV identifies all gains/losses
view.s382.rb.s1374_approach: § 1374 approach: track items individually recognized in 5-year window
view.s382.rb.s382_h_8: § 382(h)(8) — deferred CODI included if subject to attribute reduction
view.s382.cobe.purpose: Anti-trafficking: prevents acquiring loss corp + immediately shutting down
view.s382.cobe.test: Must EITHER continue historic business OR use significant historic assets
view.s382.cobe.failure: If FAIL: pre-change NOLs ELIMINATED entirely (not just limited)
view.s382.relief.s382_l_5: § 382(l)(5) bankruptcy: title 11 reorg — exempt if old shareholders retain 50%+
view.s382.relief.s382_l_6: § 382(l)(6) bankruptcy alternative: increased FMV based on post-reorg value
view.s382.relief.s269_b: § 269(b)(2) — relief from § 269 if no tax avoidance purpose
view.s382.relief.s338_election: § 338 / § 336(e) election: step-up basis avoids inheriting limitation in some cases
view.s382.relief.f_reorganization: F-reorganization: NO ownership change (single corp continuation)
view.s382.relief.qstk: QSST + ESBT for S-corp ownership testing
view.s382.relief.poison_pill: Poison pill / shareholder rights plan — defends against unintentional change
view.s351.req.stock: Receives ONLY STOCK in exchange (boot allowed but recognized)
view.s351.req.control: Transferor(s) immediately after exchange CONTROL 80%+ (voting + total)
view.s351.req.s368c: § 368(c) control = 80% of voting + 80% of each non-voting class
view.s351.req.transferors_aggregate: "Transferors" aggregate — all who transfer in single transaction
view.s351.req.related_steps: Related step transactions: integrated under § 351 (Rev. Rul. 70-140)
view.s351.req.s368b: § 368(b) — single class of stock not required (vs S-corp)
view.s351.boot.property: Other property = boot — basis carries over
view.s351.boot.securities: Securities (notes / bonds) treated as boot under § 354(a)(2)
view.s351.boot.short_term: Short-term debt instruments treated as cash
view.s351.boot.gain_limit: Recognize LESSER of (gain realized) OR (boot received)
view.s351.boot.allocation: Multiple properties: allocate boot proportionally by FMV
view.s351.boot.character: Boot gain character = character of property (cap vs ordinary)
view.s351.liab.s357b: § 357(b) tax avoidance / non-business purpose: liab = boot
view.s351.liab.s357c: § 357(c) liabilities exceed basis: gain to extent of excess
view.s351.liab.s357c_3: § 357(c)(3) deductible liabilities (A/P, accrued exp) NOT counted in liab
view.s351.liab.s357d: § 357(d) — recourse vs nonrecourse — only deemed assumed if economically borne
view.s351.liab.s358d: § 358(d) treat assumed liability as money received for basis (for stock)
view.s351.liab.s362e_1: § 362(e)(1) limited to FMV for net built-in loss property
view.s351.tbl.t_formula: Property basis + gain recognized − boot − liabilities
view.s351.rel.s7701l: § 7701(l) anti-conduit for cross-border § 351 with foreign acquirer
view.s351.rel.s367a: § 367(a) outbound transfers to foreign corp — gain recognition
view.s351.rel.s897: § 897 FIRPTA — US real property to foreign corp
view.s2503.h1.title: // § 2503 GIFT ANNUAL EXCLUSION
view.s2503.amt.spouse_split: Spousal gift splitting (consent both spouses): $36K / $38K combined
view.s2503.amt.s529_forward: § 529 plan 5-year election: $90K / $95K front-loaded (cannot exceed in next 4 yrs)
view.s2503.amt.non_us_spouse: Non-US citizen spouse: $185K (2024), $190K (2025) (vs unlimited marital deduction)
view.s2503.amt.sunset: 2026 sunset: lifetime exemption potentially drops ~$7M if TCJA expires
view.s2503.pf.present: PRESENT INTEREST: donee has immediate, unrestricted right to use / enjoy / possess
view.s2503.pf.future: FUTURE INTEREST: donee's enjoyment delayed (e.g., remainder after life estate)
view.s2503.pf.crummey: Crummey trust: future interest converted to present via Crummey withdrawal right
view.s2503.pf.minor_trust: § 2503(c) minor's trust: future converted via specific rules (age 21 vesting)
view.s2503.pf.life_estate: Life estate to grandchild: future interest, no annual exclusion
view.s2503.pf.discretionary_distrib: Discretionary distrib: future interest unless Crummey power exists
view.s2503.pf.actuarial: Actuarial valuation for split-interest gifts (§ 7520 tables)
view.s2503.cr.theory: Theory: brief withdrawal right makes future interest = present interest
view.s2503.cr.notification: Crummey notice: beneficiary must be NOTIFIED of withdrawal right
view.s2503.cr.duration: Duration: 30 days typical; minimum 30 days IRS approved
view.s2503.cr.amount: Withdrawal amount: ≤ lesser of $5K or 5% of trust (5-or-5 rule § 678)
view.s2503.cr.lapse: If lapses ≥ 5-or-5 amount → grantor power retained → § 678
view.s2503.cr.commissioner_v_crummey: Crummey v. Comm'r (9th Cir. 1968): authorized this technique
view.s2503.cr.estate_planning: Estate planning workhorse: ILITs, family trusts
view.s2503.cr.documentation: Document: written notice + retain copies + actually withhold withdrawal if requested
view.s2503.e.educational: Education: tuition payments DIRECTLY to school (any level)
view.s2503.e.medical: Medical: payments DIRECTLY to medical provider (any qualifying medical)
view.s2503.e.direct_only: DIRECT payment required — pay school / hospital, NOT donee
view.s2503.e.tuition_only: Education: tuition only; NOT room + board, books, fees
view.s2503.e.combined_strategy: Combined: $19K annual + unlimited § 2503(e) = optimal grandparent gifting
view.s2503.e.alternative: Alternative: 529 plan for general education (more flexible)
view.s2503.future_note: FUTURE INTEREST: annual exclusion NOT available. Entire gift uses lifetime exemption (or triggers tax if exhausted). Workarounds: (1) Crummey withdrawal right, (2) § 2503(c) minor's trust until age 21, (3) outright gift instead of trust, (4) § 529 plan (statutory present interest exception).
view.s1202.req.ccorp: Domestic C-corporation (NOT S-corp, NOT partnership, NOT LLC default)
view.s1202.req.gross_assets: ≤ $50M aggregate gross assets at issuance + IMMEDIATELY after
view.s1202.req.active_business: ≥ 80% (by value) used in active qualified trade or business
view.s1202.req.original_issuance: Acquired at ORIGINAL issuance (NOT secondary purchase)
view.s1202.req.qualified_business: Qualified business — excludes banking, insurance, services, farming, extraction, hospitality
view.s1202.req.s1202_e_3: § 1202(e)(3): excluded fields = health, law, engineering, architecture, accounting, actuarial science, performing arts, consulting, athletics, financial services, brokerage, "reputation/skill" principal asset
view.s1202.req.real_estate_excl: Real estate held for ≥ 10% of value disqualifies
view.s1202.req.s1202_e_5: § 1202(e)(5): no more than 10% of value in stock or securities of unrelated corporations
view.s1202.cap.greater: Cap = GREATER of $10M (lifetime) OR 10× aggregate basis per ISSUER
view.s1202.cap.per_issuer: Cap applied per ISSUER (not per stock holding) per TAXPAYER
view.s1202.cap.cumulative: Cumulative across multiple sale years (Form 8949 reporting)
view.s1202.cap.basis_advantage: 10× basis advantage: $500K basis → $5M cap from basis alone (so $10M baseline still controls)
view.s1202.cap.stacking: Stacking with multiple beneficiaries: trust + family members each get separate cap
view.s1202.cap.gift_split: Lifetime gifts: recipient inherits holding period + acquisition date + basis
view.s1202.cap.deathbed: Death: stepped-up basis terminates QSBS treatment for heirs (§ 1014)
view.s1202.s1045.purpose: Defer gain on QSBS sale by reinvesting in another QSBS
view.s1202.s1045.holding_carryover: Holding period carries over to replacement
view.s1202.s1045.basis_carryover: Basis = cost of replacement reduced by deferred gain
view.s1202.s1045.preserves_qsbs: Preserves QSBS character + cumulative holding for eventual § 1202 sale
view.s1202.s1045.election: Election made on Schedule D + Form 8949
view.s1202.s1045.individuals: Individuals only — NOT corporations or partnerships at entity level
view.s1202.strat.gift: Lifetime gift to trust / family: each donee gets separate $10M cap
view.s1202.strat.trust: Multiple irrevocable trusts ("QSBS multipliers"): each trust = separate cap
view.s1202.strat.dynasty: Dynasty trust: pass to grandchildren skipping § 1202 cap exhaustion
view.s1202.strat.qbsg: Qualified small business growing: convert to C-corp BEFORE valuation > $50M
view.s1202.strat.timing: 5-year clock starts: ORIGINAL issuance date of QSBS
view.s1202.strat.f_reorg: F reorganization can preserve QSBS — careful planning
view.s1202.strat.s83b: § 83(b) election: tacks holding period for restricted stock
view.s1202.strat.exchanges_pre: § 351 stock-for-stock exchange may continue QSBS clock
view.s1202.strat.s1202h: § 1202(h) gift/inheritance — donee tacks holding period + basis
view.s989.fc.us_default: US person: USD default functional currency
view.s989.fc.cfc: CFC: local currency of country of incorporation typically
view.s989.fc.qbu: QBU: currency of primary economic environment
view.s989.fc.economic: Economic environment: where prices set, financing obtained, day-to-day operations
view.s989.fc.s989_a_2: § 989(a)(2) — DAST (Dollar Approximate Separate Transactions) method for high-inflation
view.s989.fc.election: Election to change functional currency: limited (Reg § 1.985-2/3)
view.s989.fc.consistency: Once established: applies to all subsequent years (consistency required)
view.s989.fc.partnership: Partnership: foreign partnership may have its own functional currency
view.s989.qbu.separate: SEPARATE — distinct economic unit from rest of enterprise
view.s989.qbu.books: SEPARATE BOOKS — clearly identified set of accounting records
view.s989.qbu.trade_business: TRADE OR BUSINESS — § 1.989(a)-1 — substantial business activities
view.s989.qbu.entity_qbu: Entity (foreign sub, CFC) automatically a QBU
view.s989.qbu.dre_qbu: Disregarded entity: separate QBU possible if standalone
view.s989.qbu.multiple_qbu: Multiple QBUs within same entity possible (different geographies / lines of business)
view.s989.s987.recognize_remittance: Recognize net unrec FX gain/loss on remittance to owner
view.s989.s987.deferral_election: Deferral election: defer FX g/l until specific events
view.s989.s987.s987_3: § 987(3) gain/loss on remittance recognized at FMV
view.s989.rel.s884: § 884 — branch profits tax + branch interest tax
view.s989.rel.s951a: § 951A GILTI — currency translations of tested income
view.s6045a.req.method: Cost basis method (FIFO / specific ID / average)
view.s6045a.method.fifo: FIFO (First In First Out) — default for equities
view.s6045a.method.specific: Specific identification — must elect by sale date
view.s6045a.method.average: Average cost — only mutual funds / DRIPs eligible
view.s6045a.method.average_election: Average cost election: binding for that asset class + can change account-by-account
view.s6045a.method.spec_id: Specific ID: must identify before settlement date (Rev. Rul. 73-330)
view.s6045a.method.high_low: "High cost" or "low cost" allowed within FIFO/specific ID framework
view.s6045a.method.tax_loss_harvest: Tax loss harvesting: specific ID strongly favored for control
view.s6045a.tbl.aug1: Furnished after 30 days but by Aug 1
view.s6045a.tbl.aftaug1: Furnished after Aug 1 or not at all
view.s6045a.rel.s1091: § 1091 wash sale — track 30-day window across accounts
view.s6045a.rel.s1259: § 1259 constructive sale of appreciated financial position
view.s6045a.rel.foreign: FATCA Chapter 4 withholding interacts with reporting
view.s6601.calc.daily: DAILY COMPOUND from due date until paid
view.s6601.calc.rate_change: Rate changes quarterly — must use applicable rate per period
view.s6601.calc.s6621_d_netting: § 6621(d) interest netting: simultaneous over + under → use overpayment rate
view.s6601.timing.due_date: § 6601(a) interest from "last date prescribed for payment"
view.s6601.timing.extension: Extension to file ≠ extension to pay (interest from original due)
view.s6601.timing.nol_carryback: § 6601(e)(1) NOL carryback: interest only AFTER loss year filing
view.s6601.timing.ftc: § 6601(e)(3) FTC: interest accrues for portion attributable to FTC limitation
view.s6601.timing.amended: Amended return: interest from original due date
view.s6601.timing.transcript: Account transcript may show daily interest accruals
view.s6601.timing.s6601_b_3: § 6601(b)(3) — payments applied as of received date
view.s6601.abatement.scope: IRS delays of 18+ months in audit + collection (limited)
view.s6601.abatement.factors: Factors: tax type, year, IRS delay attributable
view.s6601.abatement.s6404_e_1: § 6404(e)(1) — discretionary, IRS may abate "any portion" attributable to delay
view.s6601.abatement.unilateral: Court review of IRS denial limited (abuse of discretion standard)
view.s6601.abatement.s6404_h: § 6404(h) — Tax Court jurisdiction over abatement denial
view.s6601.abatement.f843: Form 843 — claim for abatement of interest/penalties
view.s6601.net.purpose: Eliminates rate differential when taxpayer has simultaneous over + under
view.s6601.net.scope: Applies when: same period + both balances exist on books
view.s6601.net.amount: Lesser of (overpayment balance) AND (underpayment balance) → net at OVERPAYMENT rate
view.s6601.net.benefit: Reduces interest exposure by ~1-3pp × balance × years
view.s6601.net.application_request: Must claim netting via formal request — not automatic
view.s6601.net.refund_claim: Often claimed via refund claim + Form 843
view.s269a.test.first: "Substantially all" services performed for ONE other entity (~95%)
view.s269a.test.second: Principal purpose: tax avoidance or evasion
view.s269a.test.s269a_b_psc: § 269A(b) "PSC" definition: corp where employee-owner performs substantially all services
view.s269a.test.taxpayer_response: Taxpayer defenses: business purpose, economic substance, profit motive
view.s269a.test.allocation_methods: IRS allocation methods: arm's length compensation, distribution timing changes
view.s269a.qpsc.post_tcja: Post-TCJA: 21% flat rate same as all C-corps
view.s269a.qpsc.deferral: Calendar year required UNLESS § 444 election made
view.s269a.s444.required_payment: REQUIRED PAYMENT = approx federal tax that would be owed on deferral period
view.s269a.s444.5_5_5: 5% of comp + 5% of distributions + 5% of either — must distribute at least amount
view.s269a.s444.s280h: § 280H: limits PSC deductions if 5-5-5 not met
view.s269a.s444.no_economic_purpose: "No business purpose" required — § 444 simply opts in to fiscal year
view.s269a.s444.terminates: Terminates if 5-5-5 fails OR ceasing to be PSC
view.s269a.ex.loan_out: "Loan-out" corp: actor/athlete with single primary studio/team
view.s269a.ex.doctor: Solo doctor incorporating + working primarily at one hospital
view.s269a.ex.consultant: Solo consultant w/ one primary client
view.s269a.ex.deferral: Tax deferral via PSC fiscal year (now limited by § 280H)
view.s269a.ex.pre_tcja_arb: Pre-TCJA bracket arbitrage (PSC 35% vs individual 39.6%) — repealed
view.s269a.ex.bonus_year_end: Year-end bonus to employee-owner to zero out PSC income
view.s269a.ex.s162_unreasonable_comp: § 162 unreasonable compensation: separate doctrine — PSC normal context
view.s269a.ex.s269a_minimal: § 269A in practice: rarely invoked vs other doctrines (substance over form, § 482)
view.s269a.def.legitimate: Insurance liability, employee benefit, employee retention, succession planning
view.s269a.def.reasonable_comp: Reasonable compensation to employee-owner (no excessive deferral)
view.s269a.def.actual_operations: Actual operations: separate office, equipment, hiring, marketing
view.s269a.def.calendar_default: Use calendar year by default — avoid § 444 election scrutiny
view.s269a.def.economic_substance: Maintain economic substance: bona fide salary, benefits, retirement plan
view.s269a.def.s199a: Consider S-corp + § 199A QBI vs PSC C-corp for tax efficiency
view.s269a.def.s162_m: § 162(m) excess comp limit for public co — separate
view.s1231.prop.depreciable: Depreciable property used in trade or business — held > 1 year
view.s1231.prop.real_estate: Real property used in trade or business — held > 1 year
view.s1231.prop.crops: Unharvested crops on land sold simultaneously
view.s1231.hp.step1: Step 1: Net all § 1231 gains and losses for the year
view.s1231.hp.step2: Step 2: If net is GAIN → LTCG (subject to look-back recapture)
view.s1231.hp.step3: Step 3: If net is LOSS → ordinary loss (Schedule D, line 1)
view.s1231.hp.firepot_first: "Firepot" sub-netting: net casualty losses + involuntary conversions
view.s1231.hp.firepot_loss: If firepot is NET LOSS → goes to ordinary (Schedule A or Schedule C)
view.s1231.hp.firepot_gain: If firepot is NET GAIN → enters main hotchpot for LTCG/loss netting
view.s1231.hp.s1245_first: § 1245 / § 1250 recapture applied FIRST — only excess enters hotchpot
view.s1231.hp.character: Character determined ENTITY-LEVEL at partnership / S-corp
view.s1231.lb.purpose: Anti-abuse: prevents alternating loss + gain years to game character
view.s1231.lb.5yr: Looks back 5 most recent tax years
view.s1231.lb.cumulative: Cumulative net § 1231 losses in those 5 years
view.s1231.lb.recapture: Current year § 1231 GAIN recaptured as ORDINARY up to cumulative losses
view.s1231.lb.example: Example: prior 5 yrs $50K cumulative loss + current $80K gain → $50K ordinary + $30K LTCG
view.s1231.lb.no_carryover: Recaptured losses do NOT carryforward — used up in current year
view.s1231.lb.individual_partnership: Tested at INDIVIDUAL partner level (not partnership)
view.s1231.tbl.unrec_s1250: Unrecaptured § 1250 gain split out for 25% rate
view.s1231.spec.casualty: Casualty / theft losses on personal-use property: separate treatment
view.s1231.spec.partnership: Partnership: § 1231 character flows through to partners
view.s1231.spec.s1239: § 1239 transfer to controlled entity: ordinary recapture
view.s1231.spec.s1245_complete: § 1245 recapture is COMPLETE — no § 1231 if full recapture
view.s1250.unr.amount: Amount = post-1986 straight-line depreciation that ALREADY claimed
view.s1250.unr.rate: Taxed at MAXIMUM 25% — vs 15%/20% LTCG, vs 37% ordinary
view.s1250.unr.individuals: Individuals only — corporations taxed at flat 21% anyway
view.s1250.unr.netting: Netted against capital losses BEFORE applying rate (Schedule D Worksheet)
view.s1250.unr.calculation: = LESSER of (depreciation taken) OR (gain on sale)
view.s1250.unr.niit: § 1411 NIIT 3.8% adds on top → effective 28.8% max
view.s1250.unr.s_residential: Residential rental 27.5 yr: $100K depreciation after 27.5 yrs full
view.s1250.unr.recapture_total: Total tax burden on long-held real estate disposition can be significant
view.s1250.s291.amount: 20% × amount that WOULD be § 1245 recapture if § 1245 applied
view.s1250.s291.corp_only: C-corporations only — not individuals or S-corps
view.s1250.s291.ordinary: Treated as additional ordinary income (not capital gain)
view.s1250.s291.effective_rate: Effective: 21% × 20% = 4.2% additional federal tax on corporate sales
view.s1250.s291.book_tax: Book vs tax difference — § 291 creates temporary BTD
view.s1250.strat.s1031: § 1031 like-kind exchange defers gain + unrecaptured § 1250 gain
view.s1250.strat.s453: § 453 installment sale spreads recognition across receipts
view.s1250.strat.cost_seg: Cost segregation accelerates dep (15-yr) but creates § 1245 recapture exposure
view.s1250.strat.s1033: § 1033 involuntary conversion defers gain + recapture potential preserved
view.s1250.strat.opportunity_zone: QOZ deferral § 1400Z-2 — basis step-up after 10 years
view.s1250.strat.charitable_remainder: CRT split-interest: defer gain over income payment period
view.s1250.strat.death: § 1014 stepped-up basis at death extinguishes unrealized recapture
view.s1250.strat.gifts: § 1015 carryover basis on gift preserves recapture potential
view.s1250.strat.partnership: Hold through partnership: § 743(b) step-up may reset for new partners
view.s6404.e.scope: Abate INTEREST attributable to IRS error/delay (managerial / ministerial act)
view.s6404.e.discretionary: DISCRETIONARY — IRS may abate "any portion attributable to" delay
view.s6404.e.18_months: Significant delay: typically 18+ months as benchmark
view.s6404.e.managerial: Managerial act: lost file, supervisor approval, internal review delays
view.s6404.e.ministerial: Ministerial act: typing, computation, routine processing
view.s6404.e.factors: Factors: cause of delay, IRS responsibility, taxpayer cooperation
view.s6404.e.no_substantive: NOT applicable: substantive legal decisions, audit position changes
view.s6404.e.tax_court: § 6404(h) Tax Court review post-1996 (abuse of discretion)
view.s6404.e.f843: Form 843 — attach detailed explanation of IRS delay
view.s6404.f.scope: Abate interest + penalty arising from reliance on written IRS advice
view.s6404.f.written: Must be WRITTEN advice (oral advice insufficient)
view.s6404.f.complete: Taxpayer provided COMPLETE + ACCURATE information when seeking advice
view.s6404.f.f843_with_supporting: Form 843 + copies of original request + advice received
view.s6404.f.scope_advice: Limited to advice from IRS DIRECTLY (not website / publications)
view.s6404.g.purpose: Suspends interest + penalty if IRS doesn't notify within 36 MONTHS
view.s6404.g.scope: Applies to: § 6651 FTP + interest on amount in notice
view.s6404.g.commencement: Suspension period: from day after due date until 18 days after notice
view.s6404.g.s6404_g_2_a: § 6404(g)(2)(A) — extensions of time (no suspension during extension period)
view.s6404.g.timely: Critical for taxpayers receiving long-delayed adjustment notices
view.s6404.g.30_day_letter: 30-day letter or revenue agent report counts
view.s6404.fta.criteria: Eligibility: NO penalties in PRIOR 3 years + currently in compliance + filed all required returns
view.s6404.fta.no_reasonable: No reasonable cause required — automatic 1-time waiver
view.s6404.fta.request: Request via Form 843, phone, or written letter
view.s6404.fta.first_time_only: First-time only — cannot use again for same penalty type
view.s6404.rc.boyle: United States v. Boyle (1985): cannot rely on agent for filing
view.s6404.rc.factors: Factors: serious illness, family death, natural disaster, fire, etc.
view.s6404.rc.reliance_on_advisor: Reliance on tax advisor for SUBSTANTIVE issue (not filing)
view.s6404.rc.complete_disclosure: Complete disclosure of facts to advisor
view.s6404.proc.f843: Form 843 — claim for refund or request for abatement
view.s6404.proc.documentation: Attach: detailed explanation, supporting documents, copies of relevant correspondence
view.s6404.proc.tc_review: § 6404(h) Tax Court review of § 6404(e) denial — 180-day deadline
view.s6404.proc.sol_refund: § 6511 SOL for refund claims: 3 yrs from return filing OR 2 yrs from payment
view.s6404.proc.cdp_coordination: § 6320 / § 6330 CDP hearing can include abatement requests
view.s6404.proc.s7430: § 7430 attorney fees if prevailing on abuse of discretion claim
view.s6404.proc.tao: Taxpayer Advocate Service (TAS) when systemic burden or unreasonable delay
view.s134.cp.enlisted: ENLISTED + WARRANT officers: FULL combat pay excluded
view.s134.cp.officer_cap: Officers: capped at highest enlisted basic pay + Hostile Fire / Imminent Danger pay (HFP/IDP)
