view.s134.cp.hospitalized: Hospitalized due to wounds/injury sustained in combat zone: continues exclusion
view.s134.cp.fica: Combat pay EXEMPT from FICA (SS + Medicare)
view.s134.cp.w2_box_12: Reported W-2 Box 12 Code Q (separately from Box 1)
view.s134.cp.s32_eitc: § 32(c)(2)(B)(vi) — taxpayer may ELECT to include combat pay for EITC computation
view.s134.cp.s24_ctc: § 24(d)(1)(B)(ii) — combat pay included for refundable CTC computation
view.s134.cp.s219_g: § 219(g)(3)(B) — combat pay treated as earned income for IRA
view.s134.cz.iraq: Iraq + adjacent waters (since 2003 — Operation Iraqi Freedom)
view.s134.cz.balkan: Federal Republic of Yugoslavia + Adriatic Sea (designated)
view.s134.cz.qhda: "Qualified Hazardous Duty Areas": Bosnia + Herzegovina + Croatia (similar treatment per § 112(c)(3))
view.s134.cz.update: List updated via EO + Public Law
view.s134.cz.adjacent: Adjacent waters / airspace + countries where direct combat support operations
view.s134.s121.standard: § 121 standard: lived in home as principal residence 2 of last 5 years
view.s134.s121.qualified_service: "Qualified official extended duty" - active duty 50+ miles from principal residence OR in govt quarters
view.s134.s121.electing: Taxpayer ELECTS suspension on Form 5405 + Schedule D
view.s134.s121.intelligence: Intelligence community personnel: similar rule under § 121(d)(9)
view.s134.s121.peace_corps: Peace Corps + foreign service: similar election
view.s134.s121.s121_b_4_one_year: § 121(b)(4) — once-per-year rule waived for armed forces during qualified service
view.s134.s7508.scope: Active duty + service in combat zone OR contingency operation
view.s134.s7508.deadline_extension: 180 DAYS after last day in combat zone
view.s134.s7508.hospitalization: + days hospitalized due to combat zone injury
view.s134.s7508.applies_to: Applies to: filing, payment, collection, refund SOL
view.s134.s7508.s7508a_overlap: § 7508A disaster zone — separate but may overlap
view.s134.s7508.scra_state: SCRA state tax + interest rate cap protections separately
view.s134.s7508.notice_2024: IRS Notice 2024-xx updates list of combat zones each year
view.s165.calc.lesser: Loss = LESSER of (FMV before − FMV after) OR (adjusted basis)
view.s165.calc.netting: Net all losses + gains within same disaster
view.s165.calc.special_business: Business casualty: NO $100 floor + NO 10% AGI
view.s165.calc.qualified_disaster_relief: Qualified disaster relief: additional rules per § 139
view.s165.ponzi.deduction: 75% of qualified investment in discovery year (95% if NO third-party recovery action)
view.s165.ponzi.theft: Treated as § 165(c)(2) theft loss (NOT capital loss)
view.s165.ponzi.year_of_discovery: Year of discovery — not year of investment
view.s165.ponzi.investment_basis: Qualified investment = cash invested − cash + property received − amounts claimed
view.s165.ponzi.claim_adj: Subsequent recoveries: include as ordinary income (claim of right § 1341)
view.s165.ponzi.docs: Documentation: criminal indictment + ponzi scheme classification by IRS
view.s165.ponzi.election: Election made on Form 4684 + Section C (theft loss)
view.s165.ponzi.no_amortization: Above-line deduction — NOT subject to 10% AGI floor + § 67 misc 2% limit
view.s165.worth.year: Capital loss as of LAST DAY of taxable year (regardless of when worthless)
view.s165.worth.character: Capital character: LTCG if held > 1 year, STCG if held ≤ 1 year
view.s165.worth.identifiable: Identifiable event required (bankruptcy filing, dissolution, etc.)
view.s165.worth.s165g3: § 165(g)(3) ordinary loss treatment for affiliated subsidiaries
view.s165.worth.sale: If sold for nominal amount → not worthless, capital loss instead
view.s165.worth.contrast_bad: Contrast § 166 bad debt: § 165(g) for securities + § 166 for non-security debts
view.s165.wag.limited: Limited to wagering GAINS (not other income)
view.s165.wag.pro: Professional gambler: above-line Schedule C deduction (Comm. v. Groetzinger, 480 US 23 (1987))
view.s165.wag.expenses: Pro: § 162 ordinary + necessary expenses (travel, lodging, supplies)
view.s165.wag.documentation: Diary requirement: dates, amounts, types, places, witnesses (Rev. Proc. 77-29)
view.s165.wag.illegal: Illegal gambling still deductible (Comm. v. Tellier, 383 US 687 (1966))
view.s362.e2.purpose: Anti-abuse: prevents shifting same loss to both transferor stock + corp asset basis
view.s362.e2.applies: Applies when AGGREGATE adjusted basis of contributed property > AGGREGATE FMV
view.s362.e2.default: DEFAULT (no election): reduce CORP's basis to AGGREGATE FMV — pro-rata across NBIL properties only
view.s362.e2.election: § 362(e)(2)(C) ELECTION: reduce TRANSFEROR's OUTSIDE stock basis instead → preserves corp basis at carryover
view.s362.e2.timing: Election made by ATTACHING STATEMENT to timely filed return for year of transfer
view.s362.e2.benefit: Election benefit: preserves loss inside corp where it may be realized later
view.s362.e2.s382: Election may HURT later if ownership change triggers § 382 limitation
view.s362.e2.binding: Election BINDING — cannot be revoked except with IRS consent
view.s362.hp.s1223_2: § 1223(2) — corp's holding period TACKS to transferor's
view.s362.hp.capital_only: Tacking applies to CAPITAL assets + § 1231 property (not inventory/ord-income)
view.s362.hp.s1223_1: § 1223(1) — transferor's stock basis tacks to property
view.s362.hp.inventory: Inventory: NO tacking (becomes ordinary income to corp)
view.s362.hp.s355: § 355 spin-off: tacking applies for distributing + controlled corps
view.s362.hp.boot_property: Boot property: FMV basis + start of new holding period
view.s362.s338.purpose: Stock acquisition treated as DEEMED ASSET ACQUISITION
view.s362.s338.g: § 338(g): buyer-only election — buyer pays double layer of tax (buyer + seller)
view.s362.s338.h10: § 338(h)(10): joint election — seller treats as asset sale (S-corp + consolidated)
view.s362.s338.depreciation: Stepped-up basis: enhanced depreciation + amortization for buyer
view.s362.s338.foreign: Foreign targets: § 338(g) election to step up basis (no seller side)
view.s362.tbl.f: Identity / form / place change (1 corp only)
view.s362.rel.s334: § 334 — basis of sub's property in liquidation
view.s362.rel.s338: § 338 — election to treat stock as asset purchase
view.s362.rel.s1032: § 1032 — corp not taxable on its own stock issuance
view.s1296.pfic.income: Income test: 75%+ of gross income is PASSIVE
view.s1296.pfic.asset: Asset test: 50%+ of assets (FMV or basis) produce passive income
view.s1296.pfic.passive_examples: Passive: interest, dividends, royalties, rents, annuities
view.s1296.pfic.s1297_b_2: § 1297(b)(2) — exclude active rents + dividends from operating subsidiary
view.s1296.tbl.s1291_treat: Throwback over holding period + interest charge + highest rate
view.s1296.tbl.s1296_treat: Annual FMV mark; gain ordinary + loss limited to unreversed
view.s1296.mtm.election: Election made by attaching statement to timely-filed return for first year
view.s1296.mtm.binding: BINDING for that year + all subsequent years (until stock sold or election revoked with IRS consent)
view.s1296.mtm.fmv: Year-end FMV - adjusted basis = MTM gain or loss
view.s1296.mtm.gain_ordinary: GAIN: ORDINARY income (no LTCG even if held > 1 year)
view.s1296.mtm.loss_limited: LOSS: ORDINARY deduction limited to "unreversed inclusions" (cumulative prior gains minus prior losses)
view.s1296.mtm.basis_adjusted: Basis ADJUSTED by gain/loss recognized — no double recognition
view.s1296.mtm.gain_first_year: First-year election: catch-up — recognize built-in gain through purging
view.s1296.mtm.coordination_s1291: Coordinates with § 1291: prior years treated as § 1291 if not pedigreed QEF/MTM
view.s1296.mtm.form_8621: Form 8621 reports election + annual MTM gain/loss
view.s1296.mkt.us: US national securities exchanges (NYSE, NASDAQ, NYSE American)
view.s1296.mkt.foreign: Foreign qualifying exchanges (LSE, Tokyo, Euronext, Toronto, ASX, Hong Kong, etc.)
view.s1296.mkt.regularly_traded: "Regularly traded" = 15+ days each quarter OR 1/6 of trading days
view.s1296.mkt.de_minimis: De minimis trading not qualifying — facts & circumstances
view.s1296.mkt.adr_ok: ADR (American Depositary Receipt): often qualifying via US exchange
view.s1296.choice.s1295_qef: § 1295 QEF: best if PFIC produces capital gain — preserves LTCG rate
view.s1296.choice.s1295_problem: § 1295 requires PFIC to provide annual statement — many will NOT
view.s1296.choice.s1296_simple: § 1296 MTM: simpler — no PFIC cooperation needed
view.s1296.choice.s1296_loss: § 1296 disadvantage: loss limited to unreversed inclusions
view.s1296.choice.s1296_ordinary: § 1296 disadvantage: gain ordinary even on held > 1 yr
view.s1296.choice.s1296_only_marketable: § 1296 limited to marketable stock — many small/private PFICs don't qualify
view.s1296.choice.purging: Purging distribution: can convert § 1291 PFIC to § 1295/§ 1296 (catch-up tax)
view.s1296.choice.holding_period: Length of holding: § 1291 interest charge accrues over holding period — bad for long holds
view.s1296.f.required: Required ANNUALLY by US PFIC shareholder
view.s1296.f.thresholds: 2014+: Form 8621 required regardless of value (post-HIRE Act + Reg § 1.1298-1T)
view.s1296.f.s6664_d_4: § 6664(d)(4) — civil fraud SOL never starts on PFIC items
view.s1296.f.s6038d_combine: Form 8938 + Form 8621 cross-references — different reporting regime
view.s1234.h.character: Option in/on capital asset → option gain/loss is CAPITAL
view.s1234.h.sale: Sale of option: ordinary gain/loss UNLESS § 1234(a) applies (capital)
view.s1234.h.lapse_loss: Lapse without exercise: capital LOSS treated as if sale for $0 (Reg § 1.1234-1(b))
view.s1234.h.exercise_basis: Exercise: premium added to basis of underlying acquired
view.s1234.h.exercise_no_gain: No gain/loss at exercise — built into underlying's basis
view.s1234.h.put_exercise: Put exercise (sale of underlying): premium REDUCES sale proceeds
view.s1234.h.holding_starts_separately: Holding period for underlying STARTS at exercise — does NOT tack to option period
view.s1234.h.s1234_a_3: § 1234(a)(3) special holding period rule for cash-settled options on capital assets
view.s1234.w.sale: Sale or exchange by writer: SHORT-TERM CAPITAL gain/loss
view.s1234.w.lapse: Lapse with premium retained: ORDINARY income (premium received, no offsetting outflow)
view.s1234.w.s1234_b_repeal: § 1234(b) special lapse rule applies BEFORE § 1234A general termination rule
view.s1234.w.assignment_call: Assignment of call: writer's basis in underlying + premium = adjusted sale price
view.s1234.w.assignment_put: Assignment of put: writer's basis in underlying = strike - premium received
view.s1234.w.close_out: Close-out: STCG/STCL (writer's position generally short-term character)
view.s1234.s1256.types: Non-equity options, broad-based stock indices, regulated futures, foreign currency contracts
view.s1234.s1256.split: 60% LONG-term + 40% SHORT-term regardless of actual holding
view.s1234.s1256.mtm: Mark-to-market at year-end: deemed sold at FMV Dec 31
view.s1234.s1256.spx: SPX, SPY ARE different: SPY (single-stock ETF) NOT § 1256; SPX (index option) IS § 1256
view.s1234.s1256.qualified_board: Must trade on qualified board / exchange (CFTC-regulated typically)
view.s1234.s1256.s1256_d: § 1256(d) hedging exception: bona fide hedge → ordinary character
view.s1234.str.defined: "Straddle" = offsetting positions in personal property (e.g., long stock + put)
view.s1234.str.s1092_a: § 1092(a) — loss deferral: loss recognized only to extent NOT offset by unrecognized gain
view.s1234.str.s1092_b: § 1092(b) — capitalization: interest/carrying charges capitalized to basis
view.s1234.str.s1092_c_qcc: § 1092(c)(4)(A) — Qualified Covered Call exception (limited stocks + LT positions)
view.s1234.str.s263_g: § 263(g) — capitalize carrying charges on straddle positions
view.s1234.str.identified: Identified straddle: special set of rules — basis adjustments
view.s1234.str.s1234_b_holding_term: Straddle terminates LT holding period of long leg (rolls back to date of offsetting position)
view.s1234.spec.lap: LEAP (Long-term Equity AnticiPation Securities) — 1+ year expiry, same § 1234 treatment
view.s1234.spec.warrant: Warrants: § 1234 applies; cost basis adjusts by premium
view.s1234.spec.bond_call: Bond call/put features: § 1271(a) interest treatment, not § 1234
view.s1234.spec.s1233_short: § 1233 short sales: holding period rules differ for matched positions
view.s1234.spec.s1259: § 1259 constructive sale — short-against-box + similar offsetting positions
view.s164.salt.cap_2018: 2018-2025: $10,000 cap on combined state + local income + sales + property
view.s164.salt.business: Business property tax excluded — Schedule C / E / F fully deductible
view.s164.salt.rental: Rental property tax: Schedule E fully deductible (NOT subject to cap)
view.s164.salt.foreign: Foreign income tax excluded — separate § 27 FTC choice

view.s164.salt.notice_2020_75: Notice 2020-75 blessed PTET — IRS confirms entity-level state tax fully deductible
view.s164.salt.sunset_2025: Cap sunsets Dec 31, 2025 — back to no-cap pre-TCJA absent OBBBA extension
view.s164.ptet.election: Pass-through entity (S-corp / partnership) elects to pay state tax at entity level
view.s164.ptet.entity_dedn: Entity deducts state tax fully — reduces federal taxable income
view.s164.ptet.partner_credit: Partner gets state tax credit on personal return (for federal SALT cap purpose, NOT a deduction)

view.s164.ptet.states_36: 36 states + DC offer PTET as of 2024

view.s164.foreign.ftc: § 27 FTC: dollar-for-dollar reduction in federal tax (100% benefit)
view.s164.foreign.almost_always_ftc: FTC almost always preferred — except when limited by FTC limitations (§ 904 baskets)
view.s164.foreign.baskets: § 904 baskets: GILTI, passive, general, foreign branch — limits FTC
view.s164.foreign.high_tax_jurisdiction: Highly-taxed CFC: § 954(b)(4) high-tax exclusion may avoid GILTI
view.s164.foreign.foreign_tax: "Foreign income tax" defined broadly — includes income, war profits, excess profits
view.s164.foreign.s903_in_lieu: § 903 in-lieu-of tax (gross receipts substitute) — also creditable
view.s303.req.estate_inclusion: Stock includable in decedent's gross estate
view.s303.req.35pct: Stock value > 35% of ADJUSTED gross estate (§ 303(b)(2)(A))
view.s303.req.qualifying: Distribution must be for tax payment / funeral / administration expenses
view.s303.req.timing: Within 3 yrs 90 days OR § 6166 election period
view.s303.req.shareholder: Shareholder must bear estate tax burden (or executor for estate)
view.s303.req.s303_b_3: § 303(b)(3) — multiple corps treated as 1 if each > 20%
view.s303.req.character: Treated as SALE / EXCHANGE — capital gain (typically minimal with § 1014 step-up)
view.s303.comp.amount: Amount = SUM of: (a) federal + state DEATH taxes, (b) funeral expenses, (c) admin expenses
view.s303.comp.estate_tax_deduction: Federal estate tax already reduces taxable estate (§ 2053)
view.s303.comp.deductions_allowed: Admin expenses deductible from estate (§ 2053) — coordination required
view.s303.comp.excess: Excess of redemption over qualifying amount → § 301 dividend (potentially)
view.s303.comp.multiple_redemptions: Multiple redemptions: cumulative limit on qualifying amount
view.s303.comp.partial: Partial redemption: applied to qualifying expenses first, excess separately tested
view.s303.comp.coordination_s6166: Coordination with § 6166: installment payments included as qualifying
view.s303.t.standard: Standard: within 3 YEARS + 90 DAYS after due date of estate tax return
view.s303.t.s6166_installment: § 6166 15-yr installment: redemption may occur during installment period
view.s303.t.4yr: § 303(b)(1)(B): 4 years if Tax Court petition filed
view.s303.t.estate_tax_paid: Period runs from estate tax actually paid (when § 6166 elected)
view.s303.agg.purpose: Aggregate multiple closely-held corporations as single for 35% test
view.s303.agg.each_20: Each corp must be > 20% of decedent's interest
view.s303.agg.s303_b_4: § 303(b)(4) — surviving spouse joint tenancy treated as decedent's
view.s303.agg.s303_b_5: § 303(b)(5) — community property treated as decedent's per state law
view.s303.agg.holding_company: Holding company structure: NOT a corporation for § 303 (look-through)
view.s303.agg.s6166_aggregation: § 6166(c) parallel aggregation rule (45% test for installment)
view.s303.s6166.purpose: § 6166 — 15-year installment for estate tax on closely-held interest
view.s303.s6166.4yr_grace: 4-year grace period before principal payments begin
view.s303.s6166.2pct_special: § 6601(j) 2% special interest rate on first ~$1.6M of deferred tax
view.s303.s6166.requirement: Closely-held business value > 35% adjusted gross estate
view.s303.s6166.acceleration: Acceleration triggers: 50%+ business sold, liquidation, defaulted payment
view.s303.s6166.election: Election on Form 706 by extended due date
view.s303.s6166.s303_combined: § 303 + § 6166 = preserve closely-held business without forced sale
view.s303.s6166.bond: IRS may require lien or bond (§ 6324A) for installments
view.s303.s6166.s2032a: § 2032A special-use valuation may also apply (additional planning)
view.s170.tbl.ack_content: Must state amount + whether donor received goods/services + value
view.s170.daf.deduction_timing: Deduction in year of DAF contribution (NOT distribution)
view.s170.daf.no_partial: Donor retains advisory rights, NOT control — § 170(f)(18)
view.s170.daf.qas_eligible: Qualified appreciated stock to DAF: FMV deduction (treated as public)
view.s170.daf.s4958: § 4958 excise tax on disqualified person benefits
view.s170.daf.bunching: "Bunching" strategy: front-load multiple years into one
view.s170.daf.standard_alternative: Useful when SALT cap + standard deduction limit benefit of small donations
view.s170.qcd.no_daf: Cannot go to DAF or supporting organization
view.s170.qcd.no_deduction: NOT deductible — excludes from AGI instead
view.s170.qcd.medicare: AGI reduction benefits: Medicare IRMAA + SS taxation + § 1411 NIIT
view.s170.qcd.s_qcd_split: SECURE 2.0: $50K one-time QCD to CRT or CGA (counted within limit)
view.s170.qcd.gold_age: Strategy: gold for high-income retirees with no itemized benefit
view.s170.split.crat: CRAT: charitable remainder annuity trust — fixed annuity to non-charity
view.s170.split.crut: CRUT: charitable remainder unitrust — % of FMV revalued annually
view.s170.split.clat: CLAT: charitable lead annuity — annuity to charity first, remainder to family
view.s170.split.pooled: Pooled income fund (PIF) — donor's gift pooled with others

view.s280c.a.s51: § 51 Work Opportunity Tax Credit (WOTC) — targeted groups
view.s280c.a.consequence: § 162 deduction REDUCED by credit amount — no election available
view.s280c.a.character: Wages paid still deductible — only matching credit reduces deduction
view.s280c.c.default: Default: reduce § 174 R&D expense by full § 41 credit
view.s280c.c.election: § 280C(c)(2) ELECTION: reduce credit by (federal rate × credit) — currently 21% × credit
view.s280c.c.formula_corp: Reduced credit = credit × (1 - federal corporate tax rate)

view.s280c.c.pre_tcja: Pre-TCJA 35% corporate rate: reduced credit = 65% × normal credit
view.s280c.c.election_timing: Election made on Form 6765 + timely filed (with extensions)
view.s280c.c.binding: Election BINDING for that year (cannot be revoked without consent)
view.s280c.c.passthrough: Pass-through entities: election at entity level binds all members

view.s280c.post.s174_basis_recovery: Basis recovery over 5/15 yrs reduces benefit of immediate § 280C(c) reduction
view.s6672.resp.officer: Corporate officer or director with PRACTICAL AUTHORITY (titles alone not dispositive)
view.s6672.resp.signature: Signature authority on corporate bank accounts
view.s6672.resp.check_signing: Check-signing authority (actual exercise, not just title)
view.s6672.resp.payroll_authority: Authority to direct payroll + creditor payments
view.s6672.resp.tax_filing: Authority over filing tax returns + paying taxes
view.s6672.resp.bookkeeper: Bookkeeper / accountant: typically NOT responsible (no authority to disregard)
view.s6672.resp.outside_director: Outside director without practical authority: typically NOT responsible
view.s6672.resp.multiple_persons: Multiple responsible persons common in same corporation
view.s6672.will.chose: CHOSE to pay other creditors instead
view.s6672.will.no_evil_motive: No "evil motive" required — paying creditors over taxes IS willful
view.s6672.will.reckless: Reckless disregard counts as willful (Slodov v. United States)

view.s6672.will.encumbered: "Encumbered" funds (i.e., security interest holder claim): NOT available — exception
view.s6672.will.partial: Partial payment of trust fund taxes: STILL willful for remainder
view.s6672.proc.investigation: IRS investigation: Form 4180 interview + financial records
view.s6672.proc.form_2751: Form 2751 — Proposed Assessment of Trust Fund Recovery Penalty
view.s6672.proc.s6321_lien: § 6321 lien — automatic on failure to pay
view.s6672.proc.s6320_cdp: § 6320 CDP hearing — lien notice within 5 business days
view.s6672.proc.refund: Refund suit: Flora rule — pay 1 quarter + 1 employee's TFRP, sue for refund
view.s6672.def.not_responsible: "Not responsible person" — limited authority + no payment decision power
view.s6672.def.not_willful: "Not willful" — didn't know taxes due + reasonable belief paid + no unencumbered funds
view.s6672.def.encumbered: "Funds were encumbered" — bank loan covenants required other creditor payment
view.s6672.def.unauthorized: Unauthorized actions by junior employees — not personally responsible
view.s6672.def.early_knowledge: Recently joined + had no opportunity to act before taxes due
view.s6672.def.actual_payments: Made actual payments on tax liability when funds available
view.s6672.def.bona_fide_dispute: Bona fide dispute over employee classification (1099 vs W-2)
view.s6672.def.partial_designation: Designate partial payments to trust fund vs employer portions
view.s6672.def.consider_chapter_11: Chapter 11 bankruptcy can suspend personal collection action
view.s6672.def.s6672_contribution: § 6672(c) seek contribution from other responsible persons
view.s1245.cat.a3b: § 1245(a)(3)(B) — other tangible property used in trade/business (storage facilities)
view.s1245.cat.s197: § 197 amortizable intangibles (goodwill, going concern, customer lists, etc.)
view.s1245.cat.livestock: Livestock held for breeding / dairy / draft (§ 1245(a)(3)(A) per Treas. Reg.)
view.s1245.calc.realized: Realized gain = sale proceeds − adjusted basis
view.s1245.calc.depreciation: Depreciation recapture potential = ALL depreciation (incl § 179 + bonus + § 168 MACRS)
view.s1245.calc.recapture: Recapture amount = LESSER of (gain) OR (recapture potential)
view.s1245.calc.ordinary: Recapture amount taxed as ORDINARY INCOME
view.s1245.calc.excess: Excess gain = § 1231 gain (LTCG if net § 1231 gain)
view.s1245.calc.full_recap: Full recapture if cost > adjusted basis (i.e., depreciation taken)
view.s1245.calc.s291: § 291 corporate add'l recapture: 20% × (§ 1250 amount that would be § 1245 recapture)
view.s1245.spec.gift: Gift: § 1245(b)(1) — recapture potential carries over to donee
view.s1245.spec.death: Death: § 1245(b)(2) — basis stepped up; recapture potential extinguished
view.s1245.spec.casualty_destruction: Casualty: § 1245(b)(4) — gain to extent insurance > basis triggers recapture
view.s1245.spec.s1033: § 1033 replacement: defer gain; recapture potential preserved in replacement property
view.s1245.spec.like_kind: § 1031 like-kind: TCJA limits to real property; § 1245 personal property NO LONGER ELIGIBLE
view.s1245.spec.installment: § 453 installment sale: recapture in YEAR 1 — pulls forward all ordinary income
view.s1245.spec.cost_segregation: Cost segregation study: identifies § 1245 vs § 1250 split — accelerates depreciation but creates recapture exposure
view.s1245.spec.s1250_real_property: Cost seg-identified § 1245 components from real estate trigger recapture on sale
view.s1245.int.s197_15yr: § 197 amortization 15 years straight-line on goodwill, going concern, etc.
view.s1245.int.gain_ordinary: Sale of goodwill: full § 1245 recapture as ORDINARY income
view.s1245.int.no_capital: No capital gain treatment for amortized portion
view.s1245.int.partial_sale: Partial sale of asset group: recapture allocated by FMV (Reg § 1.1245-1)
view.s1245.int.s1060: § 1060 residual method allocates basis among classes (Class VI = goodwill)
view.s1245.int.contrast_pre197: Pre-§ 197 (pre-Aug 10, 1993): goodwill not amortizable, capital gain on sale
view.s6662.def.reliance: Reliance on professional advice: Boyle factors + specific issue analyzed
view.s6662.def.competent: Advisor must be competent + provided ALL relevant facts
view.s6662.def.subst_auth: Substantial authority: more likely than not (40%+ chance of prevailing)
view.s6662.def.reasonable_basis: Reasonable basis: less than substantial authority but reasonable (20-30% chance)
view.s6662.def.limit_tax_shelter: LIMITED for tax shelters (§ 6662(d)(2)(C)): must satisfy MLTN standard
view.s6662.def.s6664_d: § 6664(d) — defense LIMITED for listed transactions / reportable transactions
view.s6662.def.s6664_d_must: Must show: (a) full disclosure + (b) substantial authority + (c) reasonable belief MLTN
view.s6662.def.s6662_h_no_defense: § 6662(h) NO § 6664 defense for gross valuation misstatement (post-PPA)
view.s6662.def.qualified_amended: Qualified amended return (filed before audit notice) avoids penalty
view.s6662.su.individual: Individual: GREATER of $5,000 OR 10% of correct tax
view.s6662.su.corp_under_eligible: C-corp (NOT S-corp): LESSER of $10M OR 10% of correct tax
view.s6662.su.s_corp: S-corp / partnership: tested at INDIVIDUAL level on K-1 items
view.s6662.su.amounts: Computed on UNDERSTATEMENT before adjustment for substantial authority + disclosure
view.s6662.su.s6662_d_2_a: § 6662(d)(2)(A) — items WITH substantial authority excluded from understatement calculation
view.s6662.su.s6662_d_2_b: § 6662(d)(2)(B) — items WITH disclosure + reasonable basis excluded
view.s6662.su.s6662_d_2_c: § 6662(d)(2)(C) — tax shelter items require MLTN substantial authority
view.s6662.su.subst_auth_examples: Substantial authority sources: Code, regs, court cases, IRS publications, revenue rulings
view.s6662.val.s_substantial: SUBSTANTIAL: 150% or more of correct value (post-PPA 2007 — was 200%)
view.s6662.val.charitable: Charitable contribution: appraisal required for $5K+ — failure invalidates substantial authority
view.s6662.val.estate_gift: Estate/gift: 65% or less of correct value (substantial) / 40% (gross)
view.s6662.val.basis_step_up: Built-in gain valuations on basis step-ups
view.s6662.val.fair_market: FMV determined as of relevant date
view.s6662.val.qualified_appraisal: Qualified appraisal required for charitable contributions (Reg § 1.170A-13)
view.s6662.s6751.required: Written supervisor approval required BEFORE penalty assessed
view.s6662.s6751.timing: Approval must be made before initial determination communicated
view.s6662.s6751.graev: Graev v. Comm. + Chai v. Comm. + Laidlaw — judicial scrutiny
view.s6662.s6751.kestin: Kestin v. Comm. — initial determination = formal communication to taxpayer
view.s6662.s6751.belair_w: Belair Woods v. Comm. — Form 30-day letter triggers § 6751(b)
view.s6662.s6751.litigation: Litigation tactic: penalties often vacated where § 6751(b) not satisfied
view.s481.spread.favorable: FAVORABLE (decrease income): 1-year (full in year of change)
view.s481.spread.small_dollar: $50K or less unfavorable: ANNUAL election for 1-year (taxpayer's choice)
view.s481.spread.cessation: If business ceases / no longer using method: remaining § 481(a) accelerated
view.s481.spread.s481_b: § 481(b) relief: 3-yr average tax computation limits tax increase
view.s481.spread.s481_b_election: Election: tax = 3-yr avg × 3 OR 1-yr × current rate — taxpayer chooses lower
view.s481.spread.audit_initiated: IRS-initiated change: typically 1-year for unfavorable (no taxpayer relief)
view.s481.spread.s481_a_post_tcja: Post-TCJA: § 451(b) AFS inclusion conformity — major mass change
view.s481.ex.cash_accrual: Cash → Accrual (small business hit $30M GR threshold)
view.s481.ex.depreciation: Depreciation method (incl bonus depreciation election out)
view.s481.ex.inventory: Inventory: FIFO vs LIFO vs average vs lower of cost or market
view.s481.ex.completed_contract: Completed contract → percentage of completion (long-term contracts)
view.s481.proc.identify: Identify if method change needed (Rev. Proc. 2022-14 etc. lists automatic changes)
view.s481.proc.automatic: Automatic: Form 3115 with return, no IRS consent, no user fee, late filing relief available
view.s481.proc.attach: Attach Form 3115 to first year's return (signed schedule + supporting computation)
view.s481.proc.audit_consequences: If IRS-audit-initiated: may treat as taxpayer-initiated under Rev. Proc. 2002-18
view.s481.proc.s481_d: § 481(d) recovery of items omitted under prior method - special rules
view.s481.s446.consent: § 446(e) requires consent of Secretary for method change
view.s481.s446.first_year: First year using method: NOT a method change (no § 481(a))
view.s481.s446.correction_of_error: Correction of error in prior method NOT subject to consent (revocation possible)
view.s481.s446.s481_safe: Compliance: filing Form 3115 = "filed return" with method
view.s481.s446.s481_aceflyer: Schedule M-3 / M-1 reconciliation may reveal book/tax difference triggering change
view.s481.s446.short_form: Short Form 3115: certain automatic changes (less detailed)
view.s481.rel.s9100: § 9100 relief — automatic 12-month extension for late Form 3115
view.s481.rel.s6662: § 6662 accuracy-related penalty — may apply if § 481 not properly reported
view.s6038d.sffa.financial_account: Financial accounts maintained by foreign financial institution
view.s6038d.sffa.stock_directly: Foreign stock or securities NOT held in financial account
view.s6038d.sffa.interest_foreign_entity: Interest in foreign entity (PFIC, foreign mutual fund, etc.)
view.s6038d.sffa.financial_instrument: Financial instrument or contract with foreign counterparty
view.s6038d.sffa.life_insurance: Life insurance contracts with cash value with foreign issuer
view.s6038d.pen.base: $10,000 base penalty for failure to file
view.s6038d.pen.continued: $10,000 per 30-day period after IRS notice (capped at $50,000)
view.s6038d.pen.s6662j: § 6662(j) 40% accuracy-related penalty on undisclosed SFFA understatement
view.s6038d.pen.s6501c8: § 6501(c)(8) ASED extension: indefinite for omitted income
view.s6038d.pen.crim_no: No criminal penalty under § 6038D itself (vs § 6038A potentially $25K)
view.s6038d.pen.fbar_overlap: FBAR penalties separate (FinCEN 114): up to $13,481 or 50% balance/willful
view.s6038d.pen.reasonable_cause: Reasonable cause defense (§ 6038D(g)): "reasonable cause + not willful neglect"
view.s6038d.pen.foreign_law: Foreign law non-disclosure does NOT establish reasonable cause
view.s6038d.ent.corp: Domestic corporations: ≥ 50% owned by US persons
view.s6038d.ent.partnership: Domestic partnerships: > 50% ownership by US persons
view.s6038d.ent.trust: Domestic trusts: distribution power held by US person
view.s6038d.ent.threshold_corp: Entity threshold: $50K end-year / $75K anytime (lower than individual)
view.s6038d.ent.cfc: CFC reporting on Form 5471 separate from 8938
view.s318.fam.s302_c2a: § 302(c)(2)(A) waiver: family attribution can be WAIVED for complete-termination redemption
view.s318.opt.included: Person treated as owning underlying stock
view.s318.opt.scope: Options on stock not yet issued — covered
view.s318.opt.s382_options: § 382 has separate options regs — typically more inclusive
view.s318.opt.employee: Employee options: included if currently exercisable
view.s318.opt.no_exercise_no_attribution: Out-of-money options: still treated as ownership (no in-the-money requirement under § 318)
view.s318.reat.family_to_family: § 318(a)(5)(A): NO family-to-family re-attribution (parent's stock → child → child's spouse)
view.s318.reat.option_to_family: § 318(a)(5)(D): NO option-to-family attribution chain (option treated AS stock, then family rule)
view.s318.reat.option_first: Option always attributed FIRST before family
view.s318.reat.s_to_s_yes: Family → S → owner (partnership) → up to top: YES allowed
view.s318.reat.partial_chain: Family → entity → entity OK (no family link to start chain blocked)
view.s318.reat.s302: Important for § 302 redemption tests — count ownership pre/post
view.s318.s302.s302_b_2: § 302(b)(2): substantial disproportion — reduced ownership < 50% AND < 80% × prior
view.s318.s302.s302_e: § 302(e): partial liq — at least 2 active businesses, one continuation
view.s318.s302.qualifying: If qualifies under § 302(b): redemption = sale/exchange (capital gain treatment)
view.s318.s302.non_qual: If not: dividend treatment (ordinary, then capital gain on basis recovery)
view.s1058.req.identical: IDENTICAL securities (same class, same issuer) must be returned
view.s1058.req.substitute_dividends: Substitute dividend / interest payments equal to amounts paid on borrowed securities
view.s1058.req.economic_position: Lender retains risk of loss + opportunity for gain (i.e., economic ownership)
view.s1058.req.at_will: Agreement terminable on DEMAND (at-will) by lender
view.s1058.req.s1058_b: § 1058(b) regs add: collateral requirement, fee documentation, separate account
view.s1058.req.qualified_borrower: Borrower typically registered broker-dealer or qualifying institution
view.s1058.req.documentation: Written master securities lending agreement (e.g., GMSLA, MSLA)
view.s1058.req.collateral_typical: Typical: 102-105% of FMV cash collateral, daily mark-to-market
view.s1058.sub.no_qualified: NOT qualified dividend income (no LTCG rate)
view.s1058.sub.s301_c: Compare § 301(c) actual dividend: capital + cost basis treatment
view.s1058.sub.s871m_30pct: NRA: § 871(m) 30% withholding (or treaty rate) on dividend substitutes
view.s1058.sub.fdap: Treated as US-source FDAP for NRA withholding
view.s1058.sub.s1059: § 1059 extraordinary dividend rules do NOT apply to substitutes
view.s1058.sub.s246_a_3: § 246(a)(3) holding period interruption — STARTS over when securities returned
view.s1058.sub.s263_g: § 263(g) carrying charges: capitalize if offsetting position exists
view.s1058.borrow.s1233: § 1233 short sale rules apply to borrower
view.s1058.borrow.s1259: § 1259 constructive sale if borrower has appreciated position
view.s1058.borrow.s263_g: § 263(g) capitalization of fees + substitute payments to carrying charges
view.s1058.borrow.s1058_c: § 1058(c) — substitute payments paid by borrower = deductible (subject to § 263(g))
view.s1058.borrow.s871m_paymt: § 871(m) dividend equivalent — borrower pays + withholds 30% on NRA payee
view.s1058.borrow.s1259_d: § 1259(c) constructive sale exceptions: closed within 30 days + maintained
view.s1058.s263.scope: Applies to taxpayer holding offsetting position (long stock + securities loan)
view.s1058.s263.capitalize: Substitute payments paid + interest + fees CAPITALIZED to basis
view.s1058.s263.exempt: Net cost basis when offsetting position disposed
view.s1058.s263.applies_borrower: Borrower with appreciated stock long: capitalize substitute payments paid
view.s1058.s263.no_offsetting: If no offsetting position: deductible as ordinary expense (not capitalized)
view.s1058.s263.investment_interest: Net carrying charges may convert to § 163(d) investment interest later
view.s1058.fail.recognition: Recognition event — treated as SALE at FMV (gain to lender)
view.s1058.fail.basis_collateral: Collateral becomes lender's basis in cash received
view.s1058.fail.fail_to_borrow: Fail-to-borrow buy-in: collateral converted to cash, securities buy-back required
view.s1058.fail.identical_returned: If non-identical securities returned: gain/loss on difference
view.s1058.fail.holding_resets: Holding period of returned securities starts new (Reg § 1.1058-2(b)(3))
view.s1058.fail.constructive_sale: § 1259 may apply to lender's position if borrower-disposal triggers
view.s199a.tbl.lower: Lower threshold (no W2/UBIA limit + SSTB OK)
view.s199a.tbl.upper: Upper threshold (full SSTB exclusion, full W2/UBIA limit)
view.s199a.sstb.trading: Trading / dealing in securities, partnership interests, commodities
view.s199a.sstb.reputation: Principal asset of which is reputation or skill
view.s199a.formula.combined: Combined QBI amount = sum of (20% QBI per trade) limited per § 199A(b)(2)
view.s199a.formula.w2_ubia: W2/UBIA limit: greater of (50% × W-2) OR (25% × W-2 + 2.5% × UBIA)
view.s199a.formula.below: Below lower threshold: no W2/UBIA limit, SSTB allowed
view.s199a.formula.phaseout: In phase-in range: partial W2/UBIA limit + partial SSTB allowed
view.s199a.formula.above: Above upper threshold: full W2/UBIA limit + SSTB excluded
view.s199a.formula.reit_ptp: REIT dividends + PTP income: separate 20% category — no W2/UBIA limit
view.s199a.formula.cap: Overall cap: 20% × (taxable income - net capital gain)
view.s199a.formula.aggregation: Aggregation election: aggregate W2/UBIA across same-owner same-class businesses
view.s199a.qbi.included: Included: net income from US trade or business via pass-through (sole prop, partnership, S-corp)
view.s199a.qbi.excluded_reasonable_comp: EXCLUDED: S-corp reasonable compensation to shareholder
view.s199a.qbi.excluded_capital: EXCLUDED: capital gain/loss + dividend income + interest income (most)
view.s199a.qbi.included_se: Included: self-employment income (Schedule C / partnership K-1)
view.s199a.qbi.included_rental: Included: rental real estate IF rises to "trade or business" level (250hr safe harbor)
view.s24.req.relationship: Son, daughter, stepchild, foster child, sibling, half-sibling, stepsibling, descendant
view.s24.req.support: Did NOT provide more than ½ of own support
view.s24.req.citizenship: US citizen / US national / US resident alien
view.s24.req.dependent: Claimed as dependent on taxpayer's return
view.s24.req.joint: Did NOT file joint return (except for refund only)
view.s24.req.ssn: § 24(h)(7) valid SSN by due date of return — post-PATH Act
view.s24.req.itin: ITIN children: get ODC $500 only, NOT $2,000 CTC
view.s24.po.complete_calc: CTC fully phased out at: $240K single (1-child) / $440K MFJ (1-child) — depends on # children
view.s24.po.s32_eitc_no_double: No double-dipping with § 32 EITC for same earned income
view.s24.po.s24_g_3child_more: § 24(g) alternative formula for 3+ children — incl. uncollected SS/Medicare tax
view.s24.po.s8812: Schedule 8812 reports + computes CTC + ODC + ACTC
view.s24.odc.eligible: Qualifying relative (§ 152(d)) — child age 17+, parents, other relatives
view.s24.odc.college: College students up to age 24 (full-time + 5+ months)
view.s24.odc.parents: Parents living separately if claimed as dependent
view.s24.odc.support_test: Support test: taxpayer provides > ½ support
view.s24.odc.no_residency: No residency requirement — qualifying relative need NOT live with taxpayer
view.s24.arpa.fully: Fully refundable — no earned income required
view.s24.arpa.expansion_expired: Expired Dec 31, 2021 — NOT extended by IRA 2022
view.s24.arpa.repayment: Safe harbor protection for excess advance payments based on income
view.s24.arpa.proposals: Reinstatement proposed in BBB / Inflation Reduction Act — not enacted
view.s2032.h1.title: // § 2032 ALTERNATE VALUATION
view.s2032.hint.intro: ELECTION to value gross estate at
view.s2032.req.election: Election made by executor on Form 706 (Part 1, Line 17)
view.s2032.req.reduce_tax: Must REDUCE total federal estate + GST tax
view.s2032.req.both_required: BOTH conditions required (cannot have one without other)
view.s2032.req.timing: Election deadline: due date of Form 706 (incl. extensions) — 9 months + 6 month extension
view.s2032.req.irrevocable: Election IRREVOCABLE once made on timely return
view.s2032.req.all_or_nothing: All or nothing: applies to ENTIRE gross estate
view.s2032.req.purpose: Purpose: relief for estates of declining value (post-mortem market crash)
view.s2032.spec.sold: Sold during 6-month period: use SALE date FMV
view.s2032.spec.distributed: Distributed during period: use DISTRIBUTION date FMV
view.s2032.spec.depreciable: Mere passage of time: use AVD if no significant change
view.s2032.spec.section_2032a: Cannot combine with § 2032A special use valuation (must choose one)
view.s2032.spec.farm_real_estate: Farm real estate: AVD often less than § 2032A special use
view.s2032.spec.partnership_interests: Partnership interests: subject to ongoing valuation discounts
view.s2032.spec.life_insurance: Life insurance: paid in cash before 6 months — payout date used
view.s2032.bd.benefit_estate_tax: BENEFIT: reduce estate tax (40% top rate) in declining market
view.s2032.bd.benefit_appreciated: BENEFIT: also reduces income tax basis (§ 1014 → AVD value)
view.s2032.bd.benefit_post_mortem: BENEFIT: hedge against post-mortem market crash
view.s2032.bd.drawback_basis: DRAWBACK: lower step-up basis = more capital gain on future sale
view.s2032.bd.drawback_appreciated: DRAWBACK: NOT useful for estate near exemption (no tax to save)
view.s2032.bd.consideration_growth: CONSIDERATION: if estate grew, AVD increases tax — election rejected
view.s2032.bd.coordination_marital: Marital deduction: AVD may slightly affect QTIP calculations
view.s2032.bd.executor_judgment: Executor judgment: monitor 6-month value, decide before return deadline
view.s2032.coord.s1014: § 1014 basis step-up: heirs' basis = AVD value (lower step-up if AVD lower)
view.s2032.coord.s2032a: § 2032A special use: alternative valuation method (real property only)
view.s2032.coord.s2056: § 2056 marital deduction: applies to AVD-valued property
view.s2032.coord.s6166: § 6166 installment: extended payment if AVD applied
view.s2032.coord.s2503: § 2503 gift tax: prior 3-yr gifts NOT subject to AVD
view.s2032.coord.portability: DSUE portability (§ 2010(c)(4)): consider impact on surviving spouse
view.s2032.coord.disclaimer: § 2518 disclaimers: separate election affecting AVD analysis
view.s2032.not_eligible_note: Election NOT eligible: estate value INCREASED OR estate tax NOT reduced. § 2032 requires BOTH conditions. Likely scenarios: market recovery, exemption fully covers estate (no tax benefit), or growing assets. Default to date-of-death valuation. Consider § 2032A special use for real property if applicable.
view.s956.usp.rights: Right to acquire US property (option, contract)
view.s956.usp.partnership: Interest in domestic partnership (with US partners)
view.s956.usp.exclusions: EXCLUSIONS § 956(c)(2): inventory, obligations < 30 days, Treasury securities, certain insurance, US bank deposits ($1.4M cap)
view.s956.usp.s245a_dividend_eligible: Post-TCJA: § 956 inclusion reduced if § 245A would otherwise apply (Reg § 1.956-1(a)(2))
view.s956.comp.average: Calculate AVERAGE quarterly balance of US property
view.s956.comp.increase: Increase over prior year = US property "invested"
view.s956.comp.lesser: § 956(a) inclusion = LESSER of: (1) increase OR (2) E&P
view.s956.comp.character: Inclusion treated as ORDINARY (NOT qualified dividend)
view.s956.comp.no_drd: NO § 245A DRD on § 956 inclusion (subject to hybrid mismatch rules)
view.s956.comp.reduces_ep: Subsequent dividend tax-free via PTI ordering — pre-2018 timing
view.s956.exc.inventory: Property used in active US trade or business (limited)
view.s956.exc.bank_deposits: US bank deposits up to $1.4M aggregate per CFC
view.s956.exc.s956_c_2_g: § 956(c)(2)(G) — assets used for foreign branch operations
view.s956.exc.s956_c_3: § 956(c)(3) — special rules for shipping, aircraft, container business
view.s956.pg.included: CFC guarantee of US obligation = investment in US property
view.s956.pg.s956_d_1: § 956(d)(1) — pledge of stock of US affiliate
view.s956.pg.s956_d_2: § 956(d)(2) — guarantee of debt incurred for US benefit
view.s956.pg.economic_substance: No requirement that guarantee be CALLED — mere existence triggers
view.s956.pg.indirect_pledge: Indirect pledges (assignment of CFC receivables) — covered
view.s956.pg.common_structure: Common: US parent borrows + CFC subsidiary guarantees → § 956 inclusion
view.s956.pg.s245a_relief: Post-TCJA § 245A reduces 65/35 split for hybrid CFCs
view.s956.pg.fmv_basis: FMV of pledged stock / guaranteed obligation determines amount
view.s956.s245a.purpose: Eliminates § 956 to extent dividend would qualify for § 245A 100% DRD
view.s956.s245a.partnerships_not_covered: Partnerships NOT eligible for § 245A — full § 956 applies
view.s956.s245a.gilti_treatment: GILTI subpart F inclusions: separate analysis
view.s962.mech.rate: Pay tax at CORPORATE 21% rate (vs individual 37%)
view.s962.mech.s960: § 960 indirect FTC: 80% of foreign tax × pro-rata share
view.s962.mech.basis_pti: § 962(b): basis in PTI = corporate tax paid
view.s962.mech.dividend: Subsequent CFC dividends: taxable to extent exceed § 962(b) basis
view.s962.mech.no_step_up: NO step-up in CFC stock basis from inclusions
view.s962.mech.binding: Binding for that year — cannot revoke
view.s962.uc.high_tax_cfc: CFC in high-tax jurisdiction (Ireland 12.5%, Israel 23%, etc.)
view.s962.uc.large_inclusion: Large GILTI inclusion that would otherwise be at 37%
view.s962.uc.no_distribution: Plan NOT to distribute CFC earnings near-term
view.s962.uc.tested_loss: Multiple CFCs with mixed tested income/loss (aggregation benefits)
view.s962.uc.qualified_basis: Sufficient QBAI to reduce tested income inclusion
view.s962.uc.basis_growth: Plan to grow basis via PTI for later tax-free distribution
view.s962.trap.dividend: Subsequent CFC dividend: taxable AGAIN (no § 245A DRD for individuals)
view.s962.trap.smith_v_commissioner: Smith v. Comm. — pre-2018 case held § 962 dividend treated as PTI distribution
view.s962.trap.s962b_basis_dividend: § 962(b)(2) basis only reduces dividend to extent of corp tax paid
view.s962.trap.state_tax: State tax: most states do not respect § 962 election — full individual rate
view.s962.trap.amt: Pre-TCJA AMT: § 962 didn't apply to AMT
view.s962.trap.s250_loss: If tested loss, § 250 deduction limited to tested income
view.s962.trap.s86: Social Security taxation NOT affected (still individual income)
view.s962.alt.entity_blocker: Insert US C-corp blocker between individual + CFC
view.s962.alt.partnership: Use partnership instead of CFC (no § 951(a) inclusion)
view.s962.alt.expatriation: Expatriation (§ 877A) — but covered expatriate rules apply
view.s962.alt.distribute_first: Distribute pre-2018 E&P first (transition tax already paid)
view.s962.alt.dividend_treaty: Treaty country: reduce withholding on later dividend
view.s731.basic.gain: § 731(a)(1) — gain only to extent cash > outside basis
view.s731.basic.loss: § 731(a)(2) — loss only on LIQUIDATION + cash + receivables + inventory distributed
view.s731.basic.s731c_reduction: § 731(c)(3) reduction: pro-rata share of partnership's gain in securities
view.s731.basic.no_intermediate: No "intermediate" gain on appreciated non-cash property distributions
view.s731.tbl.lesser: Lesser of (P/S basis in property) OR (partner outside basis)
view.s731.tbl.equal_basis: Partner outside basis allocated among property
view.s731.tbl.r_basis: Carryover basis (or lower of basis / FMV)
view.s731.tbl.remaining: Remaining basis allocated by relative basis + FMV
view.s731.s737.purpose: Anti-abuse: contributing partner recognizes precontribution gain if other property distributed within 7 years
view.s731.s737.formula: Gain = LESSER of (precontribution gain) OR (excess of distributed FMV over outside basis)
view.s731.s737.character: Character: same as contributed property would have on partnership sale
view.s731.s737.basis_inc: Basis in distributed property: § 732 — but PLUS § 737(d) gain recognized
view.s731.s737.partner_only: Only contributing partner — not other partners
view.s731.s737.s704c_relation: Coordinates with § 704(c)(1)(B) similar gain recognition rule
view.s731.s737.s721_exception: § 721 contributions not triggering: gift, like-kind, charitable, etc.
view.s731.s731c.fmv: Distributed at FMV — treated as MONEY for § 731(a)(1)
view.s731.s731c.defined: Securities = stock, bonds, options, futures, foreign currency, derivatives
view.s731.s731c.actively_traded: "Actively traded" = OTC market or established exchange
view.s731.s731c.reduction: § 731(c)(3) reduces FMV by partner's share of unrealized gain
view.s731.s731c.formula: Reduction = (partner's distributive share) × (PS unrealized gain in security)
view.s731.s731c.s721_contributed: Securities CONTRIBUTED by partner: exempt from § 731(c) treatment
view.s731.s731c.s721_purchased: Securities PURCHASED by partnership: subject to § 731(c)
view.s731.s731c.basis_property: Partner's basis in distributed securities: § 732 (cash treatment)
view.s731.s751b.hot_assets: "Hot assets" = unrealized receivables + substantially appreciated inventory
view.s731.s751b.disproportionate: If partner receives MORE / LESS hot assets than pro-rata share
view.s731.s751b.exchange: Treated as TAXABLE EXCHANGE between partner + partnership
view.s731.s751b.deemed_sale: Deemed sale at FMV of disproportionate amount
view.s731.s751b.character: Ordinary income to recipient on hot assets received in excess
view.s731.s751b.capital_gain: Capital gain to recipient on cold assets received in excess
view.s731.s751b.s751a: § 751(a) different: applies to sale of partnership interest (always)
view.s731.s751b.aggregate_basis: Partnership aggregate basis in hot assets adjusted accordingly
view.s6221.eo.eligible: ALL partners ELIGIBLE: individuals, C-corps, S-corps, estates, foreign individuals, eligible foreign entities
view.s6221.eo.ineligible: INELIGIBLE: partnerships (any), non-grantor trusts, disregarded entities, foreign LLCs
view.s6221.eo.election_annual: Election made ANNUALLY on timely-filed Form 1065 + Schedule B-2
view.s6221.eo.consequences: If elected: TEFRA-like partner-level audits — each partner's return adjusted separately
view.s6221.eo.disclosure: Election requires disclosure of each partner's TIN + ownership %
view.s6221.eo.s_corp_count: S-corp counted as 1 partner BUT S-corp shareholders counted toward 100 limit
view.s6221.eo.bba_default: Default if no election: BBA centralized regime applies
view.s6221.imp.adjustments: Net all partnership-level adjustments to reviewed year items
view.s6221.imp.modifications: § 6225 modifications reduce: amended partner returns + lower rates for specific partners
view.s6221.imp.netting: Netting only across SAME taxable year + partnership
view.s6221.imp.interest: Interest from due date of reviewed year return
view.s6221.push.purpose: Shift tax liability from partnership to REVIEWED-YEAR partners
view.s6221.push.timing: Elected within 45 DAYS of final partnership adjustment
view.s6221.push.notice: Partnership issues PUSH-OUT STATEMENT to each reviewed-year partner
view.s6221.push.partners_amend: Each partner files amended return for reviewed year (or pays tax via current year)
view.s6221.push.tiered: Tiered partnerships: passes through to UPPER partners
view.s6221.push.s6226_b: § 6226(b) — payable in CURRENT (adjustment) year by partners
view.s6221.push.s6226_c: § 6226(c) — partners include interest in adjustment year
view.s6221.push.partner_character: Character preserved at PARTNER level (different from default § 6225)
view.s6221.push.s6226_d: § 6226(d) — penalties at partnership AND partner level
view.s6221.push.coordination: Often preferred where partners' actual tax rate < 37%
view.s6221.pr.replaces_tmp: REPLACES TMP (Tax Matters Partner) in TEFRA
view.s6221.pr.single_contact: SINGLE point of contact with IRS
view.s6221.pr.binding: PR's actions BIND partnership + all partners
view.s6221.pr.individual_or_entity: Can be INDIVIDUAL or ENTITY (entity must have substantial US presence)
view.s6221.pr.designated_individual: If entity PR: must designate INDIVIDUAL to act for entity
view.s6221.pr.cannot_revoke: PR cannot revoke once designated except during audit (special procedure)
view.s6221.pr.no_partner_status: PR need NOT be partner — independent professional acceptable
view.s6221.pr.s6223_b: § 6223(b) — IRS may designate PR if none designated
view.s6221.pr.indemnification: Common: indemnification + insurance for PR liability
view.s6221.aar.purpose: PARTNERSHIP-level amended return (replaces amended Schedule K-1)
view.s6221.aar.no_partner_amend: Partners CANNOT file amended return for partnership items individually
view.s6221.aar.timing: Within 3 years of original return filing + before issuance of notice of audit
view.s6221.aar.imputed: Imputed underpayment OR push-out election available
view.s6221.aar.s6227_b: § 6227(b) — special rules for partnership-level adjustments increasing income
view.s6221.aar.qualified_amended: Qualifies for § 6664(c) penalty relief (qualified amended return)
view.s6330.proc.notice: IRS issues Final Notice of Intent to Levy + Right to a Hearing (Letter 1058 / CP90 / Letter 11)
view.s6330.proc.30_days: Taxpayer has 30 DAYS from notice DATE to request CDP hearing
view.s6330.proc.f12153: File Form 12153 — Request for Collection Due Process Hearing
view.s6330.proc.suspension: Filing SUSPENDS collection (no levy) until hearing complete + appeals exhausted
view.s6330.proc.sol_tolled: Collection SOL (§ 6502 — 10 years) is TOLLED during hearing + appeal
view.s6330.proc.appeals: Appeals officer conducts hearing — taxpayer can raise § 6330(c) matters
view.s6330.proc.notice_det: Notice of Determination issued by Appeals — concludes hearing
view.s6330.proc.petition_30: 30 days from Notice of Determination to petition Tax Court (§ 6330(d))
view.s6330.proc.tax_court: Tax Court hearing — reviews CDP determination
view.s6330.proc.collection_resumes: If no petition or after Tax Court rules: collection action may resume
view.s6330.m.underlying: Challenges to underlying liability — ONLY if no prior opportunity
view.s6330.m.appropriate: Appropriateness of collection action — alternatives vs intrusion
view.s6330.m.spousal_relief_first_time: Spousal defense allowed even if previously could have raised
view.s6330.m.under_liab_test: "Prior opportunity" = received Statutory Notice of Deficiency OR participated in pre-assessment audit
view.s6330.tbl.ia_note: Streamlined ≤$50K / Direct Debit / Partial Pay IA
view.s6330.tbl.oic_note: Doubt as to liability / collectibility / ETA
view.s6330.std.abuse: "Abuse of discretion" — standard for collection alternatives, spousal defenses, appropriateness
view.s6330.std.de_novo: "De novo" — review of underlying liability (when properly at issue)
view.s6330.std.factors: Abuse of discretion factors: arbitrary, capricious, without rational basis
view.s6330.std.s6330_c_3: § 6330(c)(3) — Appeals officer required to balance need for collection vs intrusion
view.s6330.std.lunsford: Lunsford v. Comm. — equivalent hearing not subject to Tax Court review (vs CDP)
view.s6330.std.davis: Davis v. Comm. — Tax Court reviews Appeals' OIC denial for abuse of discretion
view.s6330.std.thomas: Thomas v. Comm. — taxpayer must offer alternative + cooperate (cannot simply refuse)
view.s6330.std.administrative_record: Administrative record review (no new evidence outside hearing typically)
view.s6330.std.s6330_c_2_a: § 6330(c)(2)(A)(i) — verification IRS followed all law + admin procedure
view.s6330.coord.s6320: § 6320 — parallel CDP for LIEN notice (within 5 business days of NFTL)
view.s6330.coord.s6502: § 6502 — collection SOL (10 years) TOLLED during CDP + appeal
view.s6330.coord.jeopardy: Jeopardy levy: § 6330(f) NO CDP — § 7429 separate review
view.s6330.coord.state_levy: State refund levy: NO CDP (§ 6330(f) exception)
view.s6330.coord.federal_employee_pay: Federal employee pay levy: NO CDP for some IRS-determined cases
view.s6330.coord.equivalent: Equivalent hearing: missed 30-day window — within 1 year possible (NO Tax Court review)
view.s6330.coord.s7345: § 7345 passport revocation for seriously delinquent tax debts ($62K+ in 2024)
view.s6330.coord.audit_recon: Audit reconsideration: separately request review of underlying tax
view.s6330.coord.tco: Taxpayer Advocate Service (TAO) — collaborative review when systemic burden
view.s6330.coord.bankruptcy: Bankruptcy filing: automatic stay (§ 362) suspends collection + CDP
view.s6112.contents.name: Name + address of each advisee
view.s6112.contents.advice: COPY of all written advice provided
view.s6112.contents.fees: All fees received from each advisee
view.s6112.contents.separate: Separate list for each REPORTABLE TRANSACTION category
view.s6112.prod.request: IRS written request from Office of Tax Shelter Analysis (OTSA)
view.s6112.prod.no_cap: NO CAP on penalty — can exceed $1M easily
view.s6112.prod.attorney_general: Attorney general may also pursue § 7402 summons
view.s6112.prod.s6708_b: § 6708(b) — reasonable cause + good-faith effort defense available
view.s6112.prod.compliance: Voluntary delivery within 21 days completely avoids penalty
view.s6112.priv.s7525_tax_shelter: § 7525 does NOT cover written tax shelter promotion
view.s6112.priv.attorney_client: Attorney-client: protects legal advice, NOT lists of clients
view.s6112.priv.crime_fraud: Crime-fraud exception: if used in furtherance of fraud (Notice 2009-7)
view.s6112.priv.work_product: Work product: NOT shielded for client lists or fee disclosures
view.s6112.priv.privilege_log: Must provide privilege log + specific identification
view.s6112.priv.in_camera: In camera review by judge possible (limited scope)
view.s6112.priv.identifying: Identifying clients NOT privileged in most circuits (Fisher v. US)
view.s6112.coord.s6707: § 6707 — material advisor penalty for failure to file 8918
view.s6112.coord.s6701: § 6701 — aiding understatement of liability ($1K-$10K per occurrence)
view.s6112.coord.opr: OPR Circular 230 disciplinary proceedings — license sanctions
view.s1059.ex.s1059_c_2: 85-day aggregation also for 365-day window if cumulative ≥ 20% basis
view.s1059.ex.no_actual_distribution: Constructive dividends + § 305(c) deemed distributions also subject
view.s1059.ex.s1059_a_4_election: § 1059(a)(4) FMV election: substitute FMV basis instead of adjusted basis
view.s1059.ex.fmv_election: Useful when stock acquired at significant premium
view.s1059.hp.exemption: Holding period > 2 YEARS before declaration date: § 1059 typically NOT apply
view.s1059.hp.failed_2yr: < 2-year holding: § 1059 ALWAYS applies regardless of size
view.s1059.hp.measurement: Measured from acquisition to declaration date
view.s1059.hp.s246_a_2: § 246(a)(2) — actual holding period requirements for DRD eligibility (45 days)
view.s1059.hp.qualified_purchase: Purchased post-Aug 31, 1995: post-TIPRA amendments accelerated gain rather than disposition deferral
view.s1059.br.nontaxed: Reduce by NON-TAXED portion = dividend × DRD %
view.s1059.br.s245: § 245 100% DRD: dividends from less-than-20% owned domestic that is foreign-source
view.s1059.br.s1059_b_below_zero: Basis reduced below $0 → excess = capital gain CURRENT year
view.s1059.br.s1059_c_calculation: For multiple stocks held, allocate basis reduction pro-rata
view.s1059.br.holding_period: Holding period of remaining stock NOT affected
view.s1059.ex.dividend_stripping: "Dividend stripping" historically: buy at $100, $50 dividend with DRD, sell at $50 = $50 loss
view.s1059.ex.now_blocked: Post-§ 1059: $50 dividend × 50% DRD = $25 basis reduction → no artificial loss
view.s1059.ex.s246a_no_drd: § 246A — partially debt-financed stock: reduced DRD percentage
view.s1059.ex.s246c_45day: § 246(c) 45-day holding (90 days for preferred) — to qualify for DRD
view.s1059.ex.s246d_short: § 246(d) — short sale + similar offsetting position eliminates DRD
view.s1059.ex.s1059_e_partial_liquidatio: § 1059(e) partial liquidation: treated as extraordinary regardless of size
view.s1059.ex.s301_excess: § 301(c)(2) basis recovery + § 301(c)(3) capital gain — separate timing
view.s1059.rel.s162a: § 162(k) — golden parachute → ordinary deduction denied (similar anti-abuse)

tile.yield-curve.desc: UST 3M/2Y/5Y/10Y/30Y shape classifier: normal / flat / inverted / humped
tile.cost-basis.label: Cost Basis
tile.cost-basis.desc: FIFO / LIFO / HIFO / LOFO lot selection + tax-optimal recommender
tile.accounts-overview.label: Accounts Overview
tile.walk-forward.label: Walk-forward
tile.strategy-alerts.label: Strategy Alerts
tile.csv-wizard.label: CSV Wizard
tile.keyboard-shortcuts.label: Shortcuts
tile.keyboard-shortcuts.desc: Cheat sheet of every keyboard shortcut (also `?` hotkey)
tile.kyles-lambda.label: Kyle's Lambda
tile.kyles-lambda.desc: Kyle (1985) rolling price-impact slope λ = Σxy / Σx². Liquidity depth + flow-sign regime detector
tile.atr-trailing-stop.label: ATR Trail Stop
tile.atr-trailing-stop.desc: Long & short trailing stops at N×ATR from close, ratcheted in favorable direction — companion to Chandelier / Parabolic SAR
tile.bartlett-variance.label: Bartlett σ² Test
tile.bartlett-variance.desc: Bartlett (1937) test for equality of variances across ≥ 2 groups — χ² statistic + Wilson-Hilferty p-value
tile.bid-ask-volume-ratio.label: Bid/Ask Vol
tile.bid-ask-volume-ratio.desc: Rolling Σ bid / Σ ask order-flow imbalance — 7-tier flow verdict + trend + imbalance magnitude
tile.bollinger-band-width.label: BBW + %B
tile.bollinger-band-width.desc: Bollinger Band Width + %B — squeeze/expansion detector + 7-tier %B position; dual uPlot (bands + width)
tile.bollinger-bandwidth-percentile.desc: Bollinger Bandwidth Percentile — vol-regime rank [0, 100] over 252-bar lookback; squeeze trigger detector
tile.bollinger-percent-b.label: Bollinger %B
tile.bollinger-percent-b.desc: Standalone Bollinger %B — close position in band envelope; 7-tier zone + breakout/breakdown cross detector
tile.bollinger-band-distance.label: BB Distance
tile.bollinger-band-distance.desc: Distance to nearest Bollinger Band normalized by width — 0 = at band, 0.5 = midline, > 0.5 = outside
tile.bollinger-oscillators.label: BB Oscillators
tile.bollinger-oscillators.desc: Combined %B + Bandwidth oscillators — 7-tier %B zone + 5-tier bandwidth-percentile vol regime + TTM-squeeze detector
tile.borrow-rate-indicator.label: Borrow Rate
tile.borrow-rate-indicator.desc: Annualized securities-lending fee + N-bar Δ% — 5-tier hard-to-borrow stress classifier; squeeze-risk gauge
tile.candle-strength-index.label: CSI
tile.candle-strength-index.desc: Candle Strength Index — EMA of (close − open) / (high − low) body-to-range ratio; ±1 = marubozu
tile.carhart-4.label: Carhart 4-Factor
tile.carhart-4.desc: Carhart (1997) Mkt + SMB + HML + WML factor regression — α, β loadings, t-stats, R²; style tilt verdict
tile.centered-smoothed-momentum.label: CSM
tile.centered-smoothed-momentum.desc: Ehlers Centered Smoothed Momentum — SuperSmoother-filtered momentum; zero-cross signals trend turn
tile.chaikin-oscillator.label: Chaikin Osc
tile.chaikin-oscillator.desc: Chaikin Oscillator — MACD-style EMA(ADL, fast) − EMA(ADL, slow); zero-crosses + price divergences
tile.chande-dynamic-momentum.label: Chande DMI
tile.chande-dynamic-momentum.desc: Chande Dynamic Momentum Index — volatility-adaptive RSI (period stretches in quiet markets, shrinks in vol)
tile.chande-kroll-stop.label: Chande-Kroll
tile.chande-kroll-stop.desc: Chande-Kroll Stop — two-pass volatility trailing stop (HH/LL ± x·ATR, smoothed); long+short bands + regime
tile.chande-momentum-oscillator.label: Chande CMO
tile.chande-momentum-oscillator.desc: Chande Momentum Oscillator — unsmoothed RSI variant; ±100 range; 6-state cross detector + 7-tier zones
tile.chande-trend-index.label: Chande CTI
tile.chande-trend-index.desc: Chande Trend Index — correlation of closes vs linear ramp; pure trend-strength metric ∈ [−1, +1]
tile.chande-volatility-index.label: Chande CVI
tile.chande-volatility-index.desc: Chande Volatility Index — % change in EMA of high-low range; flags expanding/contracting volatility regimes
tile.backtest-presets.label: BT Presets
tile.custom-indicators.label: Indicators

view.bond_duration.validate.ytm_finite: ytm must be a finite number
view.bond_duration.validate.compounding: compounding must be a positive integer
view.cost_basis.validate.lots_array: lots must be a non-empty array
view.cost_basis.validate.price_finite: price_per_share must be a finite number
view.cost_basis.validate.method: method must be FIFO / LIFO / HIFO / specific
view.hawkes.validate.events_sorted: events must be sorted ascending by timestamp
view.hawkes_intensity.parse.timestamp_fi: timestamp must be a finite number
view.hhi.validate.weights_array: weights must be a non-empty array
view.hhi.validate.weight_finite: each weight must be a finite number ≥ 0
view.monte_carlo.validate.n_curves: n_curves must be a positive integer
view.monte_carlo.validate.trades_per_cur: trades_per_curve must be a positive integer
view.stop_loss_backtest.validate.trades_: trades must be a non-empty array
view.stop_loss_backtest.validate.method: method must be one of: none / fixed_dollar / fixed_pct / atr_multiple
view.stop_loss_backtest.validate.value_f: stop value must be a finite number
view.stop_loss_backtest.validate.side_lo: side_long must be a boolean (long: true / short: false)
view.var_estimator.validate.returns_arra: returns must be a non-empty array
view.vpin.validate.return_window: return_window must be a positive integer
view.vpin.validate.window_buckets: window_buckets must be a positive integer
view.dividend_calendar.hint.finnhub: Market-wide upcoming ex-dividend dates across all listed companies, sourced from the Nasdaq dividend feed. Yield (annualized ÷ price) is filled best-effort for the soonest names.
view.dividend_calendar.tip.filter: Filter the calendar by ticker or company name (client-side, no refetch)
